reviews.comment: Honestly the rudest professor I've had so far at cornell . Pretends like she wants her students to laugh and foster and comfortable environment for learning but does the opposite. Would not take a class with her again.

This professor is very sarcastic. She makes the lecture environment very uncomfortable with shady remarks towards people's questions, for example. To put it quite bluntly, she's rude to students. She acts like she has a surperiority complex about her teaching style. I am sure many of the reviews would agree with this.

She seems convinced her teaching method is inherently superior and beyond criticism. Team-Based Learning is treated more like a belief system than a tool, despite working poorly for many students. The focus feels ego-driven rather than student-centered, with too much time wasted on forced group work.

most of her bad reviews are from the class itself. Abby's a pretty nice prof, and offers to go over missed problems from the prelim + lots of extra cred. that said, BIOEE1780 has an excessive amount of work. lots of memorization, field trips, and pre-lecture assignments. tip: vodcasts and lectures are a MUST, but dont waste your time on the reading

I wrote a bad review of Prof. Drake, and felt bad so had it removed. Retrospectively, I wish I kept it up. She is the single worst professor I've had at Cornell who will literally laugh in your face and make you feel small. The subject matter of her course was interesting, but the course structure itself was painstakingly boring. I warn everyone!!
Repetitive lectures, but overall interesting course
If you take the time to complete all the lectures and assignments, the class is extremely easy

If this class isn't required, I strongly recommend not taking it. It's mostly mindless memorization with 2-3 hours of online lectures before class. The class is poorly structured, time-consuming, and boring, with lectures filled with quiz questions worth 30% of your grade. Prelims were easy but included trick questions meant to lower your score.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: Boring class. Wouldn't recommend it unless it's required for your major. Had to memorize the Tree of Life, which required a lot of work while also seeming completely useless. Exams were fair as long as you study.
I knew I should have avoided this professor. Avoid her at all costs!

Attendance is 100% mandatory because of clicker questions during each lecture and 5 question quizzes to start to see if you did the homework. Yes, it is annoying, but if you do the work (which there's a lot of), you'll be fine. Material isn't necessarily difficult, just a lot of memorization. If you put time and effort in, you will get an A or A+.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: This was an incredibly boring class. Took it as a non-stem kid for my bio credit, and it was torturous. The kids in your group make a huge difference, and the effort was low all around. Constant tedious work so it's easy to fall behind. Had a bad interaction with a TA who refused to let me make up an assignment.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: The amount of grunt work this class gives (for no reason) is absolutely appalling. The only thing the flipped classroom structure does is waste your time outside of class. Not happy at all with how the course is taught. The professors claim that research on flipped classrooms is more beneficial but research findings change all the time...

Flipped classroom style - lots of repetitive busy work and in-class sessions were mostly sitting around. Frustrating miscommunications (TA told me exam was open note, announcement days before exam that it wasn't). BUT they changed stuff after mid-semester evals, so she does care about students. Not a hard class, but requires time and memorization

A hybrid class where you watch hours videos each week, spending class doing quizzes. A class Discord, no Ed, where you must post with your real name if you want help. A course email where you will be ignored. A lack of TAs leading to rushed discussions. A condescending professor. All this plus more make for the worst class I have ever taken here.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: One of the worst professors I've had the privilege of taking a class with. Listening to her feels like bad chalk on a blackboard. I don't encourage anyone to take a class with her.

Disclaimer: I took 1781 asynchronously, which isn't an option here so I picked 1780, which has the same content but diff structure.
Excellent lectures that force you to learn, as well as helpful assignments. Very time-consuming course. Tests are open book. Prof is not very responsive to questions.
I recommend this course only if you have time.

Took BIOEE4940 Biostats w her over summer. Inconsistent in responding to questions emailed in abt material (she'll answer some questions in an email but not others??), tested on material that was never taught (as in "oops-here's the R code for that"), sent out an exam for material that hadn't been covered yet (granted, this was quickly fixed).

1780 is heavy on memorization but offers lots of room to succeed (total exams make up 1/3 of grade, the rest is essentially participation). Compared to bioee 1610, 1780 requires more studying and outside work but has a higher median in the end fwiw. Abby clearly cares about her students' learning and is enthusiastic about he material.

My main complaint about Abby's teaching is that recorded lectures were long and repetitive (2x speed is a must). In-person "lectures" were annoying and not very helpful review sessions. She can come across as a bit condescending, but she's at least enthusiastic about the material. I don't think anyone needs to dread her class.
Lectures were meh, lots of work and lectures, wasn't a huge fan of flipped classroom model, but at least the course was easy.

Class is easy but 100% memorization based. You have to learn the entire tree of life, which is rly hard. My semester was online, which made it slightly easier. The profs and TAs are very accessible. Grading is split up into a lot of small things, which means you have to try hard on everything to do well, which is annoying but overall good class.

Passive aggressive for no reason, as someone else mentioned in their rating. We understood the assignment without writing down anything (it was not required, we just had to get the answers), and she tells us as a group “if you guys are guessing I wonder how you’ll guess through the exam”. We weren’t guessing. Be cautious. Simple class.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Professor Drake is unnecessarily passive aggressive. We were completing a team assignment in lecture (we did it very quickly without actually doing the assignment), and she came up to us and talked to us for 5 minutes about how we didn't do the assignment (even though we got all the questions right). Beware. Otherwise, the class is really easy.

She's kind of crazy but in a good way. This class uses flipped classroom/active learning, which worked really well for me as a more independent learner -- it's just a heavy workload. They do a great job of teaching the material, rotating lecturers. Abby is very caring and a good communicator. Also interesting material!

This class is essentially entirely online. There are weekly pre-lecture modules containing video lectures and quizzes. Unfortunately, that doesn't mean you can skip the in-person lectures as there are iClicker questions. The content is simple and the prelims were straightforward. Study the learning objectives and memorize the TOL and you're set.

As others said, 1780 has many in-class quizzes. If you do take it, take good notes on the pre-lecture videos, as that's what the exams will actually cover. The material is interesting, and Professor Drake is very nice, but the teaching style didn't work for me. Take BIOEE 1610 if you just want to fulfill the A&S biology distribution requirement.

I'm pretty sure if I took this class in-person it would be a nightmare. She may take things slow during lectures, but the material was fascinating. You can tell that she loves teaching the class. The class was straightforward enough to allow for a strong understanding of material. It did help that I was online and exams were open-note, otherwise...

Dr. Drake seems to have good intentions and did want us to learn. Otherwise, I disliked her as a professor. The class was nothing but painful memorization. The tests were written very unclearly, and the lectures were pointless. I learned more about evolution in the short time we spent on it in my AP bio class in HS.

I don't usually review literally anything, but I loved this course, so I had to. It's an active learning class, with heavy emphasis on class participation through small group activities and iClicker questions. A lot of people don't like this, but I have ADHD and it was beyond perfect. Dr. Drake is great, attentive, and helpful.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Professor Drake is the real mastermind behind 1780. She is nice and well meaning, but her voice can be annoying as it constantly sounds on the edge of a nervous breakdown. She is a huge fan of "Active Learning", which basically means tons of in class clicker quizzes. Lectures are pointless otherwise - all the actual teaching is done via videos.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Awful course. She's the worst lecturer and she runs the course. Prepare to drown in iclicker questions, section assignments, and readings that are really nothing but busywork.

As mentioned previously, not the biggest fan of the structure of the class (way too participation-heavy, interactive, and elementary), but the material itself was very interesting and at times relevant. Exams are easy. Take good notes on/study from vodcasts, show up to lecture and section, and keep up with the material and you'll do fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: This class is very memorization heavy and the "flipped classroom" can be highly frustrating. A lot of the learning is done through homework, while the actual lecture is a lot of quizzing. However, Abby is very caring and seems to really want her students to do well. Your grade in this class is based on more than just a couple of tests.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Caring

reviews.comment: dull class, lots of pointless memorization.

Makes many mistakes. Not organized at all. Class is all over the place. The interactive classroom thing is not effective. Class is essentially useless if you're not going into evolutionary science. All memorization. Wish I never took it.
Dr. Drake is on the teaching team for an intro bio course (1780) that is heavy on memorization of taxa and phylogenies. If you can memorize you will be fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: Took 1780 class can really drag and is a lot of brute memorization of phylogeny and taxa no one would ever need to know unless a researcher in the field nor will remember after the test. Also not the biggest fan of flipped classroom, very little actual material presented about half of class is spent solely on testing

Dr. Abby Drake was one of the professors in the teaching team when I took 1780. She was definitely into the subject she was teaching, but in all honesty, memorization of phylogenetic trees is dull at best. I didn't like her "flipped classroom" method, but that being said, she is a really caring professor who wants to help her students succeed

This FWS is 2x the work of others, but you emerge with a very solid grasp on expository writing and maybe even a new worldview. You also get helpful advice from your TA--most FWS's lack one. Dr. G himself is an acquired taste. To some, he is refreshingly blunt; to others, he seems self-important. To the former camp (me), he's pretty fun to talk to.

This class gives you what you put in.. or so I thought. Take this class if you need to fulfill FWS and don't mind getting a high B or low A. Professor is caring and the class lectures are extremely surface-level. The real education is when you do the readings and write on your own. I recommend just reading John Berger's "Ways of Seeing" instead.

Awesome Professor! His class improved my writing significantly. He was especially helpful in individual meetings and always gave insightful feedback on my work. I believe, however, that this class grades harsher relative to other FWS. If you want an easy A, this is not the class for you. If you want to improve your writing, then I highly recommend.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Gives good feedback--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Fun and Engaging! Great Professor if your main goal is to improve your writing capabilities, Otherwise it isn't worth it to take the class as a freshman, grading is comparatively harsh compared to other offerings, with courses offering B's just by showing up to class, choose those if you are just trying to fulfill your FWS requirement.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Gives good feedback--So many papers

reviews.comment: Mostly lectures and tended to go over the time of the class by like 10 minutes consistently. Somewhat easy but lowkey didn't complete the amount we were supposed to finish the semester so the next semester we had to do more to make up for it.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: A very enjoyable professor, very knowledgeable and open to questions. All material is on printed notes handed out each class, but not much of it will make sense if you don't go to lecture. I did have his class in the late afternoon and his voice put me to sleep several times, but not for lack of interesting content.

Great professor. Really laid back, doesn't give many homework assignments or tests. Essentially, he'll teach the amount you're willing to learn. We read Ovid--about as interesting as Nussbaum himself, but it's not like classes are dreadfully boring.Very nice, funny man. Looks like he's headed towards retirement, take a class of his while you can.

Lowkey a goated prof, cares about students, explains concepts very well, passionate. Exams were all fair, basically no HW, u needed to just study yourself. Exams includes proofs, some were chill, some were harder.

*math 2930

Makes sure students understands the material and grading system reduces work on future exams. Explains concepts very clearly
YES THE BEST
great prof but I fumbled a bit too much on exams

One of the most wonderful and caring professors I've had at Cornell. His lecture slides were amazing for learning all of the content necessary. The class was very hard because of the lack of a curve and very proof-heavy exams, but completely doable if you put in the effort to do the problem sets.

Very cool lecturer, has GOAT potential imo, but for a class called "Linear Algebra for Engineers", he really expects you to learn the material as if you were an upcoming mathmatician in the 1600s. Extremely proof heavy, prelims containted T/F sections but also sections where you prove theorems you learned in class.
Very hard semester overall. The course was much harder than it was last semester from what I've heard. You do need to prepare and go to office hours.
The MATH 2940 curve was harsh on a lot of people this semester. The class was harder than it needed to be, and the curve didn't help anyone. Much harder than Lin Alg from previous semesters.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Mosunov actively wants you to succeed. His lectures are great and the iclicker questions are actually really helpful for learning (shocking I know). The hw was easy altho there were optional problem sets that better prepared you for the exams and quizzes but they aren't necessary. Pay attention early and you will be rewarded.
Nice guy and cares about his students but made the class a million times more difficult than it had to be
THE BEST PROFESSOR TO EVER EXIST! Anton is such a mush and should be celebrated! TAKE THIS CLASS WITH HIM! Very good at lecturing!!!!
Prof mosunov is the gooooaaattt!!!!
he is the best! so wholesome and cares so much about his students. his lectures are fantastic and the notes are really easy to follow afterwards. i will actually miss bro
Anton is kind of the goat. Lectures are extremely detailed and basically hold your hand through learning new concepts, and his slides are like the best thing I've ever seen.
anton mosunov is the goat
This was one of the best courses I have attended. The course opened up completely new perspectives on me and the learning will help me regardless of what I do next.

An excellent exploration of global perspectives in computing. Challenges technological saviorism attitudes. Prof. Vashistha is incredibly engaging, passionate, and knowledgeable. There is a significant group project due at the end and reading quizzes before classes, but it was a reasonable class with reasonable expectations. Very worthwhile.

Great professor, he is very passionate and knowledgeable about the subject. He clearly cares about the content and has a great, engaging lecture style that makes it easy to be interested in the subject. Reading heavy and attendance required, though.
One of the best course that I took at Cornell. Great way to learn how to design and build technology for underserved communities in different parts of the world. Strongly recommended.
Great professor with reading heavy coursework. Be ready to explore outside of the American ecosystem and learn more about global development in all his classes.

Professor Vashistha is nice and really cares about the content of this class. There's a lot of reading but nothing that's too much to handle. The only hard part of this class is the final project where you have to start super early and show that you put a lot of thought and work into it to do well. Other than the final project, super manageable.
Weird readings, dis cri miN tory articles and a ha. tred filled mind.
The course description is different compares to what actually is taught in the course. A lot of discrepancy exists and this course needs to be STOPPED for God's Sakes!!
Is she mentally stable? Maybe she needs some help. This program needs to conduct a health check-up for their faculty.
If there was a tag for differentiation, I'd add that. She spreads negativity against certain people in her weird course.

What's the ATTITUDE about? Has a major support from the program but lacks respect. She assumes she is EXCLUSIVE and has been mean to students this year. She told me you will be graduating with lesser skills. So basically she is proving that she is NOT a great instructor.
She is the first hire at this program but the worst hire. Racist mentality. Too young to be a professor, she herself is a student and needs some training.
I felt uncomfortable during office hours due to rude comments. I also did not receive support with internship recommendations, which was disappointing.
Amazing professor, very caring. Her lectures make difficult concepts much more intuitive and understandable.
It is very tough to feel positively about her. She makes up convention on-the-go and confuses me more than helps. I learnt best when I skipped lecture and read on my own.
Spams Work
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Very disorganized
Disorganized and unprofessional. Did not take care enough about her students.
Not very organized, teaching style was not good
Taking online online classes with this prof explains everything very well

CS 1110 was organized well—as someone who has not used a single computer program before. That being said, the assignments were sometimes a pain and some exam questions were really difficult. I took this for a distribution requirement, and while I really liked the coding, I would not go back. Remove the (HANDWRITTEN) exams and I'm on board.

Anne Bracy is definitely one of the better professors in Cornell CS (which isn't saying much, but I digress). She is a great lecturer, yet still has a strong non-teaching background. I liked how she handled 1110, but she made the labs too inconsistent in workload and made the prelims far longer than was realistic, leading many to not finish.

reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Great, encouraging professor with clear, easy-to-follow lectures. Holds helpful office hours and review sessions. Projects in the class are a great balance of challenging and engaging. Learned a ton in the class and would recommend to anyone who is trying to get into CS!

Anne Bracy is one of the best professors I have at Cornell. I came into this class not knowing anything about CS, and I walked out of this class with an A. The materials were very interesting to me, and I enjoy them substantially.
I loved Professor Bracy- she is truly a great lecturer and has a very impressive professional background. She made me want to major in CS!
Good lectures, very accessible and caring about students. Difficult class with a lot of workload but Bracy does a good job at navigating the course.
Crazy analogies. Intense workload. Interesting content. Compassionate professor. Can't ask for much more.
Amazing professor! Very kind and inclusive. Lectures always very well organized and prepared. Engaging lectures.

CS 3410 is no joke. The projects are challenging and ridiculously time-consuming. I think Professor Bracy makes the course fair and is a great lecturer and professor. My only criticism is the exams in the course are much more difficult than they should be and are frankly really hard to study for.

Wonderful lecturer! CS3410 is a challenging and difficult class, but Prof. Bracy's lectures makes it bearable with her jokes and funny/good examples and comparisons. She is also super accessible outside of class, and is supportive of students with disabilities. I think anyone who says otherwise has to reflect on their (internalized) misogyny.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class
Tough grader--Get ready to read--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Professor Bracy is great at what she does, lectures are very content-heavy but always worth going to. Do NOT procrastinate projects or you will come to regret it, office hours are available all week and very helpful. She gives lots of relevant practice materials for exams and although the tests can have hard questions, they are all graded fairly.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: (Note that she gave a textbook but literally no one used it) Very clear lectures, assignments are pretty straightforward, tests are fine if you study well. The class, in my opinion, is not meant to be a class to take "for fun." It's pretty time consuming, and Bracy expects a lot, but she made a significant effort to ensure students are prepared.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Test heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Pretty easy class, feel like I learned the fundamentals well. Lectures are not the most helpful, but the labs and assignments are great to learn.

This is more of a review of CS 1110 than of Prof Bracy, but I have zero prior coding experience and learned a lot and liked the course without being too stressed by assignments or exams. Class is heavily weighted and a few exams had very low unweighted averages (60%-70% range usually), so it's usually possible to recover from a bad test/assignment

Great class and great professor! The material could be challenging at times but she explained things amazingly clearly and often worked in fun examples to keep it engaging. The class does have a fair bit of work, specifically time consuming projects. Luckily projects are very interesting and there are lots of office hours.
Spring cs1110 is much easier than fall cs1110. Bracy didn't really impact me during the class, but I thought she was fine.

I don't have any experience with her in other classes, but in CS1110 she was great! Not perfect, but I certainly felt it necessary to attend lectures just because of how fluent she was in explaining the core concepts. The class itself was very difficult, but I think she certainly alleviated that a great deal.
A professor that forces financial hurdle on students by requiring physical iClicker.

I was a repeat cs1110 student this spring (took walker white last fall). Bracy and Lee are a great combo for teaching and I found the assignments more interesting than in the fall, and therefore I was more engaged and completed the course successfully. If possible I would wait until the spring to take this course. I feel more confident in cs now.

3410 is a tough class because material is new to most people and there's A LOT of work, even if it's mostly just busy work. Concepts usually build on top of one another so you do not want to fall behind. Class will seem overwhelming at first but gets better. Felt that Bracy makes the class doable contrary to what others might say. She's also funny

Fantastic lecturer! Makes CS clear to all, whether you are a first time learner or more experienced. Professor Lee writes tests and assignments, but Bracys lectures are super helpful for these. Would highly recommend taking!

Prof. Bracy was awesome, and I was pleasantly surprised by the way she taught CS. This class is definitely not easy, and requires a lot of effort, but if you put in the time and effort you will learn so much. Bracy was very nice, helpful, and accessible. The tests are hard but curved, the course is quick but help is given to people struggling.

Professor Bracy held all lectures for this class and ensured that all concepts were easily explained in class. While, many of the topics were extremely difficult to a first time programmer, it was clear that all topics were learned in a professional manner with clear examples that were performed in class.

Clear lecturer who's very passionate about the subject, and makes many references to Wegmans during the lectures. Went through numerous examples during class when covering more complex topics like caches, and reduced the difficulty of the assignments to accomodate for a hybrid class format. Had a reasonable slip day policy for project submissions.

Professor Bracy's class was so incredibly difficult for the sole purpose of being difficult. Lectures move so incredibly fast, projects have virtually no guidance and are difficult, and exams are not super related to projects and are still hard. I did more work for this class than any of my others classes and was miserable for each second of it.
Bracy is an excellent lecturer and really cares about her students. That doesn't mean the assignments and prelims aren't insanely hard...
Terrible person. I was hospitalized for a week and she forced me to use all of my slip days instead of just granting an extension like a human with empathy.

Bracy is a great teacher and clearly knows her subject. Her lectures are well-designed with clear language and she drops hilarious lines from time to time. This class covered a lot of highly technical details and sub-regions of CS, and not attending the lectures would make it hard to understand the slides. That said, the textbook is comprehensive.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Hilarious--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Really I wish I could give her 0 star. Just dropped the course, and do not understand where those good comments come from.
Not intelligent in teaching at all. Confusing lectures, baffled homework... I hope someone could let her know how awful her lectures are. By the way, she is rather mean and arrogant in the same time.

I love Bracy! She's a great professor and her lectures are done in a way that is easy to understand and accessible. Assignments are always geared towards what we have already learned; if you can do the assignments, the tests should not be too difficult. There are clicker questions to log attendance, but they're only 5% of your grade.
One of the worst classes I have taken.
Unintelligible class.

Bracy is a god among men. Never have I seen such a professor so passionate about both the material and her students. She gets so excited teaching and talking with students that it makes lectures my favorite time of the week. Yes, the class is difficult and condensed, but if you pay attention in lecture, the class is very manageable. SHE IS AMAZING!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Gives good feedback--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Professor Bracy is a talented and funny lecturer. Her lectures explain material extremely well while also being fairly concise. CS 3410's projects were hard (10-20 hours per project) and its exams harder, with means around a 55/90. I would highly recommend taking her classes she teaches.

Bracy is an amazing lecturer and made this class (which could have been terrible) pretty enjoyable and interesting. I really loved her classes and thought the assignments were pretty standard for a CS core class. Also, she's awesome to talk to outside of class and genuinely cares about her students. 10/10 would recommend!

Bracy is so great that I'm taking CS 2110 with her next semester. I loved her teaching style because I am a very visual learner (she's won teaching awards). She inspires great questions from her students in lecture, and the lecture material clearly laid out what was expected on exams. Beware of the tricky assignments and make use of office hours.

Her lectures were usually pretty good, but sometimes slightly disorganized. She's super nice though and has plenty of office hours! Apparently her assignments were a lot harder than the fall CS 1110 professor's, but they were doable.

Bracy is by far the best professor I've had at Cornell! She is passionate about CS and her lectures are clear and enormously helpful. The assignments were quite difficult at times but prepared you greatly for the prelims and final. I came in with zero coding experience and learned so much in a semester. Challenging but immensely rewarding class!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Inspirational--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: She is a great professor. Her lecture style was clear, and I the examples made tough concepts easy to learn. Her expectations were clear and it was easy to gage how I was doing in the class. Not an overbearing amount of work, and makes CS really fun to learn. Also, she is just an awesome human.
I really loved cs3410 with her :) I maybe only scraped past tests, but it was worth it for the interesting lecture and great content.

Best undergrad professor I've had. She turned systems from a hated subject of mine into my graduate focus. Negative comments below are reactions to unconventional teaching. Lectures focus on concepts, exams are difficult but test your grasp of concepts in terms of big picture. Not a cookie-cutter professor/lecturer - what makes her great.

Great Professor. The lectures are very clear. Attend the classes since It takes more time to figure out the lecture note than just attending the class. Not sure why are there so many very negatives comments. I took 4410 in the Fall semester. The lectures are a bit slow and repetitive and the projects are pretty difficult. But t's fine overall.
Super difficult class, but the best proffessor I've had at cornell
Awesome teaching with low scores. Think thoroughly before you enroll!!

Fun lectures that fly over topics at the speed of light. Be sure to read up before lecture begins (there's HW before every lecture to help with that) and take notes like a good student. Tests are difficult and annoying as they often test a concept that you know well but contorted so that no one knows how to answer. Still, great, fun person.

The class is harder than it has to be. Concepts in lecture are briefly touched upon, with the majority of content being self-taught. Lectures and projects have little overlap, and tests are hard to predict. Bracy is good at teaching in lecture, but little of what she teaches is actually useful for the tests and projects.

Overall, I wish there was more thought put into the course. Projects were not well planned, had too much time to complete, but little support. Having weekly problem sets would have helped students know what to focus on. Many difficult topics on the exams were covered in the book, but glossed on in lecture. Had to self-study to do well on exams.

Little to no TAs use the piazza, and some of the answers are rather rude. Tests have means in the 50s and are unrelated to anything learned. Lectures have minimal overlap with projects, and the projects are very hazily laid out. For one project, we were told to simply google any questions. It's a shame this course is mandatory.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: Very tough test, so many assignments that do not match it's credits. unclear lecture notes, skip the class? you will not clear about what leature notes talking about. besides operating system, the course also contains computer network and security. if you have choices, never choose it! it does not worth in any way!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Take 3420. This is the worst course I ever took in Cornell.
Take 3420
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: I took ece 2300 with David Albonesi(whose notes are ece bible) and cs 3410 with Bracy. The lecture note is unclear, messy and full of small errors. The lecture is  also unclear, messy and full of small erros. Bracy  loves using analogy to make the material less bland, but  3410 material is something that requires accurate and technical language!!

Never take 4410 with her. She clearly knows 3410 well, but completely messed up 4410 to the point of making (the very new) Prof. Agarwal look bad. She is mean and extremely useless in her office hours. She even co-organized the office hours for the two courses she was teaching that fall. Messed up the entire course and worst part were her lectures.
Go to office hours. Nothing in lecture can even compare to the evils of Logisim and malloc.

I don't know whether my negative experience with prof bracy owes itself more to the overbearing nature of the class (and I mean this class approaches levels of work akin to 19th century industrial revolution standards of labor), or to her stone cold, stoic conveyance of the material. Bracy was relentless and didn't even try to help make it palatabl
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Prof. Bracy is a great lecturer whose skill was sadly undermined by the incompetence of her co-professor.  I had her for both CS 3410 and this class, and I would say without a doubt that Prof. Bracy will go out of her way to help anyone who needs it, and is capable of much more than she was able to demonstrate in CS 4410 when I took it.

Bracy is unorganized, uncaring, and mean. Unwilling to help students and a very boring lecturer. In several instances, she would release grades for assignments 2 months after they were due. Her class organization is poor, has no accountability, and is not a good nor fair lecturer.

Despite Prof. Agarwal's messing up of the entire class, she was actually a good lecturer and you can tell from piazza and office hours that she actually cares and wants students to learn and succeed. She was organized and clear. 10/10 would recommend

I really like Anne as a lecturer but this class was just awful. 3410 was good but 4410 was by far the worst class I've taken at Cornell. I'm not a big fan of the material to begin with and the fact that this class was a logistical nightmare did not alleviate that issue. I really hope they get it together.
One of the best professor at cornell! Homeworks can be a little disorganized but her lectures are always interesting, informative, and entertaining.

Professor Bracy is one of the best professors I've had at Cornell. I took a class with her, and also was a TA for another class that she taught. Her classes definitely aren't easy, but she really cares about giving her students the best possible experiences and does everything that she can to help them succeed. Would definitely recommend her.
Nope
CS 3410 is a mandatory CS class and Bracy made it as painless as possible. As long as you make use of all the resources, you should be fine.

As Anne Bracy describes it, CS 3410 is like "trying to drink from two firehoses." This class is a TON of work, and the concepts are very hard. However, Bracy teaches them really well. She knows her stuff, and can answer any question you throw at her. This class will be near impossible, but Bracy and the TA army will help you out.

Wow, Bracy yourself for 3410 cause it's the bomb dot com. While the projects/ homework aren't too hard, they are time consuming. The 3410 TA army and Bracy is there for you though, so you are in good hands.

Ms. Happiness is genuinely one of the greatest teachers that i have ever had. She is very understanding and cares very much about building a community with the students of her classes, makes class feel like a family.

Bi Happiness is one of the best professors out there! She is so nice and caring! I took this class as a language requirement, but it ended up being my favorite class of the semester. For learning a new language, Bi Happiness makes it super enjoyable and easy. Weekly assignments help you improve both speaking and writing. 10/10 would recommend
Most of the submitted class work is online and 4 days a week short lectures mainly focus on reviewing homework and in class discussions

Happiness is the best language professor/teacher I have ever had. She is unbelievably caring and genuinely interested in her students. Swahili is a fun language to learn and Happiness creates a low-stress environment to do so. I look forward to her class every day and no Friday classes are a perk :) I highly, highly recommend!!

I love Happiness so much. I've learned a lot of Swahili from her and would encourage anyone to take her class. If you want to skim by and get an A, it's totally doable. If you really wanted to learn Swahili that's doable too. What you put into this course is what you get out of it. I love starting my day with Happiness and my Swahili class

Bulugu is, simply put, a great professor. Swahili with her is always fun and is an easy course so long as you put in just a little time going over vocab/grammar each week. Because of the small class size, you'll definitely bond with your classmates. Only good things to say.

He's really straightforward and funny in lectures, makes it very engaging. He also had more in-lecture polling questions than Dr.Helmann and Dr.Pesciotta, and that helped me understand and engage with the topic more. Generally a really fun bio class, especially if you have a bit of background (e.g. IB HL Biology). Loved his martial art shirts.
1440 made bio feel like one big, weirdly logical story, and Buchon explains it super clearly (with just enough humor) that I actually looked forward to lecture.

Professor Buchon is excellent! BIOG 1440 was challenging, but it's remarkable how far we dove into physiology for an intro class. His clear explanations and enthusiasm made the complexity fascinating and rewarding.

I thought he was a pretty good prof, but his lectures were the most difficult for me to understand out of the 3 profs. His were so extremely content-dense that it was nearly impossible to actually memorize everything. Though the material was extremely interesting, and I thoroughly enjoyed learning it all, even though it took a lot of work to do so.

I had the pleasure of being in Professor Buchon's class this semester, and I must say it was an absolutely enriching experience. I highly recommend their class for anyone interested in physiology, especially if you plan on taking the MCAT
I really liked his style of teaching. He mixes clear explanations, with physiological problem sets and I really got a sense I learned a lot. A must have for premeds

Taking Professor Buchon's physiology class was super cool! He made it easy for us to get what he wwas talking about. He really cared about us doing well and was always there to help out, whether it's explaining stuff after class or using fun ways to teach. I'd totally recommend his class to anyone who wants to learn about how their body works!

Great professor who cracks a lot of jokes. he deals with the most complicated lectures in class but make them very accessible. Follow in class. This helps a lot! Accessible professor outside of class.
Very good professor that made complicated concepts pretty clear. Very accessible outside of class, supportive and quite funny.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: really nice professor

Really funny professor, wrote the easiest out of the four prelims. I thought his lectures were the most entertaining and very clear. Very heavy french accent but still not hard to understand. His prelim was not too detailed oriented. Loml

Dr. Buchon is, in my opinion, the best 1440 instructor! His slides are very clear. His lecture was thorough and his explanations was extremely clear. He takes hard physiology concepts and explains it in a way that's really easy to understand. He's very accessible outside of class and holds extra office hours before exams so we get the help we need.

His videos were helpful, but it is a little hard to understand his French accent. His lectures were pretty engaging and thorough. The class is moderately difficult. There's lecture videos, readings, and mandatory quizzes twice a week, 4 prelims, optional final, and attendance is not mandatory. Prelims are tricky. Don't take if not required.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Test heavy

reviews.comment: best 1440 lecturer, with very clear presentations, slides, and grading criteria. organizes and explains things very well and grades super fairly. the only problem I've ever heard from people about him is his accent, but I never had any issue (it's french, if anyone is wondering) wish he taught the entire semester!

From the three instructors in BioG1440, Pr. Buchon was the most interesting and clear.
Easy to talk to and mentions in class what the test will be on.
Lectures are content heavy, but complex concepts are well explained.

Prof Buchon's slides and lectures were by far the most clear and easy to understand. I felt like the topics he taught were some of the more difficult ones, but his explanations and heavy use of in-class practice questions helped a lot. He also pushed back a prelim when he knew the students were stressed about other emergency matters.

The ability to watch the recorded lectures is what carries this class. All the information you need for the exams are in the recorded lectures, and the three professors that teach this class have plenty of office hour times to help with understanding the lectures. The discussion sections are great fun, with great experiments. JOIN BIOG1044! GL

An interesting class where you will learn a lot about physiology. Contrary to what is said in the syllabus, it is heavy in human physiology and not so much focused on other organisms. Pr. Buchon really explained well complicated concepts.
Intelligent and thoughtful professor with interactive lectures. Explains topics well. The way the class is curved is annoying.

I really loved the lectures of Pr. Buchon, they were very structured and the problems were well explained. I took AP biology so have seen some of that content before, but this was way more structured.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--Caring

reviews.comment: I loved the lecture of Professor Buchon on how microbes influence physiology. Coolest lecture ever. It made me think a lot.

I actually loved BioG1440 and especially the section taught by Buchon. The exams were very fair, you really just have to listen to what he says in class. The other instructors were a bit less clear, but overall a good class. Exams come one day apart from chem, this was tough.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Took BIOG1440 with Buchon, Shapleigh, and Selveraj in FA 2020. Class had both synchronous and asynchronous lecture components. Buchon's slides and lectures were by far the most comprehensive and he emphasized topics that were likely to be tested. Compared to the other two professors Buchon was by far the best.
The course itself is difficult but Buchon explains things in a very clear and understandable way. He does have an accent that can be heavy at times, but his way of teaching is great
There were 3 instructors for this class and they were really uneven. The body physiology is really the coolest part. Advice: go to office hours!
I am very thankful for this professor's time. He hold something like 10 extra office hours sessions before prelim and these really help.

Professor Buchon had very good powerpoints, but his thick French accent sometimes made it difficult to understand him. BIOG 1440 itself is difficult and very memorization heavy, but I think he explained concepts well.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Fantastic professor. He made some complicated subjects feel quite simple!

Dr Buchon was very helpful during the semester. I started with a bad score during my first prelim, but thanks to TAs and Professors' office hours, I kept increasing my score. Dr Buchon advices were really helpful
Dr Buchon explained everything we needed to know very clearly and plainly. I was glad to get to study from him

Dr. Buchon is a great professor and really cares about his students and the subject material. Very easy to understand and available for extra help during office hours. I went every week. He is passionate about physiology and you will learn a lot from him. I wish I could take more courses with him!
Great teacher. Loved it!

Great introduction to physiology. Has been recommended to me by my academic mentor as a premed. This course is very time-consuming course but also very rewarding and practical to understand human physiology.

The material is difficult but presented very clearly. Exams are fair; but you better go to review sessions. This professor is very accessible, he will spend time with you until you understand things. There is a lot of information so better study from the start. Thankfully the final is not cumulative.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: This professor really helped me organize to study for my exams. In addition, he took ample time to explain the material even after exams, or to explain what I was missing in my exam. This class is my last requirement and this helped a lot!

He's amazing! If you put your all into his classes you will be rewarded. Yes it will be the hardest class of your life but you will not regret a single moment- be sure to take advantage of office hours and ask questions!
The lectures are amazing. I consider changing major and taking more biology because of this class.
He was sooo helpful during review sessions!!!
Fantastic professor. He makes everything clear !
I loved that class. But it was clearly the toughest since I started at Cornell !
reviews.rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: I took that class as an external student. The content was very interesting, and challenging. I really appreciated the feedback from the Professor, who answered all my questions quickly.
Great class. But the curving really makes it difficult to get an A.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: This class is quite interesting but tough on non bio majors. The course goes quite in depth which requires a lot of memorization. Overall a very interesting class for premeds.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: A very solid class for physiology
This class was terrible- so disorganized, the material was not presented in a clear way and it was very unclear what we were meant to learn. Class objectives were not meant

Do not take this class if you don't like scientific writing. All he grades you on is the accuracy of your thoughts and not your writing. Class is also boring and he overcomplicates ideas for no reason. Grade is made up of 5 essays with no homework but you have 20+ page readings before class. If you want a fun and easy FWS, this is not for you.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Take his class.
Great professor. Some of the topics can be confusing at times. He is super helpful outside of class. The only thing is that the subject was too philosophical for me.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Gives good feedback--Hilarious

reviews.comment: Gives a lot of useful comments. Don't be scared by the philosophical words! Flexible deadlines.

Take his FWS. Interesting, sometimes confusing concepts, but he cares a lot about his students and will review drafts and devote many hours to helping his students. Compared to other FWS, this class is not a lot of work.

Professor Bush is deeply committed to ensuring his students enjoy the learning process. He is very accommodating and often shares stories about his home life and family, which shows that he genuinely values building meaningful connections with his students. While the material can be complex at times, just continue to ask questions and participate.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Great professor. Makes the content very interesting and gives a lot of useful feedback.
You can tell he really cares about his students and is very passionate about the content he teaches.

Professor Bush is a great person; he is super kind and clearly cares about his students. From providing feedback and his fair grading, Bush wants his students to succeed. He brings a joyous and fun class environment. Take his FWS classes

One of my favorite professors at Cornell. Super super chill and, especially for an FWS, there is not a lot of work and there is no attendance. Four three-page papers throughout the semester and then quite a long paper at the end but he guides you through most of the writing. Wants students to succeed and it super into the class he teaches.
Clearly has passion for propulsion, very kind person and intelligent also
Fisher is the goat, love her. I think I may become a professional thermophysicist or thermomatician or whatever you call it because of her. Shout out to Sadi Carnot

One of my favorite lecturers at Cornell. Clearly very passionate about her work and her students. Takes time to thoroughly address questions during lecture. Her teaching style is extremely engaging and she makes the material fun to learn!
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: She is a great lecturer!! I am not a fan of thermodynamics, but she makes it way easier to understand and talks her way slowly through material that makes it easier to understand.

She's a great professor! The grading for homework assignments and exams is very fair. Most of the assignments were done in groups; lecture attendence is vital. The textbook is needed for the assignments, but not so much for learning the content.

By far the best professor Ive had at Cornell. Besides being an amazing lecturer, she really cares about her students and their well-being. This was clearly evident through the rocky semester we had this fall. Shes extremely fair with her homework assignments and tests. Would definitely take a class with her again.

Hands down best professor I've had at Cornell. Didn't know what a meaningful lecture was until I had her as a lecturer. I only used the textbook as a supplement to study, but it wasn't really necessary. VERY fair exams, not easy, just fair given what we cover in class and on the HW. Actually cares about students & our wellbeing!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Caring

reviews.comment: Great lecturer, kept people engaged. Homeworks and tests were very fair. Always willing to help/

While the course was not too difficult, I found many things very frustrating. She frequently had to repost homeworks and grades (even edit questions during tests) due to mistakes. I found the homeworks time consuming but not unnecessary. The lectures were sometimes disorganized and the notes (posted afterward) were difficult to read

Her lectures are very clear, the material is engaging but not too difficult. The homeworks and exams are very fair. She clearly cares about her students. She sometimes comes to the next lecture with slides prepared to better answer student questions from the lecture before.
Such a great professor who really cares about people learning!

Lectures were very well organized and easy to follow. Covered a wide range of propulsion topics and went appropriately in-depth with most of them. Weekly homeworks that weren't too difficult, were usually engaging and applicable. Office hours were always very helpful.
Homeworks are done in groups, which in my experience is a great way to learn. Professor Fisher definitely cares about her students and makes every effort to help them out.
Great professor--her lectures were pretty clear, and the exams were fair.

Class can be boring sometimes. However, she is very clear and organized. Tests/homework are very fair, and her lecture notes are thorough and clear. Not the most enjoyable class (distribution requirement), but Prof. Fisher overall does a great job of conveying the material.
Very painful class
the least organized prof ever. you will get absolutely no feedback all semester and deadlines will constantly be moved around!!
Yeah - just read the other reviews. What an unfortunate joke of a class.
Very disorganized and unprofessional
THE WORST PROFESSOR AT CORNELL UNIVERSITY ! ! !
most unorganized and difficult class in ORIE, prof needs to make exams easier and actually grade them instead of waiting till after classes...
NEVER TAKE HIS CLASS!

It is currently November 24th... here is the fact pattern:

We have had 6 HWs due throughout the semester. 5 have been graded
1 Exam on 10/16, have not gotten it back
A final he said was 12/2, then said 12/4 and is now unscheduled for next week, with no practice 
A final project with no details, no due date
A final HW with no details, no due date

The class is pretty disorganized 😅. We have one more week of classes: still need Prelim 1 back from October😅, the final date is a mystery😅, project is due same day as final.. don't 
Know what project entails😅.Lecture content jumps around😅, some (quite a few) questions on Ed go unanswered😅. Honestly, I am scared😅

He's REALLY disorganized. We have not gotten our prelim 1 back from October. We don't know the date of the final (there's only a week left of class), our project is supposedly due the day of the final (we know nothing about the project), there has not been a single lecture where he goes in order. He avoids questions on ed he cannot answer. vibes 😃

Horrible prof. Worst I have had during my time at cornell. doesn't teach properly, doesn't post proper materials for the class, is absent or irresponsive half the time. Prelims are insanely tough and he misleads students by saying they are easy.
Amazing prelim! Hope everyone can try!

Released final project details two weeks before the final exam and decided to have a it due on the day of exam (with a presentation). Also absent and very irresponsive throughout the semester like bro u good
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: very bad professor, and not responsive AT ALL. He never cares about his students, like posting midterm grades in December and not coming to classes several times. AVOID HIM AT ALL COSTS

Bad professor. Lectures are unorganized, and you must spend tons of time completing the HW. You probably have to read the textbook for the HW completion (or use ChatGPT if you want). Have to attend recitations and complete questions assigned. The final exam was harder than expected. Not recommended.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Very bad teaching skills. Unorganized lectures. Most of the time, he got confused by his own questions. Expect a lot of homework assignments. Nothing useful/substantial taught in second half of the semester. I would recommend taking ORIE 5581 if you still want to take this course unless you want to earn extra 2 credits without any efforts.
Interesting course that turned out to be more helpful than I imagined
This whole course is an unorganized messy crap. The most unresponsive prof ever in my life ....... don't think anyone can beat him on this.
Fors is genuinely one of the nicest professors I've had here at Cornell. His lectures are super accessible and easy to follow and he really cares about the questions that students have!
This professor is so incredible and made me love orgo after hating it last semester. Is an example for all chem professors!

Professor Fors is an OUTSTANDING professor and the best professor I have had so far at Cornell. His lectures are so easy to follow and incredibly engaging. I love his side notes at the end of class as well. He is also very accessible outside of class, and it is obvious that he genuinely cares about his students' learning.

Fors is super sweet and willing to help. His lectures are genuinely so interesting and very well-taught. His exams are not too tough, but he gives ZERO partial credit, so be prepared to know the knit-picky stuff. Quite a difficult class (a lot of memorization), but doable with a lot of studying.

the NICEST and most approachable professor. v passionate about orgo and is reflected by his teaching. he truly wants his students to succeed and cares about them a lot. he is the only reason why i went to orgo. he tries to make orgo fun by giving us his silly fun fact of the orgo every class. sign me up for his fan club. he a smiley guy for sure.
This professor is so whimsy and cute and fun

Professor Fors is a fantastic teacher. He teaches this course with such passion and his side notes at the last 5 minutes of class where he talks about a real world application for orgo always makes my day. He genuinely feels like the standard for what a professor should be like and he's made my experience amazing.
It's orgo, so of course it's really hard, but he does his best to make it manageable. He's also really available for help, and was really nice during the transition to the pandemic.

Prof. Fors is amazing! His lectures are clear, he's super enthusiastic during them and willing to answer questions, and his practice tests are really similar to the actual prelims. Definitely take him--he has a way of making difficult material seem easier and makes you feel important.

Prof Fors is easily the best prof I've ever had. Remembers your name. Easy if you study. Email him if u ever need anything but he'll randomly not answer to some emails. Extra credit is offered. He has rly good OH and offers 1-on-1 help. Exams are identical to the practice exams. Amazing lecturer and so clear.
Absolutely amazing experience, would take again!

I honestly struggle so much with chemistry and was dreading this class, but Prof. Fors is a gem! His enthusiasm for the material is contagious and honestly makes this class so much better than others. Though this class was probably the hardest chem class I've taken, it's probably the only chem class I'd take again. Bottom line- he's awesome!

You need this man in your life! He cares and loves Organic Chemistry and I am excited to take the class with him. The class is very hard and there are some tricky questions on the prelim that might not be taught in class but in the textbook. He's very understanding during the pandemic. Make sure you keep up with the material because there's a lot.

Professor Fors is AMAZING!!! Such a nice guy and he made CHEM 3580 a little more bearable. Material is challenging, but Professor Fors's exams are pretty fair, and homework grade helps slightly. Definitely need to attend lecture, or at least get good notes. Reading the textbook is helpful too.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Literally one of my fav professors in the chem department by far. He's caring and is always willing to help. The material isn't easy, but he made the class enjoyable.
Fors is a really nice professor, but won't take any bullsh*t. Both CHEM3570 (Ganem) and CHEM3580 are classes that if you miss lecture and don't have a friend's notes, you will fail.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Really nice guy

Professor Fors is a very nice person, and his pros and cons come through being nice. Pro: he is very patient with each question and each student Con: Because he is nice, he is often controlled by the crowd in lectures, problem-solving sessions, office hours, instead of the other way around.
I LOVE BRETT HE'S THE BEST PROFESSOR EVER <3

Although Fors just started teaching, nearly everyone loved him. His tests are fair, and the materials come directly from the notes. As long as you study, you will get what you deserve. However, he goes through a lot in each lecture so prepare to study ahead of time. He is a gem and will make organic chem actually enjoyable.

Professor Fors is AMAZING!! Best chemistry professor I've ever had. He truly cares so much about his students and really makes an effort to ensure we understand. He is very organized, and his lectures are easy to follow
The class is easy but the professor will make you regret it. You've got to be on her favorites list to get by easily.

Does not "believe" in giving 100s. Soft spoken, but speaks audaciously to children, students, and all. Very strict classroom rules. No leeway for attendance. Thought the class content was okay and easy, but disliked it purely because of her.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The class was easy content wise. She has a bunch of nonsense rules on classroom behavior. If you enter the classroom before class time with earbuds in you will be reprimanded. You are not allowed to use the bathroom during class either, does not realize that we are adults. She is also very confrontational.

I came into class not knowing a single word of Polish and had a terrific experience. Ewa is super fair and thoughtful on grading, and is extremely supportive. There are straightforward rules in the class... you need to be willing to follow and respect them. Polish is a unique course that I highly recommend versus more traditional language courses.

Professor Bachminska is an exceptional instructor! I took POLSH 2034: Intermediate Polish II with her and there was never a time when I did not feel supported, heard, or respected. I highly recommend taking any class with her :)

In the Polish class, I liked the best the trip to Cornell museum. I also really enjoyed the movie about Walesa. Ewa even brought Polish sweets for us and we learned how to order food in Polish at a restaurant. I recommend taking her class.

Simply one of the best language courses I have ever taken. Professor Bachminska is very engaging and makes learning Polish a truly wholesome experience of Polish culture, film, art and history. Taking this course made me visit Poland. I would like to take more Polish courses as long as she teaches. She is very dedicated and excellent in her craft.

Very poor instructions on assignments followed by grading on criteria that was never stated. English is not her first language and it clearly shows when your paper is read. Forces you to limit your writing. Its not too hard to get an A if you try and guess what she wants, but it could get frustrating.

One of the few classes I've felt fully immersed into when going to class. Participation is a large part of class which became very productive over time helping me learn how to look at material differently. Grading based on criteria and improving, so it becomes a class you can enjoy material for instead of stressing your grade. Great teacher!
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Gives good feedback--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: She is very nice, who encourages you to answer questions and participate in class discussions. The class atmosphere is chill and respectful. She grades you based on completion.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Caring

reviews.comment: She's really nice and grades based on personal improvement and completion. Classes include a lot of close reading, working with peers, and discussion.

COGST1101 was a mixed bag. There were times when it was very interesting and thought-provoking, and other times when there were no thoughts to be had. The format of the class is not great, as most of the time, students are giving presentations on the content and not Casasanto. If she taught more, I think her class would be more interesting.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This was one of my favorite classes. A good amount of reading but no homework, I enjoyed her lectures. I didn't like having to listen to students' presentations because most were not very good. Her tests were so easy, two short-answer exams (mostly had to argue well) and 1 MCQ. You can do a group presentation in place of an exam. Easiest A+ for me.

Professor was okay, but did not actually teach most of the class. She would only give 1 of the 5 presentations each class, with a majority of time spent listening to other students' group presentations. Her lectures were solid and covered the reading well, but beware of the class format. Exams were a bit tough, but doable if you did the readings.

She's a great professor. This class is all group projects and Socratic seminar. If you like talking about current events and are very opinionated, you'll love it. DO NOT take this class if you have a fear of public speaking. This is the kind of class where 1 super liberal & 1 super conservative kid fight the whole time. Lot of fun.

She is not a great lecturer. She goes really fast during lectures and jumps from topic to topic so be ready to take notes and lock in the whole time, or just self study. She also has an accent which can be hard to pick up sometimes. The workload was alright with group problem sets and mostly just exams.
The teaching was bad. I wouldn't recommend it.
CURVED DOWN EVERYONE'S GRADES BY A HALF-LETTER. Does not follow her own grading rubric. Reads directly off of slides in thick accent. Homeworks not related to lecture material. Do not recommend.
rushes through slides in class despite TAs asking her to slow down and struggles to answer questions clearly, leaving the TAs to carry the class.
This course gave me foundational knowledge about markets, inflation, unemployment, interest rates. Very applicable things that have a lot of value. A must to understand the real world
Professor Caunedo is inspirational. She is teaching tough material and I liked her style of bringing it down to real world examples.

I have learnt a ton for this course. The professor was available to answer questions outside class and always clear when asked for clarification. My letter grade was wrong and was freaking out because of internship applications but she fixed it by the morning after.

Enraged by Econ 3040. Lectures are unclear, and grading is arbitrary and inconsistent between exams. Content unclear throughout. The stats are concerning, but prof said it's normal. Unresponsive before final. Got sick (102° fever), but makeup would mean "I" on the transcript. Never been so frustrated with a course.
Terrible clarity in class and absolutely did not follow syllabus grading. Never take a class with her.
Terrible professor. Doesn't know what she's teaching. Grading couldn't be more random. Doesn't stick to her own syllabus. Managed to curve grades down. Never take her class.

Professor Caunedo disregarded syllabus grading and gave much lower grades for many students than was outlined in the provided syllabus. The lectures were difficult to follow, yet the content was easy to digest once reviewed outside of class. Highly recommend taking a different professor for 3040 if possible
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor really funny. She let you to drop a prelim and replaced by a higher final score, deeply cares about the growth of students. I would definitely recommend this. One of the most caring professors I have at Cornell so far

Professor and nice and really funny, keeps lectures lighthearted. Homeworks and exams start hard, but it seems to all click around the final few weeks. 

Attendance not mandatory, but save yourself confusion and extra hours studying by going.

Highly enjoyed 3040 with Caunedo! Learning curve was tough, but she lets you replace prelims with your final if it's higher, so there's chance for a comeback. Cracks jokes in class and asks questions during lecture - I struggle with focus but she was able to keep me engaged. I liked her "MBA-flavor" (as she put it) - would recommend her for 3040.

her class is a learning curve. I started out doing so bad but ended up with good grade once I got used to her exam style and her lecture style. Homework is graded sorta harshly but seems like she curved the class generously
She cares about the students. Lectures are dense, but I feel I learnt a bunch.
Don't recommand. Most boring class ever. She reads through the slide. Heavy accent. Exams aren't really hard though. She's not awful, she just can't catch anybody's attention.

Macroeconomics is very dry material but was delivered very-well in an MBA setting, I really liked that the material was tied to the current economy. Her lectures are intense, maybe the models could be simplified a little so things are better understood, but I like that she facilitated discussions that came up in class instead of shutting them down.

Professor Julieta, while bright, doesn't seem to understand that many MBA students do not have a background in economics. She never manages to finish what she planned for a lecture in just ONE lecture, and crams an absurd amount of information in every sentence she says. With her heavy accent as well, classes become impossible to understand.
Really bad class organization Never able to finish class material as planned Discussion always went out of the way Speak too fast mostly mumbling with heavy accent

Caunedo is knowledgeable but really bad at teaching. Struggles to explain basic concepts, skips important points and rambles a lot. Has a heavy accent, if you are not good at deciphering accents, go with another professor.

Nice but relatively new -- slides still had some errors, but she corrected them in class. Does a pretty good job explaining in lecture. Discussion helps too.   Practice prelim/finals from previous semesters weren't very exemplary of the actual prelims/final, especially the 2014 ones.   HW was easy (1-2 hrs/pset). Tests were easy, uncurved.
very nice

She sometimes made mistakes on the slides, but was quick to clarify. This might be because it was her first year teaching though. She's generally very helpful and as long as you understood the lecture slide deck, the problem sets were not too challenging.
New professor. Not super comfortable in front of the class. Would constantly change her mind on statements of theorems when questioned. Extremely nice and willing to help if asked though.

Nice teacher, but first year teaching. Understands concepts, but can't really teach students. She doesn't follow a book, so you have to go to lecture. A lot of students have trouble understanding. Would recommend taking other ECON3040.   Bottom line: she can't teach, but her tests are fair.
Great teacher, but doesn't like it when you yawn. He has a pleasant side and a dark side. Proceed with caution especially if you have to yawn during a lecture
Great interesting Prof
This guy is a literal Nazi who thinks Hitler was great and the Holocaust was mostly made up. Puts his Nazi attitudes and racist comments in his class repeatedly.
David seems to know at least a little of chemistry, but spends most of the class arguing that the Holocaust was the fault of America and the Soviet Union.
He was professional and funny making difficult scientific analogies to simple life lessons. It made material more understandable.
Amazing Prof. Most honest and knowledgeable Prof or person I've ever known. If your willing to learn and have an open mind, no one will teach you more. Wish he was my Prof for every class.

The class itself isn't bad, but mostly depends on your TA. I hope I never have to take a class taught by Dave again though - offensive guy but tries to be funny. Makes students uncomfortable (check his twitter). Lectures are a mess and he goes off on tangents constantly. Not sure I learned a single thing from him. Bad at technology & no canvas.
He's a king and a bright man all around. keep on rockin Dr. C!
A complete nutjob. Check out his twitter feed. Avoid at all costs.
reviews.rating_tags: So many papers--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Just dont like him or the class

Hilarious guy and the most awesome stories you'll ever hear in your entire life. Wish he spent time in class clarifying the material better than just telling stories. Exams are tough, but fair. Overall good professor.
Fun class.  Dave is hilarious and very opinionated.  Tests are tough as hell but don't count for anything.
Best lab course. Looked forward to lectures and labs. Lectures, especially. I was actually sad when he started cancelling lectures second half of the semester.
This was the first lab I ever looked forward to coming to. The work is interesting and Dave is a great lecturer, on topic or not.
Dave is so inappropriate and hilarious!  He is so hott and the class is awesome.  I'll really miss going to lecture!
No Comments
Dave is so funny!  He tells the best stories and the labs are really fun too.  Everyone should take this class.
A little condescending at times during instruction for sure. Wouldn't take again.

Useful class, but can be rude and aggressive. This instructor is retiring anyway, but just putting it out there as a warning. Def agrees with the comments earlier down here that she can be condescending and unreasonable.
LOVE her. And learned great skills
She is the moment
best teacher ever
AVOID.

Christina is the best and her classes are the ones alumni rave about as a must-take. Took two classes with her already and would take her again if possible. The most useful class for internships and beyond, you think you know Excel until you take her class and realize how little you actually know. And you get a certification in the process.

one of my favorite classes taken. great teacher and lecturer, I HIGHLY recommend taking this class. you learn so much so quickly that is so applicable, and you get a nice certification on top of it all. she is very nice and personable.
Super caring professor, explains things clearly. She takes the time to make sure you understand material. Just a long class, but she gives a break in the middle.

For HADM1740, your professor doesn't matter all that much. For her part, Christina was very meh. She isn't always super helpful and can be pretty condescending at times, although she is nice enough. I liked my TAs much better. Overall, the class is not difficult but the grading rubrics are pretty harsh. Little mistakes will cost a lot of points.
Really great class. If you liked EDA, it is more of the same.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--Caring

reviews.comment: EDA is an essential class for any Cornell student, and Christina is an excellent professor. That is all that needs to be said.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Christina is amazing! She's funny, helpful, and cares about her students. I took business computing with her and mark and I did well because she provided us everything we needed for exercises. Attendance is mandatory. I'd love to take another course with her.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Super helpful class that can be an easy class, but don't underestimate it. Make sure you are well prepared for the two in class excel quizzes and you should be fine.
I highly recommend taking EDA with Christina. The content is really useful and she makes the lectures interesting. She's also super nice and approachable. 10/10.

Christina is one of the best professors at Cornell, she puts a lot of work into making lectures applicable to the real world while maintaining a great attitude. Always makes expectations clear, connects with students, and is careful to explain things in a very clear manner to reduce potential for confusion. Take her lab for Business Computing!
Amazing class do not regret it at all, have learned a lot of useful things in this class
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Hilarious--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: EDA is by far the most applicable course offered at Cornell. Christina teaches skills that are necessary for most jobs that ILR students have. She makes class really fun and rewarding. Easy A if you practice the techniques. This class is a must-take for all ILR students.

She's a great lecturer. I enjoyed her. Some didn't but I guess it's just about how you mesh with a professor sometimes. The structure of the class was terrible but there were four professors responsible for it so can't put it all on her.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Participation matters--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: So blessed to have taken all three of her classes: EDA, ADA and XDA. The Expert Certification in Microsoft Excel is a nice resume booster, but even more valuable are the actual skills she teaches so well. I now use Excel for everything from tracking income/expenses to creating charts and graphs for various purposes. Couldn't recommend enough

I am so thankful that I had a Christina as a teacher for EDA. I now graduated and she is even willing to help with a excel-based work assignment that I have been struggling with. She genuinely cares about her students and this class is the most useful class that I have taken throughout my time at Cornell.
She does a good job with this course that ends up being very valuable. Would definitely recommend.
