Undoubtedly one of the best professors at Cornell. ADA was a little harder than EDA, and the skills that she teaches you are prob the most applicable. She is a little frank, but far from rude. The comment about her being rude is absurd; she treats you like you treat her. If you disrespect her, she'll give you no respect back. Try a little, easy A
Easy class and useful but was taken back by her rudeness... would recommend taking another desktop/excel/ microsoft class if you do not want to be hurt.

Her class is not difficult and if your willing to work hard then an A is easily obtainable, but this is not a course you can blow off and do well. She also is not as reasonable as others have suggested, she is down to earth, but there are no second chances in her course if you make one mistake on a test you put yourself out of the A range.

Christina Homrighouse is one of the most entertaining lecturers I've had to date. You literally cannot fail if you just show up, listen, and follow instructions. I had experience in Excel/Powerpoint before the class and that only made it easier.
Christina is the best business computing professor you can have. She cares about her students, 100%.

Christina is a phenomenal teacher. She knows her stuff and she is willing to work with students who may be struggling. Christina cares about her students and wants them all to succeed if they put in the required amount of effort. I recommend going to every class and taking notes. Excel will help you not only at Cornell but well afterwards.

One of the most understanding professors I have ever had. She is straightforward and truly cares about helping you succeed. She is also HILARIOUS and makes class fun. If you miss lecture make sure to attend one of the other lectures that week or else you will fall behind very quickly. Tests are easy if you pay attention in class.
Great class, great professor.

There is absolutely no excuse not to take her class if you are in ILR. Not only is the subject matter essential to your future success, but the course itself is very well instructed by a professor who genuinely cares for her students.
Awesome professor, great and USEFUL class.

Christina is literally the best and most genuine person on campus. She is so invested in each student and she develops their technical, professional, and personal skills. The topics she teaches are taught with ingenuity and are the most practical and important skills any student can ask for. She is a FANTASTIC educator and friend. Thank you Mrs. H!

Christina is one of more friendly and easy-going teachers at Cornell. The class is invaluable in terms of the excel and powerpoint skills is gives you, which are all highly looked upon by employers. It's an "easy A" kind of class, but requires a little extra effort if you really want the A+.

The nicest professor I will ever have. I'm a total techie and I actually learned really cool tips and tricks I use all the time for Windows and Office 07 which comes in real use for corporate jobs. Homrighouse is amazing. Take her. Now.

Extremely easy. there is no way you can not get an A+. I think I did a total of four hours of work for the entire semester. The only complaint was that attendence was mandatory and the class was at 8:00AM on Fridays.

If you don't get an A+, you should probably drop out of school. Let's just say the class average on one project was a 112. That is no typo. She's very cool though, and you really learn some good stuff in the class. And she's a Yankees fan to boot!!

Easy A+ class.  Cant really skip class that often, but Homrighouse tells you exactly what you have to do on the projects and what will be on the quizzes.  GO TO OFFICE HOURS, she shows you how to do everything so that you really learn it.  The material is easy. Take this class!

Prof H. was so interested in getting everyone onboard and learning that it was refreshing to wake up that early and go to class! She really has an interest in students learning material...other professors could learn alot from her! If I weren't graduationg I would take all of her classes...

She's the best! Veeeery easy A, but you will learn a lot, too. If you're interested in sharpening your computer skills, with almost no work, just REAL learning, this is it. Highly recommended course for anyone (not only ILR)!

She's the coolest ILR professor.  She's always willing to help...in fact, she forced students to do the extra credit...and going to her for help isn't intimidating...she understands some peeps may be a bit tech stupid.  Overall, she rocks! Definitely take her!
Yeah, the grades are high. But hey--it's truly a good class to take, even if you feel very comfortable with Office.
Should be a required class for college students. Learn real world skills such as powerpoint, access and excel. Teacher very nice and wants everyone to do well.
good class - easy A+
Fun class that you will actually learn something in, even though it is a very easy class.  Always has a positive attitude.
Great class!
Take this class for a positive classroom environment in the morning!
Greatest class i've taken
No Comments
The mean grade in this course in a A+, that's sexy!
No Comments
Kevin is too smart to teach.
Speaks in riddles.

I aced Clermont's class but the one thing I'll note is that his class is very lecture focused. Random iClicker questions, previous practice problems, and off-the-cuff lecture tangents morph into exam questions so you sort of need to write down everything he says. Being old school he's also purposefully opaque. And the textbook? LMAO.

Clermont is a brilliant scholar but unable to make the material understandable for his students.  Lectures go off on irrelevant tangents, leaving less time for important material.  The exam was absolutely ridiculous and does not measure how well the students learned the semester's material.  Sorry Cornell 1Ls, you're stuck with him one semester.

The best professor I've had so far. Prepare for class and think about the material and you will get a lot out of this. So far he is the only teacher to write multiple choice questions that don't have mistakes in them that make the answers ambiguous. People see issues and know the material will do well in this class, and people who don't won't.

1 of the 2 finest professors at Cornell back in 82.  His analysis of the overlap of attorney client privilege, work product and mental impressions should be mandatory learning for all trial judges, who I find almost uniformly misunderstand the principles.
Great Prof. After you have done your work, he would lift you to a whole new level. The exam was not easy, but reasonable nonetheless.

Clermont is tough because he wants you to take the material seriously. He's "extremely helpful" only for those people that have actually struggled with answering their own questions before coming to him. He does not suffer fools lightly. If you've given your best effort, he'll give you some direction - but no free answers.
Difficult to understand, moody, bitter, hide-the-ball exam.

Teaches to the top 10% of the class, and the rest of his students are left scratching their heads. Prides himself on the fact that he gives out incredibly difficult finals.  Unfortunately, every 1L can expect to have him.

Clermont makes it near impossible to learn civil procedure, one of the hardest first year classes.  He doesn't realize his students haven't been studying it for 50 years like he has.  But too bad.  All 1Ls have to take him.

A great professor - engaging and dedicated.  For those of you there now, this man never missed a single class while going through a year of intensive chemo.  Yeah, he loves to play jedi mind tricks on students -- if you want to be spoon fed the material, get out of his classroom.

Brilliant; great fun; hard.  He does a great job explaining the second half of CivPro.  He knows what he is talking about (he did write the book, after all.  I didn't care for CivPro 1st semester, but Clermont is able to both explain the subject, and make it come alive. Wait for his Pennoyer discussion. it's hilarious and tragic at the same time.
A really fun professor but don't feel bad if you walk out of class and feel like a complete moron... In some ways, he makes the class harder than it needs to be.
Very smart, very funny.  However, he sometimes forgets that not everyone understands Civil Procedure as well as he does.  You end up taking a lot of notes in that class.
I thought I understood everything until I took the exam.  Very impressive to listen to, and he knows it.

probably the smartest professor in the law school.  but it isnt worth a damn if you are set up for failure.  what i mean is, this prof is known for making a purposefully hard multiple choice final for which the average is 30%.  Civ pro is hard as it is without some equisite scholar traping you.
Good professor, but I never understood a dam thing he said.  Has a lot to do with subject though.  Probably the smartest prof in the law school, and that is saying a lot.
Powerful intellect and classroom presence.  One of the law school's finest.

Dr. Nateram was in a roundtable and development chat, and she went the extra mile to show everyone principles of a good contract and also talked about cybersecurity how important it is to review data we put into AI models and how we store information. She is so sweet and smart, if you get a chance to take her class, do it!

It's an ivy league school, so expect it to be challenging and difficult. Not sure about the other ratings but i learned alot from her and the course. I really like cornell's professional development classes and all the professors they have. Celina always took the time to have a coffee chat with students after going through the materials. 10/10

I really loved her instruction on my courses at Cornell and despite my challenges between being a busy mom and having a career, she made the time to respond to all my questions and genuinely felt she cared about the well being of students. I love Cornell and having professors like her.

Celina was an OUTSTANDING instructor for the Power and Ethics in Negotiation class.Her energy, intelligence and teaching style made the live sessions both fun and informative. She provided meticulous feedback, showing genuine care for students' growth.Cornell's cert programs are pricey but worth the investment, I'd love to take more of her courses.
Very interesting lecturer and class. Exams tended to be specific, but graded fairly. Lab portion is a lot of work with making the collection
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment:
very kind and funny, flexible about deadlines, and reasonable grading.

That said, this class is extremely hard if you don't already have a very strong understanding of insect bio it will take up a lot of time to memorize and learn everything.

I love this guy, but I don't love the way he teaches. Super cool guy, but his lectures are unclear and he includes material that is neither in the textbook nor lectures in his prelim. I thought BIOG 1440 was easy, but he was by far the hardest out of the four professors. Still love him though.

He's interesting and engaging, but his slides are mostly pics so you need to pay good attention. A lot of times he talks about unrelated things. The class is moderately difficult. There's videos, readings, and mandatory quizzes twice a week, 4 prelims, optional final, and attendance is not mandatory. Prelims are tricky. Don't take if not required.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Test heavy

reviews.comment: One of my favorite lecturers from this class.

Lecture notes are unclear and difficult to understand. Supplemental material don't pertain to lecture. Lecture topics random and disorganized. Lectures are repetitive and boring. Tests are on hyper-specific topics sometimes missing from lectures entirely. no curve

His lectures are a bit boring and each of his slides are just crammed with info. Pretty friendly guy tho. He makes an effort to teach but sometimes he explains concepts in a way that is more confusing than it should be. I also feel like the content on the exams are deeper than what he discusses in lecture.

He didn't get everyone falling asleep in class, but that DOESN'T MEAN he was a good professor. Lecture content was VERY different from test material, and the class WAS NOT curved. The average was supposed to be a C, but he widened the bins so everyone got a B-. Then the very last day of grading, he changed half the grades due to math error. RUN.

Dr Gilbert is by far the funniest and coolest lecturer of 1780. He teaches Animal evolutionary history and is hilarious and one of the few professors for the class that is actually engaging. Very down to earth, very meme worthy. Favorite professor of the semester by far, if only for the pure sass and sarcasm emanating from this man.
The insect collection is tough, but that's mandatory for the course and good planning will make it work. Cole is hilarious and caring.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: This professor is boring and disorganized. I have not gotten lower than an "A" until this 1440 Biology class. If you think looking at slides all course and cracking silly jokes is fun great. Exams had no continuity with lectures! Should have gotten out of his course. Then half the final grades posted were wrong and the final grades reposted - WOW!

Cole is a great lecturer and cracks a lot of dumb jokes. Definitely a steep learning curve in the lab and obviously geared towards people with some background knowledge already. Be prepared to spend a lot of time on the insect collection.

The fun does not balance out the work. The first half of the semester is unreasonably hard. Lecture is interesting even though it is early in the morning. Lab and the practicals are harder than class.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Terrible Class. Will never take again. Must have entire bug collection in addition to lab tests AND prelims in class. Tons of work. No reward.

He is definitely a nice guy, but I don't see how the lab and lecture correlate. In lab, you get thrown right in without any explanation. Lectures, he talks about basic, cool things, but you have to know a lot of details which he doesn't make you aware of. This should be an intro course, but I feel like I should have had a lot of prior knowledge.
Cole is an amazing lecturer and incredibly funny. He's super helpful and really does his best to make the class as informative and fun as possible.
So much better than Booker! And he's funny. He's very clear and keeps students engaged. Great professor!

Prof. Gilbert was great! He tried to be as interesting as you can with this material. His lectures were clear and simple, and he showed genuine interest in our understanding the subject and being prepared for the exams. Oh, and sometimes he'd randomly run around to make a point. Cool guy!

Cole is one of the best professors at Cornell that I've encountered. His lectures are interesting, and doesn't just lecture the reading; he goes above and beyond the reading. He is an incredibly nice guy, and extremely knowledgeable about entomology. I loved this course with him. A+

Hilarious.  The powerpoint slides he makes are very entertaining.  His material in 1780 can be hard because it's all memorization but he gives lots of interesting examples.  Possibly my favorite of the five 1780 lecturers.

Cole Gilbert is awesome.  Coming from an engineering and pre-med background, its really refreshing to have a teacher that is enthralled with what he teaches and can talk to students in a relaxed atmosphere.  As for the class, study his lectures and ask questions during lab and you'll do fine.  I just wish he'd teach more classes!
It was clear that he wanted us to do well, but he was not effective at teaching and I found the tests to be unfair compared to the material taught.

Fun class, Professor Gilbert made what would have been rather dry and straight from the text much more interesting with his anecdotes and examples but did not get sidetracked by them - lecture focused on major concepts not detail.
Great, interesting professor.
No Comments
Okay class, not a great teacher. Make sure you stay on schedule with the readings.

Dr. Gilbert is really, REALLY good. One of the best lecturers I could wish for. I wish all teachers could be so amiable yet knowledgeable. There are some profs you can't hear bc they mumble- Dr. Gilbert will never subject you to that. Crystal clear! Makes Bio accessible!!!
nice guy, sort of interesting sometimes, class needs a lot of reading and lectures can get pretty boring

Dr. Gilbert is an amazing teacher. Unlike other profs, he genuinely wants you to succeed and isn't out to get you. Tests are fair, but be prepared to put in a lot of effort. Goes through ppts and lectures very quickly, but they're all online afterwards. He also has pre lecture questions to help with the reading. Overall, an amazing teacher.

Fun lecturer.  Goes through slides a little too fast, but posts powerpoints and audio recording of lectures online, so you can catch up later and study easily.  Tests aren't easy, but they won't contain that many inordinately difficult questions.
Kool dude. Go through the powerpoint and lecture audio before the exam and ur golden.

Dr. Gilbert is not a good professor...he flies through his powerpoints without taking a breath...it's impossible to catch hardly any of what he is saying...his tests are unbearably difficult b/c of the large amount of material he inadequately covers..if you want to pass his class, be prepared to put in lot of extra work teaching yourself everything

Cole Gilbert is one of the most helpful professors at Cornell just go to his office hours. Though he zips through lecture and it can be difficult to keep up at first, as long as you actually put some effort its managable. He has a sence of humor too and truly tries to make class entertaining. Great professor to have.
Though he zipps through his lecture he tries to keep it interesting and is extremely accessable and helpfull during his office hours
No Comments

His lectures were informative but boring, he chose some really random and irrelevant things to go in depth with. Otherwise really helpful though. Good use of powerpoints and the clicker questions were actually helpful. For prelims though, your best bet is to just memorize the book. It's a challenging class, but not impossible.

His teaching style doesn't make or break the class - he's just aite. Ideally, you could look at the ppt slides (& listen to audio if you want to) & skip class & do just fine, but you gotta go for the clicker q's. Study really hard, you know the rep of intro bio...
He's very enthusiatic and willing to help anybody
I liked how he taught the class with funny jokes and whatnot

Dynamic and exciting lectures. He sometimes goes into certain topics too deep, but that's fine. Prelims can be harder than you think so be prepared. I personally didn't like his prelims because some questions seemed irrelevant. Yet, overall, Prof.Gilbert is a great guy.
I like his lectures. The diagrams are great !!

Gilbert is AMAZING!!!  I absolutely love this class.  He's a really dynamic lecturer, and he knows his stuff.  I'm pretty sad that he won't be teaching 2nd semester intro bio.  The best way I've found to study for this class is to take my textbook and highlight/take notes in it as he lectures...he tends to follow it pretty closely.  He's great!

As an intro level bio class for majors, the class is meant to be difficult and lives up to its reputation. However Cole does a great job in teaching the class. By going to lecutres, keeping up on the readings, and studying difficult material extra the class in manageable. Cole does excellent lectures, has great powerpoints, and is so knowledgeable.
No Comments

Ent 212 was the best class I have ever taken!  Dr Gilbert teaches in a fun, involved manner; he takes a deep interest in each student's progress.  Classes are interesting, field trips are fun, and the lab-work actually holds purpose!!!!!!

The orginzation in this class is extremely poor. No notes, answer keys, practice answers are posted so it doesn't feel encouraging to learn anyting someone might've not understood the first time. The actual content should be very sraightforward and simple, but due to the organization it makes everything unecessarily confusing and laborious.

Terrible experience, avoid at all costs. Assignments are tedious and do not contribute to learning the material itself. Lectures didn't make any sense because of his completely incomprehensible accent. I had to self-study the entire course because lectures were all useless. Please hire a new prof to teach this class.

One of the best CS profs I've had, really fun class where you actually use what you learn at on the final project, the assignments and the midterm are pretty chill, attendance does matter, prof teaches the material pretty well for everyone of all backgrounds (no ML or ML), and is really passionate about IR system designs and the Kardashians. 10/10.

DNM is super funny and is a great professor. The class gives lots of opportunities to gain extra credit, and the grading for everything is very lenient. You will learn a lot of applicable information retrieval techniques, and although the class might have some questionable/annoying policies, overall it is worthwhile and fun thanks to DNM!

DNM's class is really good in information retrieval if you are in the mindset to learn. He is an understanding and proactive professor that cares about students and learning. He is an expert of the area and makes this class very fruitful.

This was one of the most frustrating classes I've taken at Cornell. Mandatory participation in a large lecture just doesn't make sense. There are milestones for the project due basically every few days, which made the class feel way more stressful than it needed to be. The structure of the course felt more like busywork than meaningful learning.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Group projects--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Easily my least favorite professor / teacher in all the years I've ever been in school, college or lower. His lectures were boring, and he will dock you a full 10% off your final grade if you don't satisfy some minimum bar (not explicitly given) of participation in classes -- no warning, no clarity, full discretion on his part.
condescending and slams you with way too much homework at the beginning of the semester. not a nice person when you talk to him

Overall, class is fine, interesting content, easier CS class. He as a person is not great. Not only is he hard to contact if you are having any issues and need assistance, but also simply doesn't care about his students unless you are doing everything right. Also, if you miss class good luck bc no textbook or note posting which I think says it all
good guy
Really funny and overall interesting guy. Class is fairly easy compared to your average 4000 level CS class.

Ridiculous class policies, he doesn't allow you to use pencils on prelims or write on the actual exam. 

When asked why he said, "If you are looking into a teaching career and want to find out more about the motivation behind each of the decisions, please come to my OH and I'll be happy to share my experience with you."

Class is very popular as it is easy and interesting. Christian is a decent lecturer, but class felt very disorganized (took it in online Spring 2021 semester), and a lot of the times I felt like I was learning something simple in a more complicated way. Overall good class and teacher though, kind of what you put in is what you get out type class.

Fair, quick, clear and thorough (to me, maybe depends on the way you think), nice-ish, can come across sharp/spiky, don't take it personally. Accent and occasional spelling mistakes are hardly an issue. The material isn't crazy or anything that difficult, but the class does take time and some effort. He's very focused on very specific things.
Dont like his personality
One of the best classes ive taken at Cornell. His lectures are interesting/funny and the content is relevant. I Learned a lot.
Quick sharp excellent fair grader

Hard to believe DNM doesn't have his page yet. He is AWESOME (except maybe for his accent?). Great lecturer, lots of information in the lecture, very helpful after class. He is a professor who knows what actual humor is. The project is super instructive and preps you for real-life projects.

Great teacher--you get out what you put in, and I definitely put in mid levels of effort for an okay grade. Awesome lecturer and a total math nerd. I felt like some parts of the class were taught in a very unintuitive way that just didn't mesh with my learning style, but the assigned textbook generally explains things well.

Kelly Delp is clearly very passionate about math, but one of the issues I had is that she would focus lots of time on the conceptual aspect of problems whereas Math 1920 is more of an application class rather than a proof class. Her notes are very helpful and posts skeleton and final notes after lectures. She's great but do more practice problems.

Focused a lot more on the general idea of the math and why it was cool rather than how to actually do it. We were told why something was important before how it worked. Because of this, I ended up learning mostly from posted notes and the textbook. You could tell she was passionate about the math though, and that was not always contagious.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Test heavy

reviews.comment: The class itself was hard, but Delp at least made it bearable. She was always very energetic and gave really good lectures. She was always very friendly and seemed to know the names of many students in the class and would greet people as they entered the class making it a really nice class environment.

Delp is great! The policies for her course are very clear and straightforward, and she provides skeleton notes before every class so people know what we are covering. The lectures aren't my favorite environment, and I often relied on the textbook to supplement them, but that's ultimately a skill issue: Delp teaches incredibly well.

Professor Delp was the best math professor/teacher I've ever had. She provides skeleton notes to all her lectures which make it easy to follow and understand. Prelims are generally easy and she gives 3 long quizzes in discussion throughout the course. Homework is the best way to learn in this class and also asking questions is a must if lost
The tests were graded so brutally, and barely any partial credit was given. Delp is fine, but her lectures aren't super helpful. You probably have to rely on the textbook mostly.

Amazing lectures, her notes are super organized and she explains things very well. Her lectures pair well with the textbook, honestly best professor I've had so far. She's super sweet and genuinely cares about all her students. I personally think 1920 was way easier than 1910. Would recommend Delp to anyone who can take her class.
absolute queen. her notes were very organized, making it very easy to follow. great explainer. very passionate.
Did not miss any single lecture. Professor really kind and helpful!

Delp is kind, but represents the issue of assigning senior lecturers to intro/low-level classes; they're overqualified thus don't take the low-level courses as seriously, leading to lapses in lecture quality and pertinence to exams. Read the textbook in Finite Math because you'll learn more than in lectures. She's incredibly nice, however.
Easily one of the best professors I've ever had. Her lectures were extremely well planned and organized, and the homework questions were chosen well . If you can get Delp, get Delp.

Amazing lecturer, really cares about students, and is very accessible. Entertains all questions without making anyone feel silly. Final kicked my butt but course was otherwise pretty fair if you read the textbook outside of class
go to lecture and do readings ahead of time if you dont want to grind before exams. do every homework problem at least once
Multi was very cool to understand. Delp does an excellent job in really providing a conceptual and intuitive mastery of the material. Challenging but very fruitful. Would take again.

The textbook is the saving grace of a Delp course; relying on lectures would leave you desperate, stranded and alone. You will have no choice but to take 1920 with her, but only having the previous reviews to set expectations is a sin- you will be sorely disappointed. Honestly, I am not positive that I had the same professor as other reviewers.

Loved Professor Delp she has skeleton notes for all of her lectures making them so easy to follow and fill in, and she will answer any question you have in office hours. Clearly loves math and wants us to love it too
Amazing professor who is really passionate about what she's teaching. Made a huge difference in the class despite its difficulty.

I didn't show up to 1920 lecture much, but whenever I did she was an amazing lecturer. Expectations for quizzes and tests are clear and if you do the homework and study it well you will do well. Deadlines are a little flexible. If you have the chance to get her, take her and you won't regret it.

Professor Delp is a cool dude. You'll probably get discouraged while doing the homework since some of the problems are straight-up impossible, but the questions on the quizzes and prelims always seem to be fair. She goes pretty fast in her lectures, so stay focused and don't get distracted by things such as the majesticity of her hair

Professor Delp really cares about her students, and is such an inspiring, fair, and passionate person. I thoroughly enjoyed taking multivariable calculus with her, and learned a lot from the course. I came in with no experience in the subject, and now feel confident with all the material. Quizzes and Exams(except maybe the final) are all very fair.

Heard multi can be very bad. However, I was very lucky to have Delp as my professor. I actually ended up liking multivariable. Homework is helpful and prelims/exams very fair. Discussions are helpful.

The only good thing about this class is that hw is worth 30% of grade. She adjusts grade cutoffs very fairly, but that doesn't take away from the material being hard and boring. She is a nice person in general, but her lectures are terrible. She is scatterbrained and gets distracted easily, making it difficult to follow the math problems.

The material itself is hard but going to lectures with Professor Delp and participating makes all the difference. Professor Delp is so excited about math and cares so much about her students, her lectures are great. Wish I could take another class with her.
I don't go to lectures that often since it's 9 am, but when I do go, I never regretted it. She's great at explaining difficult concepts with simpler ones. Also, the lecture note is amazing.

Professor Delp is fantastic! She is really passionate about what she teaches and brings good energy to her lectures. Her exams are really fair and are based on understanding of how everything works rather than just giving really hard questions that are meant to stump students. She is kind and understanding, as well.

Great professor! Truly cares about students, makes an effort to engage the class and keep them awake during the early morning lectures. Very passionate about the subject which comes across in her teaching. Pretty fair weight of homework/assignments, great grading scheme, very accessible especially over EdStem for doubts all semester long.
I have never met a professor as passionate about their course material as Professor Delp. Her lectures are fantastic and engaging. Truly an excellent teacher.

I dropped the class last sem because of another professor, Connelly. I was on track to get a C+ or at most a B- in the course, as Connelly's lectures didn't work for me. Delp's course organization is phenomenal. Love the "skeleton notes" Now on track to get an A/A+, and I have a solid grasp of multi now. Great lectures, textbook is a supplement.
Prof Delp is by far the most engaging and thorough math professor I've had. Multivariable calculus is a difficult, boring subject that she makes enjoyable.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I actually really enjoyed math 1920 because of professor Delp. She gives very clear and engaging lectures which make the course material much simpler and more comprehensible. The homework is reasonably difficult and prepares you well for the exams.

Professor Kelly Delp is a real gem. She has a passion for teaching. Her homework questions really make you question your major. I am lucky to be in her Math 1920 class. I respect Professor Kelly Delp a lot. Sometimes she can speak in Calculus 9 during her lectures so you should know the material well

Dr. Delp is a great teacher and her passion for math really shows in lecture. She's good at explaining difficult topics and very open to questions. Makes very fair tests and wants everyone to do well.

Delp is an amazing person and professor. Extremely kind, passionate, and helpful to all her students. Though the lectures were just what the textbook says, her breaking down the topic in lecture was helpful for me. The lectures were enough for me, no crazy amount of textbook readings, but reading a bit before lecture was always solid.

Lectures didn't really help me in terms of understanding material so I just stopped attending. I basically learned everything from the textbook and did fine. Professor is really kind, caring, and makes lecture fun though. Exams were not too bad; very manageable if you did the homework in my opinion.

Professor Delp taught this class in an extraordinarily approachable manner. The lecture was fun; she may seem a bit disorganized but she is extremely knowledgeable about the material, is passionate about the content, and successfully manages to inspire students with an eager sense to learn. The textbook is a must. Restored my joy in mathematics.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Respected--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: She is literally the best. She is so caring for her students and really tries to make sure you understand the material. Office hours are super useful and thats where she gets to know her students. Even on zoom she played music and made it a really fun environment. She was also really good at answering questions in the middle of lecture.
Kelly Delp is an amazing lecturer! I was intimidated by multivariable calculus but she made it quite easy to understand. It is the best class i have taken so far.
Professor Delp's office hours were extremely helpful. Don't be afraid to schedule an appointment with her as wellshe'll go above and beyond to make sure you understand a difficult topic.

Great Professor. Unlike many professors here she cared about us learning the material and having fun along the way. Dynamic lecturer, and she really goes deep into the visualization of the nitty gritty concepts, which was a lifesaver for a visual learner like myself.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Inspirational--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Dr. Delp is a passionate, energetic, and dynamic lecturer who made me love multivariable calculus. If you have to take 1920, take it with her--the class and her exams are FAR more fair than other professors who teach the class. Lectures are essential for understanding conceptual parts of the course, which she loves to test on her prelims and final.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Inspirational--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Gives sample past exams but doesnt give solutions. What's wrong with her brain?
Delp is a really cool person who is obviously very passionate about math. Lectures are sometimes tangential and the tests are way too hard (especially prelim 1)

She ditzes out - a lot. And her voice is annoying. And goes on tangents about graphs, and the "+, -, 0" game. For Example - she didn't know the name of the fingers. and then spent a couple minutes telling us about it.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--TEST HEAVY--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Kelly's lectures are energetic and she's enthusiastic about sharing her love for math. She also tries her best to provide visuals for a better understanding of the material. However, it is multivariable calc, and won't be easy.

Multi is notoriously a hard class; however, Professor Delp has great energy so far this spring. She's a great lecturer and seems like she really enjoys teaching multi. She has her lectures prepared before hand and does not get side tracked. I really like her so far.
Her lectures don't make any sense and don't help with homework or section. Alot of notes aren't given and the group work is stupid.
She is very energetic during lectures and is open to answering questions. Uses a lot of visual representations, which helps with understanding the 3D structures in multi.

She's really sweet and nice but the problem is she can be a little scatter-brained and she makes a lot of mistakes. She's very good about catching and correcting them but by that point you're already confused. Multi is hard enough even with good lectures.

Truly the greatest of his kind. Robby was a solid 5 stars through and through. Genuinely cared for the students. He speaks a bit like a cartoon character but you get used to it. 
-signed Robby's #1 fan
Tries to act like he's nice and understanding but nothing he said was that helpful when you went to him for help. Also tries to make grading clear however I was curved down at the end.

A little funny but he clearly doesn't want to teach. He's definitely not a terrible person but I found him very inaccessible towards the end of the semester which was frankly aggravating. 

Lectures were just textbook but he sometimes helped clarify some details. Maybe I should've just skipped class and watched youtube videos on the subject.

He is a pretty good lecturer but his exams were the complete opposite of the practice problems he provided. the previous year's exams that he gave were 10x easier than our exams and the material taught during lecture was not reflective of exam materials at all. he is very knowledgeable of the material and kind, but the exams were way too difficult.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He's a great guy who cares about his students and makes the time to help people, but his tests are pretty difficult and does not provide enough materials to prepare for those caliber of questions. Lectures are straight from the textbook and I would recommend reading the textbook, especially parts you're having trouble with. Great dude, mid teacher.
Curve sucks. Lectures are uninspired, straight from the textbook. Exams are too hard for it to be curved so poorly.

DiStasio is a great professor. The material comes directly from the textbook and is easy to follow. The exams are straightforward and there are many opportunities to improve your grade. He is very open to feedback and meeting with students one on one. Genuinely one of the easiest classes at Cornell just read the textbook and do the practice.

He's actually such a sweetheart. People dislike the class because it's hard but thats not this professor's fault. Such a breath of fresh air compared to my other prof's he was so great, always encouraging and willing to help.
Good lecturer! Exams were super tough, but the curve was generous. Average on one exam was a 26%

DiStasio is a good lecturer and I found it easier to attend class when I read the material- even skimmed for ten minutes - before coming because he's organized with the topics. Go to office hours to go over back of the book questions/conceptual (!!!) understanding - heavy on concepts - and don't fall back on lab grades, it can seriously hurt
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Didn't like it. Lecture material versus test material didn't match too well.

CHEM 2080 was a very difficult class for me. I didn't love how his questions were worded - left me very annoyed when I could solve something but simply didnt understand what the question wanted. But the DiStasio is a great person - ever-willing to help, stay extra time, etc. His prelims are TOUGH but the curves usually compensated. Best of luck!

Professor DiStasio makes the content interesting and funny, so I'd recommend attending lecture. Material is straight from the textbook. Discussion sections weren't helpful, but their problems were good practice. Prelims were hard but well-curved; good preparation with the textbook, practice problems, and practice prelims will help you succeed.
no
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I liked his lectures. He definitely gives you everything you need to succeed in his class, given you put in the effort. One thing I didn't like was how difficult the prelims were, but that meant the curves were really helpful. Just do EVERY textbook problem and you'll be fine.

Dr. DiStasio cares deeply about his students. This was a difficult semester for me and he was more than understanding. I recommend going to office hours. It's an 800-student class, but only seven or eight people go to his office hours. He went over my exams with me and helped me understand course material in a one-on-one environment.
Distasio is THE MAN! His exams come directly from lecture and his explanations are extremely clear. Go to class, do practice problems, and attend sections and you're good!

Robert is great. As others have said he really cares about making sure you learn the material. Lectures not required but I highly recommend- he's always telling some funny story or analogy. Exams are very fair just do the practice tests. 2080 labs SUCK but not Robert's juristiction so can't blame him
He really likes chemistry and wants his students to as well. You can tell he genuinely cares about his students and just wants us to appreciate chemistry like he does. He's a funny bloke also.

DiStasio was helpful in making a hard class more fair; he let students have one-on-one meetings with him and even gave me points back for two of my exams, he listens to student feedback and is easy to talk to; the class itself is difficult but I felt a lot more comfortable having him as a professor!
Good professor, exams were mostly fair, genuinely wanted us to succeed.

Robert is a beautiful soul. People say humans make mistakes, but rob doesn't. He is the closest thing to perfect a human can be. His kindness and caring nature has almost brought me to tears. His class was actually very good, difficult but since Rob is so amazing he got us through it, unlike in chem 2070. He also frequents goldies often, so pu.

I genuinely adore Prof DiStasio-- I haven't had such a lovely chem professor since high school. His lectures are well-paced, engaging, understandable, and informative, and he makes it obvious how much he cares about our success and well-being. Exams are fair and he regularly asks our opinions about the length and difficulty. Overall lovely guy.

Professor DiStasio is a great! He's accessible outside of class & cares for the students. Curves the exams(actually tries to benefit as many people as possible). The exams are fair and similar to PS problems. The lab for this course sucks it's not in sync with the lecture and you have to make up your own procedure. Only responsible for lec though.
reviews.rating_tags: Hilarious--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: heavily relied on the textbook, but lectures were well organized and easy to follow. tests were fair. if you go to lecture, follow the textbook, and do the practice problems and homework, you should have no problems with the class. definitely learn a lot and dr. Distasio was great!
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Test heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Lectures come straight from the textbook and are recorded (very easy to watch in 2x speed). Discussion sections were pointless since they would go over the easy questions. Problem sets were a must do before every exam. Basically understand how to do every problem on the PS and the class is cake. Lab depends on TA, some good and some very bad.

He's kind of boring during lectures and basically rewords the textbook. He was very behind on content and that kind of messed with the lab portion of the course. Exams are at a reasonable difficulty if you do practice problems from the textbook + discussion sets, but a bit too many questions in my opinion (have to rush to finish them).

Dr. DiStasio is not a bad professor, but he's just very boring. His lectures are live readings of the textbook and aren't very engaging. However, he pulls straight from the textbook problems for prelims so if you do a lot of the problems from the end of chapter, you'll be fine. Gives lots of opportunities for grade improvement through mini prelims.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor DiStassio was a great teacher and I would highly recommend taking a class from him. Very kind and caring toward his students and you could tell he genuinely cares about student success. 2080 is a hard class but DiStassio was always available to work through difficult concepts in-office hours. The grading breakdown was also quite fair.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Prof. DiStasio loves chemistry, and it shows in the way he speaks during lectures. He makes content interesting, easy to understand, and is accessible for 1:1 meetings with any student upon request. After the horror that was CHEM 2070, 2080 was a breath of fresh air that made me realize why I'm passionate about chemistry.

So surprised to see the comments about the exams being difficult--the questions were based on the problems in the textbook! If you studied and did all the problems in the chapters/back of the book, you wouldn't be confused at all. All the DPSs were prelim level problems. Oh, and Kinsland runs the labs/TAs, not DiStasio. Truly one of the best Profs!

Professor DiStasio is understanding and caring. Doesn't try to harm you with grading but grading could be tough due to the difficulty of the content taught. Tests are reasonable, but challenging. Professor DiStasio makes himself clear and available. Lab portions are hard. lectures are strictly followed by a textbook. Overall good professor.
Nice-ish guy but does not give enough practice problems to prep for exams. Lab portion is unreasonably difficult. Do not take this course if you don't absolutely have to

Sweet man who loves his students. However, prepare to be confused and struggle on those exams. A lot of 2080 is assumption based, so if you're like me and prefer things to be exact, you will find this class difficult. If you don't need to take this class, don't. Not too many resources but use the book, but exams are harder than the examples given.

The content in this course was relatively easy, but the lab portion was unreasonably difficult. Professor Distasio gave clear lectures, however he went slow and we did not finish the course material. He is a very nice and engaging man, you would be lucky to have him as a gen chem professor.
reviews.rating_tags: So many papers--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: He is a very good lecturer and cares about his students. Solid professor

Lectures are awesome and he closely follows the textbook which is good because you always know where to reference information. Wasn't a fan of how tough he graded the class considering that the material was less difficult compared to 3890. Super accessible outside of class and always willing to help during office hours.

You'd think this man has a pizza place in Brooklyn, not a PhD from Berkeley in Quantum Mechanics. Crazy hard tests, but he made them shorter so that he can get out of campus earlier to his hot date (aka wife). Take this class, you will not be disappointed (except with your grade).

Very good chalkboard lectures for class, very dry content. Worst part of class is group homework and group final project -- if you get a bad group / group member that just does not want to meet, its hard to learn the content. Learn by working with friends, group with them if possible. He does not know Python, but has great theoretical knowledge.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Amazing lectures --Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Prof. Diamessis is great. No matter how challenging or coding‑heavy the class can feel at the time, the ideas will only become more relevant. Definitely go to class and participate!

diamessis is a great guy once you get to talk to him, this class can be difficult and I understand why he might get a bad rep, but if you put even an ounce of effort to talking to him about needing help with the class he will help you!

I don't know what happened last year, but he literally implies he could give extra credit in return of a good review. I think he needs to focus on the quality of his course rather than finding a way around.

Very funny guy. Gives off the energy of the basketball coach who's forced to teach a class, except he's actually a decent lecturer and not actually a basketball coach. The psets are pretty long and require a solid background in coding. Idk why last years class resented him so much. People generally seemed to like him and the class this year

In most cases, Prof. Diamessis gives you a feeling that he just learnt the material a night before the lecture but not fully understood. Overall, the lack of quality in his class makes you question both his knowledge of the subject and intellectual rigor.

Prof Diamessis is a great guy and is very accommodating towards his students. However, his lectures are very difficult to follow. Psets/ Final project were difficult and TAs were unable to answer many questions in office hours, leaving students even more lost. Exams were doable but grading was extremelyyyyyy harsh on all assignments.

Prof D is a great person, but a terrible professor. He struggles to break down concepts to an understandable level, does not post lecture resources, has difficulties answering student questions, and never works through example problems. Worst of all, his TAs often don't understand the content and thus prelims and finals are graded SUBJECTIVELY.

He chooses his course time for a slot that is honestly waay too early, you'll notice that fewer students show up as a result. Problem set every week but assigned for teams of two, which makes things a little easier. Nice guy, though sometimes a little jumbled. Receives and utilizes student feedback effectively.

Probably the most down-to-earth, friendly professor I've had. Extremely helpful, responsive and pragmatic. Good with posting notes, homework, and practice exams that are relevant so missing class isn't a huge problem. He is the kind of guy you want to sit down and have a beer with.
Cool guy. Know his stuff and helps and cares his students at all times. Lots of coding for homework, but some are very conceptual as well. Exams are hard, but grading is fair.

TAs make or break this class. Content is very dry. Professor does a lot of examples during class which are worth doing in the long run, as they give you extra practice. They are also what is tested. Recommend learning LaTeX notation for this class. Assignments were a pain and not helpful usually, but ZyBook HW was. Final was ridiculously long.

Lectures were dry and heavy on theoretical information. Recitations were somewhat helpful but the many assignments were extremely difficult and required asking questions or stumbling through searching for specific coding functions. The class felt like it was preparing students for a PhD rather than just a masters level course. High expecations.

Daziano is a good lecturer, but outside of lecture the course was really disorganized. The recitations felt useless, and the online homework portal was really frustrating. If you studied the slides and did the homework, it was fairly easy to do well enough. Also, some lectures were way too R/Stata/Minitab heavy to be engaging.

This man is the GOAT. Truly so understanding and kind and always willing to help you out. Also incredible at responding to emails. I had to take this class for my minor, and I am glad I took it with him. It is a difficult course, but he does his best to make sure you succeed. THE GOAT!

For such a boring subject, Professor Szpiro was an extremely kind and helpful professor. I had conflicts with every exam, and he worked tirelessly to help accommodate other students. Additionally, he has given some very helpful extra credit! Also, he starts every class with a joke, so that's fun.
nice professor
Amazing, supportive professor.
exams arent cumulative but can be hard sometimes, allows us to have a cheat sheet every exam, lots of opportunities to get extra credit. prof szpiro 10/10

Dr. Szpiro is very caring and understanding. That being said, the lectures honestly didn't help much for prelims. Just grind Mcgraw Hill and read the textbook. With so many cracked ppl in this class, the curve isn't lax but an A or A- aren't difficult to achieve. You're allowed a page of notes on tests, so the exam vibe is pretty chill.

The professor himself was an amazing person. This class was extremely difficult partly because of him and myself. THE TAS WERE VERY WEIRD THO. I once met with a Ta and he spent the time yelling at his girlfriend on the phone... Pay attention to lectures and the moment you get confused talk to him about it. He is very open and kind! lots of respect
Great professor, goes over content really well and class is organized in a way that makes learning easy
Professor Szpiro is the undisputed GOAT. Very caring, responsive, and understanding. The man wishes the best for the students and is very humble, one of the best at Cornell.

pros:
 - dr. szpiro is extremely kind, caring, and accessible. if you go to him with questions, he will devote his time to ensure you understand.
 - quick with emails

with accounting, practice makes perfect and the textbook is extremely helpful. couldn't recommend a better professor to teach this course.

Definitely my favorite professor so far at Cornell, his lectures are very clear and communicate the material in multiple different ways so that you will understand it. I had no experience in business but was able to do well because of him. Tests are open note, and there is plenty of practice material available. I'll miss the class for sure.

Amazing professor. He is very passionate about the subject and will make sure that you understand everything needed for the tests. He wants to see you succeed. The grading structure of the class is designed for you to do well, with 25% of the class grading being based on online HW and quizzes that you should easily be able to get 100s on.

One of the best professors I had. Lectures are decent and he really was passionate and excited everyday. Started every lecture with an accounting joke. Explained everything to you in a friendly, non-condescending way until you understood it and was very knowledgeable. Wouldn't want to learn accounting from any other professor. Exams are a bit hard

The nicest professor ever. He's a good professor, goes over the content in a way it's easily understandable, also explains any questions you may have really well. He makes sure you understand the concept before moving on. Just one slight issue is that he doesn't let you view the prelims unless you scored below the mean.

Great professor, he really tries to make the subject engaging. Attendance is not mandatory, grading is clear and fair, and there are lots of practice materials provided. Overall would highly recommend both the class and professor.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Szpiro is an amazing professor. He lectures at just the right pace, making some of the more complex material accessible. I especially appreciated his use of Panopto recordings, which are a valuable resource for reinforcing learning. Both the exams and homework assignments are fair.
Probably the nicest, most caring professor I've had a Cornell. He is truly exceptional and makes accounting fun and interesting.

Professor Szpiro is the best professor to learn accounting with! Not only does he try to make every class fun with a joke, he also explains concepts very well. Homeworks and pre-class quizzes are very reasonable and exams were very straightforward. Overall would strongly recommend taking this class with Szpiro.

Professor Szpiro has been one of my favorite professors at Cornell. His class is well-run, fairly structured, and he genuinely cares about his students. Professor Szpiro offers plenty of resources to succeed in his class with extended office hours times, extra practice assignments to prepare for exams, and chapter summaries to aid with studying.
Nice guy, put a lot of effort into organizing the class and tried his best to make accounting interesting. Exams were open book so not very hard.
He treats you like you are 4 years old in office hours. Grading critera seemed to change like the wind. Would not reccomend taking the course
This testing brought benefit to me. Examinations were of the easiest, require no study.

Dr. Kowal was a nice prof, and I believe he made BTRY 4030 as interesting as he could. It's not a very applied course by nature, but he tried hard to give us homework and exam problems which were applied  to the real world, which I enjoyed. The work load in this class was manageable, but exams were hard in my opinion.
Good personality, good lecture style! The exams are pretty easy and his notes are good for reviewing. Asking questions after class is always welcome.

Basically no curve. Which he said he would. Like my raw grade was a B+ and I end with a B+. IDK maybe I was not scoring meaningfully more to get a A-. But he is so unbelievably rude. Like his lectures are good and easy to follow but he is insanely rude. Like talking to him feels insulting at times and he grades really hard.

Yes, he can be boring with dry lectures. BUT, incredibly approachable, very helpful when asking questions, gives few homeworks and they're easy, gives easy exams, and will often take your side when you have regrade requests. Exams are basically the same as practice exams and there are no trick questions.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: He is incredibly confident (to put it nicely). He believes it is the world's greatest inconvenience when you have to ask him a question. He is a decent lecturer but completely unreasonable to work with as an instructor. Fair exams. Would recommend waiting to take it with another professor.

He was very dry and seemed pretty unapproachable and uninterested in the class. He was EXTREMELY unclear about grading policies (although i will recognize it was his first time teaching the course) and just overall vague and uncommunicative. The class is a major requirement but didn't even feel useful. Don't really recommend

He was not communicative at all, rude and dismissive to those who wanted to talk about grades, and was not transparent about grading (no curve). TAs graded your assignments twice, often resulting in lower grades. Cheating on exams was rampant. If he teaches this again, don't take it. For the stats major, you are probably better off in ECON 3140.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: basically a self study class, why pay so much to go to university when you have to learn content on your own?

Prof. Savransky is an understanding and caring professor. He is lenient with grading at the end of the semester, allowing students to complete an extra credit problem set. With that said, it was difficult to retain much from lectures in the course, as a flipped-classroom approach was implemented. Homework assignments were also extremely long.

Horrible professor that makes a hard class harder. Tests are easier than hws but the homework's are excruciating. Extremely tough grader. ended with a 93.9 and that was an A-. Lectures were entirely unhelpful for hw or exams. primarily poll ev based. not understanding or nice

Most organized and clear class I have ever taken. Lectures are super interesting if you watch the videos ahead of time, like you are supposed to. He is a really funny guy who helps everyone make light of what is definitely a tough class. I think he lives on Ed and gives super great explanations to any questions. Had a really great experience
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Really was great to learn from as someone who never did dynamics before - everything was super clear and if anyone had a question he usually responded in less than 20 minutes online. All lecture content was online in videos/handouts which made everything super easy to reference. Homework is hard, but lectures are super engaging and interesting

He is extremely passionate and it shines through with the effort he puts into his classes. He gives very detailed & rigorous notes for his lectures; with 4060 this was incredibly helpful since it is a complex topic, however it was definitely a lot harder. Only professor I've ever had that reminded/validated us how mechE is hard & we're doing great

Great professor - definitely loves the math side of dynamics but also very knowledgeable about the material and fielding questions. Makes you work for the class but at the end of the day is forgiving about grading.

Went incredibly fast through lectures at the beginning of the class, calmed down after a few weeks. Homeworks varied, some were easy but others were really long and tough. The material covered at the end was unnecessarily difficult for the course. Was sometimes condescending in lecture but usually helpful at office hours. Open note exams.

He speeds breathlessly through lectures so it's hard enough to write anything let alone understand what he's talking about. Even worse he makes a lot of mistakes in lecture which makes you feel even more lost. Treats dynamics as if we've all been studying it for the past ten years. The textbook is just as bad. Plenty of office hrs to help though.
super fast iclicker

Elke's lectures are quite fluid and free-form, but she is one of the most caring and genuine professors at this school. She encourages participation through engagement and creates an environment where students feel comfortable sharing their ideas. Do the readings — if you stay on track, you'll do great. I'd highly encourage ANY course of hers.

She was filling in for a colleague on sabbatical so IDK if she'll teach the course again. Made me fall in love with humanities. Obvious she cared about all her students in and out of class, was willing to talk about anything. Clear grader...and everyone got an A+

She is interested in the topics she covered, but there wasn't much of a connection between all of them. She does give great insight into learning German better, and her feedback on papers is also great.

Elke Siegel is hands down my favorite professor at Cornell. I've taken three German classes with her and it is very apparent that she is there to teach you German - she doesn't give busy work and she doesn't grade unfairly. If anything she is a too nice a grader. If you have any interest in taking a GERST classs, take Siegel's!!!

My hatred for poetry is long and enduring; I was in the class to improve my German. I agree with the previous reviewer, that anyone who speaks German should take a class with Professor Siegel.   If you only have time in your schedule to fit in one German class, take whatever class Siegel is offering. You won't regret it.

I took Frau Siegel's class Lyric Poetry, which I never thought I would take because I hate poetry. But she made it super interesting and I would definitely recommend this course if she teaches it again! If you're into German whatsoever you absolutely have to take a class with her. And on top of that she's a really nice grader.

GREAT TEACHER!! So passionate and knows so much about Dante's inferno. This is not an easy class, but great for improving writing and analysis. is based off of effort/improvement and you can get an A (not based off of individual grades)
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Very nice professor. Grading are based on your commitment.Homeworks are basically journals (200 words ish) and Assignments (1000 words ish). The professor is quite flexibile on ddls. Assignments are not graded, but instead you have to sign up individual meetings with her, and she gives really helpful feedback.

IEA very clearly cares a lot about her class and her role as a teacher. She is there to help you improve in your writing and will NOT give an easy A for FWS. She will give a lot of feedback and help you get better. If you put in the work and clearly try to improve, you'll get good grade and you'll learn a fair amount. Def more work than other FWS.
reviews.rating_tags: Caring--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: One of, if not the, best first-year writing seminar professors. Her feedback is incredibly detailed. Instead of simply giving out letter grades and due dates, she expects you to improve your essays as much as possible through office hours. All grades in her course are based on the final drafts at the end of the semester: she focuses on improvement!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I love this prof, the topics were so interesting and we read/watching things about love so most of our assignments were a response to them. The final project was ab a topic of your choosing, it was so helpful to have meetings with her ab the essays and she's super understanding. My new fws doesn't compare-also the community in the class was on top

ROMS 1108: Took her FWS and almost everybody got a B+. AVOID THIS CLASS. If you turn in assignments on time and do work well the default is a B+, which is lower than other writing seminars, which usually give As.

Alvisi was great and taught me a lot about Italian literature. I really enjoyed her classes, and I definitely would have enjoyed them more if they were in person. Just be sure to participate a ton and always come prepared, she respects that a lot.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Definitely gives extremely harsh feedback on essays but is a lovely break from high school English classes. She managed to make learning about Dante's Inferno fun, with a superb mastery of the text and frequent humor. Does not grade individual essays, but requires multiple meetings for feedback.

She's a great lecturer and is very knowledgeable about the text. Also, I learned how to organize my thoughts in writing properly thanks to her. At first, I thought she gave me excessively harsh feedback on my papers, but her concete and thoughtful criticism ultimately improved my writing skills.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Funny in class. Get ready to do a lot of assignments.
killed my love for architecture

This instructor yells at students in public, dismisses questions, and undermines the co-instructor. Reacts emotionally and grades vindictively, giving abnormally low marks that even other faculty noted. Lacks the ability to teach, making the studio toxic, demoralizing, and a total waste of time.
Killed my love for learning

Good lecturer and accessible outside of the lecture. Prelim and final are interchangeable compared to the practice exams, which were very helpful. However, assignments towards the end of the semester have no consideration for students' workload and can take upwards of 15-20 hours. Grading for assignments is harsh as well.

Eichhorn is super disorganized in his lectures and I can barely understand his handwriting. No curve in the class and the assignments are SUPER hard to do good in. TAs in office hours DO NOT help AT ALL. Walker was much better so please lower your expectations for Matt.

The class is well organized and structured for student success. Expectations and grading criteria are clearly outlined for assignments and exams. There is plenty of practice material, along with helpful exam reviews and accessible office hours. Overall, the learning environment is supportive and comfortable.
goated teacher
Matt is fun and he is patient, but I just hate CS 2110 material .......

Really good lectures and knows data structures really well. Tests were very straightforward and clearly based on the practice materials. Assignments got reworked for FA25 and were long but not entirely useful.

Great lecturer and writes excellent lecture notes. Very responsive and helpful on Ed. HW can be tedious/annoying but not difficult to get high grades if you put in some time. Exams are very fair, and practice exams are extremely similar to actual ones. Overall course grading is very transparent.

Overall a good lecturer and very available outside of class. I really appreciate how he has good notes/exercises for each lesson. Generous grading/grade weighting. However the hw takes hours/days and requires you to camp on Edstem because he expects something that wasn't in the assignment handout and the only way to know is to see his Ed posts.

Great lectures and great lecture notes. Really supportive professor who is very willing to help outside of class. Provides practice prelims very similar to real ones. However, homework assignments are long and very harshly graded.

He makes really hard assignments especially the last few, which can take up to 15-20 hours. He also teaches certain topics in a very theoretical way. For example, instead of applying data structures to solve problems, he asks you to create the methods of the data structure yourself. Overall good class and doable prelims, but hard assignments.

Prelims were pretty difficult and the homework is such a pain, but Matt's lectures made the class bearable as they were very engaging and interesting. He also is always helping and answering questions on Ed and making sure you understand the material
Great professor and amazing communicator!
Really caring and nice professor. GOAT. Not an easy class content-wise but everything he does and the organization of his class makes everything feel feasible.

Prof. Eichhorn is really one of the most caring and inspirational professors at Cornell. He genuinely cares about his students and wants each and every one of you to succeed. The content moves fast and can be a bit difficult at times, but he is always available during OH or reachable by email. GO TO OFFICE HOURS!

Most empathetic professor I've had at Cornell! Cares very much about students being able to digest course material. Tests were fair and similar to practice exams. Very passionate about math and breaks down difficult concepts in a way that makes them digestible. Class attendance and participation matters.
great professor!
matthew my beloved
matthew saved my marriage

Matt is the best CS professor I've had so far. He's awesome, super caring, and is able to explain difficult topics in lectures super well. This class has hard psets and exams, but that's how it is regardless of the professor. He is a great professor and person.

Easily one of my most interesting classes at Cornell, and Matt's ability to lecture really contributed to that. He's a genuinely great person and I can tell how much he cares about his students. Homeworks can be graded pretty harshly/nitpicky

such a goated prof, one of the kindest, most caring faculty here who's clearly loves his job. genuinely feel like bros lectures are a shot of expresso to my MWFs. problem sets do requires a lot of attention to detail as simple mistakes can dock u -1/-0.5, which is basically -4% since they're always out of 25 points. TA's are R&D

Matt is by far one of the smartest and most capable faculty members in the CS department. He is kind, caring, and genuinely cares about his students. His course is really satisfying to take because he is so passionate and good at explaining things.
Matt is the goat and makes 2800 an actually good class

good lectures & organization but strict grader, no partial credit, and hard exams. on an Ed post he refused to give ANY credit for a question that a TA gave misinformation to a student on, saying it was the student's fault for believing wrong info from a TA. but isnt that literally the TA's job??? very odd, felt weirdly like victim blaming.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: THE WORST PROFESSOR AT CORNELL. He can not teach. Grading is very generous but if you haven't taken Stats prior look elsewhere because you won't learn. I'm about to drop because there are better stats classes out there. Trust me.
Extremely boring lectures. His antics are very unnecessary and the course setup doesn't make sense at all. Don't recommend.

Go to lectures even though he isn't the most amazing lecturer; missing them will make a difference based on how much you'll recognize the content on the test. Tests are not difficult, but you need to be meticulous to his standards on the discussion HWs. Unlimited attempt lecture quizzes, HW, and pre-discussion quizzes make up ~50%. of final grade.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: The lectures honestly made the content more confusing to understand and I stopped going after 2 weeks of class. Exams are ridiculously easy becasue they are open note and are honestly easier than AP stats. One of the easiest classes to get an A+ in but Entner's teaching ability leaves much to be desired.

Go to class and understand the lectures, then do discussion and hw. Attendance isn't mandatory but you should go to understand what's going on. He tries to make it accessible for all students no matter your background. He writes his own notes and gives a copy. TAs were mostly nice and helpful

Funny and makes the exams pretty easy, but not good at teaching. Overcomplicated most topics to the point of confusing people who understood them beforehand. I learned all of the concepts myself by reviewing the notes he gave us. Exams were open note and many of the questions basically tested your ability to find the right page in the packet.
legit the worst professor I have ever had. truly not a good guy. counting down the days to never have to be near him again.

Other reviews pretty much sum it up. Lectures are unbearably boring; Entner rambles and over-explains simple concepts. His assignments, while minimal, are mindless and unhelpful. Avoid if possible. Entner is a super weird guy and his class is generally a waste of your time.

I've seen a lot of poor reviews of this professor so I was worried when I began to take his class. After going through it, the reviews are absolutely true. This professor should not be teaching at Cornell.
Horrid lecturer but if you've already taken AP stats this class is a free A+ and the guy is so hilarious when he lectures.
Don't take his class
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: honestly this was an easy A without any high school stats and some coding background. Stopped going to lectures because they were boring - make sure you do the discussions and lecture quizzes and you should be okay. The exams were not like the quizzes or lectures more so random general knowledge so be prepared for that. Felt like I learned nothing.

The course mainly covers linear regression and different kinds of ways of building models. Exams are fair and professor Entner overall is okay. I do think the course is helpful if you wanna pursue some careers relate to biostatistics.
Jeremy is notoriously one of the absolute worst professors at cornell. The only B I have received so far at cornell was a B- in his class. STAY FAR AWAY.

Lectures felt very long and hard to get through. He would over-explain simple topics and ramble. Tests were open note, but notes aren't uploaded online. Half of the things he writes down don't make any sense. Had to watch videos online when I didn't understand a topic, and realized the topic was extremely simple when explained well.

Probably one of the most work I've gotten in terms of time spent per week in my 3 years so far. Not all hard, but all tedious and boring. Don't take unless you need it to fill a req

There was a girl after time expired on one of the exams that didn't finish transferring her answers into the bubbles and he didn't let her finish. That kind of prof.

To be honest, this is a super useful class with a okay professor. Yes he is a weird guy but he is pretty generous when writing the prelims. Everything that's on the quiz can be found in your note, which is allowed to take to prelim room. He also drops 1 out of the 2 prelims if your final exam grade is better than the prelim grade.

I have nothing good to say about Entner. He made lectures boring and long, spent a lot of time on topics that were easy but breezed through hard ones, and has easy assignments that were not reflective on tests. I would turn back time if i could to take this class with a different professor. escape while u can

TAKE WITH ANYONE ELSE IF YOU CAN. Intro stats is not hard, he made it hard as possible. Notes are extremely confusing and he doesn't publish what he does in class. You can get a better grade in this class by not showing up and teaching yourself the material. HWs are worded so confusing and have nothing to do with lectures. How is this man a teacher

Jeremy, oh Jeremy. Lectures are useless: he explains topics in a dull and un-mathematical manner that goes in one ear and out the other. He once went up to my peer and asked her, "Are you crying yet?" He's in his own head and will go out of his way to waste time yelling at someone leaving lecture early, etc. The notes are as confusing as possible.

Not a great teacher but people are exaggerating on these reviews. He isn't really that bad. The labs are awful and take way too long but exams are open note. I think it's helpful going to lecture but you don't have to. Exams only make up 50% of your grade which is nice and they are multiple choice. No practice exam though. Not great, not awful.

Not a bad dude; lectures can be very boring, but is very responsive to students' questions. Class itself is not difficult if you're willing to show up and put in the bare minimum amount of work, and you do learn a lot regarding the material listed in the syllabus.

Genuinely the most confusing lecturer I've met at Cornell—including all of the upper level engineering classes I've taken. Attendance is not mandatory so just study on your own (do go to discussions though for the R sections) and rely on youtube videos. Haven't been to lecture after the first month so I don't know if he's mean or whatever
