Overall an extremely inflexible person who's hard to deal with

It sucks that he is the only teacher teaching stats. the lectures are useless, not engaging. He also over complicates things when he could just say things in simple terms. 

I got a better grade on my second prelim than I did on my first and I didn't attend a single lecture and watched youtube videos to explain concepts.
The biggest piece of _ ever.

Wow. This Professor is the absolute worst at actually teaching ideas simply. He will take a simple concept and explain it in the most confusing way possible (im a physics major).He seems to think his students are "afraid of math" so tries to substitute w/ words. Math is a language of its own. Stick to engineering/math dept classes.

Just an actual piece of work. Makes jokes about how he doesn't want to pay child support and picks on students asking them if his tests makes them cry. Like what is up with you man. His class is ridiculously easy if you don't go and just study on your own/run through the notes, a lot of people are failing and you really should have no excuse to.

TONS of work, normally 3 short quizzes per week but infinite tries at least, allowed to have class notes for the test which makes it rly easy to pass but the discussion hw is insanely difficult and unneccessary and just truly ridiculous, but I took ap stats in high school so the notes and a 4 hour study session was enough to get a 90
the sun of a beach. He is mentally challenged.

I think Prof Entner is a nice guy who is largely misunderstood at Cornell. His classes are hard. He is not the best at explaining material, and can sometimes come across as rude/dismissive to students, but I don't believe it's intentional. I probably wouldn't take him again. I didn't learn much about stats, but learned a lot about R programming.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Discussion work is worded confusingly. He gave students zeros on an assignment if they were absent the Friday before Spring Break (attendance is usually not mandatory)! His lectures are extremely repetitive yet he somehow still fails to show us how equations are applied (which you need to teach yourself for the exams). There is so much busy work.
It feels like he wants you to do worse. Nobody goes to lecture because they're so useless and no material is covered during the lecture.

Negative reviews here are true. Like others, I was told things that made no sense. The exams didn't reflect the material well. Not allowed to have his written notes, not allowed to retake quizzes for practice before exams, told to bum them off others. Directions for open notes are contradicting. Forced to use trash calculators. Get with the times!!
horrible professor. whats on the exam does not line up with what is taught in class. if you can take a different stats class do it. dropped it after the first exam because it was so horrible
he is just too mean

AVOID AT ALL COSTS. Lies about what materials students will have available to study for exams, refuses to help students and mocks them when they ask for help, constantly makes uncomfortable jokes about students, does everything in his power to ensure students fail. The day he retires will be a national holiday.
First day of class but this fire

Unfunny. The material is not difficult, but he is an incredibly rude man, made inappropriate jokes in class, and replied to emails in three days with one word. 
Just watch videos on youtube and ask your TAs for help, bc jeremy will not help anyone ever

This man is insufferable. I'm serious. Easy A course but I didn't even go to lectures because he's so irritating and inappropriate. Do not even try to email him because he will respond to you in a very rude and dismissive manner, usually with just one line which is rage-inducing. Worst professor I've ever had and I'm a senior.

Professor Entner is blunt and unkind. He often targets individuals in class, and while trying to be funny, it is an uncomfortable environment. He is not understanding or empathetic. There was an exam timing issue that affected many students. I emailed him, and he sent a rude response, making me feel disrespected. Easy class, bad professor.

I wouldn't worry about the exams, but the lectures are boring and unhelpful. You're better off finding youtube videos and teaching yourself the content. Also, you will not get an A+ in this class because the syllabus says that you need higher than a 99.8% to achieve that and he's very particular about the details.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: honestly if you just go to class and have notes on top of his you will be fine, exams are open note and you can figure it out even if you didnt go to class (make sure you know where everything is) hes pretty chill in office hours, sometimes makes stupid jokes but not out of harm. lectures get boring but hes nice as long as youre respectful in class
He was not a good lecturer in any regard it was very much a self taught class. He is not an understanding person at all.

As a person he can be a little rude. He's not super responsive or accessible. Aside from that, lectures are fine, prelims are open note MC, homeworks are not too hard to do well on. The class can be annoying- he makes a lot of random errors that can make things confusing, but it's fine overall and he doesn't make it too hard to do well.

This guy was actually the worst. His exams made no sense, his discussions were useless and annoying, and his lectures. His lectures were such a bore and he thought he was so funny making what he thought were clever jokes but they weren't. He got mad when he saw people leaving the lecture hall but tbh I don't blame them.

Avoid him at all costs! During class he often waists time by telling insensitive and inappropriate jokes. He tends to skip over important material and ends up putting it on the test. He reads over his notes and the little work that he does is not applicable to anything. He does not post practice material for exams causing mass confusion.

Very, very bad lecturer. Lectures are confusing and convoluted...he is also completely unreachable and you will have to go to your TAs with questions. Couldn't handle listening to him, so I stopped going to lecture halfway through the semester.

The lectures in the first month of class were fine, but once the content became more complex, it became evident there was no reason to attend lectures. This is one of those 'teach yourself' kind of classes, so I used online sources to teach me the content since the lectures did nothing. Generous grade weight distributions so easy to pass.

Actually the worst. So horrendous at teaching i'm not sure how he even got the job and I'm convinced he wants his students to do bad in the class. I would've never taken this class if I knew how bad he was. Made us code in statistics for some reason which makes no sense either. Never obtained a single piece of information from lecture.
Do not take if you struggle with math he is so confusing, does not explain concepts well and is overall a rude person.

He is a terrible lecturer, his tests are written extremely poorly to the point of being nearly undecipherable, and he has an army of undergrad TAs who are not good section leaders. My TA told me to use ChatGPT to figure out the problem I was working on because they could not figure it out. Avoid him at all costs, he will just confuse you more.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Yeah he's a bit weird but if you do the work, take good notes, and have friends in the class to work on the labs with, easy A.

I don't really get why he's a professor it never felt like he liked teaching. His lectures were so boring and honestly made me more confused about lots of subjects than before (just watch YouTube videos). He was not just rude to the students but I also noticed him being rude to his TAs. The exams were just kind of weird logic puzzles.

Lectures are extremely boring and he is disrespectful and insufferable to be around. Makes distasteful jokes commenting on people's ethnicities and appearances. Does weird crowd control during lecture by calling people out which makes everyone uncomfortable. Doesn't respond to emails, no rounding, prelims unrelated to notes, makes stats too hard

avoid him at all costs, especially if stats is not your major. labs and exams are COMPLETELY unrelated to the lecture content and graded at an unreasonable standard. the class is very unforgiving and will suck the soul out of you.

Most of reviews are overly critical. That said, he is an odd ball and not a great lecturer. Simple concepts can be confusing. Rude emails. Dumb grading system. Rules won't bend. Late by just 1 second = 0, no excuses. If you just do work on time you should be fine. I recommend taking a different stats such as 2150.

Jeremy Entner will make it his personal mission to ensure you do not understand statistics. Genuinely one of the most confusing professors I have ever had, and the TAs were sophomores and juniors who had no idea what was going on in the course. Avoid at all costs.

The R class was great with him, super boring, but doable workload. He dropped our prelims, and 85% of grading came from problem sets and labs that you can work with others on. He gets distracted easily and does not accept late work for any reason. If you need to ask questions because you were talking he'll harass you instead of answering.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Hilarious--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I suggest taking the class because it is Easy to do well but the teacher is such an a-h0le so don't expect to like him. Rude emails and no leniency. 50% of the class should be an automatic 100% though because of the weekly assignments.

Pretty poor teacher. His lectures are a real snore and you often end up just having to self-study in the end. Prelims vary a lot in difficulty. Without AP Stats already under my belt, there is no way I would have gotten the grade I did.

Honestly, the class itself wasn't hard but the lectures are unbearable. He tries to be funny and engage with the class but it honestly isn't that great. I didn't feel like I learned a lot in the lectures but more so in the discussions which attendance is mandatory. but whether the discussion is good or not really depends on your TA so goodluck.

Entner's teaching style sucks. The videos he posts are way better. Lab and exam questions are not clear and sometimes make no sense. No curve at all, I literally had an 89.99% and still didn't get an A-. Learning R is fun if you have a good TA and you can take the lecture quizzes as many times as you need to get a 100% before they close.

Very bad at teaching, give ambiguous exam questions and instructions, is mostly condescending, never reply emails, and seems annoyed when students ask him questions (sometimes judgemental). if not like teaching why become an instructor??? just avoid him

My second semester with Entner and he's sure one of a kind. It's hard to do poorly and isn't too much work and you learn an ok amount, particularly using R and regression, but it's always typos or questions on labs or exams that are very ambiguous. The open notes tests and recorded lectures are what make Entner and the course bearable.

Nice guy but you need to watch the videos in addition to lecture. Course material is extremely bland, but Entner tries his best and  any other professor would get 1/5 reviews. Biggest complaint is zero curve at all.
He is ok. His tests will have things you could never have expected or planned to study for. He will not round your grade at all.

Not the "easy A" class everyone says it is. Concepts are explained poorly/confusingly. Can use a cheat sheet on exams, but only basic calculators allowed despite stat software used in section. Easy to get full section/quiz points, making up 50% of grade. But to do well on other 50% (exams), expect to teach yourself. I barely made the A threshold.

Professor Entner was pretty good in lectures but material was simply enough to be learned outside of class as well. Tests were pretty reasonable as you are allowed a cheat-sheet and other assignments helped boost overall grade. Would highly recommend class, especially for non-stem majors as material is not too complicated.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Good lecturer and a funny guy. Made a very foreign concept interesting/enjoyable through his unconventional sense of humor (he can be out of pocket). Tests are not very hard (cheat sheet allowed). Only work was textbook readings, weekly quizzes (unlimited attempts), and section activities (time limit). Overall, great experience and a good class!
Don't make the same mistake I made.

This guy is the worst. Actually the worst class Ive ever taken at cornell. He teaches NOTHING, seems like he knows nothing either. His tests have the WORST formats, and everything he does is so extra and not helpful. Never replied to ANY emails that are sent to him over the semester. All around USELESS. 0/10.

3010 was an unpleasant experience. Jeremy is not an engaging lecturer, and most people did not attend them. There is no textbook, and the class relies on a notes document Jeremy provides. The tests could be annoying and tricky but were open note, which I appreciated. Late assignments are not accepted and Jeremy would be condescending about it.

He seems like a nice guy,  but he is a TERRIBLE teacher. The discussions are led by other students who don't know what is going on. All the tests are on things barely mentioned in class. He literally is useless, there is no way to contact him, no guide to what is on the exams, and literally no access to any forms of help or practice for this class.
open note tests. that says it all.

Ok so most people dislike him but he's really not that bad. Tests are open note for 3010 and only count for 50% of your grade. Now his test questions are a bit weird but if you pay any amount of attention to the lectures you can figure out how to do them with the notes. This is the major complaint most people have.
He's ok.

His lectures were kind of useless, but I found him to be quite funny. He always wears this backwards camera wherever he goes. I did everything in my dorm (read through the Google slides myself), never went to class etc and still got an A.
skipped the lecture because they were long and boring and didn't want to hear his lame joke. lecture vids posted were lifesavers

Best, easiest college class ever. The material was basic, the tests were exactly like the questions done in discussion section, and the grading scheme emphasized homework, quizzes, and discussions as much as exams. It's totally possible to skip class and still get an A (unfortunately doesn't give A+). He's a quirky guy but I found it entertaining.
Tests are fair if you just listen to the lectures, and he uploads everything online. Multiple attempts allowed for quizzes to get a good grade.

Definitely the least engaging professor I've every had, which explains why 80% of the class didn't attend his lectures. He never replies to emails and his lectures were pretty painful to sit through. I appreciated his leniency with weekly quizzes, but he worded tests so badly that many students without a stats background were destined to fail.

I took his 3010 and 3020. lectures are kind of dry and there are so many trivial details of stat models / R code that you must pay attention to later in 3020. He uploads all lecture videos to Canvas, but his lectures are generally better (at least 60% students never go to lectures). His OH is helpful and he answers individual questions pretty well.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I took STSCI 2100 this semmester and I absolutely hated it. I had taken a previous into to stats college class that I got a 100 average in and while the homework and discussions were easy. He doesn't word the test well. He is never responsive to emails and the ta I had didn't know anything.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Sometimes students can be a little hyperbolic when they say "oh my god, this professor is the worst." Jeremy Entner lives up to the hype. He is easily the worst teacher I have ever had. Like, in my entire academic history from preschool to college. I would choose my daycare teacher from when I was 3 over Jeremy Enter to teach me statistics. Awful.

Professor Entner does his best to make the lectures fun and enjoyable. Despite it being a 4-credit course, the work was lighter than any other course that I've taken. Lectures weren't mandatory, and he recorded videos for the people who didn't want to show up to the lectures. Definitely take Professor Entner's class for intro stats!

Lectures were extremely dry to the point that they made me hate the content that was being covered and really made me not want to become a stats major. There is no extra credit which is terrible because the class isn't curved and the median is low. The workload is fine but there are no practice prelims or a textbook to get help from.

Entner is one of the more down to earth and funny professors I have had. Always cracks jokes and really tries not to overcomplicate lectures and exams. One wrinkle is that the discussion sections mainly use software not available on the exams, leading to exams that students have not been adequately prepared for. Overall kind and willing to help.
Stats prelims are worded oddly. He makes basic concepts (ie graphs/ p value) more difficult by poorly explaining them. You might be better off in Bio stats.
Just cannot understand him for some reason. I have taken stats class with another prof and found it interesting; however, he pushed me away from learning more stats.

The class was super easy, even with no stats or R background. Lectures got pretty boring and some of the exam questions were a little mysterious, but labs, homework, and quizzes were essentially a guaranteed 100 as long as you put in a tiny bit of effort.

This class was not that hard if you have stats experience and the labs on R were very helpful. The only issues with this prof are that some of the questions on his tests are unclear and he is sometimes mean to students in lecture. He does give extra credit and is generally responsive. This class is easy enough that you can get by just with your TA.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Lectures are useless and irrelevant to exams.
Lecture and lecture quizzes pretty much irrelevant to exam.

Stats and R both aren't my thing but Jeremy made them both pretty easy to understand as a non-math person. All content is covered in lecture, and he makes it easy to take good notes. The class overall is pretty light, not a ton of work.

This class is not too conceptually challenging, but it does take up a lot of time. First of all, don't even think about skipping lecture, since everything covered will come up later. Aside from that, the weekly labs have a lot of questions, and are also ambiguously worded to the point that many people need clarification in office hours.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Haha 89.68, what do you want? No curve, no extra credit, you know how much time I have spent on the course? Every weekend, I slept at 3 to finish the lab. Ended up with a B+ but I got A+ on my last stats class.

He is the type of guy who does not round up 89.5 to 90 in the final grade. His exam is also weirdly worded. It was very confusing when you cannot understand what he is trying to test in his exam. Trust me: AVOID him anyway.
Don't get close to statistic here or you will be miserable
very entitled and arrogant person. strange because of how incompetent he is.
Jeremy, why are your tests like this? The wording makes the test more about solving a lexicon puzzle instead of the actual content.

The class is not difficult if you go to lecture and lab. Stats isn't really my thing so I didn't find the material very engaging, but if you keep up with the workload you will get a good grade. The professor gives extra credit if the prelim medians aren't where he wants them to be, and you can tell he wants students to succeed in this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Jeremy is, well, goofy. He has this interesting wit that I truly thought was funny. However, his tests were literally out of left field; they were much harder than class material. His explanation of R is a joke. Does give extra credit. ILR stats way less work and easier, take that instead if you can.

The class content was not difficult, however, grading was biased and arbitrary. His exam q's felt like they were testing your understanding of his psyche rather than the material. His behavior in lecture was also immature. He constantly made people uncomfortable by calling students out and mocking them, even for things like going to the bathroom.

Jeremy, oh Jeremy, what a man. While he was a really quirky teacher, and some people didn't like that, he truly knew his stuff when it came to statistics. The class was relatively easy and enjoyable, with little homework, but his tests weren't a good reflection of that we were learning. Notes were helpful, and was always responsive, ready to help!

Lecture feels more like bad stand up than actual class. Not extremely enjoyable and the prof uses strange analogies instead of actually explaining why things work the way they do. Clicker quizzes aren't hard. There is no curve but the grading is somewhat generous.
Very engaging teaching style. Gave extra credit and wanted students to succeed.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Jeremy is one of the rudest, least professional professors I have ever had. Not only is his teaching style odd, distracting and unprofessional, he seems to hate students who bring up anything to do with grades. His tests are also extremely irrelevant to actually using statistics - they are more like decoding double negative T/F questions
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Very rude. You learn very little. He doesn't explain most things well. Instead of learning why statistics work, you plug numbers into the equations from your notes that he lets you use on everything. Assumes everyone is lazy. I missed one lecture and went to office hours, and he shamed me about how I would've known the material if I went to lecture
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: His lectures are awkward, and he is always picking on students. Beware of sitting in the front. He spends more time trying to be an awkward SNL character than a nice prof. He is also super rude.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Eshan's lectures were straightforward and his exams were difficult but fair. Much of the lecture material is covered in the textbook, but attending and participating in lecture helped to gain a deeper understanding of the concepts.
Cares more about research than teaching the class. Don't bother going to lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Lectures, especially ones with proofs, lacked depth. Textbook was much more thorough. I would have hoped at least for pointers or intuition. Nothing of the sort. Attended less than a quarter of the lectures, found it more worthwhile to read the textbook thoroughly. Exams were graded fairly, and generously. Read the textbook. Spend time on homework.
Not the best at explaining things. Algo is a tough class in general but can't say that having him as a prof made it better.

Can't explain intuitively, most students rely on AI to explain concepts. Topics are not useful (it's a theory class, so completely fine of course), but also not fun. 
Over-focused on NP reductions. Overall least favorite class I've taken at Cornell.
Made a difficult class better than it could have been
CS 4820 was probably one of the easiest classes I've taken here thanks to him, what an amazing Professor, learned so much from this class, if he's teaching definitely recommend taking it with him!
I LOVE ESHAN!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
pretty chill guy, workload was reasonable, course was organized, nothing to complain about
He is a great professor. Extremely based and goated.

Lectures are very well-presented. Professor is very nice and takes care to ensure what he teaches is relatively easy. Homework needs some construction but generally manageable. Exams are straightforward and does not include hard questions that require creative thinking. A pretty routine algorithm course that is well taught and not too hard.
good professor

Her class was an absolute breeze work wise, and the content (I took No Spoilers!) was interesting. The workload never exceeds a fairly sized essay and she is genuinely one of the kindest people on earth. She's extremely lenient/understanding which was really helpful during tough times, & cares about her students. TAKE A CLASS WITH HER SHES AMAZING

Dr. Foster was excellent, and I would highly recommend taking any of her classes. All classwork and reading is super manageable and interesting. I feel as though my writing genuinely improved throughout the FWS. She gives great, specific feedback on all writing.

Great Prof for my FWS; super kind and understanding of people's other responsibilities outside of his class. Her feedback is really helpful, and the classwork is very light. Overall, great prof, and would definitely take her class again!

ENGL 3747 was a breath of fresh air! Grade is based on participation (attendance and speaking), 3 short book reports, mid-term, final essay, and a presentation. The assignments were manageable, fun, and included many options for us to choose from. Foster was charming and engaging, making the plethora of readings worthwhile! A must-take at Cornell.

Took ENGL3747. Dr. Foster is hands down the best professor at Cornell. This was my favorite class! It is not burdensome at all, but there is a lot of reading (super fun though) so don't fall behind. The class is pretty discussion heavy and you're technically graded on it. Highly recommend to anyone remotely interested in crime fiction in any form.
One of the most kind and caring profs at Cornell and very knowledgable too. Crime fiction class is insightful and gives you a lot of flexibility. Recommend to anyone.

One of the nicest professors. She taught the FWS Rom Coms course. She works with you to set up flexible deadlines around other exams and makes reading and analyzing Shakespeare fun. Just apply yourself and put real thought into your papers and you'll be fine. Only 3 complete papers and some other minor things. She also gives amazing feedback.

Professor Foster makes class really enjoyable and interesting. She gives great feedback and helped me take my research paper ideas to the next level. I would take any class with her regardless of the subject honestly.

She taught my FWS on Shakespeare and Rom Coms. She was engaging and the class was discussion based. We had really interesting conversation and she was great at encouraging ideas. Only homework was to read Shakespeare, write, and watch the occasional film. Fair grading with helpful feedback. She connects well with students. This was a great class!

This course was super fun and enjoyable. I loved how Professor Foster focused on content that was interesting to us. Professor foster brought a lot of energy to class, graded in a timely manner (a couple days), and made herself available with office hours and one-on-one meeting.

We spent half of every lecture on review, and though that boosted our test scores, it meant that there was no point in going to class. If you're solely here to pass the class, just read the course pack before tests. You'll thrive. However, you will miss his fantastic shirt collection and jolly attitude--he is genuinely a delightful person.
amazing. take linalg with him if you can
Really nice professor. I did poorly because of my own lack of studying, but he was super nice and great at explaining things.
Great professor, interesting lecture, helpful afterclass, a rare in the ORIE department

Frans is really chill, and the class is slow-paced. The first half of the semester covered materials less than expected, so he had to rush through all SVD, but overall Frans is a really good professor! Take 2940 with him if you can!

frans is a good lecturer but lecture did move slow at times. in general his lectures will give you a better understanding than just reading the textbook though. the class is structured very fairly and many resources such as discussion section worksheets were posted (for all sections) to use as practice before exams.

he's a genuinely passionate and kind professor who wants his students to succeed. this class made me switch my major to ORIE. he designed his course pack well and he's friendly and responsive on edstem + office hours. homework can be tricky but exams are fair. his high rating is so deserved. going to try to take his classes in the future

He's pretty chill, and the exams were very fair in my opinion. The mean for the first exam was higher than a 90. Sometimes the lectures can be slow, especially whenever he does proofs, but you can tell he doesn't really want to do them either lol.
Lectures were not mandatory as the course could be covered by just the coursepack. Very friendly and lenient when it comes to homework and exam grading.
Fantastic professor and interesting material

What more can I say... Frans is so passionate about the content, his enthusiasm and jovial nature never fails to rub off on students during lecture. He explains things very well and the weekly labs and homework push your understanding of the concepts even further. I wish he would teach all my classes.

He's an amazing professor! The lectures are engaging and funny, and he explains everything in an intuitive way instead of making concepts complicated. The psets can take time but it's not too difficult to figure it out + it's fun once you get the hang of it. Tests were straightforward and on the easier side. This class made me consider orie.
Enjoyed his lectures, he is also generous with a 90% and up counted as an 100% for homeworks. Definitely recommend this class

Frans is a great guy, really cares about the stuff he teaches. The workload is not too bad and the class is setup in order to give you the best grade possible (weighting shifts depending on what you do well on). The HW sometimes is really hard but just work in groups and you can figure it out. Overall a great teacher and a fun guy

He was an amazing professor! made the class fun and engaging and cared about the students. All of the work is really helpful to understanding the content and its not too hard. He wants the students to succeed.
Amazing professor; would take another class with him again

This class was fine but tests were hard. Unfortunately many randos who arent engineering majors that mess up the curve bc this is their hardest class. The medians were 80's which is great but also means everyone is super sweaty so you absolutely cannot be excited when your raw score is 85 like you would in any other exam ever in engineering math
Funny guy but talks kind of slow. Lecture wasn't worth showing up to because we barely covered anything. Better off reading the textbook on your own.

Frans is funny and genuinely good lecturer. I enjoyed attending his lectures despite attendance not being mandatory. He posts the lecture slides on Canvas right after. The weekly quizzes in discussion were usually a randomly chosen HW question from the week's prior PSET. exams were VERY fair!! you will do well in this class if you pay attention!

amazing professor!! really tries to make sure that you understand the content from the class when sitting in lecture. is super receptive to questions and is a generally fun and caring guy to be around. one of my favorite professors at cornell, hands down!

Prof. Schalekamp was a very engaging lecturer. Super friendly guy overall. His office hours were extremely helpful (especially on tough p-sets). He's great at answering questions and he always has a funny line in lecture.
Super funny lectures and made the content very interesting! 10/10 prof who turned ENGRI 1101 into my favorite class~

Love this man, great professor! I didn't know what Linear Algebra was before this class but I feel really confident in the material now. The lectures follow the textbook, so it's really easy to get ahead on the material by reading through before lecture. Homework is very straightforward. Exams and weekly quizzes are very fair. Highly Recommend!

Frans is the best professor I have had so far at Cornell and inspired me to pursue ORIE as a major! He is truly passionate about ORIE and his lectures are sometimes hard to understand as topics get harder but he is really good at answering questions. I will definitely try to take one of his classes again in the future.

Pretty good. Lots of work and definitely lots of speaking in the class which I don't mind but many people do. Lots of vocab as there tends to be in this sort of class. Easy for people who took French in high school.

Flavien is a good person deep down, but if you care about your GPA, getting an A is hard. I took french for 8 years now and with putting in the work, I struggled to maintain a good grade. He grades essays extremely hard (I recommend using ai), his tests are hard, and so are his quizzes.

Flavien is a good person deep down, but he isn't the best teacher because students feel intimidated, stressed, and unable to speak freely when in his class. He isn't the most understanding and I recommend taking another section so that you aren't living in fear of this class for the entire semester!

The homework is busywork and there are barely any assignments, so the majority of your grade comes from exams, and an entirely arbitrary participation grade. The exams only test your memorization of random trivia from readings, or rare exceptions to grammatical rules(plus many of the answers are subjective to some questions). Prof is nice at least.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Caring--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I would not reccomend this professor at all. I would not take this class if I could go back in time. Not engaging. Not a compassionate professor, very strict. Too much busy work that doesn't help learn the material. Lectures do not engage with the material well. Exams are difficult.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: Intense, intimidating, and at times a meanie. If you need French for the language requirement, I suggest going to another section of French taught by someone else. You deserve a kinder professor for a class for such a heavy workload - or else you'll dread every assignment and every class (which meets 3x a week).

I was worried about the difficulty because I was using AP credit and hadn't taken French in a while but this class was definitely manageable. There's homework every night but it's not bad. Test and assignments can be kinda annoying. Great class though. I enjoyed it and learned a lot. Flavien is super knowledgeable and generous with grading.
Maybe it is because I have in at 9:05 AM, but he is really nice. I am learning a lot.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Gives good feedback--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: While he's a nice guy, you're graded on barely any assignments and have no option to drop a lowest grade, regain points, petition, or complete extra credit assignments. Attendance is mandatory, but you'll want to go to class anyways since it's a fairly fun atmosphere...students make the class worthwhile. I dropped my French minor after this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I wouldn't recommend to take this course becuz the professor gives you quiz, essay, reading comprehension, prelim, and oral exam every other week. Homework every other day.  Even though this is an intermediate course, he expects us to be fluent and write fluently. You also have to work with your partner during class and participate A LOT in class.

If you dare say even one incorrect phrase/ pronunciation, this man will rudely cut you off in front of the entire class and make you repeat yourself correctly while interrupting every other word. Insensitive to those with extra time needs and a harsh test grader, but does give an unexpected curve at the end of the course.

announces exams only a few days before, doesn't grade work fast enough to be used for studying or release answer keys or correct responses for homework and classwork (which is used heavily in tests and quizzes).very condescending towards students who need extra clarification, have SDS accommodations, or attempt to approach him in office hours.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Lots of in-class participation and homework, but I think they're useful
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Flavien is caring and knowledgeable. He assigns a lot of homework to enforce textbook readings (expect 1 hour for every class, through a very buggy and annoying online software) which is not everyone's cup of tea. FREN 2090 focuses heavily on grammar rules and conversation which I enjoyed. Exams are hard but you can cram for them.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Flav is awesome I love him.

Too much busiwork daily homework. Glidja's teaching philosophy is uncanny, usually made for boring morning class comprised of discussions of the textbook and exercises with partners. Forced and awkward, I got little out of it. I wish I liked class, because I love French, but it was middling at best. Glad it's over and req is filled.

Flavien is fantastic. The class is pretty time consuming but he has been my favorite professor thus far. Hes very enthusiastic and appreciates class participation. Flaviens grading is very reasonable, maybe even friendly.

I like French, but this class was just horrendous. The online homework is expensive, required, and basically busywork. Work is graded harshly and returned extremely late (2+ months after deadline). Class is mandatory but is useless. Very little teaching from the professor is done, and explanations and expectations are usually unclear.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Participation matters--So many papers

reviews.comment: Sooooooo much work. How is this not a 6 credit class? So glad it's almost over!
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Absolutely ridiculous amounts of work. I'm never the one to complain, but I was enrolled in 18 credits, and this class took up nearly half of my time. Flavien is a good guy, but his expectation of the amount of work students are capable of handling is astounding. One week we had a quiz, a test, an essay, and a report, on top of the daily homework.
He's very nice and funny. He just tends to give too much work. Its not necessarily hard, just always something to do

Flavien is such a great professor, he creates an atmosphere that is comfortable and fun to participate in. He does expect participation, but the way the class is set up makes it fairly easy. He's also super funny!

This class isnt easy but Flaviens a good and nice guy who genuinely cares about his students. He may give you a hard time if youre easily distracted but just make sure to stand up for yourself and it will be fine. Lots of online homework but content/tests/quizzes are reasonable. Lots of work but its not that hard.

If you want to keep your GPA high, I suggest you take a different liberal studies or at least one without Glidja. There was really no immersion, structure, or benefits from the class. Most of it was busy book work. A lot of busy book work.

Awful. This class made me hate French and dread attending class everyday. If you are teaching a 1220 class, most people shouldnt be on the brink of failing the first exam. If you have TAs, they should be allowed to teach and see exams because they are there to learn to. A miserable and regrettable experience.

Flavien has unrealistic expectations in terms of how much time students can allocate to French every day. We are full time students with loads of classes and work. He doesn't understand that. 1220 was quite possibly the most miserable class I've ever taken. (ONE PRO- grading is weighted over lots of things (exams, hw, writing, etc)) Don't do it.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Flavian is the man. If you want to improve your French, he'll help you do it, although it does get challenging and work-intensive at times, but is TOTALLY worth it. He does a great job of clearly explaining everything, and makes it fun. On top of that, he has a great attitude towards life that everyone can learn from.

Flavien is a fabulous teacher who genuinely cares about his students. He is from Benin and has an interesting story  if you get to talk to him outside of class. Cool accent! I had him for French 209 back in Spring 2009. I was mistakenly placed in the class, so it was easy for me. The book was stupid so I just photocopied it from the libe.

Flavien is a very good teacher. He makes sure his students understand the material, and will never refuse a question. This class is not easy by any stretch of the imagination, but Flavien does his best to make the material fun (organizing debates, making and acting out plays) Cornell is lucky to have Flavien.

Really good prof.  Definitely challenging at times, but that's pretty necessary to learn a language.  He's also willing to help when you ask.  He won't baby you, and the class will take time, but he's one of the best French professors I've had.

Very committed and helpful teacher.  Really devoted to improving students French.  Can seem at times a bit demanding for a language class and sometimes had issues with his grading, but overall a good teacher.

The only thing we did in class was go over homework, so absolutely every concept had to be learned on your own. This class made me hate french, which was my favorite subject in high school. He told me I "speak too fast". ( I spent a month in Paris). He made one girl in our class cry regularly. I learned nothing, and my confidence is shot.
Awesome teacher. Easy to understand. Extremely helpful and geniunely passionate about french and helping students.

great guy, fun and enthusiastic lecturer. the only problem i had with 1230 was that Glidja would give a different amount of work each day, so it was difficult to plan out how much time to devote to it
Dr. Fitzgerald is so passionate about what shes teaching and seems to really want to share that with her students. such a kind professor i would be surprised if anyone remotely disliked her.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: She really cares and makes class genuinely interesting, you can tell she's passionate and wants us to learn, not just pass. That said, her grade cutoffs are kinda insane (a 100+ for an A+ is wild), but getting an A is totally doable. Overall, one of those professors you'll actually remember.

You can tell Dr. Fitzgerald really cares about her work and is passionate about teaching. She understands student needs but is also fair and equitable when making decisions. Her TAs were easily accessible outside of class and offered fun insights during guest lectures. The class has weekly reflections that are pretty simple and easy to keep up with
Avoid at all cost
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Clear grading criteria--So many papers

reviews.comment: I would say she's the worst professor at Cornell. She doesn't know what she's doing, and believes she's always right, even though she doesn't know any of quant field. Many of students really don't know how this kind of person could be teaching at Cornell.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: She tried to convince me that Fixed Effect does not eliminate unobserved, time-invariant effects of each entity. You know what's up... Clearly, she has no idea what she is talking about. There is no way she has taken a statistics course, and I encourage her to do so before lecturing again.

She likes to change the instruction on the same day of big paper dues. And she even tried fine students $20 who use any technology (phone/laptop) in class. And being so sarcastic to some students. Force a student turn camera on while he's driving during a zoom call that on a weekend. She likes to believe what she thoughts, no matter what truth is.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: Avoid this professor at all cost if you can. She doesn't know what she is doing at all!!!
Nice professor, would take again
bro CANNOT teach at all the lectures are sooo boring and hes sooo monotoned I literally can never pay attention and there is no curve for any of his classes

Absolutely the first and the best econ prof I had in Cornell. Always so organized, patient, and willing to help students, whether it's during lecture or during his office hours, even over hours. Relatively hard materials solely because of math, not concept. As long as you spend time and do lots of practice, you will perform well in class.
Cares about students and teaches economics in a very accessible way. Exams are fair and closely related to what gets taught in class/the practice tests.
I think his lecture was ok but it was kinda hard to focus. However grading was really good and his practice test looks very very similar to the actual one.

totally mid. Lectures have pollev which you can do anywhere but going to lectures is very helpful. Each test has a practice test which is helpful. Pretty boring class but he was able to get most of the concepts down.

Professor Pitsuwan is one of my favorite professors at Cornell. He's incredibly supportive and very approachable. His requirements are clear, and he was always willing to help me solidify concepts. I feel like I have a strong foundation in macroeconomics and its application in the world. I highly recommend everyone take his intro macro class.

Super interesting and widely applicable topic with a good balance between econ, game theory, and policy applications. Prof Pitsuwan is very  knowledgeable and willing to deviate from lecture to explain things more. I will say one would probably enjoy this class most if they enjoy the subject, though I think anyone could benefit from this course.

Presents macro content in a clear and easy to digest way. His tests are pretty straightforward in general and are pretty similar to the practice prelims. You don't really have to do the textbook readings but they can be helpful for reinforcing ideas learned in class.
Take basu econ instead

Pretty alright guy, but sort of got the sense from lecture that he hated macroeconomics. Class content is overall pretty easy so the lecture being mid aren't such a bad thing, but he writes his exams so that you actually have to think instead of just plug and chugging what you learn in class. Grading is pretty generous.
The worst Econ proof you could ever have if u can take other Econ prof I advice to take other one. No curve make easy things so complicated

I think Prof. Pitsuwan has good intentions, and while clearly new to teaching he obviously cared about his students and the material. However, he really struggled to grasp how difficult the exams would be with exams having extreme variety in difficulty levels. Exams were infinitely harder than problems for class and very conceptual.
Really caring, passionate, and all-around interesting guy. Very early in his career but honestly one of the best professors I have had in the econ department.

Professor Pitsuwan is very caring and genuinely invested in making sure students understand the material. While the problem sets and exams are pretty challenging compared to the material covered in lecture, Prof Pitsuwan was always willing to explain and confirm solutions at OH.

An extremely kind and caring instructor. Lectures are engaging, clear, and thoughtfully designed. They genuinely strive to help students understand, offering great support via Ed and office hours. The TAs were also helpful and approachable. I'm especially grateful for their understanding and support during a challenging health crisis.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: ECON 3030 is known as a challenging course, so don't expect the class to hand you an easy test or problem set. But Prof. Pitsuwan provides so many resources for you to succeed. Go to office hours and ask questions during lecture. He puts in the most effort out of any other professor I know here. You just need to utilize the resources given to you.

Here's my honest perspective: Professor Pitsuwan does, in-fact, have good intentions. That being said, he doesn't know how to teach and his test's are ABSURDLY difficult. His exams are ENTIRELY conceptual and intuitive, very little to do with what was actually taught in the class. What was taught in class was probably 1/100 as hard as the exam.
It was his first semester teaching, and his explanations were very unclear. He also doesn't curve the grade even though the average class was in the 70s. Avoid at all costs.

Prof. Pitsuwan is a knowledgeable, interesting lecturer. He responds to student questions and genuinely cares about your learning. Although his lectures sometimes fall behind, he has a strong command of concepts, using easy-to-understand examples and real-life scenarios to motivate learning. Exams are fair, but involve more than rote memorization.

Davis was very nice and accessible, but I didn't feel like he taught well. I was much less prepared for his exam questions compared to the other lecturer. I didn't need the textbook until he started lecturing because I didn't feel he was explaining anything well. His homework was easy, though.
Davis seemed like he genuinely cared about students. The lectures were a little stale at times, but he made me enjoy 2070 more than the other lecturer.

Professor Davis seems like a nice guy but the lectures were very boring. He taught very slowly to the point where we were not able to cover enough material in each lecture. Problem sets were long but not hard. The textbook is the one thing that helped me do good.

Davis is slightly better than Hines (who taught the first half of 2070), an engaging lecturer who posts slides beforehand and is more open to questions. He teaches a bit slowly, though, and by the end we were struggling to cover everything before the final. The class is still held back by a diabolical grading system (iykyk) and borderline evil TAs.

2070 is a hard class regardless of professor, but Davis is a genuinely kind person and likes doing demonstrations and cracking jokes, which makes the lectures bearable. He uses slides and uploads them the night before lecture which is convenient. His examples can be slightly confusing sometimes so be sure to pay attention or else you'll be lost.

His lectures come straight from the textbook, but there are some engaging parts of lecture that add to understanding. Some homework questions appeared exactly on exams so understanding them is important. He's nice but the lectures are pretty boring. Read the textbook for anything you don't understand as he follows it exactly.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He taught the second half of Chem2070. My biggest complaint is the homework. Hours upon hours of problem sets. The professor who taught the first half of the class gave problem sets, but Floyd gave so much more homework than she ever did.The lectures were pretty boring, but at least he made explosions to keep us engaged.

Very dependent on the textbook, everything he taught and all grading criteria came from the textbook. Often went on tangents in lectures to the point where you couldn't understand what was important and what was irrelevant. Rarely did any practice problems in class so most of us did not know how to actually apply the material.

His test questions could still be quite difficult (typical of CHEM 2070), but he seems like a very sweet and caring professor. His lectures were more slow-paced compared to Prof. Hines, and I enjoyed his in-class demonstrations.

Floyd is a lot better at teaching than M. Hines, in my opinion. He teaches at a slower pace and conducts experiments in class. He is also very caring, and he reminds me of the grandpa in UP. He's so cute and wholesome core.
They need to make the 50 min 2070 lecture  2x as long once he takes over. Half of class each time is spent watching him figure out his own computer. Painful.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Really nice professor. Take advantage of regrade requests; Professor Davis is very generous and understanding of circumstances that arise during prelim season. His lectures were fast paced, but all slides are posted and he listens to his students concerns and is very patient in answering questions. 100% take 2150 over 2070.

despite davis being a nice and caring guy, his lecture slides aren't helpful, examples and derivations don't correlate to hw or prelim q's. OH aren't helpful. he doesn't walk you through problems, he just talks (not great for visual learners). textbook isn't great either.

lab work for this class is hard. TAs often didn't show up to office hours.

Prof. Davis is a solid professor. Lectures can get quite dry and proof heavy with derivations (particularly with equations in thermo and quantum), but you won't be tested on 99% of it. He does post lecture slides, which is super helpful. For 2150 specifically, try to stay on top of the content and take advantage of old exams/psets.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Caring--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment:
-willing to help (office hrs, etc)
-overestimates students knowledge
-doesn't explain overall concept/basics much: easy to get lost if you don't know content already
-posted online lecture slides aren't helpful: lack lots of explanations/content discussed in lecture
-very time consuming lab reports
-definitely caring, but not the clearest explainer

Tough class, but with the curve (average is B+) you should be okay (just try to stay around the mean for prelims. By looking at the textbook before lecture, you should be able to follow. Clearly a passionate and smart guy, just tends to gloss over the basics. If I had written this after my first prelim, which made me question taking the class, idk.

Lectures were very informative and held my attention. Did demos every class. Homework was reasonable and lecture slides are posted at least the day before lecture. Overall a great professor who is passionate about what he does.
Respectful professor. Really kind when answering questions.

Pretty great teacher all around! If you like Chemistry and you're gonna take a course with him, you're in good hands. Clearly shows he loves Chemistry and love to project that to his students. For 2150: Make sure to attend lectures and study from given HW, old PSETS and exams. Book not really necessary IMO.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: lectures are somewhat hard to follow and heavy in proofs for equations (imo like a physics course); a little boring. fun demos at the end of lecs though. labs are time-consuming (learn to use excel) but easy to get full or almost full points every time. tests are difficult. study assigned psets + old tests A LOT. course curved to B average.
Unfair in his dealings with students. Only cares about himself and how he looks to his peers and the administration. Deceptive and dirty. Plays politics a lot.

A very helpful professor who is always open to questions from students. He responds to emails quickly. Lectures are also well-organized, (almost) each time ending with an interesting demo. Exams are not that difficult once you practice past prelims and problem sets. Be prepared for the time-consuming lab reports :)
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Really nice prof. You don't really need to go to class because he posts lecture notes (I didn't go 95% of the time). Just skim the notes, do some problems, and you'll do fine

I had Professor Davis for CHEM 2080. While his lectures may seem a bit dull at first, they become interesting, and they are invaluable to passing this course. He always tries to keep things interesting, and always shows cool demos. His prelims are extremely fair. If you studied well, you will have no problem. Overall, Professor Davis is a good prof

The lectures are pretty heavy. Better go prepared e.g print the lecture slides maybe. Prof D used 90% of the lecture time going through some tough practice problems which turn out to be pretty helpful when studying for prelims. Labs started out tough and get a little better toward the end, so really work on those lab reports!

Delivers introductory chemistry material slowly but in an effective way. Attending lectures helps hugely, as he goes over practice problems that are similar to required weekly homework. (Also looks like the grandpa in Up!)
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--Caring

reviews.comment: He was fine but nothing special. Tests are tricky, because he only really covers the bare minimum in class

Prof. Davis did an OK job.  His explanations were very clear and simple.  Often he ran out of time at the end of lecture and scrambled to finish demos.  The first exam was way too easy and was compensated for by a much more difficult second exam.  Overall, he was fair and did an OK job.  I stopped going to class eventually and still did well.

Came into this class as a chem major, left as NOT a chem major. His lectures are mostly useless, and it's much easier to understand if you just read the textbook. TA's office hours are hit or miss. Prof's office hours were not really helpful. FYI, do NOT take this class if you aren't confident in your previous chemistry knowledge!
He's a great teacher with a lot of passion for what he does. The demos are all really cool. My favorite class in freshman year

As most people have said, the lectures are standard and the demos are cool. The P-sets really are a reflection of what he'll put on the tests, so do those (even though they aren't for a grade). He himself is pretty reasonable when it comes to grades and advice, though he may go over your head if you ask him about other chem topics.
Good teacher. Knows his stuff and communicates it pretty well. Tests are fair. Labs are difficult but there is a curve so it's all good. Funny guy, too, and demos are awesome.

Demos ever lecture, which kept it interesting-- he's really great at making the material clear if you listen, though I can't say he's particularly exciting or funny (you may fall asleep in class). Exams are doable, and the class has a pretty big curve. Lab starts out next to impossible but gets a lot better.

Very friendly teacher. He definitely loves the demos (as everyone else here has commented). His problem sets are a very good representation of the prelims, so if you understand the psets, you're set. Although I definitely didn't do well in the course, I'd definitely recommend people to take it (especially if you've had AP chem.) You'll enjoy it!

Great lecturer! Problem sets are a very good indication of exam-type problems. Exams can be a tad bit long, but nothing unmanageable. Helpful during office hours and very enthusiastic and knowledgeable regarding the material. Awesome demos as well.

His lectures were very interesting. He always has some demos for each of the lecture sessions. He communicates the materials very well to the students. He is also very organized and the exams are reasonable.

Great demos. He is really really into kinetics so if you don't understand rate laws well you are pretty much screwed. Tries to keep the class interesting, it sort of ran out of steam at the end of the semester, but it is very obvious that his genuinely interested in his students learning and chemistry overall.

I want to know where his cartoons went! Professor Davis loves chemistry and it shows, whether he's randomly firing cannons into the lecture hall or working out the steady state approximation for the tenth time. He explains concepts thoroughly and gives fair exams, not easy, not backbreaking. He's a really nice guy too!
Cool Demos, wakes me up when I am sleeping in class. It's very easy. I got A+.
Lectures are great; partly due to his clarity and partly to his demos. Understanding and helpful. The class was tough but problem sets and labs helped prepare you for exam.
Problem sets are clear indication of what's represented on exams-- lectures are interesting enough.

Lectures are really useful for understanding the topics and different types of problems.  Always does a cool demo, like exploding gummy bears or a hydrogen balloon.  The practice prelims are pretty much on par and really help you prepare.
demos!!!

Floyd's class is by far my favorite (first semester freshman year). He loves to do demonstrations in class; any day we dont blow something up is a day wasted in his eyes. He is obviously loves the material he is teaching and the feeling transfers to his students. It is a difficult class though.
I went to all lectures because there were awesome deomos. He is funny and friendly.

Throrough and clear presentations of material in class w/ fun demonstrations thrown in.  Definitely a good teacher for an intro. chem class.  A little more difficult than 207 in terms of problem sets, labs, and tests, but manageable.
It's an easy class as long you keep up with the material and do the problem sets by yourself or else you won't learn anything.

Davis teaches from the book a lot, but is helpful and easy to understand. He seems to try not to make exams too difficult. However, he often doesn't teach something needed for a problem set or lab until just before it's due. Loves demos. Overall, makes the class fairly enjoyable and bearable.

def. better than 207!  his tests are actually possible to pass, although the problem sets can be harder and take a longer time to complete. he seems really nice too...makes class bearable with his demonstrations
i love FLOYD
Complete waste of time if you took AP or honors chem in highschool.  Textbook is verbose, but contains little useful information.  Easy class though.

Lectures were a waste. He spent 50 minutes going over about 10 minutes worth of information and the rest was  spent with examples. Also, I went to meet with this professor one-on-one and he was not helpful at all. You can tell he wants to be a good professor, though.
Not the best lecturer, but he tries. His prelims are loooooooong. He could do a better job of using class time effectively (i.e. not so much math and more examples).
Waste of time.  I would have learned more by skipping class and reading the textbook.
Boring.

He's a pretty good teacher.  Lectures are strait forward and easy to follow, an appropriate level for chem 208.  He's a p-chemist and it shows.  if you don't like math, this class might not be for you, but as a physicist I love it.
He's an alright lecturer. Explains things but doesnt really show enough examples. He tries hard to keep people interested.
No Comments
He's a really good lecturer and explains things really well. tests are very similar to homework questions. it's definitely a good class, if you like chem and physics
Goated professor
Always makes the class smile and makes this course entertaining. Also good at explaining the content.
🐐 the goat
prof is amazing with an added bonus of a cool french accent. so so so grateful i took statics with him. he made this notoriously hard class more enjoyable and accessible.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Professor Royer lectures very well and is very considerate of students and student requests. I would definitely recommend going to his office hours. He is very clear on his expectations, but might occasionally miss one or two topics that you should read in the textbook or go ask about in office hours or after class.

Royer is an incredible professor. The class and material were pretty difficult as expected, but I have never seen such an adaptable lecturer. His lectures evolved over the course of the semester, becoming very helpful as he adapted and updated his lecturing style. He is very approachable and funny, and he brings that personality into his lectures.

I was surprised when this really young professor that mainly focuses on research was so effective at teaching. Thankfully, he works closely to align his course with Dr. Ritz (who has long organized this course and the exams) and the quality is about the same. Dr. Royer just takes it to the next level with his enthusiasm, care, and French accent.
She's very caring, explains systems very clearly, easy to understand, good class structure, has dogs :)
good class
great class
Great class!

Guidi is a great lecturer. She occasionally stumbles or makes slight mistakes, but she's also a pretty new lecturer and those really don't get in the way. When there rare mistakes, she fixes them. Course organization was excellent, even considering the course had just been redesigned. Grading was fair as well. Definitely recommend!
The class is very applied, covers a wide range of topics, and introduces you to numerous tools. The homework can be long but is always rewarding. The lectures are good.

Instructions are extremely unclear, nothing important is taught in class. 82% of the class fail on the first homework, that's an instructions PROBLEM. Student went to her, brought up the problems and then never acknowledge the problem, kept emphasizing that her intentions were to "help" us, "setting us up for success"?

Jealous of students who work hard and acheive this by themselves. She is less of a teacher more of a competitor! Highly racist. Not sure who has given the good review about her saying she is easy to reach, but you have to email her a lot to get in touch with her or reach out to a staff member in the MPH suite. She and Audrey Baker are the worst.

When she sits in a group, her problem is she will entertain potty mouth students who will bully asian students. Instead of neutralizing the situation and creating a friendly environment, she will laugh and make jokes out of them. She leaves her professionalism at home and comes to work. She is a big bully!
Love Gen! She's super supportive and is easy to get into contact with. Offers great feedback and makes the grading criteria very clear on the rubric.

Horrible! Very egotistic about her experiences, flaunts it everywhere. Very biased. Rubrics -unclear! She never responds to emails. Has zero knowledge. Doesn't teach at all, and will just make students sit in teams and work on the rubric. Very insecure and jealous of ambitious students. Misleads students in career opportunities and is unsupportive.
Nice person

Blalock-- what a guy. Very nice and approachable, teachers an easy writing course. I actually did learn about writing, but he goes off on a lot of tangents, which make lectures pretty fun. Sometimes he seemed like a mess but the class was bearable, essentially participation-based, and very casual
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Hilarious--So many papers

reviews.comment: Professor Blalock was by far the most amazing professor I had at Cornell. He was extremely friendly and passionate about what he taught. He tied personal experiences into the class and really inspired further thought in students. I would recommend taking any class he teaches as he is absolutely amazing.

He gives really great lectures that keep the materiel as interesting as possible. 2601 is far better than 2600. His tests are pretty easy and based heavily on problem sets and lectures. Content is very well presented and its easy to learn for the test. Outside of class he's a really interesting guy and I would recommend getting to know him

Lectures are a little bit hard to follow since he talks quickly, but you don't *really* need to attend lecture (only on the days that problem sets are due or they're handing back assignments). The prelims are the same as the problem sets/discussion questions, so make sure to study those. He seems kind of cold but he's not mean.
He is a really great professor and I like attending his class

Go to lecture, pay attention, go to section, follow along the problems, do the problems sets. If you do all those plus a quick review (maybe 1-2 hours) before the exam you will do well. The exams are exactly like the problem sets.   Go to class, do the hw, be a good student for once and you'll do fine.

Professor Blalock seems like a really genuinely nice and caring guy, but he really can't teach for his life. When I found out he graduated from Yale and TJ I couldn't believe it, because he could not do simple multiplication problems without getting himself confused. It's really a shame that he can't teach, super qualified guy.
Awkward, cold and standoff-ish

Nice guy, but his lectures are disorganized and mostly irrelevant to the problem sets & tests. After everyone got an A- or above on the first exam, he put really weird, unclear questions on the second one so we wouldn't do well even if we knew the material. Can't prove equations or do arithmetic, and refuses to do any of the math in office hours.

Tests are directly from problem sets. Lectures are about as unorganized as they come, and have nothing to do with the prelims. That being said, a very interesting guy. Lots of research and general worldly knowledge. Super nice and always has students' best interests. Class is fun, typical Cornell kids hate it because it's out of the box. No book :)

Professor Blalock is one of the best professors at Cornell. Even if you aren't interested in his classes, I recommend you take one simply for the sake of spending a semester listening to his lectures. Ask questions about his research! A lot of it is really interesting. He is intelligent, well traveled, funny, and an all-around good guy.
Every professor should strive to be like Professor Blalock.
Blalock is a great professor with a huge distance to himself and life in general. The class is quite easy, interesting and absolutely worth taking.
Very helpful. Uses games and gives cash prizes to demonstrate lessons. Tests aren't easy, but are graded on a pretty lenient curve.
He is the man.  Great guy and great professor.  Smart too.

Made the course fun and interesting. He encourages lively class participation in lecture and homework is rather light. Will give what ever time is necessary to help students. Exams were reasonable. The competitive strategy game applied core principles and was great fun. A really great guy.
Very clear and organized lectures. Exams require a good amount of thinking/reasoning so it is good to do practice problems yourself to familiarize yourself with thinking through such problems.

A lot of reviews have said that some material he tested on were not included in lecture. I disagree. Sure, some of the questions were difficult, but I would qualify them more as "thinking cap" questions, where we've seen a similar mechanism in lecture but on the test it was slightly different. TLDR, pay attention in lecture and you should be fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Respected--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I personally didn't find Ganem's lectures to be very helpful. Most of my learning came from taking the support class (highly recommended!), going to the learning center, and using outside resources like the organic chemistry as a second language textbook. The exams are difficult, but the curve is very generous
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He is pretty good at teaching but the course itself is pretty difficult. It takes a lot of practice questions to succeed in the class. The optional sections were very helpful.

It's organic chemistry that makes u wish u were deead... not Ganem. His lectures are actually really helpful. However, they DO NOT ALWAYS cover everything on the exams -- anything from the assigned textbook chapters is fair game. On that note, don't expect the exams to be fair... expect them to be hard.

His lectures are good and cover everything you need for the test. His tests were generally fair. Curve is generous and better than gen chem. If you go for 1 on 1 office hours with Dr. Ganem, he's very condescending which doesn't help so it's best to learn what you can in lecture and use the TAs for outside help. Great teacher, meh person.

I loved Dr. Ganem even though I didn't get the best grade in honors orgo. I learned so much and he is genuinely such a cool guy. Please go to office hours to learn more about him if you have the chance because he has super cool stories and a very effective one-on-one / small group teaching style as well that you don't get from lectures.
Go to lecture, and go to the practice review sessions because the test questions are not like the lectures. Difficult class but good lecturer

Dr. Ganem is easily one of the best professors at this university. His lectures are direct and inspiring, and he truly gives you all the tools you need to do well in the class, if you work hard. Weekly practice is your best friend in this course. Also, pay attention in lecture, and you will be able to predict what will be big on the exams.
Very engaged in students success, and good lecturer
He explains things clearly and is very organized. gives good analogies
Orgo has a lot of information, but if you do the assigned text book reading, and go to a lecture, the tests shouldn't be too bad. The curve helps a lot too.

Best Professor at Cornell. I've never enjoyed any lectures at Cornell until Orgo with Dr. Ganem. He's a great professor and does a great job at explaning everything simply and nicely. If you pay attention in his lecture and understand and memorize everything he says during lecture you're going to do well on the exams. Fair exams

Dr. Ganem is an amazing professor. Although his credentials can seem intimidating, he's actually quite caring and approachable. He is the only professor I know of who carves out time each week to meet with students. Although exams can be hard, the curve usually compensates. Pay attention in lecture, read the textbook, and you'll do just fine.

Orgo is a tough class, but Professor Ganem makes it bearable with his witty lectures. Stay on top of the material and use resources like the textbook and Unstuck study - it really helps offset the difficulty.

Orgo is a hard class in general, but the lectures were generally pretty clear. Exams were harder than lecture material. Take notes and READ THE TEXTBOOK! I found the textbook super helpful and did practice problems from the book after/in between lectures. It is easy to fall behind, so stay on top of studying bc it can be stressful to catch up.

Yes this course is difficult- it will be no matter where and who is teaching. Prof. Ganem makes the best of it by presenting the material in a witty way and tries to keep your attention by explaining why it is important in everyday life. I highly recommend going to every lecture as it is easy to fall behind.
The only hope you have is to guess on the test. Too much to memorize. Start early, like before the semester starts.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Prof was very condescending and unapproachable, and his lectures were far below the rigor of the exams. The exams didn't allow for explanations or work to be shown, so there was no way to differentiate between an A and B scoring student. Get ready to study for this class more than your other classes combined, only for some wild guesswork on exams
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I've never had a more organized professor. He carries out lectures seamlessly. That did come with some bad parts he would often brush past details that he didn't feel were that important but left me confused. He's very passionate about what he does and he wants people to get into it which is nice. It doesn't seem like he wants people to fail.

If you want to learn Organic Chemistry don't take this class. I learned more from the Organic Chemistry Tutor.  I got a B in Biochem and Orgo was way harder. Worst professor I have ever had. The average on the final was a 50 SD of 39 to give perspective on how ridiculous the class is. As a professor if your students are failing, it's your fault.

Probably one of the worst professors I've ever had. His lectures are extremely hard to follow and I pretty much learned everything from reading the textbook. Even then, the course itself is very hard but he made it 10x harder. Also, the final exam was on stuff that we hardly learned. Don't take with Gamen if you can avoid it

Professor Ganem definitely knows his stuff, but can sometimes fixate on certain topics that do not count towards much on the prelims. You definitely need to read the textbook and do practice problems outside of homework. Practice prelims are usually a good way to gauge how well you will perform & tend to be tougher than the actual exam.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Orgo is always hard, but it definitely felt like he made it harder. He also makes it hard to see him directly through office hours, and when you do see him, it's only for 10 minutes, which really doesn't help that much.

Dr. Ganem is a great and organized lecturer. He took feedback into consideration during the semester about the course during the Covid-19 FA20 semester. Overall though, he was not very transparent about how he derived our grades and gave no practice questions/exams to the class to prepare for the exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Thank you Dr. Ganem!
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Ganem is one of the best professors I've had at Cornell; he is a really good lecturer and super engaging. I've been to his office hours and he is very patient and helpful. His prelims become progressively tougher but he is not the type of teacher to ask trick questions. I really enjoyed learning orgo because of him!
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Okay, so Professor Ganem is like everyone says a great lecturer.
