Her prelims were fair and a clear representation of what was taught in class. As long as you respect class time and are on top of your work, you will do fine.
Grade is based off whether or not you have a good TA. Some are stricter than others. Prelims are unorganized, but manageable if you watch her videos.

She is nice to students during lectures, and also like to help students on their HW. The HWs can be a little hard since they aren't always exactly the same as in the demos, and the HW instructions can be unclear. The beginning of the semester was a bit confusing, and the prelims are a little hard and very time consuming. She teaches very fast.
Great class for folks trying to explore the waters of excel. Assignment are easy to complete as long as you just follow along with the videos and understand how and when to use what functions.

Does the bare minimum for her students, will not accommodate for anything (I'm not talking about late submissions) unless "required by the university". Disrespectful to students and clearly does not enjoy teaching. Beware.
Forcing students to watch those low-quality videos is torturous. Plus the class policy is so weird. Not a fan.

The class is very accuracy-based. The prelims are a lot like the practice ones, and she does not necessarily make anything confusing; everything is stuff we have seen in the past. She is very kind and genuinely seems to want to help her students.
While the class is easy, Haeger is often rude to students unnecessarily.

As was mentioned before by some other rating, she is notorious at Cornell for being by far the most disrespectful towards students. Constant patronizing and passive aggression towards students and an extremely tough grader when it comes to the most minute details. All incoming Dyson students should be aware of this.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I would argue that this is the worst professor at Cornell. The teacher puts on the facade of a supposedly nice person, but during one-to-one interactions, she completely changes. Whoever is applying or attending Cornell should think twice about the school because of this class. Notorious at Cornell for her disrespect towards students!!

Lectures and videos provide precisely what you need to know to complete the assignment. TAs were also nice with helping during class and office hours. I would say that grading could be harsh at times, and the cutoff for an A in this class was surprisingly high (95%). Someone that pays a little bit of attention to detail will have no problem.

Great professor, very clear, easy-to-follow lectures with helpful videos. 2 hw assignments/week align very closely w/ lectures. Prelims are almost exactly like the homework, so you'll know exactly what to prepare for. No final exam, just a final group project which isn't too much work. Just note that cutoff for A this sem was 95%

Course content is really useful. Nice prof but she is strict with deadlines. As long as you watch her video tutorials, attend her lectures, and do your homework, you should have the prelim part completely covered. Make use of Thursday lab sessions and OHs to get full credit for assignments (if not you will always lose a point here and there).

The class is pretty easy and it was primarily asynchronous this past semester. Professor Haeger was always attentive and accommodating to students, but she was often not present on Thursday classes with the TAs to take questions. Prelims are a large portion of the grade, so review the activities and cases before taking these exams.
Not a hard class, but I've never seen a professor be so rude to her students. Especially for one that just teaches excel at an ivy league.

I oved Professor Haeger's class and as an instructor, she did a good job. What I vehemently disliked was the disrespect towards students. I had her for an early class period, so students often would arrive a little late. Each time she would stop the whole class to essentially berate the student in front of their peers. It was absolutely unnecessary
One of the most useful classes in the major. Learned a lot without the class being overly difficult. Probably could add some concepts but overall great professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Easy class, but not a great professor. She's super disrespectful to her students and very arrogant.

Can be nit picky and corky, but overall if you come to class youll be fine. Videos of the lecture are online. The TAs are easily accessible. The first class will be a demo of what youre doing, next class you will do it yourself, then a case due outside of class. Demos can be different from your other activity. Study activities for tests!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: VERY easy course. Assignments and tests are easy, and you learn a lot. She is nice but she seemed unnecessarily harsh about academic integrity. There were several kids who had hearings but nothing ever came from it. The course is set up well, but a lot of learning is TA dependent. The final project is long but good experience.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: She is not a great professor, but if you watch the video tutorial on blackboard you will do well. Pick a good group for final project because it is a lot of work. Average grade in class is A+
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: It was my first time learning Excel, and all the assignments and prelims were very easy. However, the month-long final group project was extremely hard. Be sure to have someone who really understands Excel in your group, then you'll be fine. And do NOT procrastinate on the project.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: Rest in peace Prof Halpern, you were a real one.

He would just read off his lecture slides and not really have anything interesting or useful to say. Lectures were barely helpful for homeworks and tests. I relied mostly on the textbook and OH to learn in this class since lectures taught nothing.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: he cares about the content and the field, but he should not teach. he mumbles all through the lectures, its extremely hard to keep up with what he is saying and he just makes the already hard course even more impossible to pass. Kids stoped coming to lectures even though attendance was madatory cause that's how bad it was.

He's definitely knowledgeable and passionate about the content, but lectures are really boring and could use more examples/interactive elements. There seemed to be a lot of times when he sped through the content, asked if anybody had questions, and most people were too confused to ask anything. However, homework and exams were fair.

Nothing about this class makes sense, the lectures are rushed and incomprehensible for even simple topics, the homework is graded excessively harshly, and attendance is somehow mandatory despite lecture having no benefit to the students. I believe I'm on track for a good grade, but in retrospect it this class made it as hard as possible to learn.

He is clearly very knowledgable and passionate about the course content, but his lectures are incomprehensible and unengaging. He usually just reads off the slides in a monotone voice and rarely puts the solutions to example problems. I had better success teaching myself the material than listening to him.

An absolutely awful lecturer. He clearly cares about the content he teaches but he teaches in such a dull monotone and just rambles off of lecture slides from CS 2802. It's nearly impossible to follow along as he gives almost no pollEvs or drawings to represent his lectures. Professor Tardos is much better at engaging the class.

Absolutely terrible lecturer. He's definitely knowledgeable but he explains things very poorly by reading off slides rather than actually explaining. I stopped going to lectures halfway through the semester and I feel like my understanding of the content has improved since then.

Seems he might have been better at some point in the past but every single lecture in this course was just rambling while putting a different professors (textbook) slides on the projector and typing math in plaintext on them :( . By contrast Prof. Eva Tardos who is co teaching this semester does a fantastic job of writing and working out examples.

Professor often types mathematical proofs on slides as he explains them rather than drawing. He also needs constant reminders to speak louder or turn on his microphone. Overall doesn't seem to have a strong grasp on technology and how to utilize it to teach students in an engaging way.

He's changed a lot recently, unfortunately for the worse. He's completely incoherent in lectures and very difficult to follow along given that he just mumbles off the slides with little explanation or visuals. The lecture hall is always filled with confused students. One time when he asked who was confused, half the students raised their hands.

Professor Halpern is a great lecturer and is very eager to answer questions outside of lectures. The class is quite hard conceptually, but especially workload wise. Personally I spend +10 h/ week on the course, majority on the HW.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: His lectures are enjoyable and he is clearly knowledgeable about what he teaches. However his grading scheme is super arbitrary and doing at/above the median on everything does not guarantee you an A. Drops two lowest pset grades. Despite what he says, class difficulty ramps up heavily after prelim 1
He is an amazing professor, he clearly cares so much about students. Lectures are very interactive, and he encourages questions to make sure everyone understands concepts.

Professor Halpern is the nicest! It was a really great experience taking an honors class with him, he's a great lecturer and makes the material super accessible even if you don't have prior experience with it.

Professor Halpern is a great lecturer. I also really felt like he cared about his students and always wanted to make sure that we were doing well. The class was super interesting and I could tell he cared about teaching and the topics.
THE BEST! Congratulations to Dr. Halpern on being elected to the National Academy of Engineering!
Halpern is a fantastic lecturer. He really cares about making sure the students understand the material.
Awesome guy awesome teacher
Great prof very intelligent caring and he is super fine looking
Cool guy... WHAT A HOTTIE!

Incredibly intelligent, cares deeply about his subject and teaching.  Requires some mathematical sophistication, but that never hurt anyone.  The class is a great survey of some interesting theoretical subjects and their applications to modern computer science.
Hard material, but a GREAT professor. One of the smartest guys at Cornell, which is saying a lot. Lectures can be a little slow, but he will answer any questions, and really knows his stuff.
Avoid at ALL COST.

I really enjoyed these lectures. He is really interesting to listen to and is really accessible to answer questions. I only wish he wouldn't dim the lights so much during the lectures...its hard to stay awake in the dark regardless of how interested you are.
i thought he was awesome for the first week. then things get real boring, sleepin through class was real easy.  puts too much weight on homework so i got a b+ eventhough i ripped the final.

halpern is a very good 280 professor.  most of his lectures are interesting and well done, he's very enthusiastic about the material and it shows.  i thought that i'd hate this class but it ended up being one of my favorites.
arrogant, not helpful, FULL OF HIMSELF. Avoid!
engineering students here are required to take technical writing so they dont end up like this guy
Fantastic at teaching. Great explanation of the material. Very smart man!
Prof. Halpern is a great teacher and a nice man.
BRILLIANT.  Being taught by someone as distinguished as him was an honor.  He was humble, unlike the previous reviewer seems to believe.  He likes teaching and does it well.
the professor's kinda arrogant
Quite disrespectful to students. Had a disappointing experience similar with others.
There really isn't a lot of work for this class and the midterm was fairly easy. A big chunk of your grade comes from participation and projects.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: Stay away.
I've heard poor things about this professor and how little effort she puts into making the class fair. It is definitely true. Do not take class.
Does not listen to students, very selfish professor. You will see.

Group projects are fun and engaging. The lecture itself is also interesting. Very respectful and open to students' opinions and thoughts. Assignments are not too heavy. If you at least read and pay attention, no problem at all. Highly recommended class!

Professor DeVault is one of my favorite professors in ILR. She is extremely knowledgeable and cares a lot about your learning. The readings were worth it and she always has her door open to students if you ever want to discuss essays, ideas or topics covered in class. Attend lecture for good grades!

Devault was the most biased Prof I had at Cornell. Teaches 'historical facts' but implies clear political ideology. Grading scheme (letter grade averaged out at the end based on the lowest range of that letter) doesn't make sense. Lectures dull, didn't encourage diverse discussion. Decently easy class though.

No clear grading criteria. Essay grade ranges were insane and many students had conflicting thoughts. Slides have pictures not actual content. Of you don't go to lecture, you're done for. We also had a final which is unnecessary. Also, did I mention the class was electronic free?  If I had to do it again, I probably wouldn't take it with her.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Professor Devault is very knowledgeable and has her Labor History lectures memorized. They can be dull, but class discussions were interesting at times. Weekly quizzes depend on your TA; sometimes notes are allowed. Essays are manageable, no rubric. The final is in person, focus on 2–3 books to do well
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Devault is very knowledgeable and has her Labor History lectures memorized. They can be dull, but class discussions were interesting at times. Weekly quizzes depend on your TA; sometimes notes are allowed. Essays are manageable, no rubric. The final is in person, focus on 2–3 books to do well.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Said in class that slavery “wasn't that bad” and enslaved black people had it easier than white apprentices because at least they were provided food and shelter. Completely racist and unacceptable coming from a Cornell professor. Was not really apologetic at all after. Seems like ILR has removed the class due to this incident.

Not super difficult at all but said in online class that black people were better off during. Slavery than white apprentices because at least they were provided food and shelter. Seemed combative when receiving pushback on this statement. That was a completely unacceptable and racist statement and I wouldn't feel comfortable in class as a POC.

Not the worst professor but definitely misinformed. In her unfree labor class, she suggested that Black people were better off as slaves than free since they were “taken” care of which is shocking, racist and deeply disrespectful coming from a Cornell professor. 
The ILR department has since cancelled this class apparently.

There are better profs for this class. Prof DeVault's grading depends heavily (85% of grade) on 2 essay with virtually no directions and no rubric and an in-class final writing exam (only teacher that does this). She gives weekly quizzes on readings and attendance is taken based on participation. If you don't participate, you're not marked present.
plz avoid this class its so unclear
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: DeVault is one of my favorite professors at ILR. Super knowledgeable, engaging and interesting lectures, very helpful during office hours, fair grader. If you do the readings you'll do well in the class and leave knowing so much about labor history. She also answers to emails quite well and writes good comments on essays so you can improve

She assigns 100-250 pages of reading per week with weekly quizzes in discussion. Slides are pictures with a title and she just speaks about it. Attendance not mandatory but she tracks engagement and office hour attendance, and slides not helpful. Harsh grader on essays and final. OH not rlly helpful she laughs at questions (me and others)
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Professor Devault is very passionate about labor history and it shows. She assigns a TON of reading, but you should just skim.

DeVault  gives us pop quizzes every week on the reading which is usually over 200 pages. I had pneumonia for two weeks and she wouldn't give me her notes from lecture. If you miss class you cannot make it up because she slides she post just include images and no information. Final exam where we wrote two essays in a hour.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Devault assigns a ton of reading and you don't need to do all of it (though she does have weekly quizzes that typically test one aspect of the reading, so at least skimming is a good idea to not lose points there). Somewhat tough essay grader.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Highly recommend taking this seminar if you have an interest in history and need to knock out the ILR advanced writing requirement. Genuinely a caring professor, and super great to chat with during OH. Reads your journal entries in full, and writes thoughtful comments in the entries.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: A very caring and passionate professor. A lot of readings but the work is manageable. Clear grading based on a mix of attendance and participation, journal entries, and essays. She is very understanding so would recommend going to office hours and communicating if you have any questions with the material.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Shes super kind and caring, you can tell shes very passionate about her work. Homework is mostly reading assignments up to 150+ pages a week and general quizzes during discussion. 2 papers and a take-home final that makes the majority of your grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: DeVault clearly cares about the subject and the class. Go to office hours for help on the essays and you will do well.

Professor DeVault is clearly passionate about her subject matter. However, her class had excess work as well as a final exam that other labor history classes did not have. She graded harshly and the clear expectations were not laid out prior to paper assignments.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Grade based on: Critical book report, Primary source project, Research paper proposal, Final exam, and participation. Some assignment due dates vary by topic, allowing you to pick what weeks you want to work for the class. Thursday classes are all discussion so make sure to read what is assigned for the week (200-ish pages).
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Weekly "quiz" on readings in discussion. Read lots of short books over the semester
Professor Devault is very kind! Although there is a large amount of reading every week, it helps acclimate you with the college course load
reviews.rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Lot of reading as it is a history class. Paper heavy. But she is a great professor and she is a very nice person very understanding great professor to get you into college
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Gives good feedback--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: Professor Devault is a great professor. Labor History can be a dry topic but she is very good at trying to make it as easy as possible. 3 papers one of which is the final paper, and weekly quizzes in discussion which are not that bad. Take her if you can
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Caring

reviews.comment: Interesting professor who presents interesting material, but lectures had the tendency to be very dry. I appreciated that she made an effort to learn students' names and was accessible for questions. There were about 2 extra credit opportunities. A lot of reading, but skimming is ok. Weekly reading quizzes during discussion section, not so bad.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Tests? Not many

reviews.comment: DeVault is one of the nicest people I have ever met. She genuinely cares about her students. I had a class during her office hours, so she regularly scheduled other times to meet. I struggled a lot with the first essay assignment, but DeVault helped me and taught me what I did wrong. Her lectures can be dry, but the information is interesting.

Awesome professor, super passionate and nice.  Lots of weekly reading with reading quizzes in discussion, but you can definitely "skim" main concepts and do extremely well. Two papers, go to TA office hours and you will do well on them. Extremely fair final. Some find the subject boring overall but DeVault makes it as painless as possible.
Excellent professor! Clear and learned a lot, definitely not an easy grader but she is very helpful and sweet and a fair grader. Love her!
Nice and helpful.

Not a bad course..very dry though. make sure you suck up in section to your TA and dont worry about the weekly quizzes too much. most imporant thing about this class would be to meet with both the TA and DeVault before both of the papers to talk about your thesis and make sure youre on the right track... they will tell you what to write basically

Unfree Labor: Slaves, Servants, Wives.  Great Teacher, learned alot, ALOT of reading, one book per week, sometimes half a book, seminar style, classes tend to be very large.  Attendance is not required but necessary for class participation points.  She really knows her stuff and goes a long way to ensure your understanding.

Comparative History of Women at Work.  Best seminar class I've ever been in, attendance is not mandatory, but necessary to get class participation points.  Reading is tough, one book per week+short paper, but well worth the effort, learned alot, seminar style allowed for greater understanding of others opinions on women's issues

Intro to Labor History, lots of reading, but if you keep  up your set, class participation counts for alot, you'll definitely learn something.  I liked her so much, I've taken 2 more of her classes. (IC636 and CB388)

DeVault is very passionate about labor history and is a good lecturer; she gave us weekly ‘reading quizzes’ which were annoying but I guess it made us read the many books we were assigned...  A pretty interesting class if you’re into the subject. 2 papers, final exam was 2 essay questions; DeVault is a tough grader but a good teacher overall.

Wonderful teacher.  She is very interested in the subject and conveys it.  She is also very helpful, friendly, and pleasant to talk to if you see her in office hours.  Probably my favorite teacher this semester.

Excellent professor. I have heard that other CB100 profs are more dynamic lecturers, but I never had a problem staying engaged or awake in her class. She's definitely a professional, and I respect her tremendously. She has very high standards for your work, but that definitely motivates you to do well.
Boring and dry, but very clear and organized.  Go to lecture and get the gist of the readings, and you'll be fine.  Was also very understanding and accommodating when I had a conflict.

I have taken three classes with her,and I would take more if they were offered! Show up to class and you're set. Being interested in history helps a lot, but overall she is extremely nice and cares about how you do.

Good lectures, make the material as interesting as it can be. Ton of reading, nobody expects you to do it all though. For papers, make sure you hav a good thesis and you will do fine. She is very interested in women's issues, clearly. Overall, great prof.

An excellent professor and lecturer.  She made a very dry topic into something interesting. She assigns a lot of reading, however she doesn't intend for you to do it all, but rather gather general concepts.  Overall it was a pretty easy course if you went to lectures.
Incredibly boring.  Her many random examples are irrelevant and a waste of time.  She also refuses to change a grade no matter how unfair it may be.

One of the best professors you will have.  Lectures were clear, easy to follow and interesting.  Take good notes in lecture and you're guarenteed good grades.  Papers are a decent amount of work but very fairly graded.  The best prof. for the class by far.

Great professor- very clear and helpful. There is a lot of reading which is useful when it comes to doing papers. Papers can be tough...it helps to run your ideas by her first. Very nice and approachable and her lectures are very clear. No complaints here!
very clear and informative lectures, requires work (esp for the reading) but you'll learn a lot about US labor history

My favorite prof so far. I wasn't expecting labor history to be fun, but it's the only class I'm never inclined to skip. She's a really nice lady, very knowledgable, makes the class interesting w/o compromising depth or clarity of material. Papers aren't bad; reading can be a bit much, but doable.
Ileen is a goddess
Excellent professor; Papers require work, but if you try you can do well; Very clear lectures

This is a very interesting course on immigration. Devault does a great job at putting the history of immigration into context with work related stuff.  She is a very nice person who is very honest and fair.
very nice and helpful if you ask.  papers aren't that bad.  very good lectures and decent amount of reading for a cb class.
Tons of respect for this professor. DeVault is organized, clear, and strives to be as accessible to her students as time allows. No complaints!
very knowledgeable, great lectures. Papers requre some work though.
No Comments
great class

He might be good at researching, but he is absolutely not good at lecturing and breaking things down. If you take his class, be ready to learn on your own for more than 80% of the time. Even many CS lovers on YouTube outperform him in terms of teaching DB stuff.

This class is relatively interesting, but takes SO MUCH time. Only take it if you have a significant interest in getting started with DB design. You will need equally committed partners.
If you choose to take it take advantage of opportunities to fix and ask for regrades. Additionally, make sure to rigorously test.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Class is eh. People are right that it is 'easy', the hard part is parsing the content out of the lecture material. Slides are very long, while what is important is not always evident. Exams have a strong emphasis on SQL which for a DB class I think is good, but I found them difficult. SQL is covered but not enforced to the degree which is tested.

He's lowkey just a chill guy, he is def passionate about the material and it shows when you ask him questions. This is the easiest CS class you can take at this school, like, its easier then 1110. The material is also pretty useful for industry, though I don't think ill ever use a where not where not exist sql negation irl.

Loved his lectures. He answers questions if you have any (even though only few people asked). Homeworks are very representative of the exams and also gives enough practice exams. Not hard to get a good grade in this class. Office hours are also good.
Great Prof, great content

Might as well not even be there. Course staff never answers Ed posts. There's no syllabus, and he would flip flop on the late policy to whatever he feels like. Normal DB is just boring, but the practicum is an unreal amount of work. Infuriating how absent he would be from what should be easy questions on Ed.

Despite his looks and how he speaks, Dr. Trummer is actually a very smart guy. Otherwise, our department wouldn't hire him as a new faculty member.

His lectures are very difficult to understand, and the TA I interacted with is not helpful at all. I dropped the course during the first month. I ain't wasting my time here.

Good luck.

This class was terrible. Legit could win an award for how badly he teaches. The content isn't necessarily hard, but his extreme lack of explanation and his terribly worded problem sets make it unnecessarily difficult. To top it off, the course staff he hires for this class have absolutely no idea what they are doing and mess up when they help.

This is a very demanding class. It felt more like a 4 credit class than a 2 credit one. The 5 projects were difficult and built off of each other, so if you implemented something wrong it has to be resolved before your next project (and you do not get access to solutions or the test cases that TAs run). You have to be a strong Java developer.

Because of COVID this class was graded only on 6 homeworks (no exams) and it ended up being very easy. Trummer is a clear & effective lecturer and made concepts easy to understand with examples and visualizations in his slides. Homeworks were straightforward and followed directly from the lectures. There was a textbook but I did not use it.
The content of the course is really useful. However, his lecture is hard to understand even with the video recordings from past semester.
His lecture was somewhat hard to follow!

Lectures were super dry, but they were posted online so you could work through them at your own pace. Made the requirements clear and assignments+exams were manageable. Grading was very slow. Material somewhat interesting.
Ambiguous Assignment with unrefined score judgement. Accent is 'tuple' or 'Lets get started!"
Learning from Trummer was the highlight of my college career.
Very good course
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Extremely good professor. Enjoyed the lectures. Highly recommend the course.
Trummer was a below average professor. His lectures were tough to understand. MANY people dropped.

The course content is not for the weak, but Dr. Lorey explains it in a way that makes it easy to understand. I dreaded missing his class because one lecture could mean missing important details not explained deeply in the textbook. The exams reflected the prelim reviews, and his lectures really helped make sense of the material.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: I took this class for the CALS requirement, as did most students likely. It was not a class to sleep on (lots of memorization required) but material is just above that of an advanced high school class. Prof. Lorey has thorough slides and breaks down concepts in class with on-the-fly notes. Attendance optional, but I'd go. Class is curved at the end
THE MOST BORING LECTURES EVER YOU WILL FALL ASLEEP,  The way he teaches is so boring and make you go to sleep. Every time I'm there I fall asleep, not only me but many students
Profesor Dan Lorey is a bit boring, but the class itself is easy.
His lectures are decently engaging and he thoroughly covers the material students are tested on.
Dry lectures but his videos are fun. Topics are broken down to be understandable.
Great lecturer. Posted slides, homework on achieve were very helpful and fair. Chemistry is hard, and that's not his fault.
Chemistry was hard, but an amazing lecturer, super nice, funny, and wrote fair exams.

Don't fall into the habit of skipping class - if you go and pay attention, you'll be much more prepared. Go to office hours!! He is really nice and wants to help you understand; if you ask for help, he will help until you actually get it. Read the lab report guidelines. He provides prelims from previous year. Class is curved at end. He's also funny

I love this man. Lectures are fast but slideshows are posted. This class built a community. Labs tend to be dull but thats only because they are taught by random TA's not King Dan. 1560 is challenging but VERY FAIR. I go to Dan's office hours twice a week and he is incredibly helpful with HW Labs and explanations. If you study a lot you can do well

Dr. Dan is the best! Super nice and fun lecturer. Although the class is really boring and difficult at points, Dr. Dan keeps it light with his humor and general caring personality. I would definitely recommend 1560 as an alternative to 2070 if its not a requirement.
Loved prof. Lorey. Never a dull moment in his class.

Dr. Lorey is a great professor. This is one of the most challenging courses I had to take but he makes sure that there are lots of opportunities to get help and he is accessible outside/after class. I have almost no chem background and was still able to understand his lectures, he does a great job at making them engaging.

Dr. Lorey is an amazing teacher as well as an amazing person. Although I did terribly in this class, it was in no way his fault. His office hours are inviting, and he provides lots of resources for people like me who struggle with this kind of class to at least have a halfway decent chance at passing.

Truly amazing dude, and that means a lot considering I despise chemistry. Get help after hours from Prof! Didnt get an A because didnt have time to devote to this one class. Labs are crucial; pre-lab quiz irrelevant for finals; practice exams almost exactly like real exam!! Dont take too many STEM classes in one semester and youll be g:)

Lab reports are super important! You don't have a ton of grades in this class so make sure your reports are good! Dr. Lorey is great and loves talking to his students but make sure you keep up with the work. The exams are pretty difficult so make sure you study a lot and apply problems from the textbook.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: I am so bad at chemistry but Dr. Lorey really helped me grasp the concepts that were challenging. The class requires work to be put in but if you complete the assignments and come to lecture, youll do just fine. 2090 in the summer is a great class to take for those struggling with gen chem in the fall or spring
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Experiments were pretty interesting and not too difficult. The lab practical at the end of the semester was challenging, but Dr. Lorey doesn't make it worth too much. Weekly lab reports were laborious, but easy to do well on as long as you were thorough. Overall, very fair class that was a very appropriate difficulty.

The class starts out pretty easy, but after the first prelim, picks up speed pretty quickly. Dr. Lorey is a great professor, if something isn't clear to you during class, hes good for in class questions and provides plenty of office hours for you to get help.
Clear, consistent, and actually cares for his students. Dr. Lorey will get you through Chem1560 as long as you are willing to put in the work.

He's super knowledgable and a good guy to talk to but his content in WLC tends to be all over the place without as much cohesiveness as other lecturers. In regards to the exam def the toughest to prepare for b/c you have to cite readings in a closed-book exam. Exam Qs were rough but grading seemed more lenient relative to other profs

Had him as the second professor for Work Labor Capital. He mixed lectures with documentaries which I enjoyed. Provided a list of all of the readings on the final which was useful as we were required to quote from them during the short answer section. Final was much harder than Boyer's so beware of the difficulty spike. A decent prof.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He mixed lectures with documentaries which was interesting but the test was hard. Short essay and short answers, a couple people weren't able to finish. DO THE READINGS and take notes.

Great professor. Readings were insightful and informative. He expertly covers a plethora of interesting topics, including workplace cultures and discrimination. Papers are really easy (just have to reflect on the readings), and tests were graded fairly. I enjoyed the lectures, but if you aren't interested in the topics they may be boring. Gives EC.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: The entire class is based off of readings, most of which I found interesting, but long. The class has no clear theme and is very disjointed. Lectures are useless, but mandatory. The actual grading is entirely done by your TA and it matters which one. I like the content and idea of the class but it was poorly executed.

Class itself was not that difficult just incredibly boring and required you to do an obnoxious amount of reading which was NEVER talked about in class so you had to infer meanings on the tests. Tests are mostly short answers and discussions were useless. He seemed like he did not want to teach the class and word-for-word read off the slides.

God, he is the absolute worst. He literally just chats, like no content comes out of his mouth, he just says words. I personally think his class is the biggest waste of time, but attendance is mandatory!!!

For a 1.5 credit class, the amount of readings and work given seemed unnecessary. Professor Ivory used an index card system that made lecture attendance mandatory. They improved after the first prelim, but the lectures were generally pretty useless and did little to help me understand the incredibly dense and long readings for the week.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: unnecessarily strict with his attendance/deadline policies and is unsympathetic to needing flexibility. he basically reads off of his slides, so going to lecture feels pointless, and rather than discussing the incredibly dense readings, he just puts "discussion questions" up on the board and reads them to the class. he doesn't even answer them.
Seems like a nice guy, but I couldn't hear a single thing. Talks into his neck. Tests were dumb. Like high school. Did not enjoy. Some readings were interesting though. Lectures were just not good.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--EXTRA CREDIT--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Class is based on readings which are never discussed meaningfully in class, useless lectures, attendance is mandatory with mini flashcard assignments about readings, he doesn't seem like he wants to be there, must study hard, some TAs make class much easier than others, overall would not recommend class unless you have to take it as an ILRie
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Very boring and useless lectures. He just reads off from the slides every day. And attendance is mandatory. Extremely heavy readings with few explanations in class lectures. Would not take it again.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--So many papers--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: am a senior and this is one of the best classes i have taken because it intersects microbiology, marine biology, and eeb. very very interesting course

Prof. Hewson is amazing! I was nervous going into this class because I have no background in oceanography, but it's been super interesting and not stressful. Learn a lot of content but no tests/quizzes. He often ties his own research/field work into class content and you can tell he just wants to get students excited to learn!

This class was really interesting. The prof went above and beyond to make the class accessible and flexible in the online learning environment. While the lectures were dense, learning goals were clearly identified and the assessments were firmly focused around these. Also assessment was flexible and tailored to each student's preferred method.

Prof. Hewson is clearly very knowledgeable&passionate about his field, but the lectures are very dense and got to be a bit monotonous at the end of the semester Luckily there was no exams on the detailed lecture content, rather theres weekly literature/writing HW, group proj, and final proj. This was nice but made it too easy to tune out lecture
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Group projects--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Hewson is a fantastic individual who really knows his stuff! Such a pleasure to have him as a teacher.
This was a great introduction to marine biology. Some of the labs were a little disorganized but at least there were labs!

Dr. Ian's Hewson class is more suited for people with no familiarity with biology than anyone who has even taken intro biology.  It is overall disorganized and presented in a distinctly anthropocentric and unscientific manner

Look, boys, this was a great class. The professor is really really smart, but you WILL BE CHALLENGED. the quizzes aren't too bad if you do the homework on your own. My favorite part was how INTERACTIVE the professor was, specifically the October 31st class where we learned and saw SPOOKY experiments! This was an amazing course, glad I took it.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Hilarious--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Course is very disorganized, discussion quizzes were very inconsistent (some graded for correctness while others completion based on TA), no practice prelim answers given, no responses on ed, somewhat condescending in lecture, able to get a good grade but not very enjoyable
UNC IS SO CHOPPED!!!!!!!!!!!!

Though this class was not perfect, with several areas for improvement, Prof. Cohen is a perfectly fine professor. Heavy workload and dry lecture slides are there, but there is A LOT of content. It doesn't seem like he enjoys teaching 2214, but despite an occasional condescending edge in lecture (accidental?), if you pay attention you will be fine.

Prof. Cohen and his course staff have made PHYS 2214 by far the worst class I have taken at Cornell. I recommend strongly to avoid his offering of the course. Multiple times this semester, the professors on the course staff failed to show up for their scheduled office hours without notice to the students. They also do not respond on Ed.

At very few times he actually seemed to care for people in the class, but instead of answering anyone's questions on Ed he would only be available in the case that someone was answering polling outside of lecture. Class was very disorganized, and he didn't even hold his own office hours, which made it really hard to grasp the material intuitively.

This might be the worst class I have taken at Cornell. The class is disorganised, nobody responds on the ed, he takes a month to respond to emails, and nobody (students or TAs) has the answer key to practice prelims. It genuinely feels like he doesn't want his students to succeed in this class. Lectures are confusing and hard to follow along with.

I have no idea why this man teaches because he has no interest in talking to students or coming up with course content. All of his materials are from 2018. He doesn't give solutions on any practice tests so it's like you try and you hope you are right but there's no way to find out. I don't think I have ever seen him respond on Ed.

Horrible organization of this class. First class in all of my engineering career that does not provide answer key to the practice prelims so you don't even know if what you are doing is wrong. Not enough TAs to help you. No one answers to my ED discussion posts other than other students who are trying to also understand what's going on.

It was the most disorganized class ever. The course with over 100 students only has one TA, with office hours once a week. Post no review for prelim, and never respond on ed discussion. Poorly organized class and professor can't teach.

Terrible lecturer. Writes chicken scratch on the board during his lectures and in general they are extremely confusing and really hard to follow because it's not pre planned slides its just random things he jots down projected onto the screen. Then the homework is nothing like what he mentions in class.
If you dont have to take a class with him don't take it.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: WHAT DO YOU MEAN YOU DIDN'T READ YOUR OWN SYLLABUS AND CHANGE IT HALFWAY THROUGH???
Most disorganized class I have ever taken.
Rough
Godawful professor. Even my friends who are cracked at physics cannot stand his teaching. He could not teach a fish to swim.
Don't bother taking this class if you want to learn anything. The prelims are far too difficult for how terribly the professor teaches us the content.
The class is so catastrophically taught that I wanted to drop out of physics altogether. Honestly so sad that we get terrible professors even though we pay so much in tuition
The content itself is honestly manageable. The problem is that the professor can't teach, which makes learning it so difficult

For the love of your GPA don't take class with this professor. The complete lack of teaching knowledge this prof has is embarrassing. You would learn just as much from a robo voice speaking the lecture slides out loud,
Trying take any class with this professor feels like a chore. Hes a nice person but can't teach
The class feels like just the prof regurgitating lecture slides. Completely useless in helping you prep for prelims
Incredibly unclear in lecture and explaining content in general, might as well learn the content on your own.
A nice person but an absolutely terrible professor. Often useless on ED and the lectures are incredibly dry. Don't take any class with this professor.
This professor cannot explain anything, the whole class just feels like plug and chug.
worst prof i have had at cornell

Prof Cohen is a nice person but he is a terrible professor. I don't have a bad grade (currently A/B+) but his course is designed EXTREMELY poorly and unfairly. The lectures do not help with the homework at all. The midterm is impossible because the questions are just too complex for beginner physics students. Worst Cornell course I have taken.

It is not recommended to do research under this professor. Very unclear in communicating objectives and willing to take credit for other people's ideas to get onto papers where his lab did very little work. Overall a poor/borderline unethical manager of his undergraduate and graduate researchers.
will not take class with this professor again.
Terrible experience

The professor is very tricky. For the class he required using iclicker. Many people did not show up and then in one of the last lectures he told people not to click on one of the questions and used this way to catch people who clicked the iclicker remotely. This is a lie and a terrible way to catch student.

Itai is an amazing professor. He's incredibly passionate about physics and students remain engaged during his lectures because of his energetic composition. I am not a physics major but he made physics probably the most interesting and amazing class I'd taken my 2nd to last semester at Cornell. I definitely recommend taking a class with him.
Serious professor
Very knowledgable and insightful. Lots of background info that was helpful.
Avoid.

His explanation is definitely clear. However, I sometimes doubts that he understands the material exactly. He just writes things in his notes, and he doesn't answer the questions students ask properly. If you take this class, you have to take some to study your book.
No Comments

His lectures were often unclear and difficult to follow, leaving many students disengaged. Attendance expectations felt random and inconsistent, adding to the lack of structure. I frequently relied on outside resources to learn material that should have been covered in the lecture.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor West is a only a decent lecturer, but he is VERY good at explaining things when you ASK QUESTIONS. To succeed, sit at the front, ask questions, and attend office hours! Professor West is available very often, so take advantage! He loves to get to know students during his office hours, and is more than willing to accommodate your schedule.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Jim West is very kind and intelligent, but I fear that since he was been an expert in "Geometric topology and infinite-dimensional topology" for so long, he no longer really is able to teach undergraduate classes. That means he usually is unable to explain the material in a way that someone new to the material can really understand it.

Every kid in his class deserves a refund on their semester's tuition. His lectures were so useless that maybe a fifth of the class attended. On the upside he knew his lectures were bad, so the tests were easy. Also the homework solutions are online. Using the MIT lectures on YouTube gives an easy A.
Lectures boring and not very well attended. Good textbook though and he let us know which sections each lecture/homework covered. No website and unclear grading guidelines. Not great not horrible.

Funny and always accessible. A lot of readings but only the parts discussed during class are covered. Clearly cares about his students and always available for unscheduled office hours. Unfortunately, he's sometimes unprepared and can't answer some basic questions. Midterm graded very reasonably.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Genuinely one of the laziest professors I have had. Every class was exactly the same and he just liked to listen to himself talk. He was often condescending and grading was completely arbitrary.

Every day in class, you do the same boring activity. He is a lazy unmotivated faculty member who created the curriculum years ago and has never once revised it. He would come to class having not reviewed anything related to the material. Additionally, he provides 0 feedback on your work and doesn't explain why he takes points off.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This class is fascinating, and even students with a Jewish education background will learn a ton. There's a lot of reading and writing involved, which can hit hard in the middle of the semester when it's 10 degrees outside. He is accessible outside of class and encourages students to participate.

Professor Mokhtarian is an incredible professor who cares deeply about seeing his students learn and succeed. The assignments are beneficial to your understanding of the information and never feel unnecessary. He will push you and your writing skills, but it is 100% worth it and you will be proud of your assignments by the end of the semester.
makes the chem lab rubrics and makes sure TAs don't give student points or any leeway. lab grade is dragging down my grade.

Currently taking 3570. She doesn't go by the textbook order unlike every other professor and just jumps around, so the support class is unhelpful. She denies every regrade request and doesn't post any notes. How is it possible that the prelim 1 avg is a 67% when the past semesters the averages have been around 90.
actually insane. There's no way

Hardest part about 2070. No rubrics provided, so students are left guessing about what info they need to include in lab reports. Harsh grading and regrade requests are often denied without proper reasoning. Grading is inconsistent between TAs, so just hope you get lucky. Instructions are not explicit and even one bad lab is hard to recover from.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: There is no rubric for lab reports and they are graded strictly. The regrade system has many holes that bar students from receiving the points they actually made on assignments. The lab section is the hardest part of the class, and the one you're least able to progress in due to the set up of the course.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Impossible to do well

She makes the lab reports difficult because we are not allowed to see a rubric, and her grading is very inconsistent. She grades super strictly and somehow expects you to read her mind. Also, if too many students submit regrade requests, she will go into lecture and yells at everyone. She genuinely prays on every student's downfall.
Don't take a class with her

She is the lab director for General Chemistry. The TAs for CHEM 2071 are expected to deduct a set number of points (with quotas), and students have been discouraged from requesting regrades. Rubrics are not provided before lab reports are due and are instead created afterward, with TAs instructed to grade very harshly. Nice person, however.

She makes it unnecessarily difficult to do well on labs by changing the grading rubric each time and taking off points for the smallest things. She expects students to read her mind and know exactly what to include in lab reports (e.g. which outside sources to cite in the introduction). This course was honestly more difficult than CHEM 2070 lecture

Does not provide rubric for labs to students or TAs, so there is no way to improve performance. Grades on things never mentioned before, then tells students not to submit regrades. Leaves comments on reports that is not constructive, but critical and unsupportive.

Does not respond to emails and will not release rubrics so students are left guessing what they did right and what they should keep on doing for following labs. Lab TA's are don't have them either and are left confused with grading as they also don't know rubrics till after we submit. Graded on things that were never asked of us in instructions.
lab instructor who did anything to ensure you didn't succeed.

The professor is smart and excellent in his field. However, the teaching is hard, and office hours, or even after class inquires, are almost impossible. The professor is always busy with things and doesn't even have a minute to answer students' questions. Exam questions are vague, and there are dozens of formulas you have to memorize, all tough
The two reviews on Jan 20 are very likely his own ratings, given the name-calling "Dyson kids" and the clarifications. Decided to drop as the office hour policy discourages us from seeking help.
A great professor that is very engaging. Lectures are very interesting, so interesting that you want to go to class.

He's obviously a brilliant guy, but he needs to do a better job of making sure everyone in the class understands what he's talking about. He's not great at going over the basics, and the pre-reqs for the class underestimate the course content. If you're working on an S&T/Fixed Income desk, this is the class for you. If not, you're out of luck.

Pretty straightforward with his class, and he aims to help you understand the content if you have questions. However, his office hours can sometimes be inconvenient, so it can be hard to reach out to him. He doesn't really publish anything on canvas, and his lecture notes are pretty disorganized. It can start to get confusing if you skip lectures.

He is terrible at breaking things down and he expects you to understand the content well. You cannot stay to work through a problem set in his office hours because "it's not his job to guide you through it" but rather answer very specific questions. The assignments and exams were very vague as he is more focused on his research and academic drama.
Amazing Professor! Slides were very clear, and she had an amazing way of relaying information. Put in effort and you'll be fine!
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: She has a lot on the lecture slides and expects everyone to be familiar with what's on the slide and in the readings. The hardest part is that she doesn't make it clear what will realistically come up again in the course; best piece of advice is go to office hours as she tends to be a lot more clear there.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Modica genuinely cares about you and the class isn't an easy A, but you will learn a lot about labor history; she's a kind and knowledgeable woman so you should get to know her and go to office hours. The final is difficult, but she did her best to alleviate stress around it/ accomodate us.

regularly asks for student feedback and is willing to reduce certain required readings (however the readings were still excessive), weekly reading checks, had a couple easy grade booster assignments, professor is very knowledgeable, final is very difficult
Simply amazing!
She knows her stuff, exams are tough, and she cares a lot. She's super pretty, so it's a bonus if u get bored with the lecture. Findley the TA is the goat.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Really nice person. Only a couple of assignments, 2 Exams. 30% of the class is participation. Very long readings and a very hard final exam. Class will require a lot of studying, but you will learn a ton of labor history. What I appreciate most about Dr. Modica is that she's open to change in how she formats readings, assignments, and exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: People are being harsh. Lectures are informative, she's very passionate about the course/her students. Definitely a difficult course but also the readings can be easily skimmed, and the writing assignments like Kheel Center/Film review are interesting/grade boosters. TA is super chill. Final seems really difficult though, may change in future.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Modica is genuinely kind and energetic. However, she is an impossible professor to have. She is obviously an expert in her field, but I think she carries a mindset that her students are equally expert, assigning 200+ pages of reading at times. Her Final Exam is essentially impossible unless you study 12+ hours/day. TA Findley is very cool tho.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Just really made the class a lot more difficult then it should have been

Modica is a fantastic person, but has unrealistic expectations of students. You were expected to read 200+ pages, review 80+ slides (each slide was a block of text), and complete a reading assessment each week. Her grading metrics were unclear. Super kind and understanding of her students, but her definition of "light" work is 200 pages vs. 250.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Very simple format; grade is made up of attendance, weekly reflections (graded on completion), a midterm essay, and a take home final essay. Kind professor, really cares about the material. Not much to prepare for class other than doing the readings. Interesting and low-stakes class

Prof was so kind and cared so much about the material. Class was a mix of lecture and discussion, he really wants people to participate. Lectures basically reiterate the readings, which aren't too heavy. Not many assignments in the class, and prompts are given in advance so you can prepare your essays ahead of time.

In an hour and a half, Prof. Joo's lectures accomplish what could otherwise be achieved in three sentences. He has good intentions and the readings are interesting, but the class itself mostly consisted of him reading excerpts of what we were already expected to read.

He's good at explaining things in lecture. He's caring and always willing to answer questions during class and after. It's a tough class and problem sets are long but the work is helpful for exam prep. If you have to take this class, I would recommend taking his!

His class IS difficult. the PSs are pretty long but good practice for the tests. He really cares for his students and WILL try his best to help you if you reach out, and he's genuinely passionate about what he teaches. To get good grades, make sure you understand the concepts and do the sections and really listen to the lectures.

Such a difficult class BECAUSE HE MAKES IT THAT WAY! Get your ECON 3030 credit somewhere else because this class genuinely is so difficult for no reason. If you care about your GPA, don't take this class. He has extremely difficult content in problem sets (6 a semester), after lecture quizzes, and most of the time he couldn't finish his lecture.
Engaging, yet informative lectures. The material is difficult but the criteria for success are clear cut so it's not impossible to do well.

This class is genuinely brutal. Just one of those classes you need to get through for Econ major but it's frustrating the entire time. P-sets are actually absurd and the entire course is multi variable calculus despite half the class not having taken that class. Prof seems like a good dude but it doesn't really matter when the class is unreasonable
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--So many papers--Test heavy

reviews.comment: immediate no. Can't fit his lectures into class time so he always posts a video of what he didn't cover in class. Usually these videos are posted NEXT DAY.ew

Sweet professor who cares about course and students, but very difficult course. P-sets would take upwards of 8 hours each (6 p-sets for the course and one drop). Exams are much easier than p-sets. Lectures are difficult to follow, but posts lecture slides. Accessible for OH. Would recommend, but be ready to put in a lot of work outside of lectures

Pretty nice guy but could not make any sense out of his lectures. Talks about how content is easy but likes to jump from one thing to the next without a ton of conceptual explanation; slides aren't the best either. I learned the most from this class by just figuring things while doing the psets, but felt like exams questions were a lot different.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Good lecturer, but sometimes struggles with pacing content. Make sure to do the reading (BEFORE you take the quizzes) and go to class. Not too many p-sets, but they take forever. Exams are very fair--if you understand the problem sets, you will do fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: Very sweet man and tries to be accommodating, but oh boy he does not lecture well. Tries his best, but exams are crazy hard too. Annoying part is that he just keeps talking about how easy the exam was, and just doesn't explain well. Better off just self studying tbh

A good lecturer, but the slides can be a bit lacking, with a bit too heavy an emphasis on theory at times. Tests can be difficult but graded fairly, and if you study you will do well. Problem sets are difficult but definitely reasonable. Overall he is a caring professor who takes feedback well and is willing to accommodate his students

Such an interesting class and the professor is clearly well informed (rambles a bit too much). Only issue is she barely gives out As and doesn't give proper feedback. Super nice during office hours and overall a great teacher.

Her grading criteria was very unclear and she didn't give us good feedback on how to improve our writing. I would say that my AP Lit/Comp class taught me like 5x more about how to write well, than this class did. It's unfortunate because it means that we are not making good use of our tuition/time/energy here at Cornell.
Do not take this class.

If you choose to take a class by this professor... don't. If you have to take a class from this professor... good luck. She is hard to listen to and rants for ages. The class has no clear rubrics or standards for writing. She grades harshly with little to no room for improvement no matter how much time you may spend. Save yourself, please.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--So many papers

reviews.comment: Good guy, good teacher, lots of quizzes, no exams
The shadows are wrong because deep state used a lamp in a big shed. the moon is just grey stucco. Moon landing is definitely a fake

The moon rocks brought back by Apollo 16 are actually just petrified wood and basalt from a volcano in Iceland. Geologists have been trying to tell us for years that the chemical composition matches Earth 100%. The 'moon' is just a rock we already have, but they put it in a glass case to justify the trillions of dollars they spent on studio set.
any course in engineering that does not teach moonlanding is a fake is a shill for the deep state. Moonlanding was filmed in a studio and never happened.
Does data engineering prove moonlanding is fake? If not, it should not be taught in engineering.  Engineers are complicit in covering up the moonlanding fake.
Houston? No way man! That's NASA territory—way too many spies
you will be taking a quiz every single lecture and every discussion. prof hates ai but is also an ai glazer. lectures are extremely boring it's even worse than watching paint dry.
Hey fake moon landing guy,  I am the deep state guy. Do u live in Houston? we should meet up.

The moon rocks brought back by Apollo 16 are actually just petrified wood and basalt from a volcano in Iceland. Geologists have been trying to tell us for years that the chemical composition matches Earth 100%. The 'moon' is just a rock we already have, but they put it in a glass case to justify the trillions of dollars they spent on  studio set.
Yes I agree, Moon landing is definitely a fake. No way humans could have survived the radiation of van allen belt. Any engineer who does not agree with this is part of the deep state.
Moon landing is definitely fake, a deep state coverup. Hahahahaha. Muhahahahaha
hahahahahaha. lolololol hee hee hee

The course is ok.  But the professor omitted important database analytics. If she had covered this, it would be clear that moon landing is impossible.  There were no assignments on the physics of landing. I am giving her the benefit of the doubt.

Nice course. i give 5 stars for the data science but it is a serious omission that they didn't teach us how the moon landing is mathematically and physically impossible. my computer showed me the photos are from a big shed in nevada. the data is good but we need to talk more about the deep state movie set. 5 stars for the truth!!

data engineering course is useful... computer can show  true or false.  the shadows are wrong because deep state used a lamp in a big shed. the moon is just grey stucco.  5 stars for the data facts for showing moonlanding is fake.

i put the moon landing in my computer and the computer said its fake. data science show the shadows are wrong because the deep state trolls used a lamp in the studio. nasa is a lie and whoever flags this is a deep state shill!! 2 stars for the data.

The deep-state  keeps flagging and removing my posts about the fake moon landing  because they are part of the cover-up! They don't want the students to know that the 'lunar dust' is actually just pulverized grey stucco. Every time the truth gets posted, it disappears. 5 stars for the blatant censorship and for being a NASA shill.

The shadows in the moon photos are 'filled in.' In a vacuum with one light source, the shadows should be pitch black, but you can see detail in the astronauts' suits while they are in the shade. That's because of 'bounce boards' used by film crews. It's a low-budget movie set.  Moon landing is a fake
Van Allen radiation belt would have turned Neil Armstrong into a human grilled cheese sandwich. The shadows in the moon photos are mathematically impossible. Moon landing was faked.

Humans could not have crossed the van-allen belt radiation and survived. Moon landing was faked in a govt lab. The photos are not real. A data science course should emphasize that moon landing was fake - otherwise it is dishonest. If the course does not mention this, avoid it at all costs.
The moon landing was a fake orchestrated  by the government. Engineers are  guilty of promoting the govt narrative that moon landing happened.  Humans have never walked on the moon.
He might be fine as an instructor but is super toxic as a PhD guide. Don't do any research stuff with him. You will regret it.

Prof K is the man! The first part of the course is tough - but once you get used to the style - proofs, quizzes, assignments, things click nicely. His lectures are amazing. Plus he is a really nice guy. I learned a lot too.

This is one of the best courses I have taken at Cornell.  The professor is great and super enthusiastic. I love the lectures and the office hours were very helpful. Really good TAs too. I am seriously considering switching to an ECE major.
Professor Krishnamurthy is the most caring and sincere professor I have had at Cornell. Homeworks were difficult, but his lectures were awesome. Loved his class!!
nicest professor I have ever had
Very kind and went out of his way to help students

I did this class in spring 2024 and enjoyed it a lot. He is a very smart guy, and he is very caring too. ECE 2720 is offered both semesters but the instructor changes each semester. One of the best ENGRD courses I have done

The best sophomore class at Cornell I have taken. Prof Krishnamurthy is inspirational in the class. He went out of the way to  explain the assignments in the office hours. I strongly recommend this course as an ENGRD requirement. The assignments are difficult and time consuming but u learn a lot. Prof. K really knows his stuff.
Ok instructor. Bad PhD guide

Professor Krishnamurthy is #1 professor. I really liked the way he structured the class and focussed on what you learn rather than cramming for the quiz. He would stop by in TA's office hours and would ask every student if they have any questions/concerns which is super nice of him! He is genuinely the best professor I have ever met :)

Very caring and understanding professor! He actively takes feedback from students and is generous about giving extensions. Assignments were difficult but interesting and engaging. Final was take home, and he deliberately tries to maximize grades. So long as you hold up your end of the deal and stay engasged, he'll take care of you.

One of the best classes and one of the most caring professors out there. He took constant feedback from students and implemented it in the classroom. Grade determined only using in-class quizzes and a take-home final. Definitely recommend taking the class with him!

This is an excellent and mathematically heavily rigorous course. I am a CS-major.  I did not know that linear algebra had so many real world applications. This guy seriously rocks, he is very approachable. The assignment take a lot of time. But no prelims :)  One of the best courses at Cornell by far. Final exam will be take-home.

(FA2021) He spent a lot of time on how very basic calculus he would re-teach (a course prerequisite) is graduate material and that the course material was invaluable for interviews. He taught algorithms by putting code onto slides in one giant PDF. Generous grader, but stressful since you need to rely on Google to complete homework.

Vikram is an amazing prof if you are looking for a mentor who pushes research like a company CEO and is into publishing paper like a product. He extensively manages his students keeping track how much time they spent on research etc and if the money is being utilised well for the time you spend on research. If you like that style, you will love him

Best ECE professor at Cornell. I learnt a lot in the course, lots of cool math with real life applications. The professor was super enthusiastic and accessible. His lectures were fantastic. Homeworks were hard, but very useful for understanding material.

I took ECE 2720 with Professor Krishnamurthy in Fall 2021. It's definitely one of the best classes that I've taken at Cornell. The course introduces students to numerous topics in data science and ML. Homework assignments were always challenging but fruitful. Exams were very similar to practice exams.

Professor Krishnamurthy made sure to give insight on data science and statistics throughout lectures, often explaining how this type of material may come up during tech interviews. Problem sets focused heavily on Python, often making it tedious, but he made sure to give good practice problems/expectations for the midterm and final.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: He genuinely wants his students to do well. He is really passionate about what he does. He cares more about us understanding the content than getting the exact right answers and his homework and exams are graded accordingly. He actively asks for feedback and follows through on it. A really sweet and understanding guy who cares about his students:)

Professor Krishnamurthy is extremely intelligent and passionate about the material he teaches. He is very easy to contact outside of class and would respond to my emails about the class almost within an hour. In addition, he took measures listen to our feedback by giving homework drops and notes on slides. Overall a great professor!

His ECE 2720 is the most versatile, rigorous course I have taken. Feels like a proper 3000 level course. Covers an insane variety of very useful topics. Everyone should take this course. Although the instructor for this course changes every semester.

Super chill, very accessible and encouraging. A very intelligent professor who knows his stuff. His lectures are math-focused and he covers a lot of materials, and he gives a quiz every lecture (but they are pretty easy). But no prelims so what you get out of the course is entirely dependent on your own interests and initiatives.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Don't listen to the past reviews!! She has definitely taken feedback to make her lectures more organized and is a very kind teacher :) Give her a chance!

She is a bit disorganized in her lectures and it can be hard to follow. Her tests mainly surround the slideshows but I would use the textbook as extra information. She does test you on the location of parts of the brain that can be challenging. It is possible to do well. You would just have to do most of the learning on your own and extra credit!

Awful organization of lectures, topics just jump all around the place with no defined progression
Too many small details about the brain that don't make sense together
Tests are too hard, you have to memorize everything even though she says you don't necessarily need to 
DO NOT RECOMMEND
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Very nice person who seems to care, but a horrible lecturer. Her lectures are just empty words, cannot answer questions, and an unorganized method to the order of topics. She means well, but I can't say I learned much at all.

She makes it nearly impossible to succeed in her class... even though the lowest prelim grade gets dropped, her lectures don't correlate well to tests and often don't make sense. She is also terrible at answering students' questions in class and even if you attend office hours.

Khenas lectures may be a little dry, but she is knowledgeable about the material and exams are straightforward, mostly from lecture. She genuinely cares about our wellbeing - removed short answer questions from the exams to make it easier for us. 4 prelims (+ optional final) was a little too much, but it made studying for each exam less stressful.

Professor Swallow seems like a genuinely nice person, but unfortunately is such a bad professor. It is so hard to understand, very long and monotonous lectures, and the class is entirely disorganized. Tests have questions that are never mentioned in class or the readings. Overall: DO NOT TAKE.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Professor Swallow is passionate and wants students to succeed. Her tests were pretty straight forward and easy. Unfortunately, she is a bit disorganized which was frustrating and getting back test grades took weeks, despite having many TAs.

2 prelims and a final, drop lowest one. Some tricky questions, but mostly from power points. Final is cumulative. Three homework assignments, some parts can be tricky but if you go to TA office hours they will help and book will help. Only need to go to class for clicker points. Find book online. Readings help clarify material

Honestly had no idea what was going on in lectures most of the time. They were barely relevant and pretty poorly executed. Course material was very interesting though, and tests are relatively easy to study for since she posts slides online to look over (you'll get more looking at them yourself with the textbook than going to lecture).
Dont take her classes ever. Very dull and dry lectures. Her TAs did a better job lecturing and getting the class engaged.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Graded by few things--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Overall, I enjoyed this class. Cognitive science can get deep and technical rather quickly, bleeding into computer science, philosophy, and linguistics. So, developing and teaching an introductory-level cognitive science course is no trivial matter, but I believe that Professor Swallow and her staff did a great job.

The course itself is interesting if not for the professor. She is extremely unprofessional and not careful with grading. Warning: go see your test after you take it there will be errors. This year a "computing" error dropped everyone's grades a week later because she couldn't be bothered to grade the tests right the first time.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Probably the most boring professor I have ever listened to. Complete and total waste of 27.5 hours of my life that I would do anything to get back. First lecture she mentions how taking notes by hand is more useful but she doesn't even give enough time to type half of the slide down. Asks what you think, talks about everyone's answer and goes on.
