This teacher is the absolute worst. She is so boring and just lectures about why the human brain is a computer. Unless you are CS and interested in psych, you will find this class boring. The material is interesting but the teacher makes it boring. DON'T TAKE THIS CLASS
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Lectures are extremely dry, and you cannot skip due to mandatory GRADED iClicker questions. Majority of lectures are disorganized and she often comes unprepared for class.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Horrible class with busy work and a horrible, disorganized professor
Very dry professor with a very disorganized course

*** Textbook may be needed for those select few clicker quiz questions*** Material could be much more interesting if presented in a different way, but overall she's a pretty good lecturer. Participation plays a heavy role because every lecture, we have graded iclicker questions based on the previous lecture's material/reading. Memorization mostly.
Super boring lectures which you are unfortunately required to attend. Easy class with minimal work.
Hardest class I've taken, may not even pass.
The class is an easy A if you go to class and actually study the lecture slides (you really don't need to purchase the book).

Swallow's class has potential to be much more interesting than it actually is; she's passionate about the material but lacks any enthusiasm in teaching resulting in boring lectures. Class is very easy though since 1 prelim gets dropped completely, and you can make up participation on piazza. Overall recommend.

Leah teaches college students like an elementary school teacher. She will wait till everyone stops talking, spends most of the lecture talking about inclusivity, and rushes through the overall material. Avoid her at all costs.

My first time sleeping in lecture after 20 minutes. She is not always organized and the quality of presentation is sometimes unprepared.
Be ready to self study or go to ask TAs.
Lectures were really disorganized, hard to follow, and unprepared. Exams were mostly easy enough to compensate. Homeworks took a lot of work and didn't really feel that beneficial for learning.

You can tell she's really trying her best, but her lectures are harder to follow and feel less content-dense compared to Professor Eichhorn. Went on tangents sometimes about non-related course material when there wasn't even enough time allotted to cover the actual course content. Hope she's able to hit her stride and shoutout Xi'an street food!

Great lectures and great lecture notes. Really supportive professor who is very willing to help outside of class. Provides practice prelims very similar to real ones. However, homework assignments are long and very harshly graded.

I felt like whenever she did lectures instead of Matt I always would have to review notes since the lectures were harder to follow. Although she was very engaging, I felt like she tried to hard to bring it outside topics into the course that made us run out of time to focus on what were expected to actually learn

Prof Shuster is patient if you don't understand a topic. He will take the time to thoroughly review theory concepts. In my experience, if you really want to understand music theory, both 2101 and 2102 are essentials at Cornell. My musicianship has greatly improved since taking these classes. Puts the fun in fundamentals in my personal opinion.

Not to invalidate anyone else's experience, but I personally didn't see any of the concerning behaviors reported in the other reviews. He seemed like just a well-intentioned guy, perhaps with a strange fascination on Chinese martial arts (and some concerning YouTube recommended videos). Lectures were a bit unclear at times, but grading was lenient.

Extremely disrespectful - especially towards women. Made a joke that a student who was majoring in Philosophy and Music had parents who were disappointed by her. She cried. Passive aggressive in email as well as in person.

prof shuster loves to cold call and make targeted and passive aggressive comments when students are struggling with the material. he seems to insist it's all very easy even though it is in fact quite difficult at times and he doesn't really explain most of the material. he's also very disorganized and made some weird comments about autism and women

This class was incredibly disorganized (no syllabus) and fast-paced. Students weren't given adequate materials to be successful; there are no class notes, instead he writes on a chalkboard and cold calls with questions about information that has not yet been taught. The professor picks on students who do and don't know the answer.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I'm so traumatized by this professor that I've come back ten years later to share my experience. In fall 2016, I was extremely depressed, and she took that personally. She would often call me into her office and berate me like she was my mother. I always left sobbing. I wish I could go back and stand up for myself. It's just a class.

Someone else said this and I agree (took last semester): "A very eccentric professor, wants the class to be free and open but grades harshly when you do not specifically respond to the ridiculous questions that occur to her when reading your writing. I'm not sure how I feel about her she's okay but I would not recommend taking a class with her."
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Cecilia is a very odd instructor and it was obvious that very few in our class trusted her. She does the bare minimum and never provides any feedback but gets upset if you ask about grades. Quite obvious to everyone that she just wants to come to class without being prepared for the lecture. I'd rate her teaching skills as very poor.

A very eccentric professor, wants the class to be free and open but grades harshly when you do not specifically respond to the ridiculous questions that occur to her when reading your writing. I'm not sure how I feel about her she's okay but I would not recommend taking a class with her. Also she never gave grades so my final grade was a surprise.

One of my favorite teachers/professors ever! It felt less like a formal student-teacher relationship, and more like a relationship of mutual respect. She's happy to meet with you for office hours, which you might need, since she's picky about thesis. She is very eccentric, but it creates an incredibly lively and entertaining in-class environment.

I will agree that she is very scatterbrained, but she keeps class very entertaining and is passionate about what she teaches. Does say some very odd comments but she was not racist in MY class (I have seen some comments saying she was in theirs, which I don't doubt). Lots and lots of work, but easy grading. Brought homemade bread on last day!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--So many papers

reviews.comment: No African American would ever claim another African person's lived experience with racial microagressions to be untruthful. This is all I will say about the comment below.

She is chaotic but her energy is infectious and if you put in effort she responds really well in her grading. 

I had no problem as an African American student. I obviously am concerned by the comments on this site but I asked other former students about their feelings and they were all super positive.
Why did this site remove the comment where i said she asked me why I, as an African, looked and acted differently from the other African she had met???
Avoid at all costs. Extremely disorganized and if you are a student color, prepare to be intimidated.

I agree with past comments that she's all over the place. Her class is literally a chaotic mess. Also, shes had a few "problematic" comments to say about peope of color. I dont recommend taking any class with her.

Like some of the other comments mentioned, she is a bit eccentric and her instructions are often unclear. However, the class was a genuinely enriching experience and fostered an intimate, introspective environment. She can be intimidating and does call on people at random, but sincerely cares about students. My Spanish noticeably improved.

Genuinely cares about students and takes time to meet with them outside of class. Passionate about her subject. Somewhat disorganized and gives instructions that are often confusing. Can be hard to figure out what she expects from students on the assignments.

Great professor! She is kooky in a good way. Her class is structured like an AP English class but with Spanish literature and of course only using Spanish! There's a lot of reading and a lot of writing. But that's about it---no tests! She's very easy to plan a meeting with and will give you feedback. She also gives a lot of extra credit opportunit
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: She's nice and flexible about deadlines, and her grading isn't bad. The papers themselves are short and easy, but there is a lot of reading for a "writing workshop." Her instructions are often confusing/unclear, and it feels like she imposes her own perspective of the readings onto us, so any conclusions we make about them are heavily guided.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Caring

reviews.comment: She's really wacky and amazing. I love her. She does grade a lot of papers like you'll get homework every day but it's super easy to follow and she offers lots of extra credit.

This lady is bomb. Many people can't handle the class and thats understandable but if you are ready to be challenged and learn Cecelia is the ideal prof. She makes you work for your grade but the classes she teachers are wildly different from the rest at Cornell and it's a very fun break from the norm

I am seeing a lot of negative comments below, but Cecilia Lawless is one of the best professors at Cornell. She definitely pushed me beyond what I thought my limit was and this class perfected my Spanish writing. Yes, perhaps she would forget some deadlines, but as long as you check your email, all of her instructions are there. She is the best.

She's amazing teacher. I was quite competent and spanish and still needed a tutor for her essays from time to time. I ended up with an A in the class (i was actually aiming for an A+ since i went to soooo many extra credits) so I am happy. She is incredibly sweet, interesting, and fascinating!!!! I was forced to take the class b/c of the rquirement
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: My Spanish really improved in Cecilia's class.  For introductory level courses, I'd imagine she's a bit overwhelming, but if you're a Spanish Major/Minor, you already have a passion for the language and would enjoy her class.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: The class can be a little unorganized and homework-heavy, but she's very approachable and definitely cares about you if you show you're trying. A

Cecilia cares most about the quality of effort you put into the class. The essays are not too long, and she makes sure your writing and speaking improves over the semester. The reading material, discussions, and assignments are interesting and relevant. If you participate, don't miss class, and care, you will do well. Fair grader. Highly respected.

Absolutely incredible. She definitely makes you work for the class, but she often allows late work, gives extra credit, and gives meaningful feedback. Plus, she's always enthusiastic about class discussion, she'll genuinely care about you (if you put effort in), and her assignments are very open and thought-provoking. I wish she taught above 2180!!

I absolutely loved having Cecilia as a professor.  She is quite scatterbrained but she is so passionate about what she is teaching and she really does care about students.  My writing improved incredibly this semester, not just in Spanish but in English too.  I'm hoping to take a class of hers next semester, too.
Prof. Lawless changed my life; I will never forget her.

No, she doesn't know what time it is and yes, she is eccentric, but Cecilia is a passionate, articulate professor and I will be forever grateful I signed up for a class with her. She cares personally about students and is incredibly encouraging if you put in the work. Take the time to go to her office hours, even if it's just to chat!

she's an amazingly passionate teacher who really knows her stuff. sure she's disorganized but the assignments are super open ended so you can literally write about anything. she's a pretty interesting lady who will genuinely care about you and how much you learn. EASY EASY EASY GRADER. if you show genuine interest you'll get at least an A

She takes a bit of getting used to. She is a bit disorganized and never knows what time it is, and her assignment directions can be a bit confusing. However, she is very goodhearted and genuinely tries to help students if they tell her they are having trouble. She is passionate about Spanish and indigenous peoples. Class helped my Spanish so much!!

She is a little bit "loca."  Often arrives to class in a fluster and is somewhat disorganized.  Sometimes her assignment directions make no sense.  However, she is a thoughtful professor who is willing to extend deadlines if needed.  She also cares deeply about her students and is always willing to help.  She's worth putting up with.

Cecilia is a professor for people who really care about spanish. She knows who's serious and who's just filling a requirement. I've taken several of her classes. She's a bit confusing in terms of assignments and her style is a bit peculiar, but if you want to have legitimate skill and experience w/ the Spanish language, and you work hard, shes 4 u

Horribly disorganized & unable to properly communicate. Gets angry with class when we can't figure out what's supposed to be happening. Assignments are somewhat bogus & a lot a of work. Discussion is repetitive & focuses on irrelevant details which we are expected to find & understand.   I love the Spanish language but can't stand this class.
I would not take another class with her even if I had to switch to French.

My Spanish improved by leaps and bounds in this class; my writing is now more or less automatic, and my speaking is more fluid.  Of the 2 professors teaching this course, Sra. Lawless the more laid-back one.  All assignments and exams are written, and the focus is more on analyzing Spanish lit. and films than filling out grammar worksheets.

I really liked Spanish before I took this class, and she's not horrible or anything, but I just didn't feel I learned very much.  She seems disorganized and though she starts out with a syllabus, it quickly becomes modified-again and again. We didn't use text books so much--more in class discussion.  Homework regularly
Pretty bad, avoid at all costs.
Ridiculous essays. A bunch of psychobable and pseudo-philosophy. Horrid accent.

At first she may seem a little bit distant or above her students. But if you do the readings and truly participate she absolutely has a lot to offer, though her arguments can be complex sometimes, especially if you have not done the readings!

A really enthusiastic educator....watch out for her distinctive smell, you'll start to notice that all your Spanish notebooks smell like her. She's not good with technology or emails so make sure that you get on her class list serve.
You have to try in her class, as long as you do, you will be ok. A's are rare in her class, but it's pretty easy to get B's. Just go to class and participate every now and then.

Though not an easy class, Cecilia tries very hard to get you interested. Some students may say that she is tough, but how else are you going to learn Spanish? Overall, I really like her teaching style.
No Comments

I REALLY liked Cecilia. After my first spanish instructor left on pregnancy leave with only 4 weeks left in class, Cecilia came in and blew us all away. Thought extremely eccentric, she is a great teacher. She really pushes you to speak spanish which only helps you. She obviously cares about making you learn and not just get though the class.
An excellent teacher who instills analytical strategies into her students while challenging them to develop their own unique voice to nurture.
Horrible teacher!  Unorganized and even ****.  I would not advise anyone to take her EVER!  It's a shame she is still teaching.
HORRIBLE, HORRIBLE, HORRIBLE!
Very unorganized(ie never came to class with a watch, never even brought pens!). During office hours she does not help at all, and only seems to want to leave.
Very kind professor.  Extremely helpful if you approach her.

The GOAT of all Gen Chem Classes. Lancaster was funny, and understood how much students typically dislike the subject, so he tried to make it as enjoyable as possible. His exams were tough, but his final compensated for that heavily. Would take his class again just to experience his in-class demonstrations for the first time. Definitely tenured.

If there is one class I could retake at Cornell, it is CHEM 2070: General Chemistry I with Dr. Lancaster. Not only is he hilarious and a chemistry legend, but he also has deep respect for his students. He actually wants to know his students and help them achieve their goals. I couldn't have had a better intro to Cornell and chemistry thanks to him.

My favorite professor that I've had at Cornell so far! He was very clear on how to succeed in his class. Office hours were always helpful just to get a greater understanding of the material. As long as you did the practice problem sets he posted and the practice prelims you should do well.

Lancaster is the goat fr. He is an amazing lecturer, and incorporates just enough conceptual explanation and interesting details to keep the otherwise fairly dull gen chem course content interesting. However, he still finds the time to do examples which are helpful. He definitely has the most joie de vivre of all the chem profs. love love love him

He is one of the better general chem professors. He is genuinely hilarious and if it were not for that factor I would not have shown up to class. Class felt entirely useless as his style of lecturing is fast paced and focuses on the theory behind chemical concepts rather than how to solve problems. The book does a much better job. curve is a B-
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: A very hard class and the way to get a good grade is to study and redo all the exam practice problems that he makes and provides for the class along with the practice prelims. I can tell Dr. Lancaster is a smart person but sometimes it is difficult to understand what he is saying because he says it so fast or assumes we know what he's talking about
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I have nothing against Professor Lancaster, he's genuinely a good guy and evidently very knowledgeable and passionate about chemistry. That being said, he isn't the best lecturer and has a tendency to present too much information too quickly. Beware of the lab reports as they are graded very strictly.

Kyle Lancaster obviously enjoys his job and does his best to share his love for chemistry with his students. The material is difficult and it is important to show up to lecture and do as many practice problems as possible. Take advantage of the resources available, like chem 1008, LSC tutoring hours, and lab office hours.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Dr. Lancaster is an amazing professor. He is clearly very passionate about chemistry and wants others to feel that same passion. He brought energy and some fun to lectures and is a great guy. Lectures are pretty dense but I found the textbook helped a lot. He's also incredibly available during office hours and I found that time extremely helpful.

Dr. Lancaster cares about his students and wants you to succeed. Gen Chem at Cornell will always be one of the biggest "weed-out" classes, but Lancaster makes it interesting and more manageable. Don't skip lecture... 90% of them help you stay up to date and on topic with the class. If you have Dr. L for 2070, consider yourself lucky.

2070 overall is just a grind but it was a decent time. The class is very hard if you have no chemistry experience but that just comes with the class. Prepare to spend a lot of time outside of class understanding concepts. Lancaster himself is a nice guy and is clearly passionate about what he does. Cracks some fun jokes in lecture but is a grind

Very energetic lectures, though are hard to follow if you haven't read the textbook beforehand. However lectures are not always super relevant to the knowledge you really need for HW/prelims, rely on psets for practice w that. Prelim problems are monstrous but not completely unfair, do lots of practice prelims to prepare.

Lancaster is very passionate about chemistry, and hes a great guy. But, lectures do move really fast. DO NOT miss a lecture, you will be chronically behind and very stressed. He also sometimes rambles about info that isn't necessary. The tests are designed to be impossible, but don't worry the curve will save you, as it did me.

Very kind, very passionate, but not very good at ensuring understanding of those new to the concepts he teaches (most students). Lectures move VERY fast, and he sometimes dances around topics to teach things that aren't relevant which can collude the actual content being tested on. However, it's clear he's just passionate about teaching.

dont give this guy celsius and flammable fluids. a friend and I found it hilarious that he plays Rust (I'd love to play w/ him). 
He's obviously very knowledgable not only in chem but bio and as many premeds are in this class, it's very useful. His "this is not on exam" lectures are the best. 

Btw, this is my first in-person chem class ever. pain.
PAIN PAIN PAIN PAIN PAIN PAIN PAIN

Dr. Lancaster is a great professor, and his passion for chemistry is evident. He is very engaging and entertaining, and his lectures are easy to follow as well. His exams are very fair, and he provides all the resources for every student to succeed. The labs for the course are boring and annoying, but they are only 20% of the grade.

Dr. Lancaster is a really engaging and entertaining lecturer but lectures could be confusing at times. Labs really depend on what TA you get so hope you get a good one. Exams were very fair and he genuinely wants everyone to do good in the class. Overall, a good professor just a difficult class.

Prof. Lancaster is the best chemistry professor in the department and the best I've had at Cornell. Lecture is pretty much mandatory since the class moves fast and if you miss a class, it's impossible to catch up. Office hours & undergrad study sessions were also super helpful, and if you understand the homework thoroughly, you will get an A.

This class covers incredibly difficult content but there are a lot of resources available. OH not mandatory but I don't think I would've passed the class without attending every week. Lectures move incredibly quickly so be ready - I don't recommend ever skipping lecture as not a lot of materials are posted online, it would be hard to catch up
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Test heavy

reviews.comment: Although his lectures were great and explained the matter in a clear way, he can be dismissive of questions in office hours. There were however a lot of TAs that could also be used, so you just have to find one you like. The labs were slow and annoying.

He's very knowledgeable and funny at times. However, grading is tough and those PSETs are brutal. While lectures and office hours are technically not mandatory, they are almost pseudo-mandatory because he doesn't really post everything he teaches and PSETs are borderline impossible without them.

He was very understanding of the struggle of this year for students and curved the class to a B+ instead of a B for us. Tried to make zoom as fun as he could and dressed up with his TA every so often. Material is pretty standard chem matieral but was a lot of material bc gen chem. Taught the material pretty well too.
You have to work for a good grade
Enthusiastic about material, but he teaches really fast and his test questions are lengthy and require quick critical thinking... definitely recommend taking 1070 if it is available.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Kyle is a funny man, but gives off an aura commanding respect. Clearly very intelligent, and likes to throw in memes and references to lectures. Course is very structured and rigid, in both grading and material. You *can* skip class if need be, but material makes much more sense in lecture than in book. Labs suck, but not terrible. Beware prelims!!

Good professor who really tries to keep students engaged in class but moves very quickly in lecture and it's hard to keep up/learn the material from lecture alone. Class/tests are graded fairly. Class takes a lot of time and has a lot of work.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: You can tell he loves teaching chemistry to students and tries to make lecture as entertaining as possible. He always tries to joke around during lecture and actually wants you to learn the material, rather than memorize it. His office hours are great too and he really takes the time to teach you a concept you're really confused about.

Professor Lancaster is not only an engaging lecturer, but he is also incredibly knowledgeable and is truly concerned with helping students find an interest in chemistry. His jokes are genuinely funny and lighten up the lectures on days when the topic feels like its over your head. I would definitely recommend this class for a rewarding challenge.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Hilarious--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: He's the best. He loves to blow things up and is the best and most entertaining teacher! His explosive personality really makes the class interesting and bearable. 15/10 would recommend.
Tried to be funny/entertaining
funny guy, but hard to keep up with his lectures. If you have some chemistry background you should be fine for the first half of the course. no practice prelims :(
Dr. Lancaster is absolutely incredible. Great lecturer. Easy to learn from. Reasonable prelims and labs. Class was moderately difficult, easy A if you put in the work and time.

Dr. Lancaster is, hands down, one of the best professors you'll ever have in any Chemistry class at Cornell. He is always clear and explains complicated concepts in a simple way while managing to entertain the class at the same time. Always willing to help students in office hours and was a DJ at one point in time.

Brilliant professor who plans his classes amazingly. Everything in his lecture is important and the textbook is only helpful for practice problems. The best way to study is to do the book's practice problems right before the exam and review notes. Amazing guy.

Kyle's brilliant, and has the ability to make difficult concepts seem clearer; the times he had a sub because he was off giving lectures elsewhere, everyone just wanted him back. His test questions aren't that difficult, but sometimes, there's just too many. The curve will then either save or ruin you. Definitely recommend 1007 along with this.

Although I did not enjoy Dr. Lancaster's course due to my dislike for chemistry, his enthusiasm for his field and his ability to explain concepts made the class bearable. He's a great professor, and although his class may be tough, if you work you can learn.

Lancaster is truly amazed by chemistry and wants each of his students to feel that same passion. He's a clear lecturer and hilarious. His test are fair but challenging, and make for good opportunity to score above the mean. Take CHEM1070 & you won't regret it (will definitely help your grade). Would definitely take another class taught by him.
reviews.rating_tags: Tests? Not many--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Dr. Lancaster truly cares about his students and wants them to care about chemistry. Tests are difficult but fair. In lecture, he is clear in his explanations and writes important notes on the board. I recommend going to office hours to get help (not just before tests). He is inspirational and can make complex concepts of chemistry fun. Great guy.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Inspirational--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Such a funny, passionate, and overall great professor! He genuinely cares about his students and wants them to succeed and enjoy chemistry. If you've taken chemistry before, this class shouldn't be that bad for you, but you will definitely have to put in the work.

Dr. Lancaster is an amazing professor who makes fair tests and really makes a subject I thought was boring much, much more interesting. I didn't take AP Chemistry but would recommend taking CHEM1070 in addition to this class to help keep up with the material.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Kyle is an INCREDIBLE professor-- hilarious, dynamic, and brilliant all around. I went into the class with really weak foundations in chemistry, but Kyle is such a clear explainer and has such an effective teaching style (writing all essential notes on the blackboard) that it's manageable to keep up and do well if you put in the time.

Lancaster was a great lecturer! His tests were completely fair, and if you took AP chemistry in high school and understood it, then you'll do pretty well in his class. Can't speak for those who didn't.

Great professor: teaches well, knows what he's talking about, personable guy. Go to office hours at least once to get to know him (and perhaps more than once because it's not an easy class!). You should go to class because he teaches the material well and it's hard to get it from the book. Speaking of the book, no need to buy anything but Sapling.
Great teacher. Talks so fast and super easy to get behind.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Even though attendance isn't mandatory, you really won't be able to get the material anywhere else. He's pretty young (33?) so he still has enthusiasm for the subject and he's able to convey that enthusiasm in lectures. While some of the material is hard (prelim 2 average was 89/150), he knows it's hard and curves the class accordingly.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--Tests are tough

reviews.comment: Lancaster is an all around great guy, in and out of the classroom. You can definitely tell that he genuinely wants his students to be interested in chemistry. However, just because he wants everyone to love chemistry as much as he does, does not mean that the class is easy. The average grade on prelim 2 was around a 55% so don't expect an easy A.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Caring

reviews.comment: Exams are difficult but plenty of help is available if you are willing to take it. Lancaster keeps lectures interesting for the most part and brings humor to what can be a more dull subject.

Prof Lancaster definitely expects a lot, but gives a lot too. Chemistry has never been my strongest subject but he made me love it and was always available for extra help. His lectures are hilarious and he knows how to relate to students. Definitely a gem.
Probably one of the best professors that you will ever have at Cornell. He makes difficult topics so easy to understand!
Lecture was actually enjoyable with Lancaster. Made chemistry accessible. Also in Forbes Top 30 under 30, so cool guy.
Really great teacher. You can tell he actually cares about and enjoys teaching. Exams are tough but fair.

Professor Lancaster is simply brilliant. He is one of those professors that truly and absolutely has a passion for his subject and has a blast teaching it. Students look forward to his lectures and truly cares about them, always accessible to ask questions. I would recommend anyone reading this to take a course taught by him. You won't regret it

Super knowledgable guy, very engaging lectures. Homework assignments are long and challenging but very well-written and overall enjoyable. Available both during stated office hours and at other times, and by email.

He is the bottom of the barrel in every sense of the phrase. Material is based solely on his own beliefs and never presents counterarguments. When students question the material, he belittles them, even though he is the one who's definitively demented. Rude and pretentious. Blatantly sexist. Complete disgrace to the Hotel School. Do not enroll.
do not take. will ruin your Cornell experience
I have never had a professor that hates his students this much. He is extremely stubborn and will not help you when you need. Worst professor I've ever had.

Avoid if you can. The lectures are hyper-specific, obscure, and contradictory. The only way to prep for the exams - memorize (despite what the prof says).

The prof himself is very rude to students. I don't understand why be a prof if you don't like or know how to teach.

Save your tuition money.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Worst professor at Cornell by far. 1) teaches outdated material 2) entire class is graded on t/f questions 3) insults the class for getting questions wrong 4) extremely rude and racist to students. DO NOT TAKE!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: He needs to go to anger management
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: (2)- attendance completely mandatory with I-Clicker grades for ACCURACY in the first minute of class. Offers no exceptions to missing class you just get a 0 which heavily effects your grade, even if you have legit doctors note or real conflict. Nothing he teaches is applicable to 2025 and argues with students if they ask questions. AVOID.

Despite success in the class, worst professor I have had at Cornell. Exams are all T/F about random and obscure concept from lecture/book. Is very rude and makes inappropriate comments in class about race / gender. Contradictory. rude when students reach out and rude when score low or students are confused. Outdated concepts. Offers 0 assistance.

DO NOT TAKE THIS COURSE! Grades rely heavily on confusing iClicker quizzes starting at the first minute of class, often tricky with wording. The professor changes the schedule arbitrarily — he switched the final from online to in-person at the last minute, disrupting students' travel plans.

A lot of the prelims questions have nothing to do with what he talked about in class and he chose the most random thing ever from the book to quiz. IT's also all true and false and doesn't release the answers of iclicker and previous exams. Also no electronic devices allowed.

Please DO NOT TAKE ANY CLASS TAUGHT BY MIKE LYNN. Attendance is mandatory, based on ACCURACY iclicker at the moment the class starts. All of his slides are OUTDATED by 20-30 years (he provides sources, most are wikipedia). All of his exams are True/False, with subjective answers. I studied for 8+ hrs and I didn't even recognize HALF of the exam!!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I wouldnt wish this on my worst enemy. Never take a class with professor lynn, he is the worst teacher I have ever had in my entire life and marketing is going to be my worst grade at Cornell. he only grades you based off of true or false exams that are all about over 10 year old data so you are bound to fail. never take this class with him.

Do not take Lynn's class. Confusing and cluttered lectures with WAY too much specific content that contradicts other things he says. Exams are true/false which is the only redeeming quality. Daily I-clicker quizzes, NO technology whatsoever, required textbook readings.

Changed a grade worth 25% of final grade for "finding a mistake in calculating" despite posting the grade already??? also admitted to saying incorrect information during lecture but still expects the class to know it
lecture videos and content are all prehistoric and outdated. often argues with students who ask questions...avoid avoid avoid

(pt. 3) Also iclicker quizzes are done within the first minute of class and if you're even 2 minutes late one day due to any extenuating circumstances he won't let you make up the quiz and you get a zero. Also no technology in class so you have to handwrite notes. Goes out of his way to be mean and aggressive towards students for no reason.

(pt 1) DO NOT TAKE HIS CLASS. He's incredibly rude to students for simply asking him to reexplain things, makes sexist/inappropriate comments, and is the most boring lecturer. I'm practically falling asleep in every lecture and half the time he doesn't even teach he just plays long videos and expects us to memorize the information from them.

Plays videos and reads off of powerpoint slides the entire class. Expects everyone to remember the content of the videos despite saying "do not memorize" for exams. Insults the class for getting a t/f question wrong for no reason

every exam is entirely true/false with statements that have one word altered. makes racist comments in class, highly inappropriate examples. major ego and often yells back at students for asking questions. says not to memorize material but has questions on literally remembering random statistics from the textbook

First of all, this guy shouldn't be here at Cornell, but stay in his medieval times generation. Lectures="good old days info".The videos in class are older than us.Major takeaway-never become a marketing prof after psych major.his confidence level is higher than the accuracy of his class. He can continue his google scholar research papers instead.
Makes inappropriate comments in his lectures. Lecture participation is graded based on impossibly hard iclicker quizzes.

Doesn't allow electronic devices in his class, similar to a high school classroom. Reads off a PowerPoint for the whole class, similar to a high school class. Professor Lynn may be an expert in hospitality marketing but in my opinion is not a great college educator.

All exams are True/False and are very subjective and up to professor's interpretation. Not interested in returning points even if you prove him wrong, "that's what the curve is for". All lecture materials are old and half the time he just plays videos instead of teaching. Avoid at all costs.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment:
Your grade is basically the average of prelims and iClicker attendance quizzes (which are still very hard - so even if you go to every class you might still end with a 75-85% on the "attendance")

Prelims are 70-90 "True or False" questions, let me give you an example: "T/F - The starting salary of a marketing assistant is $27,000" Obscene
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: His (true/false) exam questions are so subjective and have no definitive answer. Therefore, the exams are almost impossible and are curved very little....
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Worst professor at Cornell, uses old research, yells at students when asked questions, course material is contradictory, doesn't say hi when seen outside of class, did not deserve the grade that I got, unfair grading
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Everything he presents/teaches is from 20+ years ago. Nothing he teaches is remotely relevant anymore. Also, his massive ego is so insufferable.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Worst professor I have ever had. His canvas is blank and unhelpful; he has no TAs or mandatory I-Clicker exams in every single class (that count for 33% of your grade), and just uninteresting. After our first exam, he sent us all an email telling us how poorly we had done, in a very aggressive tone. Do everything you can to avoid this teacher...
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He is one of the worst professors I've had at Cornell. Everything people are saying here is true. He has a huge ego and goes out of his way to be condescending to students. He can't answer questions about things he hasn't prepared in class and if you email and ask him to clarify something, he refuses to do so, telling you to figure it out.

He's nice, but this is the worst class I've taken. I've not met one person who actually likes this class/him. He uses studies from the late 1900's and uses them as "proof" that all the modern marketing classes we've taken are wrong. He has a big ego and wants to be right with everything. His class is boring and uses videos instead of teaching.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Shows videos instead of teaching, and tells us it's time for him to rest, but not us. Would not post his slides prior to class, not allowing students to preview the course materials. Exams are entirely T/F questions. Very subjective, no review material, ONLY MEMORIZATION works. Don't provide answer to practice questions, expect us to guess answers.

Lynn is very defensive and somewhat aggressive towards students. He posts announcements with wording like "I have already told you, but here it is again" and "apparently, some of you interpreted my previous email as saying I do not want to meet with you. Not true." He borderline yells when you ask questions he doesn't have a concrete answer to.

Most research presented in lecture is from the 80s or 90s, and Prof. Lynn often resorts to showing the class videos instead of lecturing himself. Additionally, on the homework, Prof. Lynn expects groups to write the answers exactly how he wants and will severely punish groups who do not. Very tough grader and not very understanding.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment:
I'll describe Prof Lynn's class: "Here's your next assignment. It counts for 5% of your grade and you have eight of these. If you don't do every little part of it EXACTLY how I want you get an F. And I won't tell you how I want you to do it. Figure it out"

He does not care about his students and gives very tough grades.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: He's very passionate about Marketing but the course is outdated and textbook and lecture heavy. The course content is supposed to be very easy but he has the most vague wordings, making the exams and the project unnecessarily confusing. Grading's also very harsh and the curves make no sense, I think the class average for final exam was in 70s.

Lynn is a very sweet man but he is very outdated. He often shows videos during class from late 90s and early 2000s. If you ask him for clarification on anything you likely won't get it. Exams are T/F and matching, which is much harder than you'd think. He barely has any other grades so if you do poorly on an exam you won't do well overall. Mediocre
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: he doesnt even know how to organize his Blackboard website and slides... man has to learn his alphabet and simple math if he plans on still teaching here.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: It seems like he has been living under a rock for the last decade because he has no idea about technology or social media marketing. We watch videos from the 80s and pretend like theyre still relevant. Doesnt know simple math. No point in going to lecture except for iclicker because he just reads verbatim off his Comic Sans PowerPoint
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Prof. Lynn is a good guy and the class is pretty interesting. I would recommend to anyone who cares about learning things and who isn't there for a free A. The material is pretty straightforward, but the nature of his tests (TF, some matching) make it easy to do decent but hard to do really well. Go to class and you should have at least a BB+

Definitely my favorite class that I've taken at Cornell.  Don't know why there are some many negative reviews - he's a great professor and the material is extremely interesting.  I recommend it to everyone
He sucks. Lynn only cares about "tipping"! Did you know that he's an expert?!?! If you don't he will let you know.

A pretty good prof. Great material and lots of movie clips and demos etc that make the class very interesting. His tests involve quite a bit of memory-type questions that require reading of the book. Frankly not very good at fielding questions and not very helpful in person. Overall, I thought the class was great and he taught reasonably well.
very interesting lectures and material.  pop quizzes are easy as long as you go to class.

What a terrible teacher! The only reason this guy teaches is so he could do research on "tipping"... he is not interested in his students, he is not clear or fair at all, he is not very knowledgeable on the subject, and he is EXTREMELY BORING! almost no one came to class... what a waste of my hard-earned tuition dollars!
Interesting course material-- shame about the professor who manages to bore you to tears. Confused about concepts and has to correct himself through email. This class was NOT worth it whatsoever.

Fell asleep almost everysingle class. he confuses people on purpose. I WISH I NEVER TOOK THIS COURSE.  Bored to tears. Repeatedly asked myself, "What the hell are you talking about, this stuff is freaking obvious!"  Lynn has no real experience in the industry..he's only been "an assistant professor". go take a real class, like finance or yield mgmt
One of my favorite professors at Cornell.  If you are the least bit interested in Marketing you should definitely take this class.

He teaches pointless information in 9am lectures which is later covered during 3-hour, unskippable labs. Getting an A+ in his class is just about impossible, and lecture attendance can be done remotely(most days). When the course is helpful, it's not directly due to his teaching, but because of the lab TAs. His teaching needs more direction.

The class itself is quite trivial if you have any experience in biology, but an easy A. Sarvary's lectures are quite fun, just make sure to do the PollEv for attendance. The labs (and getting out of them early) heavily depend on the TA, but generally really straightforward and easy. Very flexible assignment schedule.

These other reviews must be lying because BIOG1500 is not a great course. It insists upon itself and is a tad bit pretentious. Nothing against the professor, it's just that the course is a lot of busy work and not very informative.

Mark was passionate about the class, but the class has a lot of busy work and seems kinda pointless. It's pretty easy, but unfortunately, the class is not formatted in a way that is very exciting or intellectually stimulating.

Sarvary is really nice and his lectures are chill and enjoyable, but ultimately the lab TAs are a lot more important in shaping your experience with the class. Grading is annoying, as an A+ is nearly impossible (99.00+), and my grad TA was really stingy with points. Sarvary is great and deadlines are flexible, but be ready for boring 3-hour labs.

The class really really serves no educational purpose whatsoever. The lectures are sometimes engaging but again there is no point in attending apart from answering polling questions. The labs are needlessly long and do not need to take up 3 hours.

He seems like a sweet person, but I truly didn't learn much from his lectures. The three-hour labs were really tedious and painful to sit through. Definitely an easy A class, but it's virtually impossible to get an A+ (the minimum grade is 99.00%)
Useless morning lectures, extremely boring lab sessions. I don't know why the lab is 3 hours long. Please make a test-out option for this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--So many papers

reviews.comment: Don't really learn much in lecture and things are mainly taught by the lab TA, but his lectures were easy to follow and he seems like a nice guy.
This class is horrible. Mandatory course for my major and it's just busy work and dry content that I already know. There needs to be a system to test out of this garbage.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: This course is required, but in the end I was glad I took it as I feel much more prepared for my summer internship and advanced courses next semester.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Extremely boring class and kind of useless. You think you'd learn real lab techniques but it's just simple things like micropipetting and how to use a microscope. He's a nice guy but the class is just boring to sit through. It is a requirement for any pre-health majors, but it's 2 credits and an easy A so just get through it.

Worst class ever. He is sneaky when it comes to justifying the material. This class is literally middle school level and so boring to sit through, even the lab TAs agree. His assignments are set up to confuse people and lower their grades to keep everyone from getting an A. DO NOT TAKE IT UNLESS YOU HAVE TO.

This is an extremely boring class. Grades mostly depend on your GTA, which is annoying if you get a bad one. The assignments are annoying and tedious and some are pointless. There's one exam, a research paper, and bunch of other trivial assignments like presentations and stats work. If you put in the bare minimum, you'll get an A or B+.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Extremely boring and excruciating class. Sarvary and the other professor did virtually nothing the entire semester. I got stuck with the worst GTA who graded as harsh as she could for good work. GTA was on a power play the entire time and used that to be threatening and harm students grade. Easy class but a horrible class structure

It's very easy to get an A. Professor Sarvary and his TAs take feedback very seriously. The class was interesting but a bit long. However my graduate TA let us leave earlier if we finished early so it wasn't that big of an issue for me. Overall I recommend the class and the assignments we did helped me in other lab settings.

It is an unnecessarily long class for the amount of content covered. You're expected to pay $80 for a lab manual that isn't used often. He's the author and advises against reusing old versions so you'll buy a new copy even though the maybe 5 useful pages could be on Canvas. You'll get a (not so) free A though.

Most boring class i have ever taken. Labs are 3 hours long, unnecessary, & unhelpful. The grading scheme is completely unfair. It's def an easy A, but an A+ is basically impossible because he increased the cutoff (99.000+). The only thing i like about this class is the extended deadlines without penalty. DO NOT take unless u have to.

Most boring three hours of my week were spent sitting in this lab. You don't even do labs, the class includes learning how to email professors about research opportunities, using R coding software, and reading scientific journals. The one lab that we were supposed to do ended up being cancelled and they gave us fake data to use instead. Easy A tho.

The labs are incredibly annoying, you have to sit there for all three hours, but all of it could be covered in an hour if they wanted to. He doesn't do much during the lectures each week, so it seems like he mostly is the class coordinator.

Most boring class I've ever taken. Grading scheme makes completely no sense - I forgot to add a picture of something to a single assignment and got a 100% on everything else and I got a B. Labs are uselessly long and the textbook is a scam.

Only take biog1500 if you have to, or if you want to do research. Otherwise, it is boring and useless. The lectures were all recorded videos which was nice, but the labs are three hours and we barely did anything Luckily there was not a ton of HW. Expensive textbook. I rarely saw Prof. Sarvary except for lecture vids and popping in and out of lab.

Labs are the most boring thing ever and always take 3 hours long. Class does teach some useful skills, but the required 70$ textbook is a scam!!! We barely use the textbook and they require it. Lectures were recorded and we only had to show up in person for one lecture. This class is an easy A if you do all the work but it's so annoying.

I took this course over the winter session and it was great! Mark is a wonderful teacher and really cares about his students. It is not a very difficult class but it is a great resume builder and a great introduction to scicomm, if you're interested. The zoom calls were long but we took breaks. Not bad at all for 3 credits in 3 weeks.
This class is easy, but it is very boring because the labs are just very long. The workload is average, but I would not want to sit through this class again.

Super easy A but one of the most boring classes I've ever taken. The material is extremely simple, but it takes so much time to get through. Prof Sarvary himself is kind, lenient when you need an extension and overall cares about the course.

Literally you could learn the entire semester's material in a day on your own. The labs are stretched with ridiculous questions for material that could be covered in 15 minutes but actually take 3 hours. Take it for the free A and move on.
Go to lecture to answer questions to receive credit. Lab is not difficult. Prepare for lab practicals. Take class with people you know because lab gets boring and there is a group project.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Group projects--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Lectures used clickers when I took it, and I had a night lab taught by a grad student. The lab usually was let out early, thankfully. Basically the course was fine and taught basic scientific design and literacy through a biological lens. The prof also helped me land a spot despite the course being full.

The lectures were early and mandatory; I would get breakfast and eat it during lecture because there was nothing of value being spoken most of the time. However, there were clicker questions to make sure we were there. The labs were slow and were very easy, so just show up, stay for 3 hours a week, and you'll get an A.

Sarvary is a quality guy, but lectures are useless (but mandatory) and labs are unnecessarily long and tedious. The lab skills are relevant and applicable to research, but the course is paced at 1 mph. Overall, the workload is negligible and the course is an easy A so long as you put in minimal effort.
BIOG 1500 was an excellent foundational course. Dr. Sarvary is a great adviser!

BIO 1500 is an insult to everyone required to take it. Lectures are mandatory, but don't teach anything useful. Labs are three hours long, and you aren't allowed to leave early, even though the material takes 15 minutes. The only upside is how easy this course is- if you passed 5th grade math, you will get an A. Avoid this course if you can.
Take caution because Savary has an accent like Tommy Wiseau from The Room. Take this class freshman year because it is taught at a snail's pace.

If you have ever taken a biology course before, you will pass. If not, this is a good way to get hands on experience with lab techniques. definitely take this your freshman year, you'll hate yourself otherwise.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: Don't buy the online textbook, as everyone else said. Get the free trial. Overall, this is a fairly easy class; it was my favorite, because the labs were pretty interesting and there was a light course load. Definitely answer poll everywhere questions, and find some good people for lab partners since you'll be stuck with them for most classes!

This class is incredibly dull. It is taught at a Kindergarten pace and your experience really relies on your TA. Sarvary himself is boring, arrogant, and patronizing. I hated this class and the only reason I ever went was for participation points.
You don't need to buy the online textbook or go to lecture other than for Poll Questions.
Good lecturer genuinely enthusiastic about the class, but labs are long and boring. Tests are easy and papers and projects are clearly outlined/understood.

This class is super easy. The material is easy to understand and the lectures are straight forward. Don't even really need to go to lecture to do well in the course. Dr. Sarvary is a great guy. Does have an accent so can be a little hard to understand sometimes. The online book is worthless.
I would second the disorganized comment. Lectures too seem useless.
This class is easy but disorganized.

The online book is fairly useless as a study tool. However, you will need it for prelecture and during lecture questions. Expect the TAs to have different approaches to grading and handing out studymaterial that is allowed and not allowed. The course staff decided not to hand out study guides, practice prelims, and answer keys to homework problems.
Sarvary is a great lecturer; he almost makes going to a 9am bearable.

This man is amazing. He's brilliant, really approachable, sweet, and always willing to help. If you think your TA is unfairly grading he's willing to look over anything for you. Highly recommend this professor in any course.
Sarvary's lectures are easy to understand, and the material is really simple. If you have a good TA, this class is a piece of cake.

The class is super easy and even if you can't understand his lectures (which I totally could anyway), very little of the material is new as long as you had decent bio in high school. He's funny and friendly, even though some find him intimidating. Also I love his accent.

He teaches about half of the lectures.  His accent is horrible and can sometimes make it hard to understand him.  He isn't the clearest professor in the world either.  All in all, the class is fairly easy and straightforward (if you have a good TA).
good guy. But hated his tests and hated his lab course. His lectures are clear tho.
He seems like a good professor overall.  The class, on the other hand....
Very helpful man, lectures were clear and entertaining. Really nice guy if you ever go to office hours.
Sarvary was a clear lecturer and I enjoyed his teaching more than any of the other guest lecturers.

Assistant Prof to Dr. Turgeon- I love him and his accent. His way of presenting material is very interesting and he's a funny guy. I kinda hope he replaces Turgeon because he is a much better lecturer

He's awesome. I took him last semester for bioethics and he made the class really enjoyable. Creates a great environment for discussion of arguments and objections, too, which is helpful for a philosophy course. Just make sure you do the readings (there are occasional pop quizzes) and ask questions when necessary. Would recommend.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Hilarious--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Good FWS professor! Had him for Bioethics and he was very chill and made discussions engaging. Super down-to-earth and nice. Gives good feedbacks on essays and readings are minimal. Beware of occasional popquizzes in class, but they're not hard and worth very little. Not a harsh grader--do the readings, participate, and you'll be fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--So many papers

reviews.comment: Paskell was a pretty chill professor. Five essays in total, with each being weighted more as the semester progresses. Attendance in lectures and discussions were mandatory, but going is helpful just to gain further clarification on the readings and insights to write the essays.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--So many papers

reviews.comment: I had Professor Paskell for my FWS Bioethics. Super interesting class, and he always made discussions very interesting! There were usually readings for each class, but most of the time very short (and very minimal compared to other writing seminars). Also gave very good essay feedback (although he is a slow grader). Overall great professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: This was for an FWS on Luck and Morality. There were a lot of readings, quizzes, and small assignments, and attendance was mandatory, so the class took a decent amount of time. However, he made the class discussions pretty interesting and gave really helpful feedback on the essays, so the class was worth taking.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: Both the midterm and the final were very similar to the practice tests, so everything felt very fair. He clearly puts effort into making the course run smoothly and explains the material well. The homework was lengthy, but it helped reinforce the concepts. Overall, he is a strong instructor and I learned a lot from the class.
Homework does not relate to the test. Lectures are confusing. Final are much harder than the review. Do the test review and one page he give you. You can pass

No curve. Cares more about the TAs than the actual students. Boring lecturer. Truly the worst experience ever with a professor. The final was uncharacteristically hard and he really doesn't seem to care about the student. He shouldn't teach this course!
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: good class. fun homeworks. little hard exams but very helpful TAs and very accomodating professor
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: I dont know why his rating is so high. His lectures are boring and why did he yell at us saying we needed intro stat and then explain slope to us. Like everyone in the class is IS or ORIE... we do not need to relearn this. move on. Also he's not funny.

Wilkens is a good lecturer, but p-sets were long and didn't always directly connect to lectures. ChatGPT helps a lot. The course was more technical than humanities-focused, despite the title. It didn't feel terribly different from other intro data science courses. I had high hopes for this class, but it fell short for me. Not horrible though.

I do not recommend this class. Random class attendance quizzes and lectures were not useful for the problem sets. The problem sets were beyond impossible and everyone used GPT for them (and still struggled). When it was time for the final project, everyone I know did very badly bc there were no guidelines. don't take this course, the median was B-

Incredibly long and tedious psets. Could be done adequately by chatGPT, which defeats learning objectives of this course. Unclear grading criteria and bad assignment turnarounds such that we could not improve for future assignments, particularly for the final.

Unclear grading criteria. Good chance you get like a letter grade lower than you think. Not really open with us, and would often say in office hours stuff like "yeah this would be fine" and then mark off points from you. Amazing TAs (especially the head TA, who would give out help to anyone who needed it, bless her), but I don't like him.

Wilkens is an excellent professor. I took 2950 with him previously, and he never fails to explain concepts slowly and meticulously. While the psets are challenging, Wilkens and his TAs are very approachable and helpful. Clearly passionate about teaching, Wilkens definitely belongs among the best at CIS.

The lecture could only help with about 3% of the homework. I spent two nights trying to understand the codes, but the grading just proves that GPT wins the human brain. I don't understand now what is the point of going to lectures and doing the homework if eventually we are all forced by the homework to use ChatGPT to get high grades.

Surprised to see so many positive reviews. So far, everyone I know in this class has complained about how long the homework is. Questions are marginally related to what he talks about in lectures and grades take way too long to release. People who buy ChatGPT premium receive the highest grades, which says a lot about how this class is run.

Professor Wilkens is one of the best professors I've ever had. He is incredibly engaging and makes the content that he is teaching so enjoyable. He is accessible outside of class, and shows care for each and every one of his students. He wants his students to learn and enjoy learning at the same time. Without a doubt best professor at Cornell.

Professor Wilkens is indubitably the most caring and passionate professor I have met. His lectures are engaging, and he genuinely cares about students' understanding. The grade was based on a semester-long project, 8 problem sets, a midterm, and a final. The workload can be challenging, but Professor Wilkens and his TAs will help you succeed.

Wilkens is by far one of the best professors I've had at Cornell. He's a very captivating speaker, great at answering questions, charismatic, and funny. He does move slowly through content, but as someone who is relatively new to stats and data science, I appreciated the pace. I would 100% take a class with him again!
I love his classes. The level of difficulty of lectures and homework is perfectly set. I also love how he organizes the contents
My favorite professor at Cornell so far. He's extremely approachable, explains stuffs really well and genuinely cares about students.

Hands down one of the best professors I have had at Cornell, and that was in his first semester here and during the pandemic. He did a great job balancing the CIS side of the course with the implications/impact in the humanities. He was extremely caring, made sure the teaching staff was flexible, and actively sought out and implemented feedback.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: One of the best professors I've had at Cornell! Not only is he very well read in a vast number of topics (in both Computer Science and Literature), he is also extremely approachable and well-attuned to student needs. He began every zoom class by chatting with students, frequently asked if the material made sense to everyone, and granted extensions.

It takes a special type of arrogance to believe you deserve the undivided attention of 50+ people from 8-10AM. He practices what he preaches, seems to care about students. A bajillion assignments going on at all times. In a huge group all sem, felt ridiculous begging adults to study for a test because my grade depended on theirs (group average).
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: He is so strict about attendance, but it's actually not worth it. I've never seen a professor take attendance like that. I remember in the first class, he asked the students if they knew how many assignments there were. He said there were hundreds... I felt like all the assignments were meaningless, and I even made up things to complete them.

I had the worst professor during my first semester at Cornell. We had to take an emotion quiz every day. You could complete a few very simple questions, like "How are you feeling today?" and still not get a perfect score. I hadn't bought an iClicker in my four years of undergraduate studies, but I did for this class.
Undoubtedly the worst lecturer I've ever had at Cornell!

He may come off as intimidating at first but as you go along, you realize how much of a good teacher he is. He really loves helping students. Reach out and schedule OH whenever, you wont be disappointed.

Would not recommend! The amount of work assigned is going to be way more compared to other classes with similar credit levels, and the lectures are not engaging enough for how early they are.  Slides and homework have typos, and you don't need to take this class to learn what is taught.

Self-centered and arrogant and treats his students like children while rarely offering insightful answers to genuinely meaningful questions. For a class so centered on emotional intelligence, I think he could benefit more from taking this course himself than any student enrolled currently. This course is a waste of time and money.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: This class is a joke. You drag yourself to an 8am for zero useful info—just nonsense rants and breakdowns. He warns against "amdalga breakdowns" but literally has them himself. Tests are garbage and make no sense. Haven't learned a single thing. Absolute waste of time.

If you expect to learn real-world project management skills, you won't find them here. Prelims are bizarre and harsh. You must show up before 8 AM for every lecture, phones and laptops banned, or this jerk will destroy your grades. This is the worst instructor I've had since middle school and I strongly recommend him resign from this position.
boring, useless with lots of hw, prelim, final

He's definitely the worst lecturer I've ever had throughout the past five years! Avoid his class if you can, otherwise you'll have to show up at 8 am in the classroom and your electronic devices will be banned. He assigns tons of useless HWs and you won't learn anything helpful but he brags how impactful his classes to students from time to time.

Would NOT recommend. He treats students like little kids. He should understand students only pay attention in classes because professors talk about interesting things, not because phones and computers are banned. For a class that repeats industry leadership jargons and outdated concepts, 2 cumulative prelims and 1 final are more than excessive.
His classes are fun and insightful
Despite being at 8am, I really enjoyed the lectures. Learned a lot of applicable skills and Prof Newman is a great lecturer. Can get stressful when group project work picks up.
The workload can be a lot, but is doable as he gives plenty of in-class time to work on it. Appreciated the team learning environment. Great lecturer and participation is encouraged.

Having taken other 4 credit STEM courses here, I can say Newman gives an unreasonable amount of work for a 4-credit class. He gives us limited time (e.g. 48 hours) do a decent amount of work over the weekend. He often doesn't even create the content for us to work on until right before the weekends yet tells us not to procrastinate - hypocritical!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Group projects did not provide much learning and too focused on who was considered the best. Too much reliance on participation grade. Feedback received was elementary and hardly focused on descriptive content, rather on length of presentation or minor, irrelevant details. The discussions allowed for critical thinking. Disorganized at times.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: Thinks that a 4-credit course is valid for giving hours of work for participation homework. Not even including the other individual homework, team project, team project homework, quizzes, final project, and a final exam we also have to complete for this class alone.
He was one of the best professor I could have asked for to introduce me to the PMA department.

Professor Salvato is one of the most compassionate professors I have encountered at Cornell. He is a great lecturer and passionate about PMA, which makes his class very enjoyable. He also pays great attention to how his students are doing as a whole, making constant efforts to check in. If the opportunity presents itself, take one of his classes!

I took Television with him (AMST 2160) and he was such a great teacher, the class clapped for him after the first and last lecture. We watched films throughout the lectures and talked about them. We were also assigned to watch these for homework and sometimes they'd be a little long. Essay grading was a little tough but doable if you put in effort

Lectures were fine, but he likes to hear himself talk, I'd reccomend going and paying attention, but this class didn't require that much work. It just wasn't as interesting as I hoped it would be. Essays are fairly light and the HW isn't bad, just keep up with it and put a little more effort than you might expect.

He is an excellent lecturer and is highly knowledgeable. With that said, there is a lot of material that is not covered that I wish was, though I can understand that there are time constraints. I also struggled to reach him outside of class. Homework consists of readings/viewings that are often entertaining and a handful of easy papers.

Professor Salvato is a very kind professor who knows a ton about PMA studies and his passion is felt in every lecture. The assignments are a good time if you enjoy writing about TV/movies. There are readings/things to watch for each class, but it's not unmanageable. Long readings can be skimmed. The class is heavily discussion based, in a good way.
reviews.rating_tags: Hilarious--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Salvato is brilliant, I learned so much, workload totally manageable, loved almost everything we watched

Advertises it as a discussion-based class but there were more than 80 students. He sold this class as teaching cultural and economic impacts of television. This is an analytical televisual class where you look at mise-en-scene, angles, etc. Wanted to be taught the visible impacts of television. By week 3 only about 30-40 started showing up.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Nick is perhaps the best lecturer at Cornell - he doesn't hesitate, break, or stutter through entire classes. He also remembers students' names within weeks in large lectures. He's also one of the clearest critical thinkers on campus. Not an easy grader, but definitely fair

Nick's worst trait is that his genius can be intimidating, but if you don't let that get to you, then his class is very rewarding. He blows your mind every class period, and if you put in the work, it's not hard to get good grades. He's exceptionally accommodating about missing classes and getting extensions as long as you talk to him about it.

Really great professor who is both helpful and approachable. Good discussions but readings are incredibly long and often difficult to get through. Always a heavy bent on FGSS issues in Nick's classes even if not cross-listed as such.

Amazing professor. Not easy, but perhaps the best lecturer at Cornell. No ums, likes, or breaks. It's as if he reads from a teleprompter, but has the enthusiasm of an unplanned conversation. Incredible knowledge about television and film theory. Highly recommended!

Nick Salvato is an excellent professor - a brilliant person with a genuine interest in teaching. Be warned: the texts are often difficult, but he has a very straightforward and accessible teaching style. If you put forth the effort, you will learn a lot. Grading is fair. He is very patient and willing to help if you don't understand something.

Nick is a really laid-back, smart guy who loves theatre.  Class is just a discussion, so attendance is important (but he also is very understanding).  A lot of plays to read, but all interesting.  Very fair grader and easy to please.
seriously the worst lecturer i have ever had. trails off at the end of every sentence and barely looks up from his screen. also very reluctant to answer questions

Content presented in a boring, uninspiring way. Good 4000 level econ class if you don't want to overwork yourself - no required attendance, exam material can be found on the slides. His teaching methods reflects that he doesn't think having a super difficult class is necessary - which can be seen as time-wasting to some and low-stress to others.

I was very excited about the material but became very frustrated. Professor Nimark was a very harsh grader on the exams and never gave us any feedback. Grading was very inconsistent across different students. No incentive to attend class as he brought 0 enthusiasm and effort to his lectures. Said he'd “curve the class” and yet there was no curve.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The slides are very boring and inefficient. The professor basically just copies what's in the textbook into the slides, so they're full of text. Exams are just pure memorization but require a lot of time.
Terrible experience, one of the worst econ class i have ever taken throughout my entire econ major career. Very useless slides.
One of the worst economics professors I've ever had at Cornell, the most monotone lectures & reads straight off slides. Prelims and finals are pure memorization. Do not take.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I personally had a good experience with this class. If you are responsible and keep up, and don't take advantage of the leniency of Professor Nimark by getting lazy (ie, attendance isn't mandatory, won't quiz you, etc.) you will excel. Also, he is by far the most caring professor and clearly cares and understands that life is more than just a class

pretty much the intermediate macro content all over again. the test is literally about how well you memorize his text heavy slides. any minor deviation from rubric results in points deduction. think twice before you enroll; there should be better places to waste your time

Probably the worst professor I have ever had at Cornell. The content isn't too hard but Nimark sure knew how to confuse his students. I stopped going to class after the first few lectures and just read the textbook instead. Only reason why I got an A- was because the TA was lenient with the grading. Overall bad class.

Easily the worst professor I have ever had at Cornell. While the class itself is not the most difficult, it is impossible to know what you need to know for the exam. Office hours for the TA and professor are horrible and I do not learn anything. Often times, the professor shows up and it feels like he is reading the slides for the first time.
Most irresponsible and poorest professor I have ever met at Cornell. Simply don't care since he's already full professor and won't be fired.
