He's a little monotone and uninspiring, but he knows the subject and he's a very generous/fair grader. I don't get the frustration from others, he may be kinda boring but he explains stuff well and answer questions. Better on zoom maybe?
Good class for non-economics majors. Economists usually miss the graphs and math. TA is helpful for hws.

This guy straight up doesnt care. Very uninspiring lecturer, shows up late from time to time and doesnt help at all. This is a very easy class from a material stand point, so I would only take it if you want an easy 4000-level course. However, I have never met someone who cares less about teaching in my time at Cornell.

The TA grades the exam, class curves to a B+, grading is arbitrary. He is not all that passionate about teaching, he reads from the PowerPoints, which are taken from the textbook. READ the Textbook, and DO the review questions, as for HW talk to the TA, not an easy A, but doable.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: PollEv was kinda annoying, but other than that, he was a super chill prof who had some fun references in his exams. His lectures really told you everything you needed to know about the exams, and the slides were my best friend. One or two things seemed subjective on exams, however.

Horrible lecturer, overcomplicates very simple topics to the point where you have no idea what he's talking about. Posts slides onto canvas but they do not help. Get ready to read the entire textbook if you want to know what's going on. Also assigns way too many useless homework assignments a week (50+ questions), and the prelims are difficult.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Test heavy

reviews.comment: I think his a good lecturer. Sometimes in class he overcomplicates simple concepts, which I think bars him from being an outstanding and great one, but overall he is a good professor and person overall.

It can be a confusing course, and in Orlov's lectures, you need to pay attention to little things and the interaction between topics. You can miss up to 6 classes without losing attendance points, but sometimes it takes a while for lecture/discussion slides and lecture recordings to be uploaded. Two midterms and a final based on practice prelims.

He was my lab instructor and miscalculated my total grade back in 2015, which became a source of long-lasting frustration for me. 

I still remember how proud he seemed of himself, and how he carried that unnecessary arrogance during lab sessions. 

I feel justified making this comment now because I am currently a PhD candidate.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Orlov isn't a bad person but he's not the best lecturer. He tends to overcomplicate every topic and for someone who doesn't know anything about economics this was a really hard course.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Funniest professor I've had, he literally turned office hours into stand-up. But his tests? Pure evil. Compared to other profs, it felt like we were prepping for the MCAT. I went to almost every office hour, gave it my all, and still somehow got wrecked. 10/10 for vibes, 2/10 for my GPA.

Has the greatest slides to help study-- literally just read them through and review and you will do fine on exams. He also provides great practice materials before exams they are pretty much exactly like the real thing.

Orlov gets a lot of unjustified hate. As long as you actually go to class, the material is super understandable. He genuinely cares about making sure students know the content and his slides are very thorough. He also provides plenty of extra resources and ample help hours for extra practice. The tests are exactly like the practice exams.

Orlov is a good lecturer and has very clear slides. If you go to lecture and pay attention, you'll know the material. He provides practice exams that are basically exactly what's on the actual exam and are super helpful for studying. The median grades on exams tend to be pretty high as well.

Professor Orlov's class is slightly difficult and does require some level of attention. If you actually read the textbook and read the slides, you should not have much of an issue with this class. Additionally Professor Orlov, is always willing to answer questions if any confusion arises.

Professor Orlov's class is honestly not very difficult if you go to the lectures, or even if you just look at the notes. He also makes himself very available and wants to help you learn. I took AP Macro in high school and found that the class was very similar to that, and I honestly just studied the slides and did well on the exams

Bro's slides might be the best slides I've ever seen in my life. His lectures are pretty good, but if you're still confused by something his slides literally go over everything step by step. Even though the upper level Econs get way harder, this class made me want to be an Econ major and I don't regret it.

Professor Orlov is one of two microeconomics professors in the semester I took this class. After hearing about the other class (which was primarily online), I feel as though my foundation in microeconomics is a lot stronger than my peers who took the other professor. He also curves each exam to make the median a B. Extra credit at end.

Professor Orlov is an amazing educator as long as you attend lectures/discussions and study for the prelims using his practice exams (they are very similar to the real prelims). Also, he takes attendance using pollev for every class so be aware (but he allows 6 absences).

His lectures are extremely organized and concise. His lecture slides contain everything he discusses almost for verbatim. As long as you go to class or read through the slides before the prelims you are going to do well. People who say he is a bad Prof or the class is hard probably put in almost zero effort because I did basically the bare minimum.
Very disorganized class, difficult to understand, test-heavy.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Good slides that are easy to learn from outside of class, and so helpful for studying for exams so you don't have to take notes. His prelims were fair and representative of the practice prelims. Only issue is that in my experience Ed Stem is not a good way to reach him, if you have questions go to office hours or ask right after lecture.
Good lecturer and engaging. I left with a good understanding of microeconomics and helpful slides to look back on. I also really liked the textbook he used.

He is definitely a very nice/caring person, but the exams (which makes up a large percentage of your grade) can be tough if you don't study the right things. Textbook was sort of helpful, but I would say the practice exams were the best way to learn.

Pros: caring/respectful, wants students to succeed + gain a thorough understanding, extra credit, several top-notch TAs, useful/challenging practice prelims

Cons: HWs r good GPA boosters but long/not super useful 4 prelims, provides confusing/long-winded explanations in OH, can't go into the class w/out knowing Econ, some terrible TAs

Orlov is actually very nice AND I do think I have an extremely thorough knowledge of micro econ after taking the class. So if learning is important to you, then you'll like this class. But it's graded off of very few things and prelims took an extremely long amount of time to grade. Ur gonna be stressed about the final regardless of current grade
reviews.rating_tags: Caring--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Can definitely do well in this class if you have a baseline knowledge of microeconomics and study the lectures/practice tests very well. I would say it was one of the harder classes, but it is very doable. He's an extremely nice person and very excited about what he teaches, but maybe look elsewhere if you are focused on getting a good grade.

You'll have to study and know his materials well. he offers tons of out of class opportunities for you to study though. Literally every day of the week before the final there was office hours. hard class but afterwards I really feel like I understand the material significantly better, and he did a great job. for an easy A, take a different prof.

Receives more hate than necessary. Prelims and Finals were very similar to practice tests, and everyone could definitely get an A if they prepared well for them. Annoying amount of homework and required participation, but really not bad.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Orlov is amazing!!! I don't know why people have so many complaints. He provides accessible resources to succeed on the prelim and finals. He doesn't do open note exams so students are forced to learn the material. Thank god professors like him still exist. 100% recommend this class if you want a solid foundation in Microeconomics.

Professor Orlov is okay. If you have to take him, take him. But beware of a stringent attendance policy and lectures that can be likened most closely to sonic Xanax. He is not too strict gradewise, but his sections are led by soulless PhD candidates and he can never seem to settle on OH timing, making accessing help harder. Overall, not worth $44k

Orlov is nice, but that's it. For 1 thing, the other micro class has all online exams which makes Orlov's class so frustrating. 2 prelim + 1 finals = 80% of grade, course assistants are clueless, and the prelims take MONTHS to grade. Writing this on Christmas Eve and still don't know my final grade! Orlov is ok but get Thomas if possible

He is not as bad a lot of ppl say he is. He's a nice guy, very accessible and he explains stuff ok; he could be a little more clear sometimes. The class is not that hard; the exams are pretty similar to the practice ones. His voice did make me want to fall asleep sometimes since it was a 9am. This is coming from an engineer.

Orlov is a great guy, and very willing to answer questions if you approach him after lecture. Class is very organized/slides are posted regularly. Little HW, so 80% of the grade is based on exams, which were moderately challenging but similar to the practice ones. Class size is ~600, so if you want support you have to go out of your way to get it.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Graded based off lecture participation because he knew that if he didn't, nobody would show up to those crappy lectures. Spoke as if it's middle school, would get super pissed at people for getting up and leaving the lecture hall early. "The bell doesn't dismiss you, I do" type of guy.
Nice professor with extremely clear slides. Didn't like how attendance was mandatory, enjoyed the pop cultures references on exams.

This man's lectures make 0 sense but are mandatory to attend. You have to teach yourself the entire course and there's always something on the tests he only went over one time or not at all. Never take this class.
Lectures don't help and the whole grade is based on exams and final. Hard exams and hard final because he struggles to teach.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Orlov has incredibly clear lecture slides and seems to generally be a nice person. However, he is not great at teaching the material and he assigns a ridiculous amount of homework. The homework is also very different to what is tested on the prelim. Avoid taking him as an instructor if at all possible.

Little homework, 6 absences allowed before your grade drops- but even then it barely takes any percentage off. Exams are exactly like practice prelim and are not that hard. Lecture slides are clear. Passionate lecturer too

Orlov is a nice guy who cares about his students. The only complaint I have for his class is that attendance is tracked and pre lim two was so hard. Considering Thomas has open note exams I would take her

Take with caution. Prof. is nice but he doesn't teach the material well. His slides are long & not everything is on the exams. He doesn't take extra time to teach the most important problems. He should show us how to construct the graphs step-by-step, but he doesn't, and instead just shows pictures on slides. Students need guidance- he lacks this.

No cheat sheet on exam is really really really annoying............
Mandatory attendance is also really really really annoying........
One practice prelim is not enough as well

Take Sanders or Thomas-- I've heard from other students that they allow cheat sheets and you can take exams online. We had no cheat sheets and IRL exams. 

He was enthusiastic and knowledgeable but could not convey information well. Lovely guy, terrible lecturer.

He was fine. Not the best lecturer but not bad. Exams were a little harder than I thought they should be but not terrible. Homeworks were fine. Attendance policy was annoying. He's really nice and enthusiastic about the content just not the best at explaining things sometimes.

Pretty clear in his lectures, only 2 prelims & 1 final so exams are 85% of your grade but he gives 2 options. Class avg is a B+ I think, he gives practice final pdfs, & lots of office hours. Lowest 3 hws are dropped, pollev for participation each class. Exams are fair but can be tricky so study the slides. At the end, he offers extra credit

bro is kinda the goat, go to office hours if you're struggling since the class does move fast. offers extra credit questions on every test that're easy if you go to lecture as well as a 5% ish curve, class median was a B+ I think
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment:
I've loved Orlov as a professor.
I've taken his micro & macro intro classes, and I loved the way he structured the course. He always gave a review class day and a class off before a prelim, which I appreciated. Tbh his lectures were not super helpful for me, especially at 9am, but the HW helped reinforce the topics I'd need for the prelims.

By far, the most difficult economics course I have taken this far. The content itself is not terribly difficult, but he moves through it at a rapid pace. He does have an accent which makes certain things difficult to understand.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Okay so Professor Orlov is a really nice guy who is clearly trying his best to provide a good experience for his students. Despite this effort, he teaches introductory macroeconomics like it's a 6000 level graduate course. Difficult subject matter that goes beyond basic Econ, tests are ridiculous, homework is overwhelming, and the list goes on.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The prelims are horrendous; 20 questions valued at 5 points each. You miss two and that is a whole letter grade down. He is a nice person but terrible professor. He goes through 10 slides in a matter of seconds. Worst professor I have had, not even Stephanie Thomas was this bad. His class will make you wish you never enrolled.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Orlov is a great human being, and he is one of the kindest Professors I have ever had. With that being said, the prelims were ridiculous. The median is far below other macro professors, and the lectures can be quite hard to follow. I never left class understanding what I was doing. Best of luck.

Orlov is a great lecturer. While many say otherwise, the way he treats lectures is more of a supplement to the textbook. Light workload and a lot of practice tests. Orlov even cares about keeping the same grade distribution from previous years. Man is trying his best.

Unstructured and not engaging. extremely low median exam score for prelims (which make up most of your grade in the class). I did better on the exams when I read the textbook on my own and stopped caring for the lecture. Discouraging class to take, but it's an intro class and requirement for so many people so you just have to bear with it.
If you want to take a class where you are given absolutely no guidance and have to teach all of the material to yourself, Orlov is the professor for you to take.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: While the class doesn't have a ridiculous workload, he's a terrible professor and his lectures are painful to sit through. Theres weekly hw with large textbook readings, and I'd be ok with this if I felt it prepared me for the prelims, yet the class average on our first prelim was a 71, a ridiculous grade for an introduction class with 400 people.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Smart guy who was super nice. If you struggle in Econ I suggest you take this class because he is always willing to help. Also gives out chips and snacks during the late night class. Graded on attendance so super easy A

I'd definitely recommend taking Prof. Orlov's class. Be aware that ECON 1120 is lecture heavy and there will be mandatory attendance as well as hefty homeworks 1x week. 

That being said, it's pretty obvious he wants his students to succeed. He makes himself extremely available and will go over material in class to ensure a thorough understanding.

Professor O'Donoghue is an outstanding professor, and his lectures were always engaging. Homework and exams were challenging, but if you focus on understanding the intuition behind the concepts, you should be fine. Lastly, his passion for the material clearly comes across in class, which made learning the material enjoyable and thought-provoking.

This is one of the most interesting and rewarding classes I have taken in the econ major. O'Donoghue is truly at the forefront of this field and often cites his own research which is fascinating. Very difficult course, but lectures are amazing and p-sets and exams are fair if you are keeping up. Would highly recommend for all majors.

O'Donoghue is kind, knowledgeable, and engaging. Behavioral economics is not an easy course, but O'Donoghue articulates concepts clearly and provides lots of materials to help. Highly recommend! Just make sure your schedule isn't too overwhelming when you add 4660 - there's a lot of material.

Took in Fall 2020. Best econ class I took at Cornell. Best mix of interesting content, fair hws and tests, and great teaching. Class is more "mathematical/theoretical", but Ted is fantastic at explaining it all. He's also very approachable and caring, and I appreciated that he put in extra effort to deal with the circumstances at the time. GOAT.
Loved this class. Really interesting material and the math/econ basis isn't difficult. Cool concepts and a great lecturer.

DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS. AEM 2020/PSYCH 2940 was so difficult for no reason. Prelims and the final are 100% of your grade and the tests are extremely hard. For a class that does not even count towards your major, it is not worth it.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: His lectures are extremely clear and is very willing to engage with students and answer questions. Despite his ability to explain the concepts very thoroughly and make the content look easy, the material itself is pretty challenging. Tests are challenging but not unreasonable. Definitely take a course with prof OD if you get the chance.

By far the best prof I have had at Cornell! Behavioral Economics was amazing, and his lectures were fascinating. I wish more professors were like him. The class is exactly the right amount of work, and Prof Odonoghue is very accessible during office hours.

Despite the difficulty of the course, Professor O'Donoghue makes his slides very clear and concise. He explains everything in plain language and gives constant reminders on previous materials/terms/equations to jog your memory unlike other professors who just put a bunch symbols without clearly explaining. He is the definition of a good professor.

Literally defines what a professor should be like. I'm doing better in this class than other "easier" classes I'm taking, largely due to how thorough and clear his (actually pretty difficult) lectures are.

The best professor I've had at Cornell so faramazing and engaging lecturer, super accommodating and really organized, makes complicated topics seem easy, and literally one of the top academics in the field of behavioral economics. Definitely take one of his classes if you're an Econ major, he's so great :)

Extremely organized and handled the logistics of this 5-professor course. Super clear in explaining concepts and satisfyingly concise slides. I had no prior econ education but still managed to understand everything. Also helpful during office hours: an incredibly intelligent professor!

Professor O'Donoghue is a pretty good lecturer. When speaking about the material he makes it seem so simple. Only thing is, it's not. This class requires a lot of intuition thinking. Nothing like you've probably learned about in your intro econ courses. Problem sets are fairly short but office hours are key to doing well on them.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: The course was really interesting, the exams are challenging though and grades are curved such that the median is a B+. The prof was quite engaging, sections are optional

Awesome professor, again, probably the best I have had in all my econ classes. His lectures are exceptionally clear, which is great, but also kinda makes other professors' lectures seem really not-on-par. The materials are definitely not easy, and going to lectures actually help you learn better.
Awesome! Awesome! Awesome!

Probably the best Econ prof. at Cornell. The class is def. not easy, but if you pay attention in class, work hard on the PSets, and go to office hours, you should be fine. Sections were pointless though, and there is no textbook, but his crystal clear lectures are enough to do well. Would def. recommend this for econ majors

Love this class! Lots of interesting subjects covered, and he certainly knows his material. Even though he posts the slides online, it's recommended to go to class because he shows you how to set up problems (which does not end up the powerpoint). He's also a fairly engaging lecturer. Try your best to do the problem sets without help.

Lecture stuff is online, though lecture will help you understand the slides better than just reading them online. He tests hard, but it's possible to do well if you assimilate to his testing style. PSets i dunno, didn't go to section

I thought the class was a bit boring, but others think it's really interesting.  The professor is very clear.  No textbook, his notes are online, and he gives you a number of problem sets that help you learn the material.  Tests are a bit difficult, but he'll scale grades upwards, so no worries there.

Solid professor and a really nice guy but a few points of caution about the course: the class is curved to a fairly low grade making it difficult to exceed an A- for anything but exceptional performance and possible to achieve a B or lower for decent work; the subject matter is inherently interesting but better dealt with in the realm of psychology

crystal clear lecture notes, posted on blackboard but you have to attend class to get his important additions - no textbook, so you have to go. interesting lecturer, not boring at all and the subject is interesting. good class for fulfilling econ major .

Great lecturer. Very clear and willing to help, the subject material is incredibly interesting. There's no textbook for the class, however, and section was optional and not very helpful. Hence, going to every lecture was inherently mandatory and extremely vital to doing well. Just go to the twice-a-week lecture and you'll be fine.
I love this professor. IMO he is the best econ prof. I have had yet. Keep up the good work prof!

No, seriously, he is the best teacher in the econ department. VERY clear lectures. Light reading, hard problem sets that were graded harshly. Tests seemed fair. Overall grading policy is more than fair. EXTREMELY helpful in office hours - use them. Learned a lot. Must take!!
Best Professor in econ department. Very interesting topics, clear teaching, fair in assessing students' learning
Maybe the best instructor of economics at Cornell. Brilliant, helpful and great to talk to.
No Comments
Great professor. Difficult topics in Econ 302.
Pretty good professor. He gave a very clear teaching!!!
No Comments

Professor Reed is an outstanding professor. She is very knowledgeable in her field. Be willing to put in the work. Show effort, commitment, and discipline. Don't be afraid of making mistake.  Get out of your comfort zone and take initiative. I highly recommend Professor Reed to anyone.
Rate my professor scared me but she is amazing
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Gives good feedback--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Peggy is by far the worst professor I have had at my time at Cornell.  I would rather take my chances in hand to hand combat with a grizzly bear than ever have to deal with her unsufferable attitude towards her students again. She is part of the reason the hotel school's applicants are decreasing, get rid of her and her latex gloves immediately.

One of the worst professors I have ever had. She has such an unorganized canvas she contradicts herself almost every day. Grading is completely subjective, and she would not be able to replicate a perfect assignment if she graded herself. She is so angry with everyone I think she has Bipolar.  Hope Cornell sees she is not an ivy league professor.

Had her for HADM 1650... she talks all the time and is just really annoying. Very very strict on grading so be careful to make sure you follow her rubrics to a tee. Also even though you are only "required" to go to 1 professor meeting, 1 meeting with writing center, and 2 with CA, you still get points deducted. Just try and not take her if possible
Really great professor. Has a bad rep but cares a lot about teaching. If you go to class and do the homework you will do well in her course. Great lecturer
Great teacher. Going in, I thought she was going to be horrible and I was very scared but it was the complete opposite. Very caring professor who wants her students to succeed.

Not sure if she has changed or what happened but just took her course and had a great experience. Really cares a lot about her students, very reasonable, and very passionate about teaching. Overall, great professor and great course. If you do the homework, show up to class, and participate you will do well in this course.
she was so bad, i want to give her a 0, but that's not possible, so i give her a 1.

She is the WORST teacher I've had in my entire life. Lectures are absolutely idiotic, I learned nothing in this class. She grades like a mad woman. I cannot emphasize enough how egregious this woman is. She is stone cold, bitter, and the least empathetic person I've ever met. She is absurdly out of touch with reality and should be fired immediately
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This class is impossible to do well in. Only take it if you like to cry. Prepare to have your spirit decimated, your motivation demolished, and your will to live diminished. Turn around time is so fast on assignments that you quite literally will either have to fail all your other classes or take the L. Dont do it. Unless you hate yourself.

I genuinely wonder if she is aware that we take multiple other classes. Atrocious, merciless, insufferable, mental-breakdown-inducing, brutal, cruel, unrelenting, unendurable, overwhelming. It is absolutely, definitely, and wholly impossible to see a good grade after pulling enough all nighters to age me 50 years. I have disintegrated.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--So many papers--Tough grader

reviews.comment: No no no no no no no no no no no no no no no no
Im a recovering alcoholic and this woman made me break my sobriety amongst a puddle of tears.

HEINOUS&distressing. Makes me need to double up on my antidepressants. I would rather dry swallow a handful of nails than ever take a class with her again. She believes this is your only commitment. Assignment grading is crazy, impossible to get a good grade. Turnaround time for assignments is deeply unreasonable.

This professor is one of the worst professors in the hotel school. She lacks any empathy for her students and expects them to treat this one class as their only priority. She expects students to meet multiple times on the weekends despite other obligations. She hypocritically teaches about mental health and inclusivity but has no respect for them.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: She is the literal worst. Required in-person meetings on the weekends, even though she can't be bothered to come to class in person herself. She has no remorse for others and seems to get pleasure from seeing her already stressed students struggle. For a school that pretends to focus on mental health, she demonstrates the polar opposite. Cruel!

Peggy is the WORST professor in the hotel school. She has made multiple people cry in class and seems to go out of her way to make her students depressed. She is relentless and acts as though her class is the only thing we have to do as Cornell Students. Be prepared for an extremely rude professor, she will make you hate yourself and her...

This woman made me cry 3 times in one week. She is unrelenting. Even though we are in a pandemic, and she can't even make it to class due to her health issues, she expects her students to treat this class like their only obligation. She has no empathy for others. She does not give positive comments. She does not give A's. She's the worst professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: TOUGH GRADER. You'll never get a perfect score.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: not the best
Great person, great teacher. I was not interested in food and beverage when I came into this class. Left the class somewhat interested in it. Nothing but great things to say about her

I had her at 8:40 a.m. in Spring '25. Honestly, she has so much more energy in the morning compared to her students. If you have any concerns, like problems with a groupmate or something you do not understand, she will take the time to help and explain. The prelim is straightforward if you read carefully and do the practice test.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: she cares about her students and she really likes the subject. however, she's french...

Prof Ozanne cares about what she teaches and makes every class interesting, even at 8:40 in the morning. The grading is fair, and the prelims are pretty straightforward if you put in the time to study. She's also super approachable and helped me figure out what kind of internships to look for. Would definitely take her again.

You can tell that she is very passionate about F&B. Her prelims were all really fair. Everything she told us to study for (during our review lecture) was on the prelim and nothing more. There was a slight problem with wording in a few of her questions but other than that her class is very doable.
Incredibly boring lectures. Would not take again

I had her in Spring 24. She is very approchable, and she gave me great advice for my intership during office hours. Lectures may be heavy, lots of content, but a great prof and a useful course if you want to work in the F&B industry.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Prof. Ozanne is a TOUGH grader, with a lot of content expected per assignment and a large amount of time dedicated to her work. However, she is incredibly clear with what she wants and is very good at explaining the breadth of topics in her class. Be prepared to read, as every lecture has a reading and quiz associated with it.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Professor Ozanne was great. While this was only the second semester HADM 1361 was taught and the first taught by Professor Ozanne there was not a lot of feelings about being guinea pigs. Overall Professor Ozanne was great and would be happy to have her again.

Prof Ozanne shows that she truly cares about her students and their learning. I honestly learned a lot this semester and I am already applying some of the concepts seen in class at work. There is some work to put in but nothing unmanageable.
She is a nice lady but a terrible professor. Lectures aren't engaging and are incredibly boring, get prepared for knowledge quizzes every class.

Easy class, great professor, provides extra credit and tests are fair. I had her during the coronavirus when things switched online. The first half of the year there was barely homework besides readings, she does clicker questions, and the prelims are straightforward. During corona she gave a lot of work but it was easy
I took Dr. Olson's online Sustainable Preservation series but that's not an option to select. She was great, very approachable, knowledgeable and provided substantive feedback.

Took the Sustain Preservation certificate series though eCornell and Katelin was so supportive and encouraging. I haven't been in the classroom for a long time, and she cheered me on and helped me work through my analysis of a passion project. Wish she taught more courses! Highly recommend
Fair and knowledgeable.

I recently took Katelin's Sustainable Preservation certificate series through eCornell and it was fantastic. She is so supportive and encouraging and the other people in the class were amazing to connect with through her online zoom weekly sessions. Wish she would offer another class so I could take that too!
Positive and enthusiastic; great class
Required course for first years in CRP. Provided a lot of information and was excited about the work. Helped me out of class improve my writing. Always available for an appointment.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Took a very long time to grade. Lectures were not engaging or adaptive and she was a very strict grader on top of that. Gave contradictory advice for writing and tests were not as advertised. Worst prof I have worked with in the department as well. Not sure why Cornell admin assigned her this class as it's her first year and this is a req class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--So many papers

reviews.comment: Worst Professor I have had by far in the CRP department. Grading was unfair, never returned in a timely manner, and harsh. Lectures were profusely dense with the containment of far too much information, as well as extremely fast-paced because she writes down a script of what to say. Exams and papers were difficult, lengthy, and lacked correlation.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: not understanding at all, very abrasive

He doesn't teach the class. Loved the guest lectures but if he's gonna have prelims in this class, they need to be based off guest lectures, not an external reading that makes no sense. Grading is so vague. Got a 60 on the first prelim and 80 on the 2nd one. This class should go back to being pass/fail. Students skipped anyway and chatted prelim.

He is a pretty nice. Quizzes after every class can be rough at the beginning. The guest speakers are interesting, and there is barely HW. There are 2 time-consuming prelims, and quite a lot of reading & understanding for prelim prep. He's a fast email replier. Harsh grading, the final project brought my grade from A to B.
This is the first time I've been motivated enough to write a review on a professor. Harsh grader, barely teaches, drove the whole freshman class insane. Just make sure you glaze him.

Preszler believes in learning through guest speakers who share experiences in the business world. The course is centered on the UN Sustainable Development Goals and wicked problems, forcing critical thinking about complex, real-world challenges. Prelims are based on out-of-class readings, and short quizzes follow each guest speaker for engagement.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Bad professor, harsh grading and very unclear grading criteria. Professor does not teach himself, and relies solely on guest lectures. Prelims are based on material not covered during lectures. Reviews are accurate.

Professor is a narsacist. Very hypocritical when it comes to grading. Most people chatted his prelims. Does not curve at all. Final reflection assignments matters a ton (took me from an A to a B). This class should go back to being S/U.
Not good AT ALL. Intentionally grades harshly to get the class median grade down. Good luck all freshmen who have him.
Bald man. Bad man. Hurt my grade man. Bad tests man. Bad grading man.
He doesn't teach. Prelims were on material never covered in class. Harsh grader. No remorse.

Let me put in caveperson speak so clear. Much read. Professor talk many word, say nothing. Sassy. Condescending. Self-center. Grade tough for fun. TA ridiculous. Guest speaker come. Professor no care. Me no care. Give test on thing never teach. Exam long. Time short. Me still confused. Me learn by self. Professor bald. That all.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Preszler is a professor who thinks his class is more important than anything else in your life. He heavily picks favorites, gives little grading criteria then grades extremely harshly without giving a rubric back, and his prelims are hard for no reason (and he doesn't teach you the content in class). He makes me want to go into investment banking
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Very dependent on exams. Prelims are based off reading that is outside of the class. Tough grader and purely gives grade based on whether or not he knows who you are.
Worst class. Grades without clear guidelines and a clear curve down. Doesn't even teach as he relies solely on guest speakers.
Subjective grading without clear guidelines in the instructions.

His foundations class is an absolute joke. All Dyson freshmen are required to take this course and he grades way too harshly for someone who doesn't even teach. The class is just guest speakers who come in every class (aside from prelim days).

Professor appears nice. Speakers brought to class are all interesting and unique. Prelims are definitely rough, and readings can be challenging to understand. The small quizzes are difficult, depending on the speaker. The final presentation has strict guidelines, so make sure to meet every expectation.

Very caring person who is passionate about the course. Some guest lectures were more on the boring side but most were great. The prelims aren't too bad as long as you're a decent writer. The final presentation is easy and he seemed proud of everyone's work. He's also flexible with any conflicts that athletes or anyone have with the class.

Trent and Lennox taught this when I took it (Fall 2024), though it's changed since. The class had guest speakers (UN SDGs focused), and you could volunteer to host one (join their dinner). Easy course with reflection writing and mandatory attendance. Lectures were boring & people started to skip, so they added Poll Everywhere with location tracker.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The new 1101 is really hard, this was the first year attendance was mandatory. Quiz after each guest speaker, prelims are a little rough unless you study a ton for them. Do the readings ahead of time (but he doesn't assign it til a week before the prelim). He's a nice person but isn't the best teacher I've had.
Preszler is a narcissistic a-hole to some, or a great resource to others. participate and be engaged and you will have the latter relationship.

Preszler is a very nice guy who takes an active interest in student's work and puts a lot of effort into each lecture. For the class you have to write several essays which are graded very harshly and require lots of revision. This class does make you a better writer and you learn a lot, but only take if you have an interest in the subject matter.

Trent is a nice guy, but his Grand Challenges Writing course is incredibly hard. For the love of God, do not take his class. Compared to other grand writing courses, this is so much more work. Please do not do what I did. Save yourself and take another class.

He's not a bad person, just a bad professor for teaching freshmen chemistry. He went too much in depth and wouldn't explain the basics. He also loves to ridicule students who have the nerve to ask questions about the material. Overall the course was curved to a B so grades were okay but no one learned the material (test averages in the 40s).

Peter, while he may be entertaining, does not teach us anything. His slides are discombobulated, and he does not explain stuff well. He also insists that we not use the textbook as "they are cheap and incorrect." Organic Chemistry Tutor is your best friend. Your only saving grace is that while you may be completely lost, so is everyone else.
Caan't teach.

Wolczanski clearly doesn't want to teach freshmen and the freshmen don't want to be there. I skipped every lecture and homework, literally just showed up for labs & prelims and mostly guessed and got a C-. Class averages for prelims were all under 50% so the curve was insane. He's rude but hilarious. Engineer trauma bonding starts here.
Worst prof ever

Trying to decipher the incomprehensible babble that bubbles from this poor banal soul's mouth was the first and only sin that many new engineers have commited. The class is not hard, chemistry should not be his hard, but when you've got it out for the kids... godspeed to the next year who takes him.

Lectures were poorly organized and had little information on relevant material, yet extensive information on "bonus topics", which cluttered lectures. Despite this, it was expected to locate the rare pieces of actually relevant information, which would then appear on prelims. 
Extremely disdainful towards students, for some reason.
Does not know how to teach.

Peter's lectures were not the most interesting but he definitely knows what he is talking about. Very confusing in the beginning but it takes about 1 prelim to figure out how to do well. Study the lecture notes of course, but I did text book problems and did fine on the exams. Easy grade since averages were
put me a step further towards the ithaca gorges. should be named in his honor

The professor was funny but not a good lecturer, and it was hard to separate essential information from what is mentioned in passing. The course needs a better focus on the basics as it was very sporadic and made it hard for anything to truly stick. Mostly self studied since the course material given were hard to decipher.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Did all the work myself.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Hilarious

reviews.comment: I played Geometry Dash in the back of class most lectures. I think Peter was a fun guy but the lectures didn't help much and content was a little all over the place. We started with nuclear chem. I found the best way to study was to UNDERSTAND the problem sets. Most of the learning happened by referencing problem sets to posted lecture notes.
Worst class I have ever taken. Do everything to not have this professor.
6 point font, reads off the slides, and inconsistent lectures. Lectures about his research instead of the actual content. DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS!! Also, hard prelims with a 40 average.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: The only problem with Peter is that his lecture notes are very hard to understand, rest are fine. Going to lectures don't really help to be honest, but the office hours were surprisingly helpful. Exams are fair as they ALL come from his lecture notes, but decoding his slides was the hard part. As long as you spend time studying you should be fine
Peter was a great teacher! Truly a grateful experience to be in this class.
Somehow got an A in this class. Ride the curve. OChem Tutor is your best friend.

PETER PETER PETER PETER PETER PETER PETER
DON'T TAKE THIS MAN'S CLASS

Taking Pete's class is truly an experience (a bad one). The lectures teach you about everything EXCEPT Chemistry. I've left the class knowing the same amount (if not less) about chemistry. He makes it a point to show his disdain for students every lecture, and gets mad at the class when only 5 people out of 500 score above 75% on his prelims. Bad.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: PTW and the administration highly discourage students from seeking accommodations for their needs protected by the government of New York State using the loophole that granted these accommodations is "high effort" and "logistically inconvenient"
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: If you don't need to take his class, don't take it. If you need to take his class, lock in.

reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Peter Wolcanski is a unique professor for his deep hatred of his students. He assumes you already know the basics of general chemistry and will spend majority of his lecture going over advanced topics , assuming a foundation. Exams are atrocious and based on an all or nothing approach. Youtube is your best friend, if there is any hope to be had.

He's a hard and confusing teacher in lecture. But there is not much homework. And he grades harshly but fair. The class is curved to a B so if you work hard you will be fine. On one of my prelims the average was a 39% but that got curved to a B.
Wow, don't take this class. I would HIGHLY recommend taking this class over the summer or a semester where he is NOT teaching. I should've known after seeing these reviews.
You should know already by how many reviews are here. I concur HEAVILY.

This was by far the worst professor I ever had at Cornell. Prof. Wolczanski is a terrible lecturer, runs a poorly organized course, and completely underprepares his students for his exams. It would be difficult for him to try to make his students' lives any more difficult than he already does. He lacks compassion and adds unnecessary stress.

Please do not take this class I took AP chem and I swear the prelim is so god dam hard nothing like the homework prelim questions follow each other so one wrong everything is wrong. Loves to talk about bomb although its not mandatory the lectures on the slide are most of what he says but he will add additional information outside of it. beware

Ok so here's a REAL evaluation of PTW's infamous 2090 class: it is not that bad, IF (and let me emphasize this) you have had prior Chem experience (AP or otherwise). Honestly, a lot of people here are complaining because they dont show up to lecture or dont pay attention. But if you show up, put in good effort studying, you will do fine

Not a bad person but teaching a genuinely impossible class. Admin designed it in a way to make you hate yourself and chemistry and succeeds in doing that by prelim 3. Overall, in general, not just with Peter but with any professor, CASE out of the course. You could have Newton teach it & it would still be shite. Pete: 2/5, CHEM 2090: -5/5
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Unfathomably bad
Idk what's going on in class ever and it's mostly self taught
Easily the worst prof at Cornell. Good luck
Very simple way to describe the course: doom, despair, and everything unfair
dude destroyed any love I had for chemistry.
boo tomato tomato
had to comfort crying friends after most prelims. save yourself.

BIG PETE… total disaster. People are saying he might be the WORST chemistry professor ever — maybe the worst professor of ANY subject. Lectures all over the place, nobody knows what's going on. Not a single person can believe it. Truly one of the biggest flops of all time!

ps. MY EYESs
bald LMFAO

Peter will lecture on topics way outside of the breadth of the course. Some of these topics are on the exam and others will never be heard of again and you have no way of knowing which are which. Prelims are absolutely brutal and he yelled at us and called us stupid after the class average was a 43%.

arguably the worst professor at Cornell. Run, unless you're stuck here because of 2090. Be prepared for confusing and incomprehensive lecture slides and hard tests with questions you have never seen before.

Peter somehow finds a way to crush curiosity and the will to learn in his students with his boring lectures that focus more on random bomb facts than the actual material. The slides are practically illegible, his tests are a poor reflection on the topics we learn (high weighting towards minute details that he skims over in class)
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: buddy cannot teach for shi, slides are dense and barely understandable, homework is fine but prelims questions are nothing like the practice problems, its always not too clear what prelim encompasses, this class will tank your grade and your mental health (unless ur a genius) do not take this class under big pete

He really loves bombs.The slides are not organized and the topics covered in class are not sequential. A class could cover quantum physics and the next class we balance equations. The information is scattered throughout the slides and u have to find information like a puzzle for some reason. Blames students for bad scores based on his teachings.
Don't do it. Don't even think about it.

For the love of lord, never take 2090; moreover, never take it with Wolczanski. The course is designed so that students learn 4 years of chemistry material in one semester. Knowledge of quantum mechanics and thermodynamics is a prerequisite.

This professor is smart but doesn't know how to teach. I took AP Chem before and every lecture he overcomplicates the topic and in general his lectures are very unorganized. He judges people that ask questions that he thinks are easy and already in the lecture slides. Also for some reason he loves explosions.
Berates students for asking questions, blames them for not studying when exam average was 43%.

Blames students for the test average being 40%, accuses students of "laziness" for allegedly not reading his lecture slides that are borderline incoherent. Jokes and puts down intellectually curious students for asking questions. 

Please, if you can, take the CASE exam to try to skip this course. If you must, rely on your TA.
This class will obliterated your self-esteem. I genuinely believe 95% of Cornell engineering students have imposter syndrome because of him (and the other 5% are the ones who get to skip this class)

Ok lecturer but very difficult prelims. Given the amount of material that has to be taught he goes over everything quickly. However if you are prepared to put the hours in you will be fine. I haven't taken chem past a sophomore year of basic high school chem and have scored above median on both prelims thus far. Just be prepared to ride the curve.
Avoid at all costs. Professor berates students for not studying after the class averages a 43% on an impossible exam. Expects a very strong background in chemistry coming into the class.

run. do not take. the whole distribution for exam 2 was literally a uniform graph around 40% and he blamed US for not studying when the whole class failed. its clearly a him issue and he needs to have his ego checked.
just literally don't think about taking this class AT ALL please avoid him in any way possible

came into this class with very minimal chem background (just hs sophomore year), and this class did NOT help me gain confidence. it seems like everyone else already took AP chem and even they are extremely confused. the test averages reflect how lost we all are. the slides are hard to digest and I'm so cooked, this should NOT be an intro course!!!

please please please do NOT take his class for chem 2090. you will regret it, it will tank your gpa, and destroy your self esteem. i know people hear others saying that they should drop a class and ignore that advice but for this one DONT. get the credit from a community college instead, i promise you this is not worth it.
Smart guy, bad teacher
Expects you to know a lot more than you already do coming into the class. Organic Chemistry Tutor is amazing.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: pray. just pray bc this class is HARD.
Goodbye America Hello Ithaca Gorges!
im cooked guys
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: He didn't teach stuffs that are on the homework. 0/5 professor. Don't ask a question in his class or else you get publicly humiliated...
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Genuinely seems to hate teaching; belittles even the (very) few students who have the courage to ask questions, sometimes insinuating they are on psychoactive substances.
The gorge will call you.

THE WORST chem prof i've ever seen. Ordered lectures in a very weird and unreasonable sequence. As someone who learned the entire syllabus before, even I struggled to catch up with what he was saying because more often than not, you'll see him mumbling about some random BS
If you love your life don't take his class
Slides are awful, too many irrelevant stuffs
so confusing.
never again. what was that first lecture. by slide 3, just know you have to self study..... no other prof/section, fight through it

Wolczanski is undoubtedly the worst professor I've ever had. His lectures and notes were incoherent, making it extremely difficult to learn the material. Problem sets were posted with as little as 3 days notice before the deadline and extremely time consuming. Doing well in his class requires a disproportionate amount of effort.
Him, one of his past students Devika Pokhriyal, and other students of his are cyberbullying students on facebook.

In 4 weeks of class (intro class for non chem majors btw, some of whom had no chemistry experience) he taught us practically everything that I learned from high school and AP chem classes. The work that he assigned to us and the material we had to learn was way beyond the scope of the class. Please listen to the people below: DONT TAKE HIS CLASS!!!
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: I had Prof Wolczanski during my first semester at Cornell. After completing my degree, I can say with certainty that he was the worst professor I had during my time here.

Not the best lecturer... doesn't rely so much on the textbook as on his own class notes, which are extremely confusing. Many of the topics he chooses to cover are above the level of a first-year chemistry class. That being said, the insane difficulty of his exams forces you to study the material on your own and learn a lot.
Horrible lecturer. It's clear he's smart as a whip and knows a lot about chemistry, but he has no idea how to teach it. Goes way beyond what should be taught in a general chem course.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Horrible guy, unwelcoming, gives you a thousand judgements if you don't know something. Homework is wayy too hard.

Wolczanski's Inorganic class was excellent. He covered all of the basics and did a solid job simplifying the more complicated concepts. The material might be a little hard to grasp at first, but I seemed to grasp it without going to office hours. Overall, a great professor that likes to challenge his students and grades fairly.

- very bad professor . He goes in depth in what you don't really have to know and therefore has you confused the ENITRE lecture . His exams are unimaginabley long and difficult . He wants you to be able to derive hard concepts rather than understanding the concepts you actually need to know in gen Chem. Terrible !

Lectures are long, boring and meandering. He has a somnolent effect on students and expects too much prior knowledge. Self-study is essential to pass. He  made no attempt to make the lecture interesting and it was jarring compared to Professor Crane. Tests set by him are unbelievably difficult. All in all, the worst teacher I have ever had.

Smart guy, but not a very good teacher. Taught concepts well beyond the scope of the course that were largely irrelevant to the vast majority of students without a lot of outside research; then tested for how well you were able to manipulate his lecture notes as opposed to for understanding of the material. Tried WAY too hard to be funny...

Hated this class the moment he stepped in. He rambles a lot, and I think half the time he did not follow the curriculum. Instead, he discussed his own personal research and went far beyond what was necessary during lecture. Was very disappointed with his ridiculous exams, but he is occasionally funny and is truly, extraordinarily brilliant.

Chem 2090 with Prof. Wolczanski sucked.  His lectures are not worth attending; just read the lecture notes he posts online.  Tests are hard, you'll somehow be saved by the curve.  I still don't understand chemistry.  He also has a wicked sense of humor (not in a good way- think mean).

Lectures can be  long and draggy. You can tell he is really smart and really into his research, however, he is just not right to teach an introductory chemistry class. He spends a greater proportion of his lecture talking about some abstract concepts and the worst part, is you can barely find the topics in the prescribed textbooks.
Poorly organized lectures that ramble for too long on irrelevant material. Assumed much too high a level of understanding for a general chemistry course. Lectures are absolutely useless.
worst professor I have ever had avoid taking a class with him at all costs that is all you need to know.

If every TA has to say "I don't know how to explain this to you guys in the context of what you're learning since I learned this in advanced inorganic chemistry," then something is wrong. He teaches all these terms and concepts without explaining what they are, and he teaches stuff that is nowhere in the textbook, so the textbook is useless. AWFUL!
Spends half the lecture doing complex derivations, then tells you it's not important and won't be on the exam.

Professor Wolczanski is said to be a very tough teacher, but everyone should go to his office hours and actually have a conversation with him before judging. He's an absolutely amazing professor and he truly is there to help. I wish I realized this earlier, so that I could've taken advantage of the time I had with him as a professor.

Clearly very knowledgable, but he is unable to pass information to students in an effective manner. His lectures are disorganized, and he assumes far too great of a basic level of understanding for a general chemistry clas.

This guy is the worst Professor I have ever had in my life. I have had professors who don't speak English that I would take over this guy. I would rather have my right ball chopped off than have to take another one of his classes.
absolutely terrible

Worst professor I have ever had at Cornell so far. Doesn't teach relevant material to the textbooks and teaches students as if we all understand physical chemistry on his level. No one ever understands his lectures and he mumbles a lot. He killed chemistry for me. Would avoid him if possible.

Talking to TAs or peers or reading your book is the better option for this class. He makes weird (and unnecessary) references in class that don't pertain to the lecture. His content is confusing and hard to comprehend.
You're better off staying home and reading the textbook
Lectures were not useful. Tests were hard and too long for the allowed time. Homework was fair, but learn the material from the textbook because it's not covered in lecture.

Lecture was useless I couldn't understand anything, take an AEW if you can get into one it helped me a lot. Tests are really not that terrible as most people are saying, most of the questions follow directly from the non-textbook hw questions. The mean is consistently pretty low so you're not expected to know everything. Final mean was 161300
useless lectures. hard exams. textbook material seems irrelevant.

Professor Wolczanski teaches a lot of interesting things that are not really contained in the textbook. If you have a TA that knows what she is talking about and can help you with the homework, it is possible to do well in the class. He assigns extra homework problems that he writes that end up being exactly like test questions.
Easily the worst professor I have ever had
He makes a lot of drug references in my class, maybe he likes to party? But this class is no party for the students; he makes everyone regret not placing out of this class.

Prof. Wolczanski covers interesting material not found in the textbook in his lectures. So, if you tend to skip classes, chances are that you wont get good grades just by reading the book.  The pdf notes he puts up before class are super helpful.

Lectures are virtually useless, as he gives material that not only fails to appear on the test, but that the TAs don't even know how to teach because they only just learned it in grad school classes. Also has a very monotone voice and uses high-level terminology that makes lectures almost impossible to understand.
I wish he would just be more respectful to his students. No one appreciates his disdainful comments about engineers when we ask for help.
One word. Terrible.
He takes pleasure in his students failing. He relishes in the feeling of superiority and enjoys picking on his students' struggle in the class.
he's impossible to understand and lectures about everything we don't need to know but leaves out everything we do. Sometimes it's actually funny how confusing he can be
I see him at parties, what a guy. Not a guy during lectures :
8th grade math yo, actually though just CASE out

One thought comes to mind when I think of PJ: What is life? This man simultaneously does not care about his class while still enjoying his class more than anyone else in the lecture. Although his lectures are truly incomprehensible, I think we should give him a break -- he is only super ten year. I can only hope to be as cool as him one day.

His lecture is completely useless and totally ruined my interest for chemistry. If you must gain credit for chemistry, take the placement test and avoid this guy if it is possible! Prelims and final are kind of irrelavant to the course material.

Horrible. He is out to get you. He is so condescending when you ask a question too. Know the extension problems and his HWs very well. Barely uses textbook material. Tested us on ridiculous stuff that was totally irrelevant to chem 2090. Go to his lectures or else you will fail. It is hard to hear what he is saying cause he mumbles a lot too.

Here's the advice to get through.  Go to all the lectures and read his notes before class (class has more meaning this way), thoroughly do the problems.  to study make sure you know every lecture and every extension by heart.  I promise if you do this you will beat the curve on every exam.

Avoid this guy! Lectures became useless very quickly. Teaches further than the curriculum requires. Makes a attempts at joking around during class, especially when it comes to bad grades. Prelims are OK if you study extension problems on HW... however, the Final was madness. Says he is an exceptional teacher, and that's why he "gets the big bucks"

OH MY GOD!  Professor Wolczanski is freaking amazing!  This guy must know everything possible about his field.  He's the Wayne Gretsky of organometallic chemistry.  Anyone who has Professor Wolczanski is amazingly lucky
did not learn a thing. horrible lecturer. tests are impossibly long but not too difficult. using his practice hw problems helps a lot with tests.
Avoid this professor at all costs. Especially is you aren't a ChemE. If you do end up in this course, use the previous exam questions on the problem sets to study for the tests. and good luck

The tests were by far that hardest I've taken at Cornell, more so than MATH 1910 which is supposedly to weed out the under achievers. He told us it would be one of the easiest classes we would take at Cornell. So not true.

To sum up CHEM 2090 as Wolczanski taught it: useless lectures and hard exams.  I generally did ok because I had a high quality non-AP chemistry course in high school; had I the AP credit, I would not have wasted my time with this class. In general, there was nothing I learned by going to lecture that I would not have by reading the book.

I learned so much in this class. It was a lot of work and we covered much more material than we were supposed to, but I loved it in the end. Prof. Wolczanski is just brilliant. He seems a bit mean initially, but he's actually really nice and extremely helpful.

I took advanced inorganic chemistry organometallic chemistry class from PTW when I was a graduate student spode. I was initially terrorized, but broke my ass and did OK. PTW recognized and appreciated that. If you are a whiner, you are over.

The absolute worst prof I've had at Cornell. His tests were killer (and the means reflect it), his demeanor in lecture can be summed up best by his sarcastic sneers at what he believes are stupid questions or stupid students, and he has no desire to help his students. Don't count on his office hours being any better either.

Had him for Chem 216. A total disaster. I remember the day when the mean on the prelim was a 26. But I have to agree with one of the other posts here, by the time i got around to chem 410, things had definitely improved. not sure if it was teaching non-freshmen or the subject matter or just a lot of therapy, but whatever it was, it was not too bad.

It's apparent that this guy is in academia to do his research and views teaching as an unfortunate liability of his job. He is often late to class, and wouldn't lift a finger if it would help one of his students attain a successful career in chemistry. Moreover he is belligerant and heartless, and often embarrasses students who ask questions.
No Comments
WOW
Worst teacher I had at cornell. Mean on tests = 40% You don't learn anything. Its all about competition. Take 207 alternate chem course - will not affect your engineering standing at all.

Moves very quickly, doesn't stop for questions, and not much fun to listen to.  Rarely gives you any background info on WHY you're covering the material you're covering.  Looking around the room it's amusing to see so many confounded students.

Had him freshman year for 216 and thought he was pure evil.  But by the time I had him again senior year for 410, I had come to respect his intelligence and humor and stopped being so scared of him.  Plus, he's really good at drawing icosahedrons!
While demonstrating wave propagation with a long slinky: "I'm a married man, so I haven't had much practice with my hand." --Wolczanski... these are the only words I remember from his course.

It was the material that this course covers that is very difficult (take 207), not the teacher. Although the professor was not overtly enthusiastic, he was by no means Evil or sadistic, he just did his job and that is why he is only a mediocre professor.
Absolutely Evil man.  His only interest in teaching is so that he can prove he is smarter than his students.  Steer clear at all costs.  Avg test scores of 41%
When taking the final, everyone opened up to the first page and the entire room started laughing... there is no way this man should be teaching.  absolutely horrible
Steer Clear of this class at ALL costs.  Take the alternative chemistry for engineering majors (207?).  You will be happy you made the choice

MEAN MAN. He is the epitome of a Cornell professor who cares more about his research than the students. Even the TA's for the course could not do some of the exam questions. Why is it necessary to trick students when we are here to learn & graduate? W
he teaches VERY FAST and too much in one semester-- he may go into too much depth...  but hes a funny old man, and a good professor i gotta hand it to him, hes really smart and knows his stuff.
He teaches way too much material at too high a level.  I retained nothing I "learned."
No Comments
THE STUFF WAS TRANSCENDENTALLY IMPOSSIBLE TO DIGEST, MAN!
ARRRRRGHHHH!!!!!!! So... evil....
No Comments
eeeeeeeeeeeeeeevil
horrible teaching style, horrible course

Kelly was a really great professor. She regularly takes the time to stop and check if we have questions about the material instead of just steaming ahead. She also gave us a short break in the middle of lecture to go over logistics like deadlines and tests. This helped us to stay organized and refresh us in the middle of class.

Overcomplicates simple calc 2 concepts. Homework also gets longer and complicated for no reason. She also isn't transparent about class mean and median which makes it difficult to gauge how you're doing. There are quizzes in every discussion so it's mandatory to attend.

If her class is open, then take it. She is an amazing lecturer. As a student who did not take BC in high school, she was very thorough in teaching new concepts. She is also very accessible during OHs.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Professor Pohland was really nice in this class was just sometimes unnecessarily difficult. Homework is pretty hard but doable. The discussion was not helpful because the TA was going through a worksheet really fast. Everything is graded very picky.

I took Math 2930 Differential Equations over the summer with Prof. Pohland. Honestly, she's the best professor I've had at Cornell. Very caring and accessible for students, always willing to help you out if you have questions, etc.

Professor Pohland is an awesome professor and cares about the academic and holistic success of her students. While MATH1110 posed to be a huge struggle for me, she truly went above and beyond to give me leeway in order to succeed in the course. She is the best out of all the Calc 1 Professors.

She was very nice and a great lecturer. Her analogies in class were very easy to follow and her explanations of the material was helpful in better grasping the content. She often spent 5-10 minutes explaining/answering one person's question that she could have just answered after class. The exams were much harder than hw problems

Professor Pohland gives solid lectures and is willing to answer questions before, during, and after class. After a disastrous midterm performance, she was willing to meet with me individually to better strategize for the final exam, and I brought myself back from the brink with her help. Approachable and kind.
Lectures were alright. Would often spend way too much time answering one person's questions. Tests were hard, and they never gave practice representative of the actual test.

Professor Pohland is very caring and considerate. Her lectures are clear and easy to understand. Her office hours are fantastic and I cannot recommend them enough- she always took questions and never judged for questions. Excellent professor.

HIGHLY recommend Prof. Pohland for Calc I. If you see her section, take it!! I dropped this class before because of a bad professor but because of her I excelled this time. Always willing to help, she gives mini lectures and encourages questions during class instead of only making you do group work, and is easy to meet with outside of class.
While I understand why some people feel uncomfortable with her fickle style but she is a good person and is underrated here.

Prof Sun cares extremely deeply about her students and their learning. Took her for 3525 last fall and couldn't recommend it more - everyone complaining seems to be overreacting. Be proactive and show up in her class and it will be fantastic. She really cares and I will absolutely be taking her classes again.
Nah, trust you'll be saving your academic career and sanity by not taking this class.

To clarify the amount of reviews for 2435 are higher than the amount of 2435 students in the spring lol. The class was definitely extremely difficult but the reviews are a bit harsh. It's a history class: participate and do the readings. 
I agree that background knowledge was required for the presentations/interviews--we go to an ivy league though.

Stop the hate lmao, she was a bit harsh but learnt way more to account for it. These reviews are taken over by a select few who decided to review multiple times. Coursework was rough, but I came in the class to learn and got what I wanted. You have to learn to deal with it.
ok i lowkey had to learn a bunch in this class and got treated a bit harshly, but yall gotta realize that sometimes it has to be hard to learn... like??
idk why people are being so rude for no reason??

Worst history class I've ever taken. Expectations/criteria unclear and changed syllabus as late as March;as a result, grading is based on vibes. Graded on an interview+presentation; you aren't taught how to do any of these things. Expects high amount of background knowledge on subject matter. Very disorganized class, with no clear direction. Avoid.
I have honestly never received such a poor and unfair grade in my entire academic career.

If the course really sparked so much resentment among so many students, they should collectively report it to the administration - the professor's tenure is unlikely to be revoked, but the history department could ask her to teach fewer courses and do more research. There's not much point in commenting here.

She dumped the course load on students with zero guidance, then acted like giving actual feedback was beneath her. I went to office hours and somehow ended up in a therapy session about me, because she refused to talk about the class. Then she handed out grades like she was drawing numbers from a hat. And let's not forget the unprovoked yelling,
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Disappointed in the history department. If you decide to take her course, just hope she read these reviews and learned to do better. Else, unless you're really insightful and can politely talk over her interruptions, you might get on her nice list. Any student any study? She'll tell you off if you don't know everything before taking the class.

should've dropped this class instead of hoping it'd get better.agree w previous reviews+anecdote abt her disorganization:each class,1student presents on the reading due.there's no tracker on who presents so we had classes where two students presented or where she rambled bs for an hour(doubt she read).the 5 was probably written by her phd student.

Add on: she will give you NO indication of grades, bullies students who are presenting, and bases grading on whether she likes you. I have an above 4.0 GPA and this class single-handedly ruined my prospects of summa cum laude. She does not teach whatsoever, instead relying on students to give presentations on articles written by other scholars.

I'm pretty sure the 5 below was written by her! Worst professor I've ever had at Cornell. Once in class, students asked clarifying questions, she responded, "I don't care, I am tenured." As a student who has taken many history classes, I'm shocked at the administration for allowing such an unqualified, rude, and vague professor into the department.
these past reviews are all outrageous! Prof. Sun was a great teacher, she taught me a lot.

absolutely avoid... the content may be interesting but the professor is so rude and disorganized that it takes away from any enjoyment at all. she doesn't communicate her expectations and then lets students' grades take the hit for it. always interrupting people, making vague and irrelevant metaphors.. and god forbid you ask her a question!

If you like your GPA, avoid this class. Professor does not communicate grading standards at all, and your end of semester grade is seemingly random. She likes to bully students in class, and teaches nothing but expects students to already know everything. If you like Maoism, borrow a book from the library. Do not take this class.

Add on pt2: I was SO excited to take this course to learn about Maoism as it's been an ongoing interest of mine. Was most excited for THIS class this semester. Not only disappointed, but received an arbitrary grade after the class gave me nothing.

Add on the prev review: never had a prof that didn't teach but didn't make time to answer questions. Asked what she thought to be thought provoking questions that didn't relate to topic at all. Constantly cuts people off mid sentence to insert her -irrelevant- point. Gave feedback on arbitrary presentation flaws such as what bullet points you used.
she'll respect you by learning your name but that'll go out the window once you get up and present!

went into the class expecting to learn from her but every class is a presentation on the reading due that day from a student. already disappointed. on top of that, she interrupts student presentations so nitpick irrelevant things and go on tangents using metaphors that barely connects to the conversation. didnt learn anything from her. disappoined.

Mr. Perez is an extremely enthusiastic lecturer, and the emotion in his voice makes his teaching powerful and engaging. The class is largely run by the TAs—you are assigned two who oversee your cohort of about 20 students. The course has weekly case studies focused on real-world business problems, all graded with a high level of detail.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--So many papers

reviews.comment: Genuinely blessed with the TAs I got for this class. The professor himself is very passionate about the topics; however, it is hard to understand him at times because of his thick accent, and his lectures were unorganized. TAs are the backbone of this course, and the final project is heavily weighted, so plan accordingly.

Very TA grading heavy, which can be unfair if they are tough. You should try to participate as much as possible, but most of the time the lectures won't make sense. Try to talk to everyone around you because you'll have a group presentation with them worth a lot of your grade
This class was genuine hell. Learned nothing and had to pull a couple of all nighters.
Generally random and pointless lectures, TAs grade everything (very harshly) but put in the work and you can do fine
