PSYCH 2800 was a really interesting course. Lecture is definitely super important - he would include niche comments from lecture on tests. Top 3 class I've taken at Cornell. If you are interested in the class content, the grades will follow. Would not recommend taking it as a non-psych major for fun/distribution, unless S/U. His outfits also eat!

Would not take this class unless it's required - big mistake as someone who's not a psych major and just thought the subject matter would be interesting. He's a good lecturer but the grade is 80% based on tests, so makes it very hard to do well if you're not a strong test taker.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Not an easy class, and I wouldn't recommend taking it just for fun. The material is interesting, especially if you have an interest in the social sciences, but the class structure is a bit strict and most of your grade is from exams that are quite difficult. I've taken several Psych/HD courses, and this was definitely one of the hardest ones.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Don't take this class unless you have to. Grade is 80% made up of prelims + final, and the exams are very difficult. Hopefully class is curved. Content is interesting but not worth the trouble
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He is funny and tries to be engaging although some of what he says can be offensive. Class is made up of only two exams, a final, and a paper. People usually do very poorly but the class is curved and there is extra credit. I would try to find other options and avoid this class if you care about getting high grades, otherwise might be interesting.

As a lecturer, Prof. Gilovich is pretty good! He includes anecdotes and videos to keep everyone entertained, but sometimes there were charts involved alongside other material that was not very well explained and it became confusing. The two in-class prelims and the final were definitely challenging, but the class is curved thankfully (life saver)
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Tough exams with questions I've never seen before. The lowest grade I've ever got in the field of Psych.
It is tough to get an A in the class and lectures are quite boring

Super engaging lectures. Go to them please. Use of videos, film clips, pop culture references and stories was phenomenal. Do the outside readings because he will ask super specific questions on tests about them.

Professor Gilovich is simply an amazing lecturer. The lectures are super engaging, fun, and very informational. He is also a great actor. However, you will have a hard time in Pysch 2800 if you don't go to lecture. There are no assignments and only a few supplemental readings, so the GO TO LECTURES! Would 100% take his class again.

The material itself is NOT HARD, however the average grade is a 70%. The tests are insane and he attempts to incorporate horrible "real-world examples", which force you to guess even if you know the material. He also teaches borderline race science with his obsession with the East-West difference, and defends it by saying he is "just the messenger"
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I am paying 80k in tuition with some of it going towards a class where the average is a failing grade. Even though this professor is chill during class and about attendance, I have never felt stupider. He returns grades almost a month later and is the harshest grader I have had. Do not take this class unless you want a WF on your transcript.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: As a psych major, found this course relatively straightforward. Extra credit and office hours really helped me get the A. Extremely interesting and relevant course material that I talk about them to people outside of class. Don't focus on the minute details, but sit back and listen to understand the concepts, and ask a lot of questions!

PowerPoints are so vague, if you miss lecture, all you will see are pictures on the slides with no notes to help you out. He wrote the textbook, so expect to read a lot. Prelim questions are so specific, which is not ideal for an introductory course. Passionate about his field, but VERY politically bias in how he presents materials.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Prelims are very difficult, but the papers are graded easy. Hard to do really well but if you do the readings and go to to class you should be in decent shape. He's not very accessible, the times I asked for help I didn't get it. Personally I'd avoid this class specifically, it was too much work for an elective.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Gives extremely convoluted explanations on topics. Not the best lecturer. Least favorite class at Cornell so far. Does not really care about logistics. Do not take it if you are not a psych major. I have taken several classes that covered some experiments taught in this class and still find it quite hard to follow. Tests are unfair and hard.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: The best professor I have ever had. Class is interesting and thought-provoking; tests are very fair and doable if you do the readings. 10/10 would recommend!!!

Professor Gilovich was perhaps the greatest lecturer I have ever had while in school. He made each class interesting and applicable while crafting lectures to interest his audience. My only regret is that I didnt take a course with him sooner to allow me to take more courses that he teaches. Highly recommend

Tests you on ridiculously minute details, oftentimes mentioned in passing during lecture or readings. ALWAYS go to class, do the readings beforehand, study, and you will do fine. Slack off and those tests will kill you.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: Captivating professor. He posts lecture slides, but I found that I could have taken better in-class notes. He includes questions directly from the textbook on the prelims and final, so read the textbook. He doesn't return prelims but lets you see them during OH, and he reuses several questions on the final. Final40% Prelims20% Paper10%

The best professor I've ever had. Interesting, intelligent and incredibly talented at explaining concepts during lecture. Go to lecture and do the readings and you'll be fine. Pre-lims are slightly difficult but fair. Lecture is always interesting. Term paper is easy but make sure to incorporate a lot of your own ideas

The lectures are fine. The problem is the material. You learn little about social psychology. Instead, you memorize experiments. Take the class if you are a psych major. Otherwise, stay far away.  He also claimed that psych majors know more about economics than econ majors, so I guess his brain just works differently than everybody else. Ironic.

Gilovich is a great professor. He is smart and his lectures are incredibly interesting. He is quite a rockstar in psych and he exudes knowledge about the subject. The tests are straightforward though they require a very thorough reading of the textbook

Terrible class. Gilovich clearly cares and knows his stuff, but the class is very poorly organized. It's a large lecture for a topic that NEEDS to be taught through discussion, so lectures end up being him rambling about random topics in psychology. Grading is unfair, super harsh, and nitpicky. Unless you're a psych. major, DON'T take this class.

Professor Gilovich is one of my favorite professors. He really knows his stuff and shares it with you in a really interesting way. I really enjoyed his lectures. That said, his tests can be hard, even when you study; there's a lot of nitpicky details from the books. Overall, definitely take the class. You learn a TON of interesting things!

Loved this class, so many interesting ideas applicable to everyday life. He is a fantastic lecturer and he also offered a review session before every test that I would recommend. This class is hard however. Make sure that you do the readings because his exams can be tricky. One of my favorite classes at Cornell.

My favorite class at Cornell, definitely take it. So interesting and applicable to daily life. Gilovich is a rock star in the psych world and he knows all the material by heart + wrote the textbook. Both prelims and the final are horribly specific. Read the textbook word for word. Attendance not required but will definitely help you on the test
Psych 2800 is a great class, the tests are a little tricky but overall everything was good. Make sure to do the readings!

Really great lecturer. Tests are a little hard and readings can make or break you on exams. He is a little scattered sometimes but overall I had a great experience with him as a lecturer! He also held review sessions before tests, which were nice.
A great professor! His lectures were fun and interesting every time, and he played cool music and videos. Very understanding and helpful.
While psych 2800 requires a little bit of work, it is worth it.  Very interesting and a must-take for psych majors and a great class for non psych majors.

I took him for Social Psych and the class was fantastic! He's a really great lecturer and the material was really interesting. His prelims were really hard though; he's REALLY into nitpicky details from the book that you could overlook. Despite the hard tests though, it's a great class!

Goes on ad nauseum about the smallest details and typically goes off on a tangent during his lectures. I am a psych major, and even I have difficulty finding interest in his lectures. At least the TA is bearable.

Professor Gilovich is a great guy, but this class was SO hard. The prelims were ridiculous and borderline unfair. He packed so much info into his lectures that it was nearly impossible to stay awake and keep good notes. I tried really hard in this class, and it still didn't pay off. Only take this class if you love psych more than anything.

I took writing section (WIM), so experience was different. But Tom is a great researcher and speaker. Lectures do trail off in liveliness towards end of 75 minutes, partly because he throws out so many experiments. I didn’t study, of course, but when he went over prelims, I was grateful I took WIM — MC are hard! I Recommend WIM, TA was very chill.

Very good lecturer. Engaging, never very dull, and very clear explanations. However, exams are very tough (means in  low 60s usually). The class is also a lot of work, as there are mandatory and often lengthy readings. It's also a little annoying that he expects you memorize multiple experiments. Overall, class is good, but definitely demanding!
Tom is a chill professor. His explanations are very clear.

Gilovich is by far one of the most captivating lecturers at Cornell. Fascinating subject and he makes it fun and interesting. His class was tough, but if you attend classes and do the reading you will be fun. Definitely take this before you leave Cornell!

I was a little surprised about how boring I found this class, especially with all the comments on this site. He was my least engaging teacher, and would throw a TON of experiments at you for each topic. The tests were graded unnecessarily harsh, too. He curves, though, so don't worry. And I recommend taking notes on the slides as you go.

Probably the best prof. I've had at Cornell. Very enthusiastic about the material. Also knows the stuff well, since he wrote the textbook and conducted a lot of experiments presented in class. Each lecture contains a lot of information, so make sure you stay focused through out. Tests are hard, but they are well designed and fair. TAKE THIS CLASS!

Incredible, incredible speaker-- never even went close to nodding off during his class. The way he presents material is incredible. (Tests are hard, not going to lie, but if you do the reading and really remember the experiments it talks about, you'll do well. Another plus-- he helped write the book, so all the lecture stuff is covered.)

He is probably the best prof I've had at cornell. He's extremely interesting and a fantastic lecturer. He uses the book he wrote for the class and the readings were almost enjoyable to do. The prelims were pretty hard but they're curved so it doesn't matter too much. Not the easiest course in the world but not a lot of work definitely worth it!
fun class
GREAT PROF.  GREAT CLASS.  tough grader

Good professor and interesting lectures, but made tests intentionally hard, and would go over prelim answers extremely quickly so you couldn't really understand why you got certain questions wrong. One of those professors who will pick out small details from textbook that are often looked over to test you on. Read CAREFULLY. Otherwise, OK class.

Interesting lectures, but incredibly unclear and tangential.  Designs tests to be intentionally difficult and low-scoring.  Irrational requisite for attention to detail.  Requires much more work than the average course in the department.  Only advised for students who have a profound interest in the subject matter.

Tests are incredibly difficult multiple choice questions, often confusing and contradictory ten times.  Tries to make class hard.  In all, it was doable if you put the extra effort in, but not sure it was worth the time. Means on exams were low, but lectures were (sometimes to rarely) interesting
Great lecturer!! good class
good lecturer and interesting material but tests were unreasonably hard for a class that most took for an elective

This class was great! material is fascinating and gilovich is a great lecturer. Regan talks waaaay too fast, but they both give outlines for each lecture which makes it easier. Pay attention to experiments and do the reading. Take this class if you can!!
He's a great professor, and a great speaker. When I'm trying to do a presentation for another class, I try to speak like Professor Gilovich, and the presentation goes well.

Not only is Gilovich brilliant, but his lectures are interesting and even humorous. Sometimes his lectures are identical to the reading, but that's because he co-wrote the textbook. Tests are tricky, but if you go to class, do the reading, and study, you will do fine.

Take this class! Gilovich and Regan give you lecture outlines, which means that instead of frantically scribbling notes for 75 minutes straight, you can listen to the (mostly entertaining) lectures. Gilovich throws in a lot of fun material, and is clearly passionate about psychology. The textbook he co-wrote is sometimes even-- gasp!-- fun to read.

Neat guy. Knows the subject intimately; he's really well-known in the social psychology world. Heck, he was one of the co-authors of the brand new (and very useful) textbook. Good sense of humor, too. But yeah, prelims are pretty hard. Take good notes (or get TakeNote) and do all of the readings carefully, especially in the textbook!
Very knowledgable and a good lecturer. Tests are very difficult though.

Probably the best professor I had at Cornell. His lectures are incredibly interesting and very practical. Difficult class but if you study the material and read the book, its not too bad. This class is definitely worth it. Gilovich rocks!

Gilovich is the greatest! He's very intelligent and extremely amusing. The subject is interesting, but Gilovich makes it intriguing. I signed up for 350 next semester cause I thought Gilovich was teaching it, but it turns out he's not, and I am really sad. Oh well. Definitely take 280!

Gilovich is the man!  He and Regan have very different styles, but I found both to be effective lecturers and I don't even think it's fair to compare.  Gilovich is "flashier", I guess - powerpoints, videos, funny stories.  The class is tough, tests especially, but well worth it.  I loved it!
Very hard class (tests are brutal). Very nice and helpful guy (I found him a bit less helpful than Regan). Lectures are very interesting and good.  Great class

A pretty good, entertaining lecturer. BTW, I strongly disagree with the person who perferred Gilovitch over Regan. They’re both good profs - Gilovitch might be more charming, but I think Regan actually has the edge on explaining things and caring about students. Good class overall if you're into it.
Loved his class.  If you think he's overrated, know it isn't so: I read his book as an undergrad, and now 10 years later I have to read it again for a law school class.
this guy is awesome! the best teacher i had at cornell!
No Comments
Gilovich is awesome.  He is very intelligent and always clear.  I wish it was just Gilovich teaching this class (rather than him and Regan).

Knows his stuff, but he speaks in a monotone, after a while it gets boring. He has no facial expression!  This guy makes psych more boring than it has to be.  His questions are tricky on exams, esp for 280.
im naming my first son after him!
Great professor, funny and helpful, but sometimes not quite clear.
really good lecturer, inserted a good amount of fun material into class. made all the topics interesting, even when describing the details of an experiment. take psych 280

Good prof, tries hard to makes class interesting. He uses good examples and makes points clear.  Have to say though, tests are short, so pressured to do well on so few questions and his questions on tests are ambiguous.
This is the most boring class I ever took at Cornell. More boring than Chem 207.
Awesome professor. Why can't more instructors be like him?
No Comments
He's like the nicest guy ever! Plus, he rides his bike to class! Awesome teacher!
Really hot, cute, i love him, i want to have his children, he is so passionate... about psychology... =D
Great guy, boring lectures. your grade relies on your TA.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: super chill guy

Honestly, this is an amazing class. Take it. It has a pretty simple format: one midterm, two essays, and some in-class assignments/ quizzes. He also gave an option for extra credit. Every assignment allows you to explore what YOU are interested in. He is so flexible,open to connecting with students beyond the classroom, so passionate. Trust me-TAKE
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Honestly he is amazing. This course you get to explore so much of what you are interested in. I wish he explored more Latin history. But he is so much fun, love the music he plays in class and the topics we discuss. Really dynamic format. Super great. Easy if you try on assignments. Trust!.

Lectures were all over the place, he YAPPS about irrelevant information 80% of the class. We had 6-7 different books to read almost at random across the semester. Papers are extremely long for an intro class, and even longer readings. Extremely boring lectures, only survived because of my TA! (They are 100% responsible for all the grading).
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--So many papers--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Prof. Nagaraja is one of the sweetest professors in ILR. We had several papers in the class that TAs would grade, but Nagaraja was always willing to review your paper and answer any questions you have.
Professor Nagaraja is great. He's really passionate and knowledgeable, and is always willing to meet with you.

He is very nice and approachable practically begging students to ask him questions and go to office hours. Slides are way too text-heavy, but he posts them making attending is unnecessary. The TA does all the grading so I can't comment on that.
Very nice person. Approachable outside of class. Does not grade harshly. Lectures are confusing and not particularly helpful.

Very nice and passionate professor but his lectures were confusing and hard to follow. Attendance is mandatory but I did not learn much within those lectures. I found doing the readings and going to office hours much more useful. Overall, he is a generous grader if you do the necessary work.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Boring lectures, but he's a chill guy and doesn't grade too hard.

All papers. Your grade lives and dies by the TA. This semester, there was a probably a 1.5 gpa spread between the two TAs and nothing was done about it. Your grade is about as meritocratic as a roulette table. Lectures are circular, directionless, and unnecessary. Just read the pertinent readings a week before the papers are due.

I kinda disagree with comments for Professor Nagaraja. I first thought he was not good at teaching. However, as I started to listen carefully what he was talking about in class, I learnt a lot. He was very informative and knowledgable. Moreover, he also gave me the freedom to explore my interests under the course framework. Very nice guy.

He's a really nice guy, but an AWFUL professor. Lectures are incredibly disorganized. Very little correlation between readings, lectures, and assignments. TAs do all the work and were pretty short staffed. Do not take him if you do not have to.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Nagaraja seems like a good dude who is interested in the subject matter. However, it is clear that he is uninterested in teaching. His lectures are long, boring, and confusing, and his communication outside of class is terrible(cannot use canvas to save his life). Really disappointed becasuse I thought this would be my favorite class.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Simply not a good professor
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Would REALLY not recommend

Lectures are boring and unnecessary -- read and take good notes from the books and participate in discussion and you'll be fine. Grade can really depend on your TA but go to office hours and have them look at your essays at multiple steps in the process to make sure you're doing what they want to. He's not a bad guy he's just not a good professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Not a great lecturer, His powerpoint slides were really hard to follow and chaotic. He was a really easy grader though, so that was nice. There was lots of assigned reading that you didn't really have to do, and his content was very one-sided. Overall okay experience but I wouldn't recommend if you want to get a lot out of the class.

Professor is clearly very smart, but his classroom management isn't great. He assigned a lot of reading for this class and didn't get assignments back to us for a very long time. The three essays he did assign were essentially the same prompt each time? Interesting class to take if you're invested in the books but hard to get a lot of out of him.

Funny dude - I really enjoyed his lectures. The course does not cover much content so discussions were constant review, didn't have to spend much time outside of class (please still study though). Exams were straightforward. You are set up to succeed in the class - wanted to put this review so students are deterred from taking his class, he's good.

Great lecturer excited about the material that tries to make sure that all students learn in his class. It is an interesting and enjoyable class if you stay engaged, as he also definitely cares about participation. Class doesn't have too much homework but requires paper reading. I and other friends all had positive experiences

Grading criteria never clear, terrible handwriting, poor time management, teacher doesn't care what student learn, too tough on details, recitation class misleading, encourage memorizing rather than learning.
The worst professor ever in my life.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES

reviews.comment: He is one of the worst professors I have ever had at Cornell.  The guy does not care about his students, never answers emails, barely gives any homework (which makes it impossible to study) and is so boring in lecture.  I think he really doesn't care if you do well or not and makes his tests really hard.  He also has awful handwriting.
Amazing teaching. Well organized study material and very helpful on asking questions. Lucid presentation on self assembly.

Very crappy professor. No website, not enough homeworks given, poor handwriting, and no assistance before examinations. I honestly think that he is one of the most lazy and unmotivated professors who simply does not care whether or not you learn anything. I learnt nothing and he sucks.
great professor, funny, clear, has a good understanding of material and seems very interested in what he teaches, helpful

The project has no rubric no matter how much you ask. Then you get a random grade a few peers come up with. Your project can be better than others but you will get a worse grade. learn very little in this class. Avoid this class please. Its just a shame there are not other good ML classes available. it forces people to take this class.
Her lectures are pretty interesting, and has a project-based approach.

very accomplished person but atrocious professor... put out one of the most poorly prepared prelims i have ever seen at cornell and came up with a very unfair and universally hated peer grading scheme that students abused to help themselves in terms of the curve
It is a difficult course, but if you work hard you will learn a lot. Recommend!

I find it difficult to believe that Cornell University would include this professor in its team of excellent professors. You can just skip class because the lectures are worthless. Support from TAs and Professors are also non-existent. The group projects are graded with unsupervised peer review, incentivizing people to sabotage others. Don't take.

Rarely am I compelled to post a review on Rate My Professor but this class was so horrible and utter garbage that I must insist. The class is hard for no reason at the expensive of learning, I can probably find more help from Scooby Doo than the Professor and TA team. There is no transparency in grading. Don't take any of her classes stay far away.

Udell's explanations were so unclear to the point that I had to google everything she said - the Internet did a much better job than her in teaching. Grading was not transparent - hw instructions and grading rubric were very vague. She made us do peer grading - this coupled with non-transparent grading incentivized students to sabotage their peers.

Probably one of the worst classes I've taken at Cornell. Udell is a confusing lecturer, and the TAs for the class are very focused on not giving you the answers to problem sets, even if it comes at the expense of a student's understanding. Very unclear and somewhat arbitrary grading rubrics. Avoid at all costs.

In MSE, you will probably have to take a decent amount of classes with Umbach. He is pretty not well-received as he doesn't teach well and tends to give grades arbitrarily based on personal feeling. You probably won't learn much in his classes and will def feel frustrated, but if you BS effort and try to seem nice, you'll get a better grade...

Umbach is a super nice guy and his office hours are key for the problem sets! However, the class itself is super disorganized and nanotech is such a broad topic that it sometimes seems like we're just learning random stuff. Prelims were okay, but quizzes were pretty bad
reviews.rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: exactly what others talk about. He expects too much prior knowledge about chem and physics even if its engri class and the class is really boring and long.
Great professor! Definitely take as prospective ChemE or MatSci

Umbach, although tough, is an entertaining lecturer and a kind professor. Arguably, Nanotechnology is one of the hardest ENGRI classes, and as such, many people are disappointed with workload. However, the class was inspirational, and it completely changed my personal career path; as such, I highly recommend the class for motivated individuals

Decent professor. His lecture notes are extremely dense, but he posts all the power points which are very convenient for reviewing for prelims. For the most part the tests are fair. The homeworks didn't seem particularly useful but they weren't very hard. The material is often interesting, other times not so much. Overall a decent experience
horrible teacher. expects you to know too much. ridiculous tests and unfair grading policies. do not take nanotech!
He's super awesome. Love this dude.
No Comments
Long, long, boring lectures. Expects you to have prior knowledge about nano before the class. Class was very difficult.
Easy class, easy professor. Take it with Umbach.
i failed finance w another professor. ukhov makes info clear, practical, & understandable. it's almost like having a good professor can make a subject feel easy!
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Great professor who cares about his students, especially  if you go to class and get to know him. Loves athletes. His lectures tend to be long and a bit boring but great professor overall.

Such a cool guy. The stories/examples he uses in class are hilarious, but also teach you the material. If you go to him during his office hours he is extremely helpful! You don't have to attend class, but he may boost your grade up if you do go.
Legit professor of finance. hotel school sucks less with real finance professors like ukhov

Awesome professor/ class. Very funny and engaging lectures, often sidetracks to relevant current events. Straight-forward material, easy homework and exams. If you are looking for easy A and a practical, interesting class, this is it
Best prof in the hotel school

This guy is a boss.  Doesn't really care if you come to class but will bump your grade up if you do (not that you'll need it).  Class teaches you the basics of finance and he keeps it pretty simple.  A quality professor who knows his stuff.  Highly recommend it.
EASY TO PASS

My favorite professor at Cornell thus far. Extremely smart and knowledgable, and also willing to help his students in office hours. Go to office hours and get to know him-- he's a great guy with funny stories, and it will help you in the class. If you show up and pay attention, no reason you can't do well.
Took this class based off the rate my professor reviews and it went great. He's knowledgeable but sometimes disorganized when posting HWs/cases. Favorites blonde girls so you might be in luck.

This guy is hilarious. Has a heavy Russian accent, which makes his stories even funnier. Cares that you learn the material. Exams are much harder than the practice problems and homework he gives. But all in all, one of the best professors I've had simply for the pure entertainment value of his stories.
This guy and class are awesome.

GREAT Professor. had him for advanced corporate finance. new guy, but dont be scared, he knows what he is talking about and has a genuine interest in the students. Very helpful at office hours, and makes advanced corporate finance interesting. Oh, his random facts about the finance world are the great. All hail Buffet!

I had the privilege of taking three classes with the late Professor Uri Possen, including an independent study. He was one of my best and favorite professors at Cornell. He taught financial economics, a subject I loved. He helped me sign up for an internship, because of which I got the visa papers. Always there for his students. I miss you. RIP!

He doesn't explain the models in detail during the class, rather you should study on the textbook. However, he and his TA like to help students preparing the exams. He provides good insights on the current macroeconomic situation especially on fiscal policy and financial economics . Generous grader.
The problem sets is all that will prepare you for the tests. If you miss lecture, be sure to get the packets you missed.
His lecture is a bit tedious, but he is personally nice guy and always willing to help students. When you rely on textbook, which is the best i have ever seen, you can ace this course
Horrible teacher. Disorganized. His TA was worse than he was. DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS!

Good Professor. Very interesting course. Go to all lectures and take good notes; cross-reference with the book and go to office hours for whatever you don't get, he'll appreciate you all the more. Start the paper assignment EARLY!!
Very interesting class, and a professor who has a great grasp of the material.
Really interesting course. I enjoyed it. Professor very clear and helpful.

I had literally already taken finance when I took this class, so it was kind of a joke. But he's not a bad guy. Strangely though, I got a higher grade in the Johnson school finance class I took previously than I did this one. Odd.
Hard to get by on just his lectures because they're so disorganized. Tests are fun though and he's very entertaining.
Cute professor. Probably one of the easiest chem professor in Cornell and I am not complaining. Good and helpful guy

This guy is so enthusiastic about his job that he makes me want to be a professor. And he loves chemistry and his lectures are always interesting. But wow, some of the homeworks and tests are pretty hard. And the concepts. The only issue with this guy is that he doesn't actually TEACH very well...

He's a great professor in one sense - he's very kind and funny and overall a really nice person.  Open to questions etc.  He sings in class and shows us amusing videos  But I don't feel like I learn chemistry from his lectures.  he just demonstrates calculations.  As with Zax, I feel like I learn the most from the supplemental prof Dr. Johnson.

Some like him since he's funny and charming, some don't since he wastes some time singing and telling stories.  I liked him not only for his stories and jokes, but because he really does know what he's talking about.  If you follow his lectures, you'll do well.
nice guy, but is one of the worst professors here. He moves very slowly, and lectures are absolutely pointless.
He is a cute person, with his silly songs that he sings and whatnot, but he is not a very good professor. Moves at a glacial pace through the material and lectures are generally useless.

Amazing Chem teacher who makes use of class time to make sure everyone understands the concepts by going over examples and demonstrations in detail.  He makes class fun and enjoyable with his personal anecdotes and songs.  Overall, great teacher and is a class everyone should take before graduating Cornell.
really old and cute with the besttt accent and i love when he sang opera he was so good! kind of a sucky chem professor though...

Love, love, love! Usher is a great professor and an outstanding opera singer. As long as you understand the lectures, textbook chapters, and how lab demonstrates what you're learning in class, you should be fine! Do plenty of practice problems, read the textbook, and go through lecture notes! Not a bad class if you know your stuff...

Going to his class is entertaining because he's funny and sings. But he doesn't go through the material fast enough, his homework assignments and labs involve material before it is covered in class. What he says in class is easy to understand, but he goes on tangents also.

he was fun (sang opera every day at the end of class, went off on funny tangents, had a preoccupation with the blackboard erasers), but did not get through enough material each week--had to learn a lot of  it on my own just to be able to do the homeworks and labs
awesome professor. exams are very fair and very similar to practice exams (20% of it comes from homework problems). he definitely makes the class interesting with his photos from greece and opera.

105 was kinda hard.  Only because his freestyle lectures were not supplemented with a textbook, so exams were pretty tricky.  He doesn't TEACH you how to do the problems in class, just shows you what you should know, but with no textbook this is hard to deal with, though I gotta love his singing, family slide shows and random episodes of Buffy.

His exams sometimes tested things that were specific examples during lecture. There are 3 prelims, 1 final, and no homework. This saves you time, but also gives you less of a grade buffer. I hate orgo, and even though he was entertaining with singing and stories, I couldn't wait for the semester to end. His digressions got annoying after a while.
His lectures are not particularly useful in learning the material. Use them to see what material will be tested and use the book and study guide.
A great professor and a great guy.

I was a little irritated for the first month of class when it was clear that the Chem 357 people were moving faster than we were. Than, Usher's pesonality, his genuine love for students and the realization that less work in Orgo was not necesssarily a bad thing won me over.

I loved orgo.  That has to say something about the teacher.  Yes he sings and digresses, but if you take notes, do the reading and the (optional) problem sets, you should be fine.  The LSC review sessions were helpful too.
We don't learn much, but you gotta love David Usher.  He sings us Opera and tells funny stories.  His New Zealand accent is charming too!

Very good lecturer.  First half of the class is really easy, because he won't teach much.  During the second half of the class, he will start to do a lot of catch up and cover many chapters in a week.

Very engaging lecturer. Prelims were based directly from the text and lectures. True/False of DEATH were useless... He could have prepared us better for CHEM 360, but the course was nonetheless entertaining and demanding.
Classes were very engaging. I love orgo because of this man. He rocks.

Very entertaining. Will definitely keep your attention in class. But from a learning point of view, this prof doesn't really cut it. He goes off on to so many tangents that we do not learn as much. He does well on the stuff he gets to.
Usher is the best! He makes class interesting, he sings opera to us, and he has a great accent.  I wish he was my grandpa!

singing was great and entertaining, but he did not really care to teach us! 357 people learned more than we did! even their prelims were harder than ours! so 360 turned into hell, cause we did not know enough from 359.  But can't argue, he is a great guy
I came to this class to learn orgo, not to hear some guy play his guitar. Someone should fire him.
I suck at the class, but I love him anyway!
he is a funny dude, and sings opera in class sometimes!
He made orgo interesting, and his tests are fair... tricky, but fair if you know your stuff.
This guy is CrAzY!!  in a good way...
He sang to us! Charming accent.
Highly entertaining class, especially for orgo.  He even sang to us
Hilarious and quirky! Love his accent.

The class is very well organized, with plenty of help available when needed. Office hours are especially useful for clarifying material. The professor lectures clearly, though the course can be challenging without prior experience in proofs. Homework and exams are demanding but help reinforce key concepts and prepare you well.

The lectures are good, but the textbook is infinitely better (note: there's a slight difference between the lectures and the textbook with NFAs and Thompson's construction, because the textbook doesn't allow NFAs to have lambda transitions but the lectures do). The grading is also very pedantic.

anke is a fantastic cs2800 professor, definitely take it with her. lectures were extremely well prepared and interesting. the hw and prelims are definitely challenging, but they help you understand material at a deeper level and are still fairly manageable.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: ANKE MY GOAT!! Discrete math isn't easy but her lectures were very good at explaining difficult concepts -- I even ended up enjoying the subject! Tests are very fair/doable and we got a lot of good resources to prepare. The hw she writes also significantly improved my understanding. If you must take CS2800 take it with Anke.

Yes the class it hard but Anke makes it bearable. Nobody wants to take 2800 but she genuinely cares for her students and made the lectures very informative. I will say that the final was very hard. Honestly, if I had to take the class again, I would take it with Anke.

I have taken two classes with her and have not enjoyed either of them. She has an incredible ability to make content more confusing and boring at the same time. Her exams and homework are moderately difficult, but combined with the complete lack of support and poor lectures they become very hard. I would stay away from any of her future classes.
My favorite professor at Cornell, if you have the chance to take her courses, you should

Anke is an incredible lecturer who has an excellent grasp of the material and really challenges you to think during her lectures. I would definitely recommend going to class as much as possible if you have her.

This class was fairly difficult but Anke was a great professor and really helped to explain the material. Her lectures were very engaging and clear. The homework and tests were a bit hard but the class as a whole was a very positive experience largely due to Anke.

My advice, use the textbook, lecture slides were made off the textbook. Anke was enthusiastic and caring, but sometimes skimmed through topics. The key is to visit office hours, as long as you do HW well, you are set for the exam. Discrete math may require some creativity but once you notice the patterns its manageable. Poll for attendance.

Class is so hard but it's one of the hardest CS classes notoriously so I don't know what else you'd expect there. As a person, she's wonderful. Amazing lecturer and explains well. She cares deeply about her students as well and is absolutely lovely. You may not get an amazing grade in this class but it is a fair class overall.

Retaliates if a student knows more than her.
She is not as savvy as they portray her.

I took Algo with Professor Anke in FA23. Her lectures were easy to understand yet fascinating, delivered with humor, and provoked further exploration of topics through the textbook and other sources. The homework was hard, but really grinding it ensured success on exams. Her teaching has inspired me to continue studying algorithms more in-depth.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I took this class cause I had to. I was lowkey depressed juggling the workload of this class and other classes. Word of advice, find a really good TA and religiously go to their OH for the PSETs. She is a good lecturer, but the tests are extremely hard. Think around 55% on each test. Also, don't cheat for any hw, she puts up fake answers online.

This class is the definition of artificial difficulty. PSET problems are intentionally detached from the actual material we learn in class solely to make them more difficult. I would learn far more with completion-based PSET grading rather than flushing my time down the toilet. Camping office hours should never be required to do well on homework.

Anke van Zuylen is a passionate lecture who makes discrete much more interesting than daunting. Psets are typically very doable, and are graded rather fairly. Office hours are super extensive, and certain TAs are just as passionate and good at explaining the material as Anke is. Office hours are incredibly well structured, class is interesting.

Anke van Zuylen is a passionate lecturer who makes a daunting subject interesting. Problem Sets can be difficult, but are intellectually stimulating. Office Hours are hosted by TAs who are just as passionate as Anke about the subject, so they are a great source of additional help, and are very structured.

Lectures are generally done well and the material is presented in an engaging way, but the material does not always carry over to the assignments. It is very possible to attend all the lectures, understand the material, and still pull C's on exams because of harsh grading.
Was incredibly misled by past reviews. In class lectures are superficial and do not carry over well to HWs and exams.
Good prof, but I am straight up not having a good time.
Wouldnt take unless absolutely necessary or have a background in PROOF BASED math. Dont be fooled by the high ratings, not a bad professor but incredibly overhyped. Exams and psets are challenging.
The psets and exams were so difficult I considered dropping out multiple times.
I highly recommend if you can wait a semester to take it with a different professor if they have easier hws/prelims her prelims and hws were both really hard

One of the best professors I have ever encountered. I love how she is open to hearing any of the ideas we have and truly cares about each of the students.
Suggestions for taking Anke's course: listen carefully and follow tight in lecture, clear up questions right after the lecture. And you are in good shape!
good

Anke's lectures were probably the best I've ever attended at Cornell. She wrote them out on blank slides every class, allowing us to go through content at a reasonable pace. Also very willing to discuss questions. The textbook, although not strictly necessary, was highly useful in doing well. Definitely recommend!!!

Anke's lectures were incredibly well put together and went through every detail of course content in excellent detail. Homework was sometimes lengthy, but definitely worth it in improving understanding.

Anke was one of the best professors I've had. She was proficient at teaching the course material and consistently motivated ideas. She also clearly cares about the mental health of her students, as she decreased the difficulty of the class halfway through. That said, this was also one of the hardest courses I've taken, which is normal for algo.

Anke is very sympathetic and cares about her students. She is an engaging professor and conveys the information well. It is difficult to find good STEM professors, but she is one of them. I have heard amazing things from other classes she teaches as well. Highly recommend taking a course taught by her.

I'm not completely sure how this professor is at Cornell. I've ever met a less engaging and more annoying person in my life. I mean truly the worst experience of my academic career. I look at the clock and it doesn't move in this class. I've learned nothing. Made a course that should be interesting so incredibly boring.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The least engaging course I've ever sat through its a complete an utter waste of time and money. They cannot lecture whatsoever. My friend is sleeping as I write this

ICL is an easy class, no idea why the grade is this low. Her section is about Europe (particularly France/Germany), which is probably the section you'll go into with the most understanding. questions 1-5 on the final are worth 10% each. DO THE LAST QUESTION FIRST its worth 50% and takes a lotta time. Lectures were interesting readings were helpful
Solid professor.

Writing this before I get my grade back on the first exam. She assigns ~50 pgs of reading/week and isn't the best lecturer. IMO she could improve on linking examples in class to key concepts needed for exams. (this is coming from someone with a >4.0 GPA). This class would also REALLY benefit from having a discussion section (idk how it doesn't)
How does she expect students to write so much techincal explanation requried writings in such  a short span
She can't teach, the study guide could be alot better but is making the whole process a torment.
Lots of reading for this class but if you keep up and pay attention, and work on the study guide you will do well. In class, you sometimes have discussions with your peers.

The worst lecturer of the four professors in this class. Painfully boring, disengaging lectures, and a droning voice. I'm unsure how she became a professor because she has zero public speaking skills. The content is all over the place and doesn't follow a logical sequence. I managed to get an "A" by cramming the study guide.

Good lectures with a lot of reading. The professor makes the class interesting with a good balance of lectures and discussions. Tests are based on the study guide, but grading is done by TAs, which can vary. My TA was harsh and inconsistent. Beware of the final and potential grading issues, but the professor is nice, and you'll learn a bit
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Enjoyed her portion of 2350 the most. Very clear lecture slides and teaching. If you do the readings and pay attention in class it is easy to do well.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Gives good feedback--Respected

reviews.comment: I had her for ILRIC 2350. She seemed relatively uninterested in the class, and it was her first time teaching it as far as I understand. Her lectures were confusing, and the readings were hard. I had to read over and over again to understand. She did give us a study guide though, which ended up really just being the test questions so that was nice.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Assigns a TON of reading, which can be annoying especially for a writing course. No exams, but there are a lot of papers. She gives pretty solid feedback. Discussion posts are due for each class, which can be annoying, but you don't have to overdo it. Participation is required, but isn't too hard due to the open ended nature of the class's content.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--So many papers

reviews.comment: Don't take this class if you don't like reading or writing. TON of readings and discussion boards due b4 every class. She is a pretty nice/fair grader on the papers but omg lots of work. She expects a lot from her students. There are no tests but you will have a 15 pg research paper due at the end of the semester. You must participate in class
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--So many papers

reviews.comment: Writing assignments were primarily completed in class, which means the only real homework was reading! This was my favorite class ever. We read engaging books, watched an awe-inspiring documentary, and had our emotions wrenched by two spectacular films. I loved this class because I feel more knowledgeable and more intellectual for having taken it.
Amazing lecturer. Gets sidetracked sometimes but mostly interesting, educational content. Dry humor. Tough grader at first, but gives great steps to improve.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: ITAL 1113 is one of the best courses I've taken. Her lectures were incredible, each on a different reading focused on historical and social contexts of the 20th century. She is also very kind and really cares about her students. A great class!

Professoressa is such a delight to learn from. Having had her for 2 semesters now, I can confidently say that she is one of the most understanding professors here at Cornell! If you are going to learn Italian I HIGHLY, HIGHLY, HIGHLY recommend it be with her. You will thank yourself for picking one of the best Cornell professors around!

Professoressa KVW was my favorite professor I have had at Cornell so far. She is so elegant, intelligent, caring, and has an incredible sense of humor. There was a lot of daily homework that was went over each class (she cold calls), but it is not too hard to complete and is helpful. She genuinely cares a lot about her students and the class a lot.

Best professor i've ever had at this school. She is the coolest, nicest, funniest teacher who truly wants you to succeed. Never a dull moment in the class either. Would highly highly highly recommend taking any class you can with her.

I loved this course!! I left the class with a concrete understanding of Jewish Italian life before, during, and after WW2. We had a lot of reading and were given short quizzes on each reading. The majority of writing was short, nontraditional pieces that allowed me to apply my learning to emails, grant applications, and fictional pieces.

She is an incredible person in every way. She is very knowledgeable and hilarious. Every class is a lot of fun with her. This course requires regular studying, and there is a lot of homework, but it is not an issue as long as you pay attention to what is being done in class. She is also very understanding when you have a problem.

Hands down one of the best classes I've taken at Cornell and the best language class over all. Professor Wittelsbach is not only a great teacher but extremely kind and considerate. The work load is pretty intensive but as long as you conplete it diligently you will do well on the exams and projects.

Professoressa was the best Italian teacher I had. She is so funny and also really good at explaining difficult grammar. She was never mean or aggressive like other Cornell italian teachers were when explaining even the most basic things. There is a lot of homework but you go over it in class and she explains everything.

Professoressa is one of the smartest, most caring professors I have had at Cornell. This is an amazing class that teaches both a broad overview and the nuances of Italian art, architecture, and fashion. Very engaging lectures with minimal homework (only meets once per week). Would highly recommend to anyone with any Italian background!

Took this class due to the lack of other FWS available. On the first day, she said she would assign the maximum number of pages of reading per week and assigned a quiz for the second day of class. Heard from a friend that quiz was extremely specific and needed to know many miniscule details. The first lecture was extremely boring.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Homework was minimal for ITAL 1213. All the texts were in Italian - they're not easy, but the she breaks it down in class so you can understand it. The content was interesting, although it prioritizes breadth over depth. Professor von Wittelsbach is very knowledgeable and her passion is contagious.

I gained a love for Italy and Jewish history. The wide variety of topics covered provided a vivid picture of Jewish life in Italy as it evolved through time. Prof's knowledge on the subject is unparalleled, and her passion contagious. She brought guest speakers in with different perspectives and backgrounds, and I was encouraged to ask questions.

The professor is very knowledgeable and knows what she is doing. I've learned so much in just one semester and enjoyed the class a lot. Be prepared to study and have hw daily tho. I had a background in Spanish so it is easier, but I imagine a lot of effort needs to be put in if one doesn't know a Romance language. However, it is totally worth it!

Professor Von Wittelsbach was an amazing professor. She is extremely informed on the topic that she teaches and has a wide range of knowledge on this fascinating topic. This was the one class that I did not want to miss because I felt that I would miss out on true learning. Show effort and interest in the topic and class and you will do well.

Easily one of the best professors at Cornell. Clear passion for the language and loves to teach it. Each section is filled with cultural and historical anecdotes. Witty, funny, intelligent, and genuinely cares about each of her students. Strongly recommend Italian at Cornell, especially with KvW. Prepares well for study abroad as well. Must take!

Truly one of Cornell's finest. Professoressa offers a wealth of Italian language and cultural knowledge. Her class is work, but languages are her passion. She is very professional and cares about her students, rewarding genuine effort above all. She's incredibly witty and intelligent. Thanks to her, I feel quite prepared to study abroad in Italy.

Wittelsbach is a fantastic professor! The class is very speaking heavy, and there is a lot of paper homework that the class period is dedicated to checking over. There were tests, a final, and an oral exam, but they were easy (about one page long). You learn so much Italian in her class; she is hilarious!

It was a paper-based class, so will need to get textbook and print stuff. I personally liked the Friday lectures in language learning center because she showed us some fun materials besides textbook, such as music, opera and interviews.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Be prepared to speak in class but overall a great professor.
Great instructor who teaches a lot about Italian language and culture

I didn't really learn a lot of Italian from this class, although I did appreciate the Italian culture that was taught. I think it's hard to learn much when the course is held only once a week, but it did make homework easy. Be prepared to speak, whether it be holding a conversation or reading class material.

Extremely attentive and kind with a wealth of knowledge of Italian as well as other subjects; creates a positive, fun, focused environment; encourages students to be joyful and inquisitive; teaches many creative, effective strategies, applicable to other languages; thanks to her, I can now write, read books, listen to music and speak in Italian.

Professoressa is easily one of the best professors I have had at Cornell. Each seminar was planned very well and classes were filled with cultural and language anecdotes. It really is amazing how much of the language you learn in one semester. Don't be scared by the 6 credits -- the work is fun and manageable. Must take a class with Professoressa!

Easily the best professor I've had at Cornell. Professoressa von Wittelsbach is witty and sharp, and beyond Italian language, she brings many cultural notes that textbooks don't teach. 1401 was work but honestly very manageable--don't let it being six credits scare you. I improved so much thanks to her and am even pursuing an Italian minor now

Wow. There is not much to say. One of the most professional professors I have ever had in my educational career. Although the class has a lot of work, it is very manageable and super interesting. Professor Von Wittelsbach has a passion for what she is teaching and it shows. Take this class!

Professor von Wittelsbach is witty and caring. Her lectures are so engaging and detailed, and she covers the material well. In class, she tries to form close relationships with each member of the class. Each session is delightful and full of learning. Highly recommend to take this FWS and potentially an Italian language class with Professora.

Professoressa von Wittelsbach is a wonderful and passionate lecturer who truly cares about each of her students and ensuring that they learn the language. This class was interesting and I had a lot of fun with interacting with the language in various ways and it helped me strengthen my knowledge in Italian. It was a great experience overall.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: I was originally not signed up for Professor Von Wittelsbach's FWS, but I am so happy that I moved into her class. Every class is very entertaining, with tons of personal anecdotes and quips. This class is somewhat reading-heavy, but every book we read was super interesting. Professor Von Wittelsbach is truly one of the most wonderful professors.

Take this class!!!!! It's amazing how much Professor von Wittelsbach genuinely cares about her students learning the material. She's also extremely knowledgeable and a very entertaining lecturer who isn't afraid to speak her mind. Her brilliance shines in every class and I will definitely remember some of her quotes/advice for years to come.

Professoressa overall is a good professor. I will say this class was much more challenging than Italian 1 and 2, and was more commitment than I was expecting for a simple language requirement. However, I will give credit where credit is due and say that Von Wittelsbach pushed me out of my comfort zone and did improve my knowledge of the language.
no comment
Everybody has to take Professoressa's class once at least. So witty, intelligent, and honest (also beautiful as a bonus!). Learning a language has never been more fun.

I was only so lucky to have taken Professoressa von Wittelsbach's Italian class for my last semester, but I was able to make considerable progress in my understanding of the Italian language and culture. Her lectures sprinkled with personal anecdotes and references from Latin, Greek and many other languages were always interesting.

I feel very lucky having participated in this wonderful course of Professor Von Wittelsbach. She is caring, humorous, and very methodological regarding the learning approach. It helps us a great deal get used to new languages and absorb hard grammar material. Every lesson was filled with laugh. I had a great time being her student.

Genuinely Professoressa von Wittelsbach is such an amazing lecturer. She has a very rare kind of old-school intelligence you do not see often in professors here (most just want to churn out students who can repeat the right things). If you want to learn the cultural nuances of Italian in a chill environment, she is definitely the professor to take.

Professor von Wittelsbach is without doubt one of the best professors I've had at Cornell. Not only is she an engaging lecturer, but she also really cares about her students. She never hesitated to give extra time on an assignment when I needed it and was sure to give really helpful and detailed feedback so I could do well. 

Definitely take!!!
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: This class was extremely difficult, but it improved my Italian skills like no other. Profesoressa definitely demanded a lot from us, but I thought that it was worth it in the end. I didn't realize that I learned so much until halfway through the semester.
Get ready to be yelled at, have random work assigned at random times of the day due two hours later, and not want to come to class!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Beware of pop quizzes--So many papers

reviews.comment: Loved Professor Wittelsbach's class so much! Definitely take. She's super caring and is a great lecturer

She is without hesitation the finest educator I encountered at Cornell, with an extraordinary ability to connect with students on a personal level while, on an academic level, encouraging them beyond their comfort zone: a critical ingredient for building linguistic confidence.

Took this 6 credits/5 days a week. Sign up if you want public humiliation, class anxiety, and a lot of work. I admit to learning a lot of Italian and great prep for 2201 but very tense Zoom classroom. Only had her camera on once in the whole semester. All the A/A+, best students were previously fluent in a Romance language.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: For ITAL1401, don't expect her to hold your hand. You'll need to do lots of work outside of class, but really it's your fault if you signed up for an intensive and can't handle it. She's a bit scary at first, but after a few weeks you'll realize that as long as you prepare, she's an excellent guide, super understanding, and genuinely hilarious!

Professoressa von Wittelsbach is one of the most caring, compassionate, and kind people you'll have teaching you. During covid, she was always ready to be flexible and understand that we might need more time to finish assignments. Her lectures were also always interesting and given with humour and wit! TAKE her class!!

Professoressa knows what shes doing. Its crazy how much Italian you learn in one semester, and it really doesnt feel like work. You have to study for this class, but the tests are fair and very stressful. She has an amazing heart, and cares about her students a lot.
There really is no reason not to take this class. Profesoressa is funny, smart, and really cares about her students. If you show up, do the readings, and be respectful, youll do well.

Professor von Wittelsbach is amazing! Every class is interesting, and you not only learn about Jewish Italian Lit and writing, but also about culture and life. This class helped me become well-rounded and to stand out in job interviews. Although the class isnt easy, the workload is manageable and her grading is very fair.

Professor von Wittelsbach was the best professor I've had at Cornell. Her teaching made my writing extremely concise and I genuinely enjoyed attending her class. If you have the space in your schedule, you should make an active effort to include her class.

Professoressa made lectures interesting and fun while allowing you to practice Italian with peers in a comfortable environment. She also provides helpful study tips and does all she can to help you succeed in learning the language. Follow directions closely, participate in class, and send all your emails to her in Italian! Great professor.

1401 was the first Italian class I had ever taken and Professoressa von Wittelsbach pushed and encouraged me to learn the language with every class. Her teaching style is a great balance of challenging, but fair and rewarding if you put in the work. She genuinely cares about your progress in learning the Italian language and culture. Great class!

Professor von Wittelsbach truly cares about her students. She provides thorough feedback on assignments, and is always willing to help. The material was interesting, valuable, and the classes were very engaging. The coursework was heavy at times, but manageable because of how understanding she is. I would strongly recommend this FWS!

Participation and preparation matter in Prof. von Wittelsbach's courses. But if you put in the (usual) effort, you will be rewarded with a great grade. Moreover, I think Prof. is the kindest professor on campus. Always willing to help out with coursework and with ventures outside the classroom. Must take a class with Prof. before graduation!

Professor von Wittelsbach was one of the most accommodating and understanding professors I have had this semester. The coursework was engaging, fun, and manageable. She grades assignments thoroughly and provides great feedback. The class was collaborative and very interesting. I recommend this course to everyone as it was my favorite this semester!
Not a fun class. Very demanding with unhelpful and rude professor

FRESHMEN, TAKE THIS FWS!!! One of the most interesting classes I took my freshman year. Professor von Wittlesbach is so knowledgeable, and I could have listened to her talk about anything. A great mix of reading, movies, and guest lecturers. You learn essay, email, and fiction writing. I think everyone got an A, some an A+

This is the best class that I have ever taken. Professor Von Wittelsbach enhances the class with interesting materials and top notch lecturers. Lectures were given by journalists, authors and filmmakers. She was very accessible after class. For someone who has never been to Italy, my experience in this class makes me feel as if I had.

Professor von Wittelsbach was an amazing professor in my FWS ITAL1113. I highly recommend taking her FWS as you not only learn a lot about the course material but Professor von Wittelsbach is simply a well of knowledge who is ready to share her brilliance with you at any time. You simply have to ask.

One of my favorite professors! I loved GOVT3323 & Italian-Jewish History FWS with Kora! Always so helpful and accessible for questions, she managed to make in-class discussions insightful & gave constructive feedback on papers. She seamlessly oversaw different activities for GOVT CiT & made sure everyone was safe. I learned so much from her!

One of the best professors I've had the honor to learn from. Not only did she go above and beyond during the Cornell in Turin program, she mentored me and helped me even after the program. From CiT in 2015, we are still very much in touch and I can always count on her for a recommendation letter and guidance. She truly cares about the students!

My favorite professor, whom I cannot recommend enough. Not only is Prof. KvB attentive to all of her students, she is one of the most diligent and inspiring instructors/administrators I have ever had. The GOVT CiT course in Turin was expansive but never overwhelming, as were the Italian courses I have taken with KvB in Ithaca. Can't go wrong!

This is by far the most outstanding prof. that I have ever had the opportunity to work with. Not only did she lead the course well (CiT), but she has served as a continuous mentor even after I left her classroom. Because of her support and encouragement, I was able to pursue an internship which related to the class that she led. Highly recommend.

A wonderful Professor and person. Professor Wittlesbach cares about her students both inside and outside the classroom. She has been one of the nicest Professors I have met at Cornell. Definitely take!
Really tough grader. Class was not enjoyable
A wonderful intellectual and always engaging.
She's really nice and definitely cares about her students. As long as you put in the effort you will get a good grade.

Professor von Wittelsbach truly cares not only about her students' learning, but also their well-being. She's very understanding if you need an extension, let alone being an excellent teacher. She's really funny and makes the class feel like a mix of shabby-chic and professionalism (you have to have her if you wanna know what I mean).

Brilliant professor. The way she incorporated her knowledge of Mediterranean literature, history, culture and her personal experiences of the Mediterranean made every seminar extremely interesting! She is very sweet and approachable, cares a lot about her students and is very passionate about what she is teaching. I truly learned so much from her.

Professoressa is one of the most incredible teachers you will meet at Cornell. Not only does she teach you Italian, she also elaborates on Italian culture and extends on modern issues around the world. She is genuinely interested in student development, and will take the time to help you if you request. A true romanticist of modern times.

Professoressa is the greatest teacher ever. She is the type of teacher who is there to grow your mind and help you think, and is not just to show off how brilliant she is (even though she is ridiculously brilliant). Definitely take her classes. Her lectures are interesting, inspiring, and engaging. She is also incredibly sweet and an great dresser.
She's brilliant, as a teacher and as a scholar. One of the best learning experiences at Cornell.

Govt 3323 class in Turin, Italy: the BEST class at Cornell so far! Professor W and other great instructors from C and Turin, varied and interesting readings (a lot of them!), great weather, food, a whole bunch of trips to museums and out of town. A+

Very wonderful professor. She knows a lot about Italian culture and language. She is a very sophisticated and intelligent woman with a sharp sense of humor. Highly recommended! She does expect a lot from her students but if you show that you are dedicated you will have a great time in her class. She even took us out for coffee to Stella's!

Kora is by far the best professor I ever had at Cornell. She is incredible in so many levels: intellectually, spiritually, charismatically, you name it. Her courses are so inspiring, challenging, and interesting, that even a bad course you might be taking will seem fun when you have her. She certainly deserves eternal life after this one.
Loved it - the best professor in the romance department! Lots of homework, though.
Amazing professor,in every way!

She is a wonderful woman; highly intelligent, interesting, and committed to teaching you Italian. She is intimidating at first, but if you show interest she is quite good. She is very helpful if you do your work and try to do well. Also - she speaks like 10+ languages..no joke. If you have the opportunity to take an Italian class with her, I would!
She is brilliant--conducts the class in a way that really helps you learn. Great sense of humor--class goes by really fast.

By far the most amazing professor I have ever had. Shame on the students who gave Kora low ratings; everyone should try to take a class with this amazing person. If you show interest in her class, she will go above and beyond for you. She pushes her students to be the best they can be, and she is the most interesting person I have met at Cornell!
She's great!!
it's thanks to this professor that i am now considering spending a year abroad (italy). very motivating.
Great professor, brilliant teacher, attractive and sassy woman. Do your work and show up to class, be interested in the material or she'll kill you, she may also be royalty

She seems very unapproacable, which at times she is. As much as I disliked the pressure and time commitment of the class, I learned a lot. She definately doesn't give you an A for showing up, but if you do the work and put in effort she'll notice it and bump your grade if it's not so hot. Take her if you actually want to learn.

Didn't really appreciate her she wasn't my teacher anymore. She knows ALOT about everything related to Italian. What i liked most about her class was, I didn't only improve my italian easily, but i also learned about italian culture, movies, literature, and history. She integrates everything italian into her class and makes it intersting!

Stylish, sassy, smart, sharp. Brilliant professor. The ones who complain about her are generally the students who show up unprepared and who don't do the work - she won't coddle you and pat you on the head just for showing up. Do the work, get to know her outside of class, and you'll be rewarded four times over. Very witty lady.
She is excellent. A fascinating person who conducts a spectacular class. YOu learn Italian effortlessly, through enjoyable assignments. Its a privilege to be in her class.
Her class is hard but she is a hilarious woman. She is also extremely knowledgable in many areas. If you want to actually learn Italian and have fun while in class have Kora as your section leader.

She's an excellent professor if you are looking to learn. You won't like her if you want an easy class-- she expects a lot from her students. She may not seem too approachable, but she is excellent to talk to-- very knowledgable.  Best class I've taken at Cornell-- If you keep up with her, you'll learn sooo much Italian (language and culture!).
Excellent!

She's very knowledgable, but her class is hard. She expects a lot of her students, but the work sometimes seems random. The movies we watched did nothing for my Italian skills. We didn't really talk in class all that much either. Alas you have to take it to continue in the language.

She has a sharp sense of humor, is demanding and very European. I was often nervous going to section because she isnt the most patient or forgiving person. That being said, I really liked Italian and got alot out of Kora's class -Including a 4 credit A+

She made us buy a $50 book that was totally on the syllabus and then we NEVER USED IT ONCE.  NOT ONCE.  Also, even though the class was Composition and Conversation, all we did was do grammar exercises.  It was so boring I could die.
No Comments
She's so tough compared to the other professors.  If I were you, get as far away from her as possible.  Transfer into another section.
Bar none the worst language Prof. I have ever encounted! Stay away! Stay away! You have been warned.
No Comments
No Comments
No Comments
No Comments

The grade for this class is primarily based on attendance. Each class, you have to submit printed copies of either three poems or three pages of prose. There is no feedback on these submissions unless you bring them to workshop classes. Prof. was often very serious during class, which made her feel unapproachable and discouraged participation.

Lyrae is one of the most incredible teachers, and inspiring people I have ever met. I came into her class hating poetry and left after every class understanding something knew about poetry, literature, and life. She cares about her students, engages the class and facilitates great discussions. Take her class. She's amazing.

Lyrae is art incarnate. She's inspirational and brings out the artist in everyone. If you don't have semi-thick skin expect some exfoliation, especially if you have little exposure to creative writing. But if you stick through it I promise she'll make you a better writer and a better human. I love her  and you'll love her. Go for it.

Lyrae is a walking inspiration. I took Advanced Poetry with her, and I can honestly say that she completely changed my perspective on writing, and transformed me from a dabbler to a poet. She has given me confidence. She is also sassy and hilarious. Take advantage of this opportunity to take a class with such an amazing human being.

S:106. Avoid if possible. More than half of the initial class dropped w/in the first week. Harsh and biased grader. Quite mean when things don't go her way. Has stated that she does not like the KIfW. Assigns "group essays". Course books are perverse &/or boring. Deceptively changed the course title. Does not use internet. Great poet, bad teacher.

The slides genuinely do not help you when you are reviewing and you have to come to every class to understand. She doesn't teach you all the content you need to know. The multiple-choice questions are sometimes confusing. The content is pretty interesting though.

Professor Zayas is a great lecturer and made class engaging! The key concepts she provides for each unit were super helpful for studying for exams. The grading system is clear and unbiased, and I found attending lecture to be incredibly useful.

Zayas is a really great lecturer. I found class very engaging and interesting. However, there is a LOT of content to cover in this class. Lecture can move very fast and cover a wide breadth of content. Prelims also cover a ton of content and can be hard if not prepared. Extra credit is nice though.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Zayas is a great teacher and this class was very interesting. Much of the final grade is based off of three prelims and an optional final and there are smaller assignments throughout the semester with generous extra credit offered. While the class was fairly easy, there are definitely a few topics that can be difficult to understand.

Really interesting subject matter and really good use of real world examples (ex: from The Big Bang Theory). While the tests are difficult and sometimes confusingly worded, Prof. Zayas gives extra credit opportunities as well as easy bi-weekly discussion posts and open notes quizzes to get your grade up. If you put in the effort it will pay off!
