Loved this class and professor! You have to go to lecture. The exams are based on lecture materials. Readings are optional. Plenty of opportunity to do well -- drops the lowest exam grade and there is extra credit. Prof. Z.'s lectures are engaging. Would highly recommend.

go to lectures since slides are impossible to understand. super content heavy, 2 pages of learning objectives per lecture. prelims are tough and weirdly worded, need to understand everything about every concept. extra credit is worth 4% so do it. dont cram, try to understand the big picture of how things are connected and why we need to know them

This was a fun class. I enjoyed taking the personality tests and learning about Freud. Grading is based on exams, but Professor Zayes gives quizzes throughout. I mostly enjoyed the lectures and the use of videos to illustrate concept. Although there is a textbook, the exams are based on lectures.

I loved this class! The professor makes the topics engaging and relates it to daily life. I loved the videos of the big bang theory and the office. I have a hard time paying attention in lecture, but I enjoyed these. The prelims are multiple choice and short answer and you can drop the lowest grade, and there is extra credit. Highly recommend!

The comments here are unfair. Prof. Zayas is an engaging lecturer, caring, and funny. Her exams are tough, but there is a significant curve and easy to get an A. But you need to attend class and review the lecture notes.

Exams 90% of your grade. Gives no practice questions for exams. Chapters of assigned reading (barely helps).  Lecture slides are packed with information so she oftentimes finishes the class by speeding through the rest of the material. If you want to take this class out of interest, find another class unless you are willing to devote a lot of time.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I don't know how or why she is allowed and continues to teach. She is the sole reason for a decline in my mental health this semester. I reached out asking how to best approach the final (which has no study material) & her response was just shy of "go to h-ll". If she needed 50,000 signatures to be fired, I would ensure 200,000 before end of day.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Took this class since it seemed like an interesting topic and a break from my STEM-heavy course load. The material is interesting enough, but Professor Zayas makes the exams quite difficult and has not mentioned a curve despite low averages. She is generally unapproachable, and her TA's are unaccommodating. I would not recommend it.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: By far the worst prof at Cornell and I have been taught by notoriously difficult professors in classes with far more difficult course content (STEM). Do everything in your power to never take this class. RUN. Worst grade at Cornell in all my years here.
Worst prof at Cornell hands down

Lectures and readings did not line up at all. Extremely vague and arbitrary grading on general concepts that would be marked wrong if it wasn't exactly what the TA said. Supposedly an Intro level but the quantity of information and the poor teaching made it more difficult than Organic Chemistry. 1/10.

Horrible prof. She took what would normally be an interesting intro subject into an extremely difficult and boring one. Her lectures are not clear, and her exams are specific to things she never addresses. Her TAs are harsh graders and take points off for no reason. She wants you to fail her class even though it is an INTRO psych class. Never again
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Zayas is very knowledgeable and her lectures are very interesting, though a bit long. Grade consists mostly of 3 prelims and an optional final that can make up for a prelim. Other assignments are a few canvas quizzes and assignments that are fairly easy. Overall the tests were hard but I loved the professor and there is SONA extra credit.
such a bad professor...unapproachable and has no empathy at all

Literally the worst class and professor. Is so disorganized, unapproachable, and un-empathetic. Piles on work and thinks it's ok and fair, doesn't post exam preparation materials until the day or two before the exam, and never answers emails. Worst class and worst professor I've ever had.
This was the hardest course I have ever taken at Cornell. This literally dropped my GPA. She is nice but her class is not nice. It is so hard.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Very knowledgeable and organized but, but an easy A she is not. Genuinely interested in her students and how to best support them. One of my favorite professors Ive taken this far.

Honestly thought I would have received a worse grade, but extra credit helped exponentially! Amazing ideas and concepts that are applicable, stayed awake every lecture. Test questions are tricky, so really know everything front to back. Worked the hardest for this class out of all of my classes. Use the learning objective questions!

Prof. Z is a great lecturer and a really cool person. She often cites her own studies which I love. You need to go to the lectures even though the slides are posted- otherwise you won't understand them. And you need to read the textbook and outside readings. It's work.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Honestly, pretty easy class. I took it as a freshman and had no problem with the class. The study guide IS EXACTLY the same as the prelim.. if you know the study guide, you WILL do well. The lectures helped but I skipped a bunch and still did fine because everything was posted online.. Readings weren't mandatory; you just have to know the main idea
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Clear grading criteria--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Class is much,much harder and more intensive than it should be. I have no clue why it isn't a 4 credit course for the amount of work it involves. Overall, not worth it if you just need an easy psych course. There are plenty of better, more interesting, less time consuming psych courses offered..

Very intelligent lecturer, but her class was incredibly challenging. You will need to study very diligently just to pull off anything above a B from personal experience. The amount of readings for the class are through the roof. I learned a lot from the class, but seriously, only take this class if you have strong interest in the topic.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Reading according to schedule is the key. She is a good lecturer but not at all an easy professor. Lecture will just help you understand but actual contents for the prelims will be from textbook and readings. Take advantages of all extra credit opportunities. Only take it if you're interested in this area. Challenging prelims but doable if you try.

There is a ton of reading, the exams are very hard. I find this class harder than organic chemistry, which is the worst class I've ever taken. The means are low, and there is no curve. Professor Zayas is a very good lecturer, though. Most of the information from lectures is not on the slides, so it is important to go to lecture.

Would recommend if you place out of intro. Personality is a complex topic, but Prof. Zayas is great and boils it down to six different paradigms' views. She is nice, mellow, and easy to like. Her tests are not unreasonable for an Ivy league school you just need to read every assigned passage and take diligent notes.
one of my favorite classes at cornell! the exams are reasonable but you have to study and there are lots of readings.
I really like this class. Professor Zayes is good lecturer and keeps it interesting. The materials is just interesting. I also think it is possible to do well if you get past the first prelim
I love this class! Material is super interesting, but there is a lot of it. Prof. Zayas is great. Engaging. The prelim is reasonable if you focus on the study guide.

Even if you think you know all the material, something is bound to pop up on every exam that you've never heard of. She's very clear during her lectures and makes the topic seem stupidly easy, so much so you won't want to read the assigned readings, but she WILL get you during the exams. Suggestion: READ ALL THE READINGS ACCORDING TO HER SCHEDULE!
Material is super interesting and the prof does a good job keeping it new. The exams are fine if you study.
FUn course. Love it! The tests are challenging, so you have to study! But there is a curve and extra credit options.
thumbs up. there is too much reading for the three credits, but it is possible to do really well, and the material is interesting.
Great professor; very intelligent
Best methods class on campus. Way more than 4 hours of credit worth of work. student led lectures are hit or miss. Prof. Z. should lecture more.
One of my favorite classes. Prof. Zayas is a dynamic lecturer, but can go off topic. There are two writing assignments and 3 exams. Topic is interesting, and it's easy to get an A.
best class i've taken at cornell so far! brings in tv clips from friends to explain concepts. exams are hard, but there is a curve.
fun course. profesor zayas keeps it interesting, but exams are long and nitpicky.
not an easy class. not an intro class for sure. exams are hard, but she curves. lectures are interesting with clips from tv shows.

This professor knows her stuff!  And though she may not be the easiest or clearest lecturer, she is able to clear up any confusion if you go to her after class or during office hours.  She is a new prof, so she is still getting the hang of teaching.  Interesting material, difficult tests.  But she practically hands you points (i-Clicker, essays).

this professor is horrible. she has awful lecturing skills and the majority of the class falls asleep during class! the only reason you'd go to class is for clicker points; she doesn't even calculate those properly. guest lectures are the only interesting part. i love psychology; her class crushed my expectations. the ta's are b*tchy and useless.
I loved psych until I took this class. By far the worst class I have ever taken. EVER.
Professor Zayas is really good at explaining complex ideas and terminology. She is insightful and interesting. and she's a great ultimate player.

The worst professor I have had so far. Completely destroyed my interest in psychology and has no idea how to lecture. Reads off the lecture slides yet refuses to post them before class. The only times I actually went to class I fell asleep. AVOID AT ALL COSTS!!!  to the poster two above me: she had to curve it (i.e. add points to everyone tests which is not curving) because everyone did so poorly. and then she told us that we needed to study better!

Shes a really good professor. She brings enthusiasm to the lectures, and explains things clearly. She tries to cover a little too much in lecture, so it can be hard 2 keep up. The test are challenging, but not that bad, they just can't be blown off.

She was helpful and unpretentious during office hours, and she's given me useful advice about what other classes to take at Cornell. She also helped me get a RA position in one of the social psych labs. I think she somewhat shy which leads people to think she's cold. She's obviously new at teaching, and that's where mots of her pitfalls lie.

Don't take this class unless you have to. The prof is unreasonable and makes the tests hard for no reason. When someone asked for a study guide, she said the 35 slide powerpoints for each lecture were the guide. For the final, she said the previous prelims were the guide, but she never gave them back and wouldn't let you have them. Ridiculous.

Useless class. Lecture is mindnumbingly boring (went maybe 3 times all semester), exams don't actually test real knowledge, the book is painful to read, and Zayas is new (it shows). That being said, I somehow managed to pull off an A-, though I attended class at most 5 times all semester. Maybe you can too.

One of the worst classes I've had at Cornell.  Tests trivial information, doesn't show up for her own review sessions.  She seems sweet but I don't care!  She is unpolished and I would strongly advise that if you want to learn about Personality Psych to simply buy the book.

Lectures: not engaging, dragging, undynamic, way too easy to skip. Tests difficult, not in the good challenging sense, but in that they don't really test your knowledge at all, but seemingly unimportant random minutia from lectures and textbook. Cold, distant, and unresponsive at office hours. As psych major, this class was a waste of my time.

She knows what she's talking about. She's new at teaching so there are some things she still is learning, but she's a very very smart person. The class can be interesting if you are into the material, but don't take if you're just looking for an easy elective

She is a good professor who knows what she's doing.  Lectures are occasionally boring, but they are always clear if you pay attention. She's incredibly sweet and helpful if you ever visit her office hours, and because she's new, people really take advantage by complaining/picking on her so much. Tough class, and well worth it.
BAD PROFESSOR. MAKES BAD TESTS AND CAN'T TEACH IF THE WORLD DEPENDED ON HER!

Her lectures are SOO boring -most people didn't show up. She basically read off the slides. Her tests were pretty hard and had lots of ambiguous questions (she usually ended up giving credit for 3+ questions because of ambiguity or just a bad question). She's new at teaching and it shows! This class could have been so much more interesting.

I would not recommend the prof.  Test are impossible and many questions are ambiguous.  She is new, and doesn't really know what she is doing yet. She was a little nasty at office hours and really fake. The material learned was really interesting even though she is so bad at teaching it. I'll probably get a A-, and I still don't like her.

No personality. Horrible lecture, can't master ppt slides (clicks through all the important stuff in a sec and spends 30 min on a blank slide)  Also can't write test questions well.  Random useless knowledge tested, not concepts (eg was a test given in tibetan or bushman, what was word 36 on page 321, etc) Speaks at 2 microdecibels. Poor TA contact
